Exodus27:3 Make pots for removing the ashes, as well as shovels, basins, forks and fire pans, all of which shall be of bronze." 
Exodus27:4 Make a grating of bronze network for it; this to have four bronze rings, one at each of its four corners." 
Exodus27:5 Put it down around the altar, on the ground. This network is to be half as high as the altar." 
Exodus27:6 You shall also make poles of acacia wood for the altar, and plate them with bronze." 
Exodus27:7 These poles are to be put through the rings, so that they are on either side of the altar when it is carried." 
Exodus27:8 Make the altar itself in the form of a hollow box, just as it was shown you on the mountain." 
Exodus27:9 You shall also make a court for the Dwelling. On the south side the court shall have hangings a hundred cubits long, woven of fine linen twined," 
Exodus27:10 with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze; the hooks and bands on the columns shall be of silver. 
Exodus27:11 On the north side there shall be similar hangings, a hundred cubits long, with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze; the hooks and bands on the columns shall be of silver." 
Exodus27:12 On the west side, across the width of the court, there shall be hangings, fifty cubits long, with ten columns and ten pedestals." 
Exodus27:13 The width of the court on the east side shall be fifty cubits. 
Exodus27:14 On one side there shall be hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals;" 
Exodus27:15 on the other side there shall be hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals." 
Exodus27:16 At the entrance of the court there shall be a variegated curtain, twenty cubits long, woven of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined. It shall have four columns and four pedestals." 
Exodus27:17 All the columns around the court shall have bands and hooks of silver, and pedestals of bronze." 
Exodus27:18 The enclosure of the court is to be one hundred cubits long, fifty cubits wide, and five cubits high. Fine linen twined must be used, and the pedestals must be of bronze." 
Exodus27:19 All the fittings of the Dwelling, whatever be their use, as well as all its tent pegs and all the tent pegs of the court, must be of bronze." 
Exodus27:20 You shall order the Israelites to bring you clear oil of crushed olives, to be used for the light, so that you may keep lamps burning regularly." 
Exodus27:21 From evening to morning Aaron and his sons shall maintain them before the LORD in the meeting tent, outside the veil which hangs in front of the commandments. This shall be a perpetual ordinance for the Israelites throughout their generations." 
Exodus28:1 From among the Israelites have your brother Aaron, together with his sons Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, brought to you, that they may be my priests." 
Exodus28:2 For the glorious adornment of your brother Aaron you shall have sacred vestments made. 
Exodus28:3 Therefore, to the various expert workmen whom I have endowed with skill, you shall give instructions to make such vestments for Aaron as will set him apart for his sacred service as my priest." 
Exodus28:4 These are the vestments they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a brocaded tunic, a miter and a sash. In making these sacred vestments which your brother Aaron and his sons are to wear in serving as my priests," 
Exodus28:5 they shall use gold, violet, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen." 
Exodus28:6 The ephod they shall make of gold thread and of violet, purple and scarlet yarn, embroidered on cloth of fine linen twined." 
Exodus28:7 It shall have a pair of shoulder straps joined to its two upper ends. 
Exodus28:8 The embroidered belt of the ephod shall extend out from it and, like it, be made of gold thread, of violet, purple and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined." 
Exodus28:9 Get two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel: 
Exodus28:10 six of their names on one stone, and the other six on the other stone, in the order of their birth." 
Exodus28:11 As a gem-cutter engraves a seal, so shall you have the two stones engraved with the names of the sons of Israel and then mounted in gold filigree work." 
Exodus28:12 Set these two stones on the shoulder straps of the ephod as memorial stones of the sons of Israel. Thus Aaron shall bear their names on his shoulders as a reminder before the LORD. 
Exodus28:13 Make filigree rosettes of gold," 
Exodus28:14 as well as two chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, and fasten the cordlike chains to the filigree rosettes." 
Exodus28:15 The breastpiece of decision you shall also have made, embroidered like the ephod with gold thread and violet, purple and scarlet yarn on cloth of fine linen twined." 
Exodus28:16 It is to be square when folded double, a span high and a span wide." 
Exodus28:17 On it you shall mount four rows of precious stones: in the first row, a carnelian, a topaz and an emerald;" 
Exodus28:18 in the second row, a garnet, a sapphire and a beryl;" 
Exodus28:19 in the third row, a jacinth, an agate and an amethyst;" 
Exodus28:20 in the fourth row, a chrysolite, an onyx and a jasper. These stones are to be mounted in gold filigree work," 
Exodus28:21 twelve of them to match the names of the sons of Israel, each stone engraved like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes." 
Exodus28:22 When the chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, have been made for the breastpiece," 
Exodus28:23 you shall then make two rings of gold for it and fasten them to the two upper ends of the breastpiece. 
Exodus28:24 The gold cords are then to be fastened to the two rings at the upper ends of the breastpiece," 
Exodus28:25 the other two ends of the cords being fastened in front to the two filigree rosettes which are attached to the shoulder straps of the ephod. 
Exodus28:26 Make two other rings of gold and put them on the two lower ends of the breastpiece, on its edge that faces the ephod." 
Exodus28:27 Then make two more rings of gold and fasten them to the bottom of the shoulder straps next to where they join the ephod in front, just above its embroidered belt." 
Exodus28:28 Violet ribbons shall bind the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod, so that the breastpiece will stay right above the embroidered belt of the ephod and not swing loose from it." 
Exodus28:29 Whenever Aaron enters the sanctuary, he will thus bear the names of the sons of Israel on the breastpiece of decision over his heart as a constant reminder before the LORD." 
Exodus28:30 In this breastpiece of decision you shall put the Urim and Thummim, that they may be over Aaron's heart whenever he enters the presence of the LORD. Thus he shall always bear the decisions for the Israelites over his heart in the LORD'S presence." 
Exodus28:31 The robe of the ephod you shall make entirely of violet material. 
Exodus28:32 It shall have an opening for the head in the center, and around this opening there shall be a selvage, woven as at the opening of a shirt, to keep it from being torn." 
Exodus28:33 All around the hem at the bottom you shall make pomegranates, woven of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen twined, with gold bells between them;" 
Exodus28:34 first a gold bell, then a pomegranate, and thus alternating all around the hem of the robe." 
Exodus28:35 Aaron shall wear it when ministering, that its tinkling may be heard as he enters and leaves the LORD'S presence in the sanctuary; else he will die." 
Exodus28:36 You shall also make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it, as on a seal engraving, 'Sacred to the LORD.'" 
Exodus28:37 This plate is to be tied over the miter with a violet ribbon in such a way that it rests on the front of the miter," 
Exodus28:38 over Aaron's forehead. Since Aaron bears whatever guilt the Israelites may incur in consecrating any of their sacred gifts, this plate must always be over his forehead, so that they may find favor with the LORD." 
Exodus28:39 The tunic of fine linen shall be brocaded. The miter shall be made of fine linen. The sash shall be of variegated work. 
Exodus28:40 Likewise, for the glorious adornment of Aaron's sons you shall have tunics and sashes and turbans made." 
Exodus28:41 With these you shall clothe your brother Aaron and his sons. Anoint and ordain them, consecrating them as my priests." 
Exodus28:42 You must also make linen drawers for them, to cover their naked flesh from their loins to their thighs." 
Exodus28:43 Aaron and his sons shall wear them whenever they go into the meeting tent or approach the altar to minister in the sanctuary, lest they incur guilt and die. This shall be a perpetual ordinance for him and for his descendants." 
Exodus29:1 This is the rite you shall perform in consecrating them as my priests. Procure a young bull and two unblemished rams. 
Exodus29:2 With fine wheat flour make unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers spread with oil," 
Exodus29:3 and put them in a basket. Take the basket of them along with the bullock and the two rams. 
Exodus29:4 Aaron and his sons you shall also bring to the entrance of the meeting tent, and there wash them with water." 
Exodus29:5 Take the vestments and clothe Aaron with the tunic, the robe of the ephod, the ephod itself, and the breastpiece, fastening the embroidered belt of the ephod around him." 
Exodus29:6 Put the miter on his head, the sacred diadem on the miter." 
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and anoint him with it, pouring it on his head." 
Exodus29:8 Bring forward his sons also and clothe them with the tunics," 
Exodus29:9 gird them with the sashes, and tie the turbans on them. Thus shall the priesthood be theirs by perpetual law, and thus shall you ordain Aaron and his sons." 
Exodus29:10 Now bring forward the bullock in front of the meeting tent. There Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on its head. 
Exodus29:11 Then slaughter the bullock before the LORD, at the entrance of the meeting tent." 
Exodus29:12 Take some of its blood and with your finger put it on the horns of the altar. All the rest of the blood you shall pour out at the base of the altar. 
Exodus29:13 All the fat that covers its inner organs, as well as the lobe of its liver and its two kidneys, together with the fat that is on them, you shall take and burn on the altar." 
Exodus29:14 But the flesh and hide and offal of the bullock you must burn up outside the camp, since this is a sin offering." 
Exodus29:15 Then take one of the rams, and after Aaron and his sons have laid their hands on its head," 
Exodus29:16 slaughter it. The blood you shall take and splash on all the sides of the altar. 
Exodus29:17 Cut the ram into pieces; its inner organs and shanks you shall first wash, and then put them with the pieces and with the head." 
Exodus29:18 The entire ram shall then be burned on the altar, since it is a holocaust, a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Exodus29:19 After this take the other ram, and when Aaron and his sons have laid their hands on its head," 
Exodus29:20 slaughter it. Some of its blood you shall take and put on the tip of Aaron's right ear and on the tips of his sons' right ears and on the thumbs of their right hands and the great toes of their right feet. Splash the rest of the blood on all the sides of the altar. 
Exodus29:21 Then take some of the blood that is on the altar, together with some of the anointing oil, and sprinkle this on Aaron and his vestments, as well as on his sons and their vestments, that his sons and their vestments may be sacred." 
Exodus29:22 Now, from this ram you shall take its fat: its fatty tail, the fat that covers its inner organs, the lobe of its liver, its two kidneys with the fat that is on them, and its right thigh, since this is the ordination ram;" 
Exodus29:23 then, out of the basket of unleavened food that you have set before the LORD, you shall take one of the loaves of bread, one of the cakes made with oil, and one of the wafers." 
Exodus29:24 All these things you shall put into the hands of Aaron and his sons, so that they may wave them as a wave offering before the LORD." 
Exodus29:25 After you have received them back from their hands, you shall burn them on top of the holocaust on the altar as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Exodus29:26 Finally, take the breast of Aaron's ordination ram and wave it as a wave offering before the LORD; this is to be your own portion." 
Exodus29:27 Thus shall you set aside the breast of whatever wave offering is waved, as well as the thigh of whatever raised offering is raised up, whether this be the ordination ram or anything else belonging to Aaron or to his sons." 
Exodus29:28 Such things are due to Aaron and his sons from the Israelites by a perpetual ordinance as a contribution. From their peace offerings, too, the Israelites shall make a contribution, their contribution to the LORD." 
Exodus29:29 The sacred vestments of Aaron shall be passed down to his descendants, that in them they may be anointed and ordained." 
Exodus29:30 The descendant who succeeds him as priest and who is to enter the meeting tent to minister in the sanctuary shall be clothed with them for seven days. 
Exodus29:31 You shall take the flesh of the ordination ram and boil it in a holy place. 
Exodus29:32 At the entrance of the meeting tent Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket. 
Exodus29:33 They themselves are to eat of these things by which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration; but no layman may eat of them, since they are sacred." 
Exodus29:34 If some of the flesh of the ordination sacrifice or some of the bread remains over on the next day, this remnant must be burned up; it is not to be eaten, since it is sacred." 
Exodus29:35 Carry out all these orders in regard to Aaron and his sons just as I have given them to you. Seven days you shall spend in ordaining them," 
Exodus29:36 sacrificing a bullock each day as a sin offering, to make atonement. Thus also shall you purge the altar in making atonement for it; you shall anoint it in order to consecrate it." 
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall spend in making atonement for the altar and in consecrating it. Then the altar will be most sacred, and whatever touches it will become sacred." 
Exodus29:38 Now, this is what you shall offer on the altar: two yearling lambs as the sacrifice established for each day;" 
Exodus29:39 one lamb in the morning and the other lamb at the evening twilight. 
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb there shall be a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil of crushed olives and, as its libation, a fourth of a hin of wine." 
Exodus29:41 The other lamb you shall offer at the evening twilight, with the same cereal offering and libation as in the morning. You shall offer this as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Exodus29:42 Throughout your generations this established holocaust shall be offered before the LORD at the entrance of the meeting tent, where I will meet you and speak to you." 
Exodus29:43 There, at the altar, I will meet the Israelites; hence, it will be made sacred by my glory." 
Exodus29:44 Thus I will consecrate the meeting tent and the altar, just as I also consecrate Aaron and his sons to be my priests." 
Exodus29:45 I will dwell in the midst of the Israelites and will be their God. 
Exodus29:46 They shall know that I, the LORD, am their God who brought them out of the land of Egypt, so that I, the LORD, their God, might dwell among them." 
Exodus30:1 For burning incense you shall make an altar of acacia wood," 
Exodus30:2 with a square surface, a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high, with horns that spring directly from it." 
Exodus30:3 Its grate on top, its walls on all four sides, and its horns you shall plate with pure gold. Put a gold molding around it." 
Exodus30:4 Underneath the molding you shall put gold rings, two on one side and two on the opposite side, as holders for the poles used in carrying it." 
Exodus30:5 Make the poles, too, of acacia wood and plate them with gold." 
Exodus30:6 This altar you are to place in front of the veil that hangs before the ark of the commandments where I will meet you. 
Exodus30:7 On it Aaron shall burn fragrant incense. Morning after morning, when he prepares the lamps," 
Exodus30:8 and again in the evening twilight, when he lights the lamps, he shall burn incense. Throughout your generations this shall be the established incense offering before the LORD." 
Exodus30:9 On this altar you shall not offer up any profane incense, or any holocaust or cereal offering; nor shall you pour out a libation upon it." 
Exodus30:10 Once a year Aaron shall perform the atonement rite on its horns. Throughout your generations this atonement is to be made once a year with the blood of the atoning sin offering. This altar is most sacred to the LORD. 
Exodus30:11 The LORD also said to Moses," 
Exodus30:12 When you take a census of the Israelites who are to be registered, each one, as he is enrolled, shall give the LORD a forfeit for his life, so that no plague may come upon them for being registered." 
Exodus30:13 Everyone who enters the registered group must pay a half-shekel, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel, twenty gerahs to the shekel. This payment of a half-shekel is a contribution to the LORD." 
Exodus30:14 Everyone of twenty years or more who enters the registered group must give this contribution to the LORD. 
Exodus30:15 The rich need not give more, nor shall the poor give less, than a half-shekel in this contribution to the LORD to pay the forfeit for their lives." 
Exodus30:16 When you receive this forfeit money from the Israelites, you shall donate it to the service of the meeting tent, that there it may be the Israelites' reminder before the LORD, of the forfeit paid for their lives.""" 
Exodus30:17 The LORD said to Moses," 
Exodus30:18 For ablutions you shall make a bronze laver with a bronze base. Place it between the meeting tent and the altar, and put water in it." 
Exodus30:19 Aaron and his sons shall use it in washing their hands and feet. 
Exodus30:20 When they are about to enter the meeting tent, they must wash with water, lest they die. Likewise when they approach the altar in their ministry, to offer an oblation to the LORD," 
Exodus30:21 they must wash their hands and feet, lest they die. This shall be a perpetual ordinance for him and his descendants throughout their generations.""" 
Exodus30:22 The LORD said to Moses," 
Exodus30:23 Take the finest spices: five hundred shekels of free-flowing myrrh; half that amount, that is, two hundred and fifty shekels, of fragrant cinnamon; two hundred and fifty shekels of fragrant cane;" 
Exodus30:24 five hundred shekels of cassia-all according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel; together with a hin of olive oil; 
Exodus30:25 and blend them into sacred anointing oil, perfumed ointment expertly prepared." 
Exodus30:26 With this sacred anointing oil you shall anoint the meeting tent and the ark of the commandments," 
Exodus30:27 the table and all its appurtenances, the lampstand and its appurtenances, the altar of incense" 
Exodus30:28 and the altar of holocausts with all its appurtenances, and the laver with its base." 
Exodus30:29 When you have consecrated them, they shall be most sacred; whatever touches them shall be sacred." 
Exodus30:30 Aaron and his sons you shall also anoint and consecrate as my priests. 
Exodus30:31 To the Israelites you shall say: As sacred anointing oil this shall belong to me throughout your generations. 
Exodus30:32 It may not be used in any ordinary anointing of the body, nor may you make any other oil of a like mixture. It is sacred, and shall be treated as sacred by you." 
Exodus30:33 Whoever prepares a perfume like this, or whoever puts any of this on a layman, shall be cut off from his kinsmen.""" 
Exodus30:34 The LORD told Moses, ""Take these aromatic substances: storax and onycha and galbanum, these and pure frankincense in equal parts;" 
Exodus30:35 and blend them into incense. This fragrant powder, expertly prepared, is to be salted and so kept pure and sacred." 
Exodus30:36 Grind some of it into fine dust and put this before the commandments in the meeting tent where I will meet you. This incense shall be treated as most sacred by you. 
Exodus30:37 You may not make incense of a like mixture for yourselves; you must treat it as sacred to the LORD. 
Exodus30:38 Whoever makes an incense like this for his own enjoyment of its fragrance, shall be cut off from his kinsmen.""" 
Exodus31:1 The LORD said to Moses," 
Exodus31:2 See, I have chosen Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah," 
Exodus31:3 and I have filled him with a divine spirit of skill and understanding and knowledge in every craft: 
Exodus31:4 in the production of embroidery, in making things of gold, silver or bronze," 
Exodus31:5 in cutting and mounting precious stones, in carving wood, and in every craft." 
Exodus31:6 As his assistant I have appointed Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan. I have also endowed all the experts with the necessary skill to make all the things I have ordered you to make:" 
Exodus31:7 the meeting tent, the ark of the commandments with the propitiatory on top of it, all the furnishings of the tent," 
Exodus31:8 the table with its appurtenances, the pure gold lampstand with all its appurtenances, the altar of incense," 
Exodus31:9 the altar of holocausts with all its appurtenances, the laver with its base," 
Exodus31:10 the service cloths, the sacred vestments for Aaron the priest, the vestments for his sons in their ministry," 
Exodus31:11 the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense for the sanctuary. All these things they shall make just as I have commanded you.""" 
Exodus31:12 The LORD said to Moses," 
Exodus31:13 You must also tell the Israelites: Take care to keep my sabbaths, for that is to be the token between you and me throughout the generations, to show that it is I, the LORD, who make you holy." 
Exodus31:14 Therefore, you must keep the sabbath as something sacred. Whoever desecrates it shall be put to death. If anyone does work on that day, he must be rooted out of his people." 
Exodus31:15 Six days there are for doing work, but the seventh day is the sabbath of complete rest, sacred to the LORD. Anyone who does work on the sabbath day shall be put to death." 
Exodus31:16 So shall the Israelites observe the sabbath, keeping it throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant." 
Exodus31:17 Between me and the Israelites it is to be an everlasting token; for in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, but on the seventh day he rested at his ease.""" 
Exodus31:18 When the LORD had finished speaking to Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the commandments, the stone tablets inscribed by God's own finger." 
Exodus32:1 When the people became aware of Moses' delay in coming down from the mountain, they gathered around Aaron and said to him, ""Come, make us a god who will be our leader; as for the man Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.""" 
Exodus32:2 Aaron replied, ""Have your wives and sons and daughters take off the golden earrings they are wearing, and bring them to me.""" 
Exodus32:3 So all the people took off their earrings and brought them to Aaron," 
Exodus32:4 who accepted their offering, and fashioning this gold with a graving tool, made a molten calf. Then they cried out, ""This is your God, O Israel, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.""" 
Exodus32:5 On seeing this, Aaron built an altar before the calf and proclaimed, ""Tomorrow is a feast of the LORD.""" 
Exodus32:6 Early the next day the people offered holocausts and brought peace offerings. Then they sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel." 
Exodus32:7 With that, the LORD said to Moses, ""Go down at once to your people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt, for they have become depraved." 
Exodus32:8 They have soon turned aside from the way I pointed out to them, making for themselves a molten calf and worshiping it, sacrificing to it and crying out, 'This is your God, O Israel, who brought you out of the land of Egypt!'" 
Exodus32:9 I see how stiff-necked this people is,"" continued the LORD to Moses." 
Exodus32:10 Let me alone, then, that my wrath may blaze up against them to consume them. Then I will make of you a great nation.""" 
Exodus32:11 But Moses implored the LORD, his God, saying, ""Why, O LORD, should your wrath blaze up against your own people, whom you brought out of the land of Egypt with such great power and with so strong a hand?" 
Exodus32:12 Why should the Egyptians say, 'With evil intent he brought them out, that he might kill them in the mountains and exterminate them from the face of the earth'? Let your blazing wrath die down; relent in punishing your people." 
Exodus32:13 Remember your servants Abraham, Isaac and Israel, and how you swore to them by your own self, saying, 'I will make your descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky; and all this land that I promised, I will give your descendants as their perpetual heritage.'""" 
Exodus32:14 So the LORD relented in the punishment he had threatened to inflict on his people. 
Exodus32:15 Moses then turned and came down the mountain with the two tablets of the commandments in his hands, tablets that were written on both sides, front and back;" 
Exodus32:16 tablets that were made by God, having inscriptions on them that were engraved by God himself." 
Exodus32:17 Now, when Joshua heard the noise of the people shouting, he said to Moses, ""That sounds like a battle in the camp.""" 
Exodus32:17 Now, when Joshua heard the noise of the people shouting, he said to Moses, ""That sounds like a battle in the camp.""" 
Exodus32:18 But Moses answered, ""It does not sound like cries of victory, nor does it sound like cries of defeat; the sounds that I hear are cries of revelry.""" 
Exodus32:19 As he drew near the camp, he saw the calf and the dancing. With that, Moses' wrath flared up, so that he threw the tablets down and broke them on the base of the mountain." 
Exodus32:20 Taking the calf they had made, he fused it in the fire and then ground it down to powder, which he scattered on the water and made the Israelites drink." 
Exodus32:21 Moses asked Aaron, ""What did this people ever do to you that you should lead them into so grave a sin?"" Aaron replied, ""Let not my lord be angry." 
Exodus32:22 You know well enough how prone the people are to evil. 
Exodus32:23 They said to me, 'Make us a god to be our leader; as for the man Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.'" 
Exodus32:24 So I told them, 'Let anyone who has gold jewelry take it off.' They gave it to me, and I threw it into the fire, and this calf came out.""" 
Exodus32:25 When Moses realized that, to the scornful joy of their foes, Aaron had let the people run wild," 
Exodus32:26 he stood at the gate of the camp and cried, ""Whoever is for the LORD, let him come to me!"" All the Levites then rallied to him," 
Exodus32:27 and he told them, ""Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Put your sword on your hip, every one of you! Now go up and down the camp, from gate to gate, and slay your own kinsmen, your friends and neighbors!""" 
Exodus32:28 The Levites carried out the command of Moses, and that day there fell about three thousand of the people." 
Exodus32:29 Then Moses said, ""Today you have been dedicated to the LORD, for you were against your own sons and kinsmen, to bring a blessing upon yourselves this day.""" 
Exodus32:30 On the next day Moses said to the people, ""You have committed a grave sin. I will go up to the LORD, then; perhaps I may be able to make atonement for your sin.""" 
Exodus32:31 So Moses went back to the LORD and said, ""Ah, this people has indeed committed a grave sin in making a god of gold for themselves!" 
Exodus32:32 If you would only forgive their sin! If you will not, then strike me out of the book that you have written.""" 
Exodus32:33 The LORD answered, ""Him only who has sinned against me will I strike out of my book." 
Exodus32:34 Now, go and lead the people whither I have told you. My angel will go before you. When it is time for me to punish, I will punish them for their sin.""" 
Exodus32:35 Thus the LORD smote the people for having had Aaron make the calf for them. 
Exodus33:1 The LORD told Moses, ""You and the people whom you have brought up from the land of Egypt, are to go up from here to the land which I swore to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob I would give to their descendants." 
Exodus33:2 Driving out the Canaanites, Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites, I will send an angel before you" 
Exodus33:3 to the land flowing with milk and honey. But I myself will not go up in your company, because you are a stiff-necked people; otherwise I might exterminate you on the way.""" 
Exodus33:4 When the people heard this bad news, they went into mourning, and no one wore his ornaments." 
Exodus33:5 The LORD said to Moses, ""Tell the Israelites: You are a stiff-necked people. Were I to go up in your company even for a moment, I would exterminate you. Take off your ornaments, therefore; I will then see what I am to do with you.""" 
Exodus33:6 So, from Mount Horeb onward, the Israelites laid aside their ornaments." 
Exodus33:7 The tent, which was called the meeting tent, Moses used to pitch at some distance away, outside the camp. Anyone who wished to consult the LORD would go to this meeting tent outside the camp." 
Exodus33:8 Whenever Moses went out to the tent, the people would all rise and stand at the entrance of their own tents, watching Moses until he entered the tent." 
Exodus33:9 As Moses entered the tent, the column of cloud would come down and stand at its entrance while the LORD spoke with Moses." 
Exodus33:10 On seeing the column of cloud stand at the entrance of the tent, all the people would rise and worship at the entrance of their own tents." 
Exodus33:11 The LORD used to speak to Moses face to face, as one man speaks to another. Moses would then return to the camp, but his young assistant, Joshua, son of Nun, would not move out of the tent." 
Exodus33:11 The LORD used to speak to Moses face to face, as one man speaks to another. Moses would then return to the camp, but his young assistant, Joshua, son of Nun, would not move out of the tent." 
Exodus33:12 Moses said to the LORD, ""You, indeed, are telling me to lead this people on; but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said, 'You are my intimate friend,' and also, 'You have found favor with me.'" 
Exodus33:13 Now, if I have found favor with you, do let me know your ways so that, in knowing you, I may continue to find favor with you. Then, too, this nation is, after all, your own people.""" 
Exodus33:14 I myself,"" the LORD answered, ""will go along, to give you rest.""" 
Exodus33:15 Moses replied, ""If you are not going yourself, do not make us go up from here." 
Exodus33:16 For how can it be known that we, your people and I, have found favor with you, except by your going with us? Then we, your people and I, will be singled out from every other people on the earth.""" 
Exodus33:17 The LORD said to Moses, ""This request, too, which you have just made, I will carry out, because you have found favor with me and you are my intimate friend.""" 
Exodus33:18 Then Moses said, ""Do let me see your glory!""" 
Exodus33:19 He answered, ""I will make all my beauty pass before you, and in your presence I will pronounce my name, 'LORD'; I who show favors to whom I will, I who grant mercy to whom I will." 
Exodus33:20 But my face you cannot see, for no man sees me and still lives." 
Exodus33:21 Here,"" continued the LORD, ""is a place near me where you shall station yourself on the rock." 
Exodus33:22 When my glory passes I will set you in the hollow of the rock and will cover you with my hand until I have passed by. 
Exodus33:23 Then I will remove my hand, so that you may see my back; but my face is not to be seen.""" 
Exodus34:1 The LORD said to Moses, ""Cut two stone tablets like the former, that I may write on them the commandments which were on the former tablets that you broke." 
Exodus34:2 Get ready for tomorrow morning, when you are to go up Mount Sinai and there present yourself to me on the top of the mountain." 
Exodus34:3 No one shall come up with you, and no one is even to be seen on any part of the mountain; even the flocks and the herds are not to go grazing toward this mountain.""" 
Exodus34:4 Moses then cut two stone tablets like the former, and early the next morning he went up Mount Sinai as the LORD had commanded him, taking along the two stone tablets." 
Exodus34:5 Having come down in a cloud, the LORD stood with him there and proclaimed his name, ""LORD.""" 
Exodus34:6 Thus the LORD passed before him and cried out, ""The LORD, the LORD, a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger and rich in kindness and fidelity," 
Exodus34:7 continuing his kindness for a thousand generations, and forgiving wickedness and crime and sin; yet not declaring the guilty guiltless, but punishing children and grandchildren to the third and fourth generation for their fathers' wickedness!""" 
Exodus34:8 Moses at once bowed down to the ground in worship. 
Exodus34:9 Then he said, ""If I find favor with you, O Lord, do come along in our company. This is indeed a stiff-necked people; yet pardon our wickedness and sins, and receive us as your own.""" 
Exodus34:10 Here, then,"" said the LORD, ""is the covenant I will make. Before the eyes of all your people I will work such marvels as have never been wrought in any nation anywhere on earth, so that this people among whom you live may see how awe-inspiring are the deeds which I, the LORD, will do at your side." 
Exodus34:11 But you, on your part, must keep the commandments I am giving you today. ""I will drive out before you the Amorites, Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites." 
Exodus34:12 Take care, therefore, not to make a covenant with these inhabitants of the land that you are to enter; else they will become a snare among you." 
Exodus34:13 Tear down their altars; smash their sacred pillars, and cut down their sacred poles." 
Exodus34:14 You shall not worship any other god, for the LORD is 'the Jealous One'; a jealous God is he." 
Exodus34:15 Do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of that land; else, when they render their wanton worship to their gods and sacrifice to them, one of them may invite you and you may partake of his sacrifice." 
Exodus34:16 Neither shall you take their daughters as wives for your sons; otherwise, when their daughters render their wanton worship to their gods, they will make your sons do the same." 
Exodus34:17 You shall not make for yourselves molten gods. 
Exodus34:18 You shall keep the feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days at the prescribed time in the month of Abib you are to eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you; for in the month of Abib you came out of Egypt." 
Exodus34:19 To me belongs every first-born male that opens the womb among all your livestock, whether in the herd or in the flock." 
Exodus34:20 The firstling of an ass you shall redeem with one of the flock; if you do not redeem it, you must break its neck. The first-born among your sons you shall redeem. ""No one shall appear before me empty-handed." 
Exodus34:21 For six days you may work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; on that day you must rest even during the seasons of plowing and harvesting." 
Exodus34:22 You shall keep the feast of Weeks with the first of the wheat harvest; likewise, the feast at the fruit harvest at the close of the year." 
Exodus34:23 Three times a year all your men shall appear before the Lord, the LORD God of Israel." 
Exodus34:24 Since I will drive out the nations before you to give you a large territory, there will be no one to covet your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the LORD, your God." 
Exodus34:25 You shall not offer me the blood of sacrifice with leavened bread, nor shall the sacrifice of the Passover feast be kept overnight for the next day." 
Exodus34:26 The choicest first fruits of your soil you shall bring to the house of the LORD, your God. ""You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.""" 
Exodus34:27 Then the LORD said to Moses, ""Write down these words, for in accordance with them I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.""" 
Exodus34:28 So Moses stayed there with the LORD for forty days and forty nights, without eating any food or drinking any water, and he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments." 
Exodus34:29 As Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the commandments in his hands, he did not know that the skin of his face had become radiant while he conversed with the LORD." 
Exodus34:30 When Aaron, then, and the other Israelites saw Moses and noticed how radiant the skin of his face had become, they were afraid to come near him." 
Exodus34:31 Only after Moses called to them did Aaron and all the rulers of the community come back to him. Moses then spoke to them. 
Exodus34:32 Later on, all the Israelites came up to him, and he enjoined on them all that the LORD had told him on Mount Sinai." 
Exodus34:33 When he finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face." 
Exodus34:34 Whenever Moses entered the presence of the LORD to converse with him, he removed the veil until he came out again. On coming out, he would tell the Israelites all that had been commanded." 
Exodus34:35 Then the Israelites would see that the skin of Moses' face was radiant; so he would again put the veil over his face until he went in to converse with the LORD. 
Exodus35:1 Moses assembled the whole Israelite community and said to them, ""This is what the LORD has commanded to be done." 
Exodus35:2 On six days work may be done, but the seventh day shall be sacred to you as the sabbath of complete rest to the LORD. Anyone who does work on that day shall be put to death." 
Exodus35:3 You shall not even light a fire in any of your dwellings on the sabbath day. 
Exodus35:4 Moses told the whole Israelite community, ""This is what the LORD has commanded:" 
Exodus35:5 Take up among you a collection for the LORD. Everyone, as his heart prompts him, shall bring, as a contribution to the LORD, gold, silver and bronze;" 
Exodus35:6 violet, purple and scarlet yarn; fine linen and goat hair;" 
Exodus35:7 rams' skins dyed red, and tahash skins; acacia wood;" 
Exodus35:8 oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense; 
Exodus35:9 onyx stones and other gems for mounting on the ephod and on the breastpiece. 
Exodus35:10 Let every expert among you come and make all that the LORD has commanded: the Dwelling," 
Exodus35:11 with its tent, its covering, its clasps, its boards, its bars, its columns and its pedestals;" 
Exodus35:12 the ark, with its poles, the propitiatory, and the curtain veil;" 
Exodus35:13 the table, with its poles and all its appurtenances, and the showbread;" 
Exodus35:14 the lampstand, with its appurtenances, the lamps, and the oil for the light;" 
Exodus35:15 the altar of incense, with its poles; the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense; the entrance curtain for the entrance of the Dwelling;" 
Exodus35:16 the altar of holocausts, with its bronze grating, its poles, and all its appurtenances; the laver, with its base;" 
Exodus35:17 the hangings of the court, with their columns and pedestals; the curtain for the entrance of the court;" 
Exodus35:18 the tent pegs for the Dwelling and for the court, with their ropes;" 
Exodus35:19 the service cloths for use in the sanctuary; the sacred vestments for Aaron, the priest, and the vestments worn by his sons in their ministry.""" 
Exodus35:20 When the whole Israelite community left Moses' presence," 
Exodus35:21 everyone, as his heart suggested and his spirit prompted, brought a contribution to the LORD for the construction of the meeting tent, for all its services, and for the sacred vestments." 
Exodus35:22 Both the men and the women, all as their heart prompted them, brought brooches, earrings, rings, necklaces and various other gold articles. Everyone who could presented an offering of gold to the LORD." 
Exodus35:23 Everyone who happened to have violet, purple or scarlet yarn, fine linen or goat hair, rams' skins dyed red or tahash skins, brought them." 
Exodus35:24 Whoever could make a contribution of silver or bronze offered it to the LORD; and everyone who happened to have acacia wood for any part of the work, brought it." 
Exodus35:25 All the women who were expert spinners brought hand-spun violet, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen thread." 
Exodus35:26 All the women who possessed the skill, spun goat hair." 
Exodus35:27 The princes brought onyx stones and other gems for mounting on the ephod and on the breastpiece; 
Exodus35:28 as well as spices, and oil for the light, anointing oil, and fragrant incense." 
Exodus35:29 Every Israelite man and woman brought to the LORD such voluntary offerings as they thought best, for the various kinds of work which the LORD had commanded Moses to have done." 
Exodus35:30 Moses said to the Israelites, ""See, the LORD has chosen Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah," 
Exodus35:31 and has filled him with a divine spirit of skill and understanding and knowledge in every craft: 
Exodus35:32 in the production of embroidery, in making things of gold, silver or bronze," 
Exodus35:33 in cutting and mounting precious stones, in carving wood, and in every other craft." 
Exodus35:34 He has also given both him and Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, the ability to teach others." 
Exodus35:35 He has endowed them with skill to execute all types of work: engraving, embroidering, the making of variegated cloth of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and fine linen thread, weaving, and all other arts and crafts." 
Exodus36:1 Bezalel, therefore, will set to work with Oholiab and with all the experts whom the LORD has endowed with skill and understanding in knowing how to execute all the work for the service of the sanctuary, just as the LORD has commanded.""" 
Exodus36:2 Moses then called Bezalel and Oholiab and all the other experts whom the LORD had endowed with skill, men whose hearts moved them to come and take part in the work." 
Exodus36:3 They received from Moses all the contributions which the Israelites had brought for establishing the service of the sanctuary. Still, morning after morning the people continued to bring their voluntary offerings to Moses." 
Exodus36:4 Thereupon the experts who were executing the various kinds of work for the sanctuary, all left the work they were doing," 
Exodus36:5 and told Moses, ""The people are bringing much more than is needed to carry out the work which the LORD has commanded us to do.""" 
Exodus36:6 Moses, therefore, ordered a proclamation to be made throughout the camp: ""Let neither man nor woman make any more contributions for the sanctuary."" So the people stopped bringing their offerings;" 
Exodus36:7 there was already enough at hand, in fact, more than enough, to complete the work to be done." 
Exodus36:8 The various experts who were executing the work, made the Dwelling with its ten sheets woven of fine linen twined, having cherubim embroidered on them with violet, purple and scarlet yarn." 
Exodus36:9 The length of each sheet was twenty-eight cubits, and the width four cubits; all the sheets were of the same size." 
Exodus36:10 Five of the sheets were sewed together, edge to edge; and the same for the other five." 
Exodus36:11 Loops of violet yarn were made along the edge of the end sheet in the first set, and the same along the edge of the end sheet in the second set." 
Exodus36:12 Fifty loops were thus put on one inner sheet, and fifty loops on the inner sheet in the other set, with the loops directly opposite each other." 
Exodus36:13 Then fifty clasps of gold were made, with which the sheets were joined so that the Dwelling formed one whole." 
Exodus36:14 Sheets of goat hair were also woven as a tent over the Dwelling. Eleven such sheets were made. 
Exodus36:15 The length of each sheet was thirty cubits and the width four cubits; all eleven sheets were the same size. 
Exodus36:16 Five of these sheets were sewed edge to edge into one set; and the other six sheets into another set. 
Exodus36:17 Fifty loops were made along the edge of the end sheet in one set, and fifty loops along the edge of the corresponding sheet in the other set." 
Exodus36:18 Fifty bronze clasps were made with which the tent was joined so that it formed one whole. 
Exodus36:19 A covering for the tent was made of rams' skins dyed red, and above that, a covering of tahash skins." 
Exodus36:20 Boards of acacia wood were made as walls for the Dwelling. 
Exodus36:21 The length of each board was ten cubits, and the width one and a half cubits." 
Exodus36:22 Each board had two arms, fastening them in line. In this way all the boards of the Dwelling were made." 
Exodus36:23 They were set up as follows: twenty boards on the south side," 
Exodus36:24 with forty silver pedestals under the twenty boards, so that there were two pedestals under each board, at its two arms;" 
Exodus36:25 twenty boards on the other side of the Dwelling, the north side," 
Exodus36:26 with their forty silver pedestals, two under each board;" 
Exodus36:27 six boards at the rear of the Dwelling, to the west;" 
Exodus36:28 and two boards at the corners in the rear of the Dwelling. 
Exodus36:29 These were double at the bottom, and likewise double at the top, to the first ring. That is how both boards in the corners were made." 
Exodus36:30 Thus, there were in the rear eight boards, with their sixteen silver pedestals, two pedestals under each board." 
Exodus36:31 Bars of acacia wood were also made, five for the boards on one side of the Dwelling," 
Exodus36:32 five for those on the other side, and five for those at the rear, to the west." 
Exodus36:33 The center bar, at the middle of the boards, was made to reach across from end to end." 
Exodus36:34 The boards were plated with gold, and gold rings were made on them as holders for the bars, which were also plated with gold." 
Exodus36:35 The veil was woven of violet, purple and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, with cherubim embroidered on it." 
Exodus36:36 Four gold-plated columns of acacia wood, with gold hooks, were made for it, and four silver pedestals were cast for them." 
Exodus36:37 The curtain for the entrance of the tent was made of violet, purple and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, woven in a variegated manner." 
Exodus36:38 Its five columns, with their hooks as well as their capitals and bands, were plated with gold; their five pedestals were of bronze." 
Exodus37:1 Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood, two and a half cubits long, one and a half cubits wide, and one and a half cubits high." 
Exodus37:2 The inside and outside were plated with gold, and a molding of gold was put around it." 
Exodus37:3 Four gold rings were cast and put on its four supports, two rings for one side and two for the opposite side." 
Exodus37:4 Poles of acacia wood were made and plated with gold; 
Exodus37:5 these were put through the rings on the sides of the ark, for carrying it." 
Exodus37:6 The propitiatory was made of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide." 
Exodus37:7 Two cherubim of beaten gold were made for the two ends of the propitiatory," 
Exodus37:8 one cherub fastened at one end, the other at the other end, springing directly from the propitiatory at its two ends." 
Exodus37:9 The cherubim had their wings spread out above, covering the propitiatory with them. They were turned toward each other, but with their faces looking toward the propitiatory." 
Exodus37:10 The table was made of acacia wood, two cubits long, one cubit wide, and one and a half cubits high." 
Exodus37:11 It was plated with pure gold, and a molding of gold was put around it." 
Exodus37:12 A frame a handbreadth high was also put around it, with a molding of gold around the frame." 
Exodus37:13 Four rings of gold were cast for it and fastened, one at each of the four corners." 
Exodus37:14 The rings were alongside the frame as holders for the poles to carry the table. 
Exodus37:15 These poles were made of acacia wood and plated with gold. 
Exodus37:16 The vessels that were set on the table, its plates and cups, as well as its pitchers and bowls for pouring libations, were of pure gold." 
Exodus37:17 The lampstand was made of pure beaten gold, its shaft and branches as well as its cups and knobs and petals springing directly from it. 
Exodus37:18 Six branches extended from its sides, three branches on one side and three on the other." 
Exodus37:19 On one branch there were three cups, shaped like almond blossoms, each with its knob and petals; on the opposite branch there were three cups, shaped like almond blossoms, each with its knob and petals; and so for the six branches that extended from the lampstand." 
Exodus37:20 On the shaft there were four cups, shaped like almond blossoms, with their knobs and petals," 
Exodus37:21 including a knob below each of the three pairs of branches that extended from the lampstand. 
Exodus37:22 The knobs and branches sprang so directly from it that the whole formed but a single piece of pure beaten gold. 
Exodus37:23 Its seven lamps, as well as its trimming shears and trays, were made of pure gold." 
Exodus37:24 A talent of pure gold was used for the lampstand and its various appurtenances. 
Exodus37:25 The altar of incense was made of acacia wood, on a square, a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high, having horns that sprang directly from it." 
Exodus37:26 Its grate on top, its walls on all four sides, and its horns were plated with pure gold; and a molding of gold was put around it." 
Exodus37:27 Underneath the molding gold rings were placed, two on one side and two on the opposite side, as holders for the poles to carry it." 
Exodus37:28 The poles, too, were made of acacia wood and plated with gold." 
Exodus37:29 The sacred anointing oil and the fragrant incense were prepared in their pure form by a perfumer. 
Exodus38:1 The altar of holocausts was made of acacia wood, on a square, five cubits long and five cubits wide; its height was three cubits." 
Exodus38:2 At the four corners horns were made that sprang directly from the altar. The whole was plated with bronze. 
Exodus38:3 All the utensils of the altar, the pots, shovels, basins, forks and fire pans, were likewise made of bronze." 
Exodus38:4 A grating of bronze network was made for the altar and placed round it, on the ground, half as high as the altar itself." 
Exodus38:5 Four rings were cast for the four corners of the bronze grating, as holders for the poles," 
Exodus38:6 which were made of acacia wood and plated with bronze. 
Exodus38:7 The poles were put through the rings on the sides of the altar for carrying it. The altar was made in the form of a hollow box. 
Exodus38:8 The bronze laver, with its bronze base, was made from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance of the meeting tent." 
Exodus38:9 The court was made as follows. On the south side of the court there were hangings, woven of fine linen twined, a hundred cubits long," 
Exodus38:10 with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze, the hooks and bands of the columns being of silver." 
Exodus38:11 On the north side there were similar hangings, one hundred cubits long, with twenty columns and twenty pedestals of bronze, the hooks and bands of the columns being of silver." 
Exodus38:12 On the west side there were hangings, fifty cubits long, with ten columns and ten pedestals, the hooks and bands of the columns being of silver." 
Exodus38:13 On the east side the court was fifty cubits long. 
Exodus38:14 Toward one side there were hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals; toward the other side," 
Exodus38:15 beyond the entrance of the court, there were likewise hangings to the extent of fifteen cubits, with three columns and three pedestals." 
Exodus38:16 The hangings on all sides of the court were woven of fine linen twined. 
Exodus38:17 The pedestals of the columns were of bronze, while the hooks and bands of the columns were of silver; the capitals were silver-plated, and all the columns of the court were banded with silver." 
Exodus38:18 At the entrance of the court there was a variegated curtain, woven of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined, twenty cubits long and five cubits wide, in keeping with the hangings of the court." 
Exodus38:19 There were four columns and four pedestals of bronze for it, while their hooks were of silver." 
Exodus38:20 All the tent pegs for the Dwelling and for the court around it were of bronze. 
Exodus38:21 The following is an account of the various amounts used on the Dwelling, the Dwelling of the commandments, drawn up at the command of Moses by the Levites under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest." 
Exodus38:22 However, it was Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, who made all that the Lord commanded Moses," 
Exodus38:23 and he was assisted by Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, who was an engraver, an embroiderer, and a weaver of variegated cloth of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and of fine linen." 
Exodus38:24 All the gold used in the entire construction of the sanctuary, having previously been given as an offering, amounted to twenty-nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel." 
Exodus38:25 The amount of the silver received from the community was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel;" 
Exodus38:26 one bekah apiece, that is, a half-shekel apiece, according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel, was received from every man of twenty years or more who entered the registered group; the number of these was six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty men." 
Exodus38:27 One hundred talents of silver were used for casting the pedestals of the sanctuary and the pedestals of the veil, one talent for each pedestal, or one hundred talents for the one hundred pedestals." 
Exodus38:28 The remaining one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels were used for making the hooks on the columns, for plating the capitals, and for banding them with silver." 
Exodus38:29 The bronze, given as an offering, amounted to seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels." 
Exodus38:30 With this were made the pedestals at the entrance of the meeting tent, the bronze altar with its bronze gratings and all the appurtenances of the altar," 
Exodus38:31 the pedestals around the court, the pedestals at the entrance of the court, and all the tent pegs for the Dwelling and for the court around it." 
Exodus39:1 With violet, purple and scarlet yarn were woven the service cloths for use in the sanctuary, as well as the sacred vestments for Aaron, as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:2 The ephod was woven of gold thread and of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined." 
Exodus39:3 Gold was first hammered into gold leaf and then cut up into threads, which were woven with the violet, purple and scarlet yarn into an embroidered pattern on the fine linen." 
Exodus39:4 Shoulder straps were made for it and joined to its two upper ends. 
Exodus39:5 The embroidered belt on the ephod extended out from it, and like it, was made of gold thread, of violet, purple and scarlet yarn, and of fine linen twined, as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:6 The onyx stones were prepared and mounted in gold filigree work; they were engraved like seal engravings with the names of the sons of Israel. 
Exodus39:7 These stones were set on the shoulder straps of the ephod as memorial stones of the sons of Israel, just as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:8 The breastpiece was embroidered like the ephod, with gold thread and violet, purple and scarlet yarn on cloth of fine linen twined." 
Exodus39:9 It was square and folded double, a span high and a span wide in its folded form." 
Exodus39:10 Four rows of precious stones were mounted on it: in the first row a carnelian, a topaz and an emerald;" 
Exodus39:11 in the second row, a garnet, a sapphire and a beryl;" 
Exodus39:12 in the third row a jacinth, an agate and an amethyst;" 
Exodus39:13 in the fourth row a chrysolite, an onyx and a jasper. They were mounted in gold filigree work." 
Exodus39:14 These stones were twelve, to match the names of the sons of Israel, and each stone was engraved like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes." 
Exodus39:15 Chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, were made for the breastpiece," 
Exodus39:16 together with two gold filigree rosettes and two gold rings. The two rings were fastened to the two upper ends of the breastpiece. 
Exodus39:17 The two gold chains were then fastened to the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece. 
Exodus39:18 The other two ends of the two chains were fastened in front to the two filigree rosettes, which were attached to the shoulder straps of the ephod." 
Exodus39:19 Two other gold rings were made and put on the two lower ends of the breastpiece, on the edge facing the ephod." 
Exodus39:20 Two more gold rings were made and fastened to the bottom of the two shoulder straps next to where they joined the ephod in front, just above its embroidered belt." 
Exodus39:21 Violet ribbons bound the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod, so that the breastpiece stayed right above the embroidered belt of the ephod and did not swing loose from it. All this was just as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:22 The robe of the ephod was woven entirely of violet yarn," 
Exodus39:23 with an opening in its center like the opening of a shirt, with selvage around the opening to keep it from being torn." 
Exodus39:24 At the hem of the robe pomegranates were made of violet, purple and scarlet yarn and of fine linen twined;" 
Exodus39:25 bells of pure gold were also made and put between the pomegranates all around the hem of the robe: 
Exodus39:26 first a bell, then a pomegranate, and thus alternating all around the hem of the robe which was to be worn in performing the ministry, all this, just as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:27 For Aaron and his sons there were also woven tunics of fine linen; 
Exodus39:28 the miter of fine linen; the ornate turbans of fine linen; drawers of linen (of fine linen twined); 
Exodus39:29 and sashes of variegated work made of fine linen twined and of violet, purple and scarlet yarn, as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:30 The plate of the sacred diadem was made of pure gold and inscribed, as on a seal engraving: ""Sacred to the LORD.""" 
Exodus39:31 It was tied over the miter with a violet ribbon, as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus39:32 Thus the entire work of the Dwelling of the meeting tent was completed. The Israelites did the work just as the LORD had commanded Moses. 
Exodus39:33 They then brought to Moses the Dwelling, the tent with all its appurtenances, the clasps, the boards, the bars, the columns, the pedestals," 
Exodus39:34 the covering of rams' skins dyed red, the covering of tahash skins, the curtain veil;" 
Exodus39:35 the ark of the commandments with its poles, the propitiatory," 
Exodus39:36 the table with all its appurtenances and the showbread," 
Exodus39:37 the pure gold lampstand with its lamps set up on it and with all its appurtenances, the oil for the light," 
Exodus39:38 the golden altar, the anointing oil, the fragrant incense; the curtain for the entrance of the tent," 
Exodus39:39 the altar of bronze with its bronze grating, its poles and all its appurtenances, the laver with its base," 
Exodus39:40 the hangings of the court with their columns and pedestals, the curtain for the entrance of the court with its ropes and tent pegs, all the equipment for the service of the Dwelling of the meeting tent;" 
Exodus39:41 the service cloths for use in the sanctuary, the sacred vestments for Aaron the priest, and the vestments to be worn by his sons in their ministry." 
Exodus39:42 The Israelites had carried out all the work just as the LORD had commanded Moses. 
Exodus39:43 So when Moses saw that all the work was done just as the LORD had commanded, he blessed them." 
Exodus40:1 Then the LORD said to Moses," 
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall erect the Dwelling of the meeting tent. 
Exodus40:3 Put the ark of the commandments in it, and screen off the ark with the veil." 
Exodus40:4 Bring in the table and set it. Then bring in the lampstand and set up the lamps on it. 
Exodus40:5 Put the golden altar of incense in front of the ark of the commandments, and hang the curtain at the entrance of the Dwelling." 
Exodus40:6 Put the altar of holocausts in front of the entrance of the Dwelling of the meeting tent. 
Exodus40:7 Place the laver between the meeting tent and the altar, and put water in it." 
Exodus40:8 Set up the court round about, and put the curtain at the entrance of the court." 
Exodus40:9 Take the anointing oil and anoint the Dwelling and everything in it, consecrating it and all its furnishings, so that it will be sacred." 
Exodus40:10 Anoint the altar of holocausts and all its appurtenances, consecrating it, so that it will be most sacred." 
Exodus40:11 Likewise, anoint the laver with its base, and thus consecrate it." 
Exodus40:12 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the meeting tent, and there wash them with water." 
Exodus40:13 Clothe Aaron with the sacred vestments and anoint him, thus consecrating him as my priest." 
Exodus40:14 Bring forward his sons also, and clothe them with the tunics." 
Exodus40:15 As you have anointed their father, anoint them also as my priests. Thus, by being anointed, shall they receive a perpetual priesthood throughout all future generations.""" 
Exodus40:16 Moses did exactly as the LORD had commanded him. 
Exodus40:17 On the first day of the first month of the second year the Dwelling was erected. 
Exodus40:18 It was Moses who erected the Dwelling. He placed its pedestals, set up its boards, put in its bars, and set up its columns." 
Exodus40:19 He spread the tent over the Dwelling and put the covering on top of the tent, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Exodus40:20 He took the commandments and put them in the ark; he placed poles alongside the ark and set the propitiatory upon it. 
Exodus40:21 He brought the ark into the Dwelling and hung the curtain veil, thus screening off the ark of the commandments, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Exodus40:22 He put the table in the meeting tent, on the north side of the Dwelling, outside the veil," 
Exodus40:23 and arranged the bread on it before the LORD, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Exodus40:24 He placed the lampstand in the meeting tent, opposite the table, on the south side of the Dwelling," 
Exodus40:25 and he set up the lamps before the LORD, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Exodus40:26 He placed the golden altar in the meeting tent, in front of the veil," 
Exodus40:27 and on it he burned fragrant incense, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Exodus40:28 He hung the curtain at the entrance of the Dwelling. 
Exodus40:29 He put the altar of holocausts in front of the entrance of the Dwelling of the meeting tent, and offered holocausts and cereal offerings on it, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Exodus40:30 He placed the laver between the meeting tent and the altar, and put water in it for washing." 
Exodus40:31 Moses and Aaron and his sons used to wash their hands and feet there," 
Exodus40:32 for they washed themselves whenever they went into the meeting tent or approached the altar, as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Exodus40:33 Finally, he set up the court around the Dwelling and the altar and hung the curtain at the entrance of the court. Thus Moses finished all the work." 
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the meeting tent, and the glory of the LORD filled the Dwelling." 
Exodus40:35 Moses could not enter the meeting tent, because the cloud settled down upon it and the glory of the LORD filled the Dwelling." 
Exodus40:36 Whenever the cloud rose from the Dwelling, the Israelites would set out on their journey." 
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud did not lift, they would not go forward; only when it lifted did they go forward." 
Exodus40:38 In the daytime the cloud of the LORD was seen over the Dwelling; whereas at night, fire was seen in the cloud by the whole house of Israel in all the stages of their journey." 
Leviticus1:1 The LORD called Moses, and from the meeting tent gave him this message:" 
Leviticus1:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When any one of you wishes to bring an animal offering to the LORD, such an offering must be from the herd or from the flock." 
Leviticus1:3 If his holocaust offering is from the herd, it must be a male without blemish. To find favor with the LORD, he shall bring it to the entrance of the meeting tent" 
Leviticus1:4 and there lay his hand on the head of the holocaust, so that it may be acceptable to make atonement for him." 
Leviticus1:5 He shall then slaughter the bull before the LORD, but Aaron's sons, the priests, shall offer up its blood by splashing it on the sides of the altar which is at the entrance of the meeting tent." 
Leviticus1:6 Then he shall skin the holocaust and cut it up into pieces. 
Leviticus1:7 After Aaron's sons, the priests, have put some burning embers on the altar and laid some wood on them," 
Leviticus1:8 they shall lay the pieces of meat, together with the head and the suet, on top of the wood and embers on the altar." 
Leviticus1:9 The inner organs and the shanks, however, the offerer shall first wash with water. The priest shall then burn the whole offering on the altar as a holocaust, a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus1:10 If his holocaust offering is from the flock, that is, a sheep or a goat, he must bring a male without blemish." 
Leviticus1:11 This he shall slaughter before the LORD at the north side of the altar. Then Aaron's sons, the priests, shall splash its blood on the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus1:12 When the offerer has cut it up into pieces, the priest shall lay these, together with the head and suet, on top of the wood and the fire on the altar." 
Leviticus1:13 The inner organs and the shanks, however, the offerer shall first wash with water. The priest shall offer them up and then burn the whole offering on the altar as a holocaust, a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus1:14 If he offers a bird as a holocaust to the LORD, he shall choose a turtledove or a pigeon as his offering." 
Leviticus1:15 Having brought it to the altar where it is to be burned, the priest shall snap its head loose and squeeze out its blood against the side of the altar." 
Leviticus1:16 Its crop and feathers shall be removed and thrown on the ash heap at the east side of the altar. 
Leviticus1:17 Then, having split the bird down the middle without separating the halves, the priest shall burn it on the altar, over the wood on the fire, as a holocaust, a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus2:1 When anyone wishes to bring a cereal offering to the LORD, his offering must consist of fine flour. He shall pour oil on it and put frankincense over it." 
Leviticus2:2 When he has brought it to Aaron's sons, the priests, one of them shall take a handful of this fine flour and oil, together with all the frankincense, and this he shall burn on the altar as a token offering, a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus2:3 The rest of the cereal offering belongs to Aaron and his sons. It is a most sacred oblation to the LORD. 
Leviticus2:4 When the cereal offering you present is baked in an oven, it must be in the form of unleavened cakes made of fine flour mixed with oil, or of unleavened wafers spread with oil." 
Leviticus2:5 If you present a cereal offering that is fried on a griddle, it must be of fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened." 
Leviticus2:6 Such a cereal offering must be broken into pieces, and oil must be poured over it." 
Leviticus2:7 If you present a cereal offering that is prepared in a pot, it must be of fine flour, deep-fried in oil." 
Leviticus2:8 A cereal offering that is made in any of these ways you shall bring to the LORD, offering it to the priest, who shall take it to the altar." 
Leviticus2:9 Its token offering the priest shall then lift from the cereal offering and burn on the altar as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD. 
Leviticus2:10 The rest of the cereal offering belongs to Aaron and his sons. It is a most sacred oblation to the LORD. 
Leviticus2:11 Every cereal offering that you present to the LORD shall be unleavened, for you shall not burn any leaven or honey as an oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus2:12 Such you may indeed present to the LORD in the offering of first fruits, but they are not to be placed on the altar for a pleasing odor." 
Leviticus2:13 However, every cereal offering that you present to the LORD shall be seasoned with salt. Do not let the salt of the covenant of your God be lacking from your cereal offering. On every offering you shall offer salt." 
Leviticus2:14 If you present a cereal offering of first fruits to the LORD, you shall offer it in the form of fresh grits of new ears of grain, roasted by fire." 
Leviticus2:15 On this cereal offering you shall put oil and frankincense. 
Leviticus2:16 For its token offering the priest shall then burn some of the grits and oil, together with all the frankincense, as an oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus3:1 If someone in presenting a peace offering makes his offering from the herd, he may offer before the LORD either a male or a female animal, but it must be without blemish." 
Leviticus3:2 He shall lay his hand on the head of his offering, and then slaughter it at the entrance of the meeting tent; but Aaron's sons, the priests, shall splash its blood on the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus3:3 From the peace offering he shall offer as an oblation to the LORD the fatty membrane over the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them," 
Leviticus3:4 as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he shall sever above the kidneys." 
Leviticus3:5 All this Aaron's sons shall then burn on the altar with the holocaust, on the wood over the fire, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Leviticus3:6 If the peace offering he presents to the LORD is from the flock, he may offer either a male or a female animal, but it must be without blemish." 
Leviticus3:7 If he presents a lamb as his offering, he shall bring it before the LORD," 
Leviticus3:8 and after laying his hand on the head of his offering, he shall slaughter it before the meeting tent; but Aaron's sons shall splash its blood on the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus3:9 As an oblation to the LORD he shall present the fat of the peace offering: the whole fatty tail, which he must sever close to the spine, the fatty membrane over the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them," 
Leviticus3:10 as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he must sever above the kidneys." 
Leviticus3:11 All this the priest shall burn on the altar as the food of the LORD'S oblation. 
Leviticus3:12 If he presents a goat, he shall bring it before the LORD," 
Leviticus3:13 and after laying his hand on its head, he shall slaughter it before the meeting tent; but Aaron's sons shall splash its blood on the sides of the altar." 
Leviticus3:14 From it he shall offer as an oblation to the LORD the fatty membrane over the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them," 
Leviticus3:15 as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he must sever above the kidneys." 
Leviticus3:16 All this the priest shall burn on the altar as the food of the sweet-smelling oblation. All the fat belongs to the LORD. 
Leviticus3:17 This shall be a perpetual ordinance for your descendants wherever they may dwell. You shall not partake of any fat or any blood. 
Leviticus4:1 The LORD said to Moses," 
Leviticus4:2 Tell the Israelites: When a person inadvertently commits a sin against some command of the LORD by doing one of the forbidden things," 
Leviticus4:3 if it is the anointed priest who thus sins and thereby makes the people also become guilty, he shall present to the LORD a young, unblemished bull as a sin offering for the sin he committed." 
Leviticus4:4 Bringing the bullock to the entrance of the meeting tent, before the LORD, he shall lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before the LORD." 
Leviticus4:5 The anointed priest shall then take some of the bullock's blood and bring it into the meeting tent," 
Leviticus4:6 where, dipping his finger in the blood, he shall sprinkle it seven times before the LORD, toward the veil of the sanctuary." 
Leviticus4:7 The priest shall also put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense which is before the LORD in the meeting tent. The rest of the bullock's blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of holocausts which is at the entrance of the meeting tent. 
Leviticus4:8 From the sin-offering bullock he shall remove all the fat: the fatty membrane over the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them," 
Leviticus4:9 as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he must sever above the kidneys." 
Leviticus4:10 This is the same as is removed from the ox of the peace offering; and the priest shall burn it on the altar of holocausts. 
Leviticus4:11 The hide of the bullock and all its flesh, with its head, legs, inner organs and offal," 
Leviticus4:12 in short, the whole bullock, shall be brought outside the camp to a clean place where the ashes are deposited and there be burned up in a wood fire. At the place of the ash heap, there it must be burned." 
Leviticus4:13 If the whole community of Israel inadvertently and without even being aware of it does something that the LORD has forbidden and thus makes itself guilty," 
Leviticus4:14 should it later on become known that the sin was committed, the community shall present a young bull as a sin offering. They shall bring it before the meeting tent," 
Leviticus4:15 and here, before the LORD, the elders of the community shall lay their hands on the bullock's head. When the bullock has been slaughtered before the LORD," 
Leviticus4:16 the anointed priest shall bring some of its blood into the meeting tent," 
Leviticus4:17 and dipping his finger in the blood, he shall sprinkle it seven times before the LORD, toward the veil." 
Leviticus4:18 He shall also put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense which is before the LORD in the meeting tent. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of holocausts which is at the entrance of the meeting tent. 
Leviticus4:19 All of its fat he shall take from it and burn on the altar," 
Leviticus4:20 doing with this bullock just as he did with the other sin-offering bullock. Thus the priest shall make atonement for them, and they will be forgiven." 
Leviticus4:21 This bullock must also be brought outside the camp and burned, just as has been prescribed for the other one. This is the sin offering for the community." 
Leviticus4:22 Should a prince commit a sin inadvertently by doing one of the things which are forbidden by some commandment of the LORD, his God, and thus become guilty," 
Leviticus4:23 if later on he learns of the sin he committed, he shall bring as his offering an unblemished male goat." 
Leviticus4:24 Having laid his hands on its head, he shall slaughter the goat as a sin offering before the LORD, in the place where the holocausts are slaughtered." 
Leviticus4:25 The priest shall then take some of the blood of the sin offering on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of holocausts. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of this altar. 
Leviticus4:26 All of the fat he shall burn on the altar like the fat of the peace offering. Thus the priest shall make atonement for the prince's sin, and it will be forgiven." 
Leviticus4:27 If a private person commits a sin inadvertently by doing one of the things which are forbidden by the commandments of the LORD, and thus becomes guilty," 
Leviticus4:28 should he later on learn of the sin he committed, he shall bring an unblemished she-goat as the offering for his sin." 
Leviticus4:29 Having laid his hand on the head of the sin offering, he shall slaughter it at the place of the holocausts." 
Leviticus4:30 The priest shall then take some of its blood on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of holocausts. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar. 
Leviticus4:31 All the fat shall be removed, just as the fat is removed from the peace offering, and the priest shall burn it on the altar for an odor pleasing to the LORD. Thus the priest shall make atonement for him, and he will be forgiven." 
Leviticus4:32 If, however, for his sin offering he presents a lamb, he shall bring an unblemished female." 
Leviticus4:33 Having laid his hand on its head, he shall slaughter this sin offering in the place where the holocausts are slaughtered." 
Leviticus4:34 The priest shall then take some of the blood of the sin offering on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of holocausts. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar. 
Leviticus4:35 All the fat shall be removed, just as the fat is removed from the peace-offering lamb, and the priest shall burn it on the altar with the other oblations of the LORD. Thus the priest shall make atonement for the man's sin, and it will be forgiven." 
Leviticus5:1 If any person refuses to give the information which, as a witness of something he has seen or learned, he has been adjured to give, and thus commits a sin and has guilt to bear;" 
Leviticus5:2 or if someone, without being aware of it, touches any unclean thing, as the carcass of an unclean wild animal, or that of an unclean domestic animal, or that of an unclean swarming creature, and thus becomes unclean and guilty;" 
Leviticus5:3 or if someone, without being aware of it, touches some human uncleanness, whatever kind of uncleanness this may be, and then recognizes his guilt;" 
Leviticus5:4 or if someone, without being aware of it, rashly utters an oath to do good or evil, such as men are accustomed to utter rashly, and then recognizes that he is guilty of such an oath;" 
Leviticus5:5 then whoever is guilty in any of these cases shall confess the sin he has incurred," 
Leviticus5:6 and as his sin offering for the sin he has committed he shall bring to the LORD a female animal from the flock, a ewe lamb or a she-goat. The priest shall then make atonement for his sin." 
Leviticus5:7 If, however, he cannot afford an animal of the flock, he shall bring to the LORD as the sin offering for his sin two turtledoves or two pigeons, one for a sin offering and the other for a holocaust."
