Numbers25:18 for they have been your enemies by their wily dealings with you as regards Peor and as regards their kinswoman Cozbi, the daughter of a Midianite prince, who was killed at the time of the slaughter because of Peor.""" 
Numbers25:19 After the slaughter 
Numbers26:1 the LORD said to Moses and Eleazar; son of Aaron the priest," 
Numbers26:2 Take a census, by ancestral houses, throughout the community of the Israelites of all those of twenty years or more who are fit for military service in Israel.""" 
Numbers26:3 So on the plains of Moab along the Jericho stretch of the Jordan, Moses and the priest Eleazar registered" 
Numbers26:4 those of twenty years or more, as the LORD had commanded Moses. The Israelites who came out of the land of Egypt were as follows:" 
Numbers26:5 Of Reuben, the first-born of Israel, the Reubenites by clans were: through Hanoch the clan of the Hanochites, through Pallu the clan of the Palluites," 
Numbers26:6 through Hezron the clan of the Hezronites, through Carmi the clan of the Carmites." 
Numbers26:7 These were the clans of the Reubenites, of whom forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty men were registered." 
Numbers26:8 From Pallu descended Eliab," 
Numbers26:9 and the descendants of Eliab were Dathan and Abiram, the same Dathan and Abiram, councilors of the community, who revolted against Moses and Aaron (like Korah's band when it rebelled against the LORD)." 
Numbers26:10 The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them as a warning (Korah too and the band that died when the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men. 
Numbers26:11 The descendents of Korah, however, did not die out)." 
Numbers26:12 The Simeonites by clans were: through Nemuel the clan of the Nemuelites, through Jamin the clan of the Jaminites, through Jachin the clan of the Jachinites," 
Numbers26:13 through Sohar the clan of the Soharites, through Shaul the clan of the Shaulites." 
Numbers26:14 These were the clans of the Simeonites, of whom twenty-two thousand two hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:15 The Gadites by clans were: through Zephon the clan of the Zephonites, through Haggi the clan of the Haggites, through Shuni the clan of the Shunites," 
Numbers26:16 through Ozni the clan of the Oznites, through Eri the clan of the Erites," 
Numbers26:17 through Arod the clan of the Arodites, through Areli the clan of the Arelites." 
Numbers26:18 These were the clans of the Gadites, of whom forty thousand five hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:19 The sons of Judah who died in the land of Canaan were Er and Onan. 
Numbers26:20 The Judahites by clans were: through Shelah the clan of the Shelahites, through Perez the clan of the Perezites, through Zerah the clan of the Zerahites." 
Numbers26:21 The Perezites were: through Hezron the clan of the Hezronites, through Hamul the clan of the Hamulites." 
Numbers26:22 These were the clans of Judah, of whom seventy-six thousand five hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:23 The Issacharites by clans were: through Tola the clan of the Tolaites, through Puvah the clan of the Puvahites," 
Numbers26:24 through Jashub the clan of the Jashubites, through Shimron the clan of the Shimronites." 
Numbers26:25 These were the clans of Issachar, of whom sixty-four thousand three hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:26 The Zebulunites by clans were: through Sered the clan of the Seredites, through Elon the clan of the Elonites, through Jahleel the clan of the Jahleelites." 
Numbers26:27 These were the clans of the Zebulunites, of whom sixty thousand five hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:28 The sons of Joseph were Manasseh and Ephraim. 
Numbers26:29 The Manassehites by clans were: through Machir the clan of the Machirites, through Gilead, a descendant of Machir, the clan of the Gileadites." 
Numbers26:30 The Gileadites were: through Abiezer the clan of the Abiezrites, through Helek the clan of the Helekites," 
Numbers26:31 through Asriel the clan of the Asrielites, through Shechem the clan of the Shechemites," 
Numbers26:32 through Shemida the clan of the Shemidaites, through Hepher the clan of the Hepherites." 
Numbers26:33 Zelophehad, son of Hepher, had no sons, but only daughters, whose names were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and Tirzah." 
Numbers26:34 These were the clans of Manasseh, of whom fifty-two thousand seven hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:35 The Ephraimites by clans were: through Shuthelah the clan of the Shuthelahites, through Becher the clan of the Bechrites, through Tahan the clan of the Tahanites." 
Numbers26:36 The Shuthelahites were: through Eran the clan of the Eranites. 
Numbers26:37 These were the clans of the Ephraimites, of whom thirty-two thousand five hundred men were registered. These were the descendants of Joseph by clans." 
Numbers26:38 The Benjaminites by clans were: through Bela the clan of the Belaites, through Ashbel the clan of the Ashbelites, through Ahiram the clan of the Ahiramites," 
Numbers26:39 through Shupham the clan of the Shuphamites, through Hupham the clan of the Huphamites." 
Numbers26:40 The descendants of Bela were Arad and Naaman: through Arad the clan of the Aradites, through Naaman the clan of the Naamanites." 
Numbers26:41 These were the Benjaminites by clans, of whom forty-five thousand six hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:42 The Danites by clans were: through Shuham the clan of the Shuhamites. These were the clans of Dan," 
Numbers26:43 of whom sixty-four thousand four hundred men were registered. 
Numbers26:44 The Asherites by clans were: through Imnah the clan of the Imnites, through Ishvi the clan of the Ishvites, through Beriah the clan of the Beriites," 
Numbers26:45 through Heber the clan of the Heberites, through Malchiel the clan of the Malchielites." 
Numbers26:46 The name of Asher's daughter was Serah. 
Numbers26:47 These were the clans of Asher, of whom fifty-three thousand four hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:48 The Naphtalites by clans were: through Jahzeel the clan of the Jahzeelites, through Guni the clan of the Gunites," 
Numbers26:49 through Jezer the clan of the Jezerites, through Shillem the clan of the Shillemites." 
Numbers26:50 These were the clans of Naphtali, of whom forty-five thousand four hundred men were registered." 
Numbers26:51 These six hundred and one thousand seven hundred and thirty were the Israelites who were registered. 
Numbers26:52 The LORD said to Moses," 
Numbers26:53 Among these groups the land shall be divided as their heritage in keeping with the number of individuals in each group. 
Numbers26:54 To a large group you shall assign a large heritage, to a small group a small heritage, each group receiving its heritage in proportion to the number of men registered in it." 
Numbers26:55 But the land shall be divided by lot, as the heritage of the various ancestral tribes." 
Numbers26:56 As the lot falls shall each group, large or small, be assigned its heritage.""" 
Numbers26:57 The Levites registered by clans were: through Gershon the clan of the Gershonites, through Kohath the clan of the Kohathites, through Merari the clan of the Merarites." 
Numbers26:58 These also were clans of Levi: the clan of the Libnites, the clan of the Hebronites, the clan of the Mahlites, the clan of the Mushites, the clan of the Korahites. Among the descendants of Kohath was Amram," 
Numbers26:59 whose wife was named Jochebed. She also was of the tribe of Levi, born to the tribe in Egypt. To Amram she bore Aaron and Moses and their sister Miriam." 
Numbers26:60 To Aaron were born Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar." 
Numbers26:61 But Nadab and Abihu died when they offered profane fire before the LORD. 
Numbers26:62 The total number of male Levites one month or more of age, who were registered, was twenty-three thousand. They were not registered with the other Israelites, however, for no heritage was given them among the Israelites." 
Numbers26:63 These, then, were the men registered by Moses and the priest Eleazar in the census of the Israelites taken on the plains of Moab along the Jericho stretch of the Jordan." 
Numbers26:64 Among them there was not a man of those who had been registered by Moses and the priest Aaron in the census of the Israelites taken in the desert of Sinai. 
Numbers26:65 For the LORD had told them that they would surely die in the desert, and not one of them was left except Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun." 
Numbers26:65 For the LORD had told them that they would surely die in the desert, and not one of them was left except Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun." 
Numbers27:1 Zelophehad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, son of Joseph, had daughters named Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah and Tirzah. They came forward," 
Numbers27:2 and standing in the presence of Moses, the priest Eleazar, the princes, and the whole community at the entrance of the meeting tent, said:" 
Numbers27:3 Our father died in the desert. Although he did not join those who banded together against the LORD (in Korah's band), he died for his own sin without leaving any sons." 
Numbers27:4 But why should our father's name be withdrawn from his clan merely because he had no son? Let us, therefore, have property among our father's kinsmen.""" 
Numbers27:5 When Moses laid their case before the LORD," 
Numbers27:6 the LORD said to him," 
Numbers27:7 The plea of Zelophehad's daughters is just; you shall give them hereditary property among their father's kinsmen, letting their father's heritage pass on to them." 
Numbers27:8 Therefore, tell the Israelites: If a man dies without leaving a son, you shall let his heritage pass on to his daughter;" 
Numbers27:9 if he has no daughter, you shall give his heritage to his brothers;" 
Numbers27:10 if he has no brothers, you shall give his heritage to his father's brothers;" 
Numbers27:11 if his father had no brothers, you shall give his heritage to his nearest relative in his clan, who shall then take possession of it."" This is the legal norm for the Israelites, as the LORD commanded Moses." 
Numbers27:12 The LORD said to Moses, ""Go up here into the Abarim Mountains and view the land that I am giving to the Israelites." 
Numbers27:13 When you have viewed it, you too shall be taken to your people, as was your brother Aaron," 
Numbers27:14 because in the rebellion of the community in the desert of Zin you both rebelled against my order to manifest my sanctity to them by means of the water. (This is the water of Meribah of Kadesh in the desert of Zin.) 
Numbers27:15 Then Moses said to the LORD," 
Numbers27:16 May the LORD, the God of the spirits of all mankind, set over the community a man" 
Numbers27:17 who shall act as their leader in all things, to guide them in all their actions; that the LORD'S community may not be like sheep without a shepherd.""" 
Numbers27:18 And the LORD replied to Moses, ""Take Joshua, son of Nun, a man of spirit, and lay your hand upon him." 
Numbers27:18 And the LORD replied to Moses, ""Take Joshua, son of Nun, a man of spirit, and lay your hand upon him." 
Numbers27:19 Have him stand in the presence of the priest Eleazar and of the whole community, and commission him before their eyes." 
Numbers27:20 Invest him with some of your own dignity, that the whole Israelite community may obey him." 
Numbers27:21 He shall present himself to the priest Eleazar, to have him seek out for him the decisions of the Urim in the LORD'S presence; and as he directs, Joshua, all the Israelites with him, and the community as a whole shall perform all their actions.""" 
Numbers27:21 He shall present himself to the priest Eleazar, to have him seek out for him the decisions of the Urim in the LORD'S presence; and as he directs, Joshua, all the Israelites with him, and the community as a whole shall perform all their actions.""" 
Numbers27:22 Moses did as the LORD had commanded him. Taking Joshua and having him stand in the presence of the priest Eleazar and of the whole community," 
Numbers27:22 Moses did as the LORD had commanded him. Taking Joshua and having him stand in the presence of the priest Eleazar and of the whole community," 
Numbers27:23 he laid his hands on him and gave him his commission, as the LORD had directed through Moses." 
Numbers28:1 The LORD said to Moses," 
Numbers28:2 Give the Israelites this commandment: At the times I have appointed, you shall be careful to present to me the food offerings that are offered to me as sweet-smelling oblations." 
Numbers28:3 You shall tell them therefore: This is the oblation which you shall offer to the LORD: two unblemished yearling lambs each day as the established holocaust," 
Numbers28:4 offering one lamb in the morning and the other during the evening twilight," 
Numbers28:5 each with a cereal offering of one tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil of crushed olives. 
Numbers28:6 This is the established holocaust that was offered at Mount Sinai as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD. 
Numbers28:7 And as the libation for the first lamb, you shall pour out to the LORD in the sanctuary a fourth of a hin of wine." 
Numbers28:8 The other lamb, to be offered during the evening twilight, you shall offer with the same cereal offering and the same libation as in the morning, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Numbers28:9 On the sabbath day you shall offer two unblemished yearling lambs, with their cereal offering, two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil, and with their libations." 
Numbers28:10 Each sabbath there shall be the sabbath holocaust in addition to the established holocaust and its libation. 
Numbers28:11 On the first of each month you shall offer as a holocaust to the LORD two bullocks, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers28:12 with three tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as the cereal offering for each bullock, two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as the cereal offering for the ram," 
Numbers28:13 and one tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as the cereal offering for each lamb, that the holocaust may be a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Numbers28:14 Their libations shall be half a hin of wine for each bullock, a third of a hin for the ram, and a fourth of a hin for each lamb. This is the new moon holocaust for every new moon of the year." 
Numbers28:15 Moreover, one goat shall be sacrificed as a sin offering to the LORD. These are to be offered in addition to the established holocaust and its libation." 
Numbers28:16 On the fourteenth day of the first month falls the Passover of the LORD," 
Numbers28:17 and the fifteenth day of this month is the pilgrimage feast. For seven days unleavened bread is to be eaten. 
Numbers28:18 On the first of these days you shall hold a sacred assembly, and do no sort of work." 
Numbers28:19 As an oblation you shall offer a holocaust to the LORD, which shall consist of two bullocks, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished," 
Numbers28:20 with their cereal offerings of fine flour mixed with oil; offering three tenths of an ephah for each bullock, two tenths for the ram," 
Numbers28:21 and one tenth for each of the seven lambs; 
Numbers28:22 and offer one goat as a sin offering in atonement for yourselves. 
Numbers28:23 These offerings you shall make in addition to the established morning holocaust: 
Numbers28:24 you shall make exactly the same offerings each day for seven days as food offerings, in addition to the established holocaust with its libation, for a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day you shall hold a sacred assembly, and do no sort of work." 
Numbers28:26 On the day of first fruits, on your feast of Weeks, when you present to the LORD the new cereal offering, you shall hold a sacred assembly, and do no sort of work." 
Numbers28:27 You shall offer as a sweet-smelling holocaust to the LORD two bullocks, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished," 
Numbers28:28 with their cereal offerings of fine flour mixed with oil; offering three tenths of an ephah for each bullock, two tenths for the ram," 
Numbers28:29 and one tenth for each of the seven lambs. 
Numbers28:30 Moreover, one goat shall be offered as a sin offering in atonement for yourselves." 
Numbers28:31 You shall make these offerings, together with their libations, in addition to the established holocaust with its cereal offering." 
Numbers29:1 On the first day of the seventh month you shall hold a sacred assembly, and do no sort of work; it shall be a day on which you sound the trumpet." 
Numbers29:2 You shall offer as a sweet-smelling holocaust to the LORD one bullock, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:3 with their cereal offerings of fine flour mixed with oil; offering three tenths of an ephah for the bullock, two tenths for the ram," 
Numbers29:4 and one tenth for each of the seven lambs. 
Numbers29:5 Moreover, one goat shall be offered as a sin offering in atonement for yourselves." 
Numbers29:6 These are to be offered in addition to the ordinary new-moon holocaust with its cereal offering, and in addition to the established holocaust with its cereal offering, together with the libations prescribed for them, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD." 
Numbers29:7 On the tenth day of this seventh month you shall hold a sacred assembly, and mortify yourselves, and do no sort of work." 
Numbers29:8 You shall offer as a sweet-smelling holocaust to the LORD one bullock, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished," 
Numbers29:9 with their cereal offerings of fine flour mixed with oil; offering three tenths of an ephah for the bullock, two tenths for the ram," 
Numbers29:10 and one tenth for each of the seven lambs. 
Numbers29:11 Moreover, one goat shall be sacrificed as a sin offering. These are to be offered in addition to the atonement sin offering, the established holocaust with its cereal offering, and their libations." 
Numbers29:12 On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall hold a sacred assembly, and do no sort of work; then, for seven days following, you shall celebrate a pilgrimage feast to the LORD." 
Numbers29:13 You shall offer as a sweet-smelling holocaust to the LORD thirteen bullocks, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs that are unblemished," 
Numbers29:14 with their cereal offerings of fine flour mixed with oil; offering three tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bullocks, two tenths for each of the two rams," 
Numbers29:15 and one tenth for each of the fourteen lambs. 
Numbers29:16 Moreover, one goat shall be sacrificed as a sin offering. These are to be offered in addition to the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:17 On the second day you shall offer twelve bullocks, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:18 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:19 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:20 On the third day you shall offer eleven bullocks, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:21 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:22 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:23 On the fourth day you shall offer ten bullocks, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:24 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:25 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:26 On the fifth day you shall offer nine bullocks, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:27 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:28 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:29 On the sixth day you shall offer eight bullocks, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:30 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:31 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:32 On the seventh day you shall offer seven bullocks, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:33 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:34 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:35 On the eighth day you shall hold a solemn meeting, and do no sort of work." 
Numbers29:36 You shall offer up in holocaust as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD one bullock, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs," 
Numbers29:37 with their cereal offerings and libations as prescribed for the bullocks, rams and lambs in proportion to their number," 
Numbers29:38 as well as one goat for a sin offering, besides the established holocaust with its cereal offering and libation." 
Numbers29:39 These are the offerings you shall make to the LORD on your festivals, besides whatever holocausts, cereal offerings, libations, and peace offerings you present as your votive or freewill offerings.""" 
Numbers30:1 Moses then gave the Israelites these instructions, just as the LORD had ordered him." 
Numbers30:2 Moses said to the heads of the Israelite tribes, ""This is what the LORD has commanded:" 
Numbers30:3 When a man makes a vow to the LORD or binds himself under oath to a pledge of abstinence, he shall not violate his word, but must fulfill exactly the promise he has uttered." 
Numbers30:4 When a woman, while still a maiden in her father's house, makes a vow to the LORD, or binds herself to a pledge," 
Numbers30:5 if her father learns of her vow or the pledge to which she bound herself and says nothing to her about it, then any vow or any pledge she has made remains valid." 
Numbers30:6 But if on the day he learns of it her father expresses to her his disapproval, then any vow or any pledge she has made becomes null and void; and the LORD releases her from it, since her father has expressed to her his disapproval." 
Numbers30:7 If she marries while under a vow or under a rash pledge to which she bound herself," 
Numbers30:8 and her husband learns of it, yet says nothing to her that day about it, then the vow or pledge she had made remains valid." 
Numbers30:9 But if on the day he learns of it her husband expresses to her his disapproval, he thereby annuls the vow she had made or the rash pledge to which she had bound herself, and the LORD releases her from it." 
Numbers30:10 The vow of a widow or of a divorced woman, or any pledge to which such a woman binds herself, is valid." 
Numbers30:11 If it is in her husband's house that she makes a vow or binds herself under oath to a pledge," 
Numbers30:12 and her husband learns of it yet says nothing to express to her his disapproval, then any vow or any pledge she has made remains valid." 
Numbers30:13 But if on the day he learns of them her husband annuls them, then whatever she has expressly promised in her vow or in her pledge becomes null and void; since her husband has annulled them, the LORD releases her from them." 
Numbers30:14 Any vow or any pledge that she makes under oath to mortify herself, her husband can either allow to remain valid or render null and void." 
Numbers30:15 But if her husband, day after day, says nothing at all to her about them, he thereby allows as valid any vow or any pledge she has made; he has allowed them to remain valid, because on the day he learned of them he said nothing to her about them." 
Numbers30:16 If, however, he countermands them some time after he first learned of them, he is responsible for her guilt.""" 
Numbers30:17 These are the statutes which the LORD prescribed through Moses concerning the relationship between a husband and his wife, as well as between a father and his daughter while she is still a maiden in her father's house." 
Numbers31:1 The LORD said to Moses," 
Numbers31:2 Avenge the Israelites on the Midianites, and then you shall be taken to your people.""" 
Numbers31:3 So Moses told the people, ""Select men from your midst and arm them for war, to attack the Midianites and execute the LORD'S vengeance on them." 
Numbers31:4 From each of the tribes of Israel you shall send a band of one thousand men to war. 
Numbers31:5 From the clans of Israel, therefore, a thousand men of each tribe were levied, so that there were twelve thousand men armed for war." 
Numbers31:6 Moses sent them out on the campaign, a thousand from each tribe, with Phinehas, son of Eleazar, the priest for the campaign, who had with him the sacred vessels and the trumpets for sounding the alarm." 
Numbers31:7 They waged war against the Midianites, as the LORD had commanded Moses, and killed every male among them." 
Numbers31:8 Besides those slain in battle, they killed the five Midianite kings: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur and Reba; and they also executed Balaam, son of Beor, with the sword." 
Numbers31:9 But the Israelites kept the women of the Midianites with their little ones as captives, and all their herds and flocks and wealth as spoil," 
Numbers31:10 while they set on fire all the towns where they had settled and all their encampments. 
Numbers31:11 Then they took all the booty, with the people and beasts they had captured, and brought the captives, together with the spoils and booty," 
Numbers31:12 to Moses and the priest Eleazar and to the Israelite community at their camp on the plains of Moab, along the Jericho stretch of the Jordan." 
Numbers31:13 When Moses and the priest Eleazar, with all the princes of the community, went outside the camp to meet them," 
Numbers31:14 Moses became angry with the officers of the army, the clan and company commanders, who were returning from combat." 
Numbers31:15 So you have spared all the women! he exclaimed. 
Numbers31:16 Why, they are the very ones who on Balaam's advice prompted the unfaithfulness of the Israelites toward the LORD in the Peor affair, which began the slaughter of the LORD'S community." 
Numbers31:17 Slay, therefore, every male child and every woman who has had intercourse with a man." 
Numbers31:18 But you may spare and keep for yourselves all girls who had no intercourse with a man. 
Numbers31:19 Moreover, you shall stay outside the camp for seven days, and those of you who have slain anyone or touched anyone slain in battle shall purify yourselves on the third and on the seventh day. This applies both to you and to your captives." 
Numbers31:20 You shall also purify every article of cloth, leather, goats' hair, or wood.""" 
Numbers31:21 Eleazar the priest told the soldiers who had returned from combat: This is what the law, as prescribed by the LORD to Moses, ordains:" 
Numbers31:22 Whatever can stand fire, such as gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin and lead," 
Numbers31:23 you shall put into the fire, that it may become clean; however, it must also be purified with lustral water. But whatever cannot stand fire you shall put into the water." 
Numbers31:24 On the seventh day you shall wash your clothes, and then you will again be clean. After that you may enter the camp.""" 
Numbers31:25 The LORD said to Moses: 
Numbers31:26 With the help of the priest Eleazar and of the heads of the ancestral houses, count up all the human captives and the beasts that have been taken;" 
Numbers31:27 then divide them evenly, giving half to those who took active part in the war by going out to combat, and half to the rest of the community." 
Numbers31:28 You shall levy a tax for the LORD on the warriors who went out to combat: one out of every five hundred persons, oxen, asses and sheep" 
Numbers31:29 in their half of the spoil you shall turn over to the priest Eleazar as a contribution to the LORD. 
Numbers31:30 From the Israelites' half you shall take one out of every fifty persons, and the same from the different beasts, oxen, asses and sheep, and give them to the Levites, who have charge of the LORD'S Dwelling.""" 
Numbers31:31 So Moses and the priest Eleazar did this, as the LORD had commanded Moses." 
Numbers31:32 This booty, what was left of the loot which the soldiers had taken, amounted to six hundred and seventy-five thousand sheep," 
Numbers31:33 seventy-two thousand oxen," 
Numbers31:34 sixty-one thousand asses," 
Numbers31:35 and thirty-two thousand girls who were still virgins. 
Numbers31:36 The half that fell to those who had gone out to combat was: three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep," 
Numbers31:37 of which six hundred and seventy-five fell as tax to the LORD; 
Numbers31:38 thirty-six thousand oxen, of which seventy-two fell as tax to the LORD;" 
Numbers31:39 thirty thousand five hundred asses, of which sixty-one fell as tax to the LORD;" 
Numbers31:40 and sixteen thousand persons, of whom thirty-two fell as tax to the LORD." 
Numbers31:41 The taxes contributed to the LORD, Moses gave to the priest Eleazar, as the LORD had commanded him." 
Numbers31:42 The half for the other Israelites, which fell to the community when Moses had taken it from the soldiers, was:" 
Numbers31:43 three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep," 
Numbers31:44 thirty-six thousand oxen," 
Numbers31:45 thirty thousand five hundred asses," 
Numbers31:46 and sixteen thousand persons. 
Numbers31:47 From this, the Israelites' share, Moses, as the LORD had ordered, took one out of every fifty, both of persons and of beasts, and gave them to the Levites, who had charge of the LORD'S Dwelling." 
Numbers31:48 Then the officers who had been clan and company commanders of the army came up to Moses 
Numbers31:49 and said to him, ""Your servants have counted up the soldiers under our command, and not one is missing." 
Numbers31:50 So, to make atonement for ourselves before the LORD, each of us will bring as an offering to the LORD some gold article he has picked up, such as an anklet, a bracelet, a ring, an earring, or a necklace.""" 
Numbers31:51 Moses and the priest Eleazar accepted this gold from them, all of it in well-wrought articles." 
Numbers31:52 The gold that they gave as a contribution to the LORD amounted in all to sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels. This was from the clan and company commanders; 
Numbers31:53 what the common soldiers had looted each one kept for himself. 
Numbers31:54 Moses, then, and the priest Eleazar accepted the gold from the clan and company commanders, and put it in the meeting tent as a memorial for the Israelites before the LORD." 
Numbers32:1 Now the Reubenites and Gadites had a very large number of livestock. Noticing that the land of Jazer and of Gilead was grazing country," 
Numbers32:2 they came to Moses and the priest Eleazar and to the princes of the community and said," 
Numbers32:3 The region of Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo and Baal-meon," 
Numbers32:4 which the LORD has laid low before the community of Israel, is grazing country. Now, since your servants have livestock,""" 
Numbers32:5 they continued, ""if we find favor with you, let this land be given to your servants as their property. Do not make us cross the Jordan.""" 
Numbers32:6 But Moses answered the Gadites and Reubenites: Are your kinsmen, then, to engage in war, while you remain here?" 
Numbers32:7 Why do you wish to discourage the Israelites from crossing to the land the LORD has given them? 
Numbers32:8 That is just what your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to reconnoiter the land. 
Numbers32:9 They went up to the Wadi Eshcol and reconnoitered the land, then so discouraged the Israelites that they would not enter the land the LORD had given them." 
Numbers32:10 At that time the wrath of the LORD flared up, and he swore," 
Numbers32:11 'Because they have not followed me unreservedly, none of these men of twenty years or more who have come up from Egypt shall ever see this country I promised under oath to Abraham and Isaac and Jacob," 
Numbers32:12 except the Kenizzite Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun, who have followed the LORD unreservedly.'" 
Numbers32:12 except the Kenizzite Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun, who have followed the LORD unreservedly.'" 
Numbers32:13 So in his anger with the Israelites the LORD made them wander in the desert forty years, until the whole generation that had done evil in the sight of the LORD had died out." 
Numbers32:14 And now here you are, a brood of sinners, rising up in your fathers' place to add still more to the LORD'S blazing wrath against the Israelites." 
Numbers32:15 If you turn away from following him, he will make them stay still longer in the desert, and so you will bring about the ruin of this whole nation.""" 
Numbers32:16 But they were insistent with him: We wish only to build sheepfolds here for our flocks, and towns for our families;" 
Numbers32:17 but we ourselves will march as troops in the van of the Israelites, until we have led them to their destination. Meanwhile our families can remain here in the fortified towns, safe from attack by the natives." 
Numbers32:18 We will not return to our homes until every one of the Israelites has taken possession of his heritage," 
Numbers32:19 and will not claim any heritage with them once we cross the Jordan, so long as we receive a heritage for ourselves on this eastern side of the Jordan.""" 
Numbers32:20 Moses said to them in reply: If you keep your word to march as troops in the LORD'S vanguard 
Numbers32:21 and to cross the Jordan in full force before the LORD until he has driven his enemies out of his way 
Numbers32:22 and the land is subdued before him, then you may return here, quit of every obligation to the LORD and to Israel, and this region shall be your possession before the LORD." 
Numbers32:23 But if you do not do this, you will sin against the LORD, and you can be sure that you will not escape the consequences of your sin." 
Numbers32:24 Build the towns, then, for your families, and the folds for your flocks, but also fulfill your express promise.""" 
Numbers32:25 The Gadites and Reubenites answered Moses, ""Your servants will do as you command, my lord." 
Numbers32:26 While our wives and children, our herds and other livestock remain in the towns of Gilead," 
Numbers32:27 all your servants will go across as armed troops to battle before the LORD, just as your lordship says.""" 
Numbers32:28 Moses, therefore, gave this order in their regard to the priest Eleazar, to Joshua, son of Nun, and to the heads of the ancestral tribes of the Israelites:" 
Numbers32:28 Moses, therefore, gave this order in their regard to the priest Eleazar, to Joshua, son of Nun, and to the heads of the ancestral tribes of the Israelites:" 
Numbers32:29 If all the Gadites and Reubenites cross the Jordan with you as combat troops before the LORD, you shall give them Gilead as their property when the land has been subdued before you." 
Numbers32:30 But if they will not go across with you as combat troops before the LORD, you shall bring their wives and children and livestock across before you into Canaan, and they shall have their property with you in the land of Canaan.""" 
Numbers32:31 To this the Gadites and Reubenites replied, ""We will do what the LORD has commanded us, your servants." 
Numbers32:32 We ourselves will go across into the land of Canaan as troops before the LORD, but we will retain our hereditary property on this side of the Jordan.""" 
Numbers32:33 So Moses gave them (the Gadites and Reubenites, as well as half the tribe of Manasseh, son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, king of Bashan,) the land with its towns and the districts that surrounded them." 
Numbers32:34 The Gadites rebuilt the fortified towns of Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer," 
Numbers32:35 Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah," 
Numbers32:36 Beth-nimrah and Beth-haran, and they built sheepfolds." 
Numbers32:37 The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim," 
Numbers32:38 Nebo, Baal-meon (names to be changed!), and Sibmah. These towns, which they rebuilt, they called by their old names." 
Numbers32:39 The descendants of Machir, son of Manasseh, invaded Gilead and captured it, driving out the Amorites who were there." 
Numbers32:40 (Moses gave Gilead to Machir, son of Manasseh, and he settled there.)" 
Numbers32:41 Jair, a Manassehite clan, campaigned against the tent villages, captured them and called them Havvoth-jair." 
Numbers32:42 Nobah also campaigned against Kenath, captured it with its dependencies and called it Nobah after his own name." 
Numbers33:1 The following are the stages by which the Israelites journeyed up by companies from the land of Egypt under the guidance of Moses and Aaron. 
Numbers33:2 By the LORD'S command Moses recorded the starting places of the various stages. The starting places of the successive stages were: 
Numbers33:3 They set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month. On the Passover morrow the Israelites went forth in triumph, in view of all Egypt," 
Numbers33:4 While the Egyptians buried their first-born all of whom the LORD had struck down; on their gods, too, the LORD executed judgments." 
Numbers33:5 Setting out from Rameses, the Israelites camped at Succoth." 
Numbers33:6 Setting out from Succoth, they camped at Etham near the edge of the desert." 
Numbers33:7 Setting out from Etham, they turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which is opposite Baal-zephon, and they camped opposite Migdol." 
Numbers33:8 Setting out from Pi-hahiroth, they crossed over through the sea into the desert, and after a three days' journey in the desert of Etham, they camped at Marah." 
Numbers33:9 Setting out from Marah, they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there." 
Numbers33:10 Setting out from Elim, they camped beside the Red Sea." 
Numbers33:11 Setting out from the Red Sea, they camped in the desert of Sin." 
Numbers33:12 Setting out from the desert of Sin, they camped at Dophkah." 
Numbers33:13 Setting out from Dophkah, they camped at Alush." 
Numbers33:14 Setting out from Alush, they camped at Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink." 
Numbers33:15 Setting out from Rephidim, they camped in the desert of Sinai." 
Numbers33:16 Setting out from the desert of Sinai, they camped at Kibroth-hattaavah." 
Numbers33:17 Setting out from Kibroth-hattaavah, they camped at Hazeroth." 
Numbers33:18 Setting out from Hazeroth, they camped at Rithmah." 
Numbers33:19 Setting out from Rithmah, they camped at Rimmon-perez." 
Numbers33:20 Setting out from Rimmon-perez, they camped at Libnah." 
Numbers33:21 Setting out from Libnah, they camped at Rissah." 
Numbers33:22 Setting out from Rissah, they camped at Kehelathah." 
Numbers33:23 Setting out from Kehelathah, they camped at Mount Shepher." 
Numbers33:24 Setting out from Mount Shepher, they camped at Haradah." 
Numbers33:25 Setting out from Haradah, they camped at Makheloth." 
Numbers33:26 Setting out from Makheloth, they camped at Tahath." 
Numbers33:27 Setting out from Tahath, they camped at Terah." 
Numbers33:28 Setting out from Terah, they camped at Mithkah." 
Numbers33:29 Setting out from Mithkah, they camped at Hashmonah." 
Numbers33:30 Setting out from Hashmonah,-They camped at Moseroth." 
Numbers33:31 Setting out from Moseroth, they camped at Bene-jaakan." 
Numbers33:32 Setting out from Bene-jaakan, they camped at Mount Gidgad." 
Numbers33:33 Setting out from Mount Gidgad, they camped at Jotbathah." 
Numbers33:34 Setting out from Jotbathah, they camped at Abronah." 
Numbers33:35 Setting out from Abronah, they camped at Ezion-geber." 
Numbers33:36 Setting out from Ezion-geber,-They camped in the desert of Zin, at Kadesh." 
Numbers33:37 Setting out from Kadesh, they camped at Mount Hor on the border of the land of Edom." 
Numbers33:38 (Aaron the priest ascended Mount Hor at the LORD'S command, and there he died in the fortieth year from the departure of the Israelites from the land of Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month." 
Numbers33:39 Aaron was a hundred and twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor. 
Numbers33:40 Now, when the Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb in the land of Canaan, heard that the Israelites were coming....)" 
Numbers33:41 Setting out from Mount Hor,-They camped at Zalmonah." 
Numbers33:42 Setting out from Zalmonah, they camped at Punon." 
Numbers33:43 Setting out from Punon, they camped at Oboth." 
Numbers33:44 Setting out from Oboth, they camped at Iye-abarim on the border of Moab." 
Numbers33:45 Setting out from Iye-abarim, they camped at Dibon-gad." 
Numbers33:46 Setting out from Dibon-gad, they camped at Almon-diblathaim." 
Numbers33:47 Setting out from Almon-diblathaim, they camped in the Abarim Mountains opposite Nebo." 
Numbers33:48 Setting out from the Abarim Mountains, they camped on the plains of Moab along the Jericho stretch of the Jordan." 
Numbers33:49 Their camp along the Jordan on the plains of Moab extended from Beth-jeshimoth to Abelshittim. 
Numbers33:50 The LORD spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab beside the Jericho stretch of the Jordan and said to him: 
Numbers33:51 Tell the Israelites: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan," 
Numbers33:52 drive out all the inhabitants of the land before you; destroy all their stone figures and molten images, and demolish all their high places." 
Numbers33:53 You shall take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given you the land as your property." 
Numbers33:54 You shall apportion the land among yourselves by lot, clan by clan, assigning a large heritage to a large group and a small heritage to a small group. Wherever anyone's lot falls, there shall his property be within the heritage of his ancestral tribe." 
Numbers33:55 But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land before you, those whom you allow to remain will become as barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides, and they will harass you in the country where you live," 
Numbers33:56 and I will treat you as I had intended to treat them. 
Numbers34:1 The LORD said to Moses," 
Numbers34:2 Give the Israelites this order: When you enter the land of Canaan, this is the territory that shall fall to you as your heritage, the land of Canaan with its boundaries:" 
Numbers34:3 Your southern boundary shall be at the desert of Zin along the border of Edom; on the east it shall begin at the end of the Salt Sea," 
Numbers34:4 and turning south of the Akrabbim Pass, it shall cross Zin, and extend south of Kadesh-barnea to Hazar-addar; thence it shall cross to Azmon," 
Numbers34:5 and turning from Azmon to the Wadi of Egypt, shall terminate at the Sea." 
Numbers34:6 For your western boundary you shall have the Great Sea with its coast; this shall be your western boundary. 
Numbers34:7 The following shall be your boundary on the north: from the Great Sea you shall draw a line to Mount Hor," 
Numbers34:8 and shall continue it from Mount Hor to Labo in the land of Hamath, with the boundary extending through Zedad." 
Numbers34:9 Thence the boundary shall reach to Ziphron and terminate at Hazar-enan. This shall be your northern boundary. 
Numbers34:10 For your eastern boundary you shall draw a line from Hazar-enan to Shepham. 
Numbers34:11 From Shepham the boundary shall go down to Ar-Baal, east of Ain, and descending further, shall strike the ridge on the east side of the Sea of Chinnereth;" 
Numbers34:12 thence the boundary shall continue along the Jordan and terminate with the Salt Sea. This is the land that shall be yours, with the boundaries that surround it.""" 
Numbers34:13 Moses also gave this order to the Israelites: This is the land, to be apportioned among you by lot, which the LORD has commanded to be given to the nine and one half tribes." 
Numbers34:14 For all the ancestral houses of the tribe of Reuben, and the ancestral houses of the tribe of Gad, as well as half of the tribe of Manasseh, have already received their heritage;" 
Numbers34:15 these two and one half tribes have received their heritage on the eastern side of the Jericho stretch of the Jordan, toward the sunrise.""" 
Numbers34:16 The LORD said to Moses," 
Numbers34:17 These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land among you: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua, son of Nun," 
Numbers34:17 These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land among you: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua, son of Nun," 
Numbers34:18 and one prince from each of the tribes whom you shall designate for this task. 
Numbers34:19 These shall be as follows: from the tribe of Judah: Caleb, son of Jephunneh," 
Numbers34:20 from the tribe of Simeon: Samuel, son of Ammihud;" 
Numbers34:21 from the tribe of Benjamin: Elidad, son of Chislon;" 
Numbers34:22 from the tribe of Dan: Bukki, son of Jogli;" 
Numbers34:23 from the tribe of Manasseh: Hanniel, son of Ephod; and" 
Numbers34:24 from the tribe of Ephraim: Kemuel, son of Shiphtan, for the descendants of Joseph;" 
Numbers34:25 from the tribe of Zebulun: Elizaphan, son of Parnach;" 
Numbers34:26 from the tribe of Issachar: Paltiel, son of Azzan;" 
Numbers34:27 from the tribe of Asher: Ahihud, son of Shelomi;" 
Numbers34:28 from the tribe of Naphtali: Pedahel, son of Ammihud.""" 
Numbers34:29 These are they whom the LORD commanded to assign the Israelites their heritage in the land of Canaan. 
Numbers35:1 The LORD gave these instructions to Moses on the plains of Moab beside the Jericho stretch of the Jordan: 
Numbers35:2 Tell the Israelites that out of their hereditary property they shall give the Levites cities for homes, as well as pasture lands around the cities." 
Numbers35:3 The cities shall serve them to dwell in, and the pasture lands shall serve their herds and flocks and other animals." 
Numbers35:4 The pasture lands of the cities to be assigned the Levites shall extend a thousand cubits from the city walls in each direction. 
Numbers35:5 Thus you shall measure out two thousand cubits outside the city along each side, east, south, west and north, with the city lying in the center. This shall serve them as the pasture lands of their cities." 
Numbers35:6 Now these are the cities you shall give to the Levites: the six cities of asylum which you must establish as places where a homicide can take refuge, and in addition forty-two other cities," 
Numbers35:7 a total of forty-eight cities with their pasture lands to be assigned the Levites. 
Numbers35:8 In assigning the cities from the property of the Israelites, take more from a larger group and fewer from a smaller one, so that each group will cede cities to the Levites in proportion to its own heritage.""" 
Numbers35:9 The LORD said to Moses," 
Numbers35:10 Tell the Israelites: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan," 
Numbers35:11 select for yourselves cities to serve as cities of asylum, where a homicide who has killed someone unintentionally may take refuge." 
Numbers35:12 These cities shall serve you as places of asylum from the avenger of blood, so that a homicide shall not be put to death unless he is first tried before the community." 
Numbers35:13 Six cities of asylum shall you assign: 
Numbers35:14 three beyond the Jordan, and three in the land of Canaan." 
Numbers35:15 These six cities of asylum shall serve not only the Israelites but all the resident or transient aliens among them, so that anyone who has killed another unintentionally may take refuge there." 
Numbers35:16 If a man strikes another with an iron instrument and causes his death, he is a murderer and shall be put to death." 
Numbers35:17 If a man strikes another with a death-dealing stone in his hand and causes his death, he is a murderer and shall be put to death." 
Numbers35:18 If a man strikes another with a death-dealing club in his hand and causes his death, he is a murderer and shall be put to death." 
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood may execute the murderer, putting him to death on sight." 
Numbers35:20 If a man pushes another out of hatred, or after lying in wait for him throws something at him, and causes his death," 
Numbers35:21 or if he strikes another out of enmity and causes his death, he shall be put to death as a murderer. The avenger of blood may execute the murderer on sight." 
Numbers35:22 However, if a man pushes another accidentally and not out of enmity, or if without lying in wait for him he throws some object at him," 
Numbers35:23 or without seeing him throws a death-dealing stone which strikes him and causes his death, although he was not his enemy nor seeking to harm him:" 
Numbers35:24 then the community, deciding the case between the slayer and the avenger of blood in accordance with these norms," 
Numbers35:25 shall free the homicide from the avenger of blood and shall remand him to the city of asylum where he took refuge; and he shall stay there until the death of the high priest who has been anointed with sacred oil. 
Numbers35:26 If the homicide of his own accord leaves the bounds of the city of asylum where he has taken refuge," 
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him beyond these bounds and kills him, the avenger incurs no bloodguilt;" 
Numbers35:28 the homicide was bound to stay in his city of asylum until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may the homicide return to his own district. 
Numbers35:29 These shall be norms for you and all your descendants, wherever you live, for rendering judgment." 
Numbers35:30 Whenever someone kills another, the evidence of witnesses is required for the execution of the murderer. The evidence of a single witness is not sufficient for putting a person to death." 
Numbers35:31 You shall not accept indemnity in place of the life of a murderer who deserves the death penalty; he must be put to death. 
Numbers35:32 Nor shall you accept indemnity to allow a refugee to leave his city of asylum and again dwell elsewhere in the land before the death of the high priest. 
Numbers35:33 You shall not desecrate the land where you live. Since bloodshed desecrates the land, the land can have no atonement for the blood shed on it except through the blood of him who shed it." 
Numbers35:34 Do not defile the land in which you live and in the midst of which I dwell; for I am the LORD who dwells in the midst of the Israelites. 
Numbers36:1 The heads of the ancestral houses in the clan of descendants of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, one of the Josephite clans, came up and laid this plea before Moses and the priest Eleazar and before the princes who were the heads of the ancestral houses of the other Israelites." 
Numbers36:2 They said: The LORD commanded you, my lord, to apportion the land by lot among the Israelites; and you, my lord, were also commanded by the LORD to give the heritage of our kinsman Zelophehad to his daughters." 
Numbers36:3 But if they marry into one of the other Israelite tribes, their heritage will be withdrawn from our ancestral heritage and will be added to that of the tribe into which they marry; thus the heritage that fell to us by lot will be diminished." 
Numbers36:4 When the Israelites celebrate the jubilee year, the heritage of these women will be permanently added to that of the tribe into which they marry and will be withdrawn from that of our ancestral tribe.""" 
Numbers36:5 So Moses gave this regulation to the Israelites according to the instructions of the LORD: The tribe of the Josephites are right in what they say. 
Numbers36:6 This is what the LORD commands with regard to the daughters of Zelophehad: They may marry anyone they please, provided they marry into a clan of their ancestral tribe," 
Numbers36:7 so that no heritage of the Israelites will pass from one tribe to another, but all the Israelites will retain their own ancestral heritage." 
Numbers36:8 Therefore, every daughter who inherits property in any of the Israelite tribes shall marry someone belonging to a clan of her own ancestral tribe, in order that all the Israelites may remain in possession of their own ancestral heritage." 
Numbers36:9 Thus, no heritage can pass from one tribe to another, but all the Israelite tribes will retain their own ancestral heritage.""" 
Numbers36:10 The daughters of Zelophehad obeyed the command which the LORD had given to Moses. 
Numbers36:11 Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah and Noah, Zelophehad's daughters, married relatives on their father's side" 
Numbers36:12 within the clans of the descendants of Manasseh, son of Joseph; hence their heritage remained in the tribe of their father's clan." 
Numbers36:13 These are the commandments and decisions which the LORD prescribed for the Israelites through Moses, on the plains of Moab beside the Jericho stretch of the Jordan." 
Deuteronomy1:1 These are the words which Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan ( in the desert, in the Arabah, opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth and Dizahab;" 
Deuteronomy1:2 it is a journey of eleven days from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea by way of the highlands of Seir). 
Deuteronomy1:3 In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites all the commands that the LORD had given him in their regard." 
Deuteronomy1:4 After he had defeated Sihon, king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, and Og, king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtaroth and in Edrei," 
Deuteronomy1:5 Moses began to explain the law in the land of Moab beyond the Jordan, as follows:" 
Deuteronomy1:6 The LORD, our God, said to us at Horeb, 'You have stayed long enough at this mountain." 
Deuteronomy1:7 Leave here and go to the hill country of the Amorites and to all the surrounding regions, the land of the Canaanites in the Arabah, the mountains, the foothills, the Negeb and the seacoast; to Lebanon, and as far as the Great River (the Euphrates)." 
Deuteronomy1:8 I have given that land over to you. Go now and occupy the land I swore to your fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, I would give to them and to their descendants.'" 
Deuteronomy1:9 At that time I said to you, 'Alone, I am unable to carry you." 
Deuteronomy1:10 The LORD, your God, has so multiplied you that you are now as numerous as the stars in the sky." 
Deuteronomy1:11 May the LORD, the God of your fathers, increase you a thousand times over, and bless you as he promised!" 
Deuteronomy1:12 But how can I alone bear the crushing burden that you are, along with your bickering?" 
Deuteronomy1:13 Choose wise, intelligent and experienced men from each of your tribes, that I may appoint them as your leaders.'" 
Deuteronomy1:14 You answered me, 'We agree to do as you have proposed.'" 
Deuteronomy1:15 So I took outstanding men of your tribes, wise and experienced, and made them your leaders as officials over thousands, over hundreds, over fifties and over tens, and other tribal officers." 
Deuteronomy1:16 I charged your judges at that time, 'Listen to complaints among your kinsmen, and administer true justice to both parties even if one of them is an alien." 
Deuteronomy1:17 In rendering judgment, do not consider who a person is; give ear to the lowly and to the great alike, fearing no man, for judgment is God's. Refer to me any case that is too hard for you and I will hear it.'" 
Deuteronomy1:18 Thereupon I gave you all the commands you were to fulfill. 
Deuteronomy1:19 Then, in obedience to the command of the LORD, our God, we set out from Horeb and journeyed through the whole desert, vast and fearful as you have seen, in the direction of the hill country of the Amorites. We had reached Kadesh-barnea" 
Deuteronomy1:20 when I said to you, 'You have come to the hill country of the Amorites, which the LORD, our God, is giving us." 
Deuteronomy1:21 The LORD, your God, has given this land over to you. Go up and occupy it, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, commands you. Do not fear or lose heart.'" 
Deuteronomy1:22 Then all of you came up to me and said, 'Let us send men ahead to reconnoiter the land for us and report to us on the road we must follow and the cities we must take.'" 
Deuteronomy1:23 Agreeing with the proposal, I chose twelve men from your number, one from each tribe." 
Deuteronomy1:24 They set out into the hill country as far as the Wadi Eshcol, and explored it." 
Deuteronomy1:25 Then, taking along some of the fruit of the land, they brought it down to us and reported, 'The land which the LORD, our God, gives us is good.'" 
Deuteronomy1:26 But you refused to go up, and after defying the command of the LORD, your God," 
Deuteronomy1:27 you set to murmuring in your tents, 'Out of hatred for us the LORD has brought us up out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the hands of the Amorites and destroy us." 
Deuteronomy1:28 What shall we meet with up there? Our kinsmen have made us fainthearted by reporting that the people are stronger and taller than we, and their cities are large and fortified to the sky; besides, they saw the Anakim there.'" 
Deuteronomy1:29 But I said to you, 'Have no dread or fear of them." 
Deuteronomy1:30 The LORD, your God, who goes before you, will himself fight for you, just as he took your part before your very eyes in Egypt," 
Deuteronomy1:31 as well as in the desert, where you saw how the LORD, your God, carried you, as a man carries his child, all along your journey until you arrived at this place.'" 
Deuteronomy1:32 Despite this, you would not trust the LORD, your God," 
Deuteronomy1:33 who journeys before you to find you a resting place, by day in the cloud, and by night in the fire, to show the way you must go." 
Deuteronomy1:34 When the LORD heard your words, he was angry;" 
Deuteronomy1:35 and he swore, 'Not one man of this evil generation shall look upon the good land I swore to give to your fathers," 
Deuteronomy1:36 except Caleb, son of Jephunneh; he shall see it. For to him and to his sons I will give the land he trod upon, because he has followed the LORD unreservedly.'" 
Deuteronomy1:37 The LORD was angered against me also on your account, and said, 'Not even you shall enter there," 
Deuteronomy1:38 but your aide Joshua, son of Nun, shall enter. Encourage him, for he is to give Israel its heritage." 
Deuteronomy1:38 but your aide Joshua, son of Nun, shall enter. Encourage him, for he is to give Israel its heritage." 
Deuteronomy1:39 Your little ones, who you said would become booty, and your children, who as yet do not know good from bad, they shall enter; to them I will give it, and they shall occupy it." 
Deuteronomy1:40 But as for yourselves: turn about and proceed into the desert on the Red Sea road.' 
Deuteronomy1:41 In reply you said to me, 'We have sinned against the LORD. We will go up ourselves and fight, just as the LORD, our God, commanded us.' And each of you girded on his weapons, making light of going up into the hill country." 
Deuteronomy1:42 But the LORD said to me, 'Warn them: Do not go up and fight, lest you be beaten down before your enemies, for I will not be in your midst.'" 
Deuteronomy1:43 I gave you this warning but you would not listen. In defiance of the LORD'S command you arrogantly marched off into the hill country. 
Deuteronomy1:44 Then the Amorites living there came out against you and, like bees, chased you, cutting you down in Seir as far as Hormah." 
Deuteronomy1:45 On your return you wept before the LORD, but he did not listen to your cry or give ear to you." 
Deuteronomy1:46 That is why you had to stay as long as you did at Kadesh. 
Deuteronomy2:1 When we did turn and proceed into the desert on the Red Sea road, as the LORD had commanded me, we circled around the highlands of Seir for a long time." 
Deuteronomy2:2 Finally the LORD said to me," 
Deuteronomy2:3 'You have wandered round these highlands long enough; turn and go north. 
Deuteronomy2:4 Give this order to the people: You are now about to pass through the territory of your kinsmen, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. Though they are afraid of you, be very careful" 
Deuteronomy2:5 not to come in conflict with them, for I will not give you so much as a foot of their land, since I have already given Esau possession of the highlands of Seir." 
Deuteronomy2:6 You shall purchase from them with silver the food you eat and the well water you drink. 
Deuteronomy2:7 The LORD, your God, has blessed you in all your undertakings; he has been concerned about your journey through this vast desert. It is now forty years that he has been with you, and you have never been in want.'" 
Deuteronomy2:8 Then we left behind us the Arabah route, Elath, Ezion-geber, and Seir, where our kinsmen, the descendants of Esau, live; and we went on toward the desert of Moab." 
Deuteronomy2:9 And the LORD said to me, 'Do not show hostility to the Moabites or engage them in battle, for I will not give you possession of any of their land, since I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot as their own." 
Deuteronomy2:10 (Formerly the Emim lived there, a people strong and numerous and tall like the Anakim;" 
Deuteronomy2:11 like them they were considered Rephaim. It was the Moabites who called them Emim. 
Deuteronomy2:12 In Seir, however, the former inhabitants were the Horites; the descendants of Esau dispossessed them, clearing them out of the way and taking their place, just as the Israelites have done in the land of their heritage which the LORD has given them.)" 
Deuteronomy2:13 Get ready, then, to cross the Wadi Zered.' So we crossed it." 
Deuteronomy2:14 Thirty-eight years had elapsed between our departure from Kadesh-barnea and that crossing; in the meantime the whole generation of soldiers had perished from the camp, as the LORD had sworn they should." 
Deuteronomy2:15 For it was the LORD'S hand that was against them, till he wiped them out of the camp completely." 
Deuteronomy2:16 When at length death had put an end to all the soldiers among the people," 
Deuteronomy2:17 the LORD said to me," 
Deuteronomy2:18 'You are now about to leave Ar and the territory of Moab behind. 
Deuteronomy2:19 As you come opposite the Ammonites, do not show hostility or come in conflict with them, for I will not give you possession of any land of the Ammonites, since I have given it to the descendants of Lot as their own." 
Deuteronomy2:20 (This also was considered a country of the Rephaim from its former inhabitants, whom the Ammonites called Zamzummim," 
Deuteronomy2:21 a people strong and numerous and tall like the Anakim. But these, too, the LORD cleared out of the way for the Ammonites, who ousted them and took their place." 
Deuteronomy2:22 He had done the same for the descendants of Esau, who dwell in Seir, by clearing the Horites out of their way, so that the descendants of Esau have taken their place down to the present." 
Deuteronomy2:23 So also the Caphtorim, migrating from Caphtor, cleared away the Avvim, who once dwelt in villages as far as Gaza, and took their place.)" 
Deuteronomy2:24 'Advance now across the Wadi Arnon. I now deliver into your hands Sihon, the Amorite king of Heshbon, and his land. Begin the occupation; engage him in battle." 
Deuteronomy2:25 This day I will begin to put a fear and dread of you into every nation under the heavens, so that at the mention of your name they will quake and tremble before you.'" 
Deuteronomy2:26 So I sent messengers from the desert of Kedemoth to Sihon, king of Heshbon, with this offer of peace:" 
Deuteronomy2:27 'Let me pass through your country by the highway; I will go along it without turning aside to the right or to the left. 
Deuteronomy2:28 For the food I eat which you will supply, and for the water you give me to drink, you shall be paid in silver. Only let me march through," 
Deuteronomy2:29 as the descendants of Esau who dwell in Seir and the Moabites who dwell in Ar have done, until I cross the Jordan into the land which the LORD, our God, is about to give us.'" 
Deuteronomy2:30 But Sihon, king of Heshbon, refused to let us pass through his land, because the LORD, your God, made him stubborn in mind and obstinate in heart that he might deliver him up to you, as indeed he has now done." 
Deuteronomy2:31 Then the LORD said to me, 'Now that I have already begun to hand over to you Sihon and his land, begin the actual occupation.'" 
Deuteronomy2:32 So Sihon and all his people advanced against us to join battle at Jahaz; 
Deuteronomy2:33 but since the LORD, our God, had delivered him to us, we defeated him and his sons and all his people." 
Deuteronomy2:34 At that time we seized all his cities and doomed them all, with their men, women and children; we left no survivor." 
Deuteronomy2:35 Our only booty was the livestock and the loot of the captured cities. 
Deuteronomy2:36 From Aroer on the edge of the Wadi Arnon and from the city in the wadi itself, as far as Gilead, no city was too well fortified for us to whom the LORD had delivered them up." 
Deuteronomy2:37 However, in obedience to the command of the LORD, our God, you did not encroach upon any of the Ammonite land, neither the region bordering on the Wadi Jabbok, nor the cities of the highlands." 
Deuteronomy3:1 Then we turned and proceeded toward Bashan. But Og, king of Bashan, advanced against us with all his people to give battle at Edrei." 
Deuteronomy3:2 The LORD, however, said to me, 'Do not be afraid of him, for I have delivered him into your hand with all his people and his land. Do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon.'" 
Deuteronomy3:3 And thus the LORD, our God, delivered into our hands Og, king of Bashan, with all his people. We defeated him so completely that we left him no survivor." 
Deuteronomy3:4 At that time we captured all his cities, none of them eluding our grasp, the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan: sixty cities in all," 
Deuteronomy3:5 to say nothing of the great number of unwalled towns. All the cities were fortified with high walls and gates and bars. 
Deuteronomy3:6 As we had done to Sihon, king of Heshbon, so also here we doomed all the cities, with their men, women and children;" 
Deuteronomy3:7 but all the livestock and the loot of each city we took as booty for ourselves. 
Deuteronomy3:8 And so at that time we took from the two kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan the territory from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon 
Deuteronomy3:9 (which is called Sirion by the Sidonians and Senir by the Amorites)," 
Deuteronomy3:10 comprising all the cities of the plateau and all Gilead and all the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan including Salecah and Edrei. 
Deuteronomy3:11 (Og, king of Bashan, was the last remaining survivor of the Rephaim. He had a bed of iron, nine regular cubits long and four wide, which is still preserved in Rabbah of the Ammonites.)" 
Deuteronomy3:12 When we occupied the land at that time, I gave Reuben and Gad the territory from Aroer, on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, halfway up into the highlands of Gilead, with the cities therein." 
Deuteronomy3:13 The rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, the kingdom of Og, the whole Argob region, I gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh. (All this region of Bashan was once called a land of the Rephaim." 
Deuteronomy3:14 Jair, a Manassehite clan, took all the region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and Maacathites, and called it after his own name Bashan Havvoth-jair, the name it bears today.)" 
Deuteronomy3:15 To Machir I gave Gilead," 
Deuteronomy3:16 and to Reuben and Gad the territory from Gilead to the Wadi Arnon, including the wadi bed and its banks, and to the Wadi Jabbok, which is the border of the Ammonites," 
Deuteronomy3:17 as well as the Arabah with the Jordan and its eastern banks from Chinnereth to the Salt Sea of the Arabah, under the slopes of Pisgah." 
Deuteronomy3:18 At that time I charged them as follows: 'The LORD, your God, has given you this land as your own. But all you troops equipped for battle must cross over in the vanguard of your brother Israelites." 
Deuteronomy3:19 Only your wives and children, as well as your livestock, of which I know you have a large number, shall remain behind in the towns I have given you," 
Deuteronomy3:20 until the LORD has settled your kinsmen as well, and they too possess the land which the LORD, your God, will give them on the other side of the Jordan. Then you may all return to the possessions I have given you.'" 
Deuteronomy3:21 It was then that I instructed Joshua, 'Your eyes have seen all that the LORD, your God, has done to both these kings; so, too, will the LORD do to all the kingdoms which you will encounter over there." 
Deuteronomy3:21 It was then that I instructed Joshua, 'Your eyes have seen all that the LORD, your God, has done to both these kings; so, too, will the LORD do to all the kingdoms which you will encounter over there." 
Deuteronomy3:22 Fear them not, for the LORD, your God, will fight for you.'" 
Deuteronomy3:23 And it was then that I besought the LORD," 
Deuteronomy3:24 'O Lord GOD, you have begun to show to your servant your greatness and might. For what god in heaven or on earth can perform deeds as mighty as yours?" 
Deuteronomy3:25 Ah, let me cross over and see this good land beyond the Jordan, this fine hill country, and the Lebanon!'" 
Deuteronomy3:26 But the LORD was angry with me on your account and would not hear me. 'Enough!' the LORD said to me. 'Speak to me no more of this. 
Deuteronomy3:27 Go up to the top of Pisgah and look out to the west, and to the north, and to the south, and to the east. Look well, for you shall not cross this Jordan." 
Deuteronomy3:28 Commission Joshua, and encourage and strengthen him, for he shall cross at the head of this people and shall put them in possession of the land you are to see.'" 
Deuteronomy3:28 Commission Joshua, and encourage and strengthen him, for he shall cross at the head of this people and shall put them in possession of the land you are to see.'" 
Deuteronomy3:29 This was while we were in the ravine opposite Beth-peor. 
Deuteronomy4:1 Now, Israel, hear the statutes and decrees which I am teaching you to observe, that you may live, and may enter in and take possession of the land which the LORD, the God of your fathers, is giving you." 
Deuteronomy4:2 In your observance of the commandments of the LORD, your God, which I enjoin upon you, you shall not add to what I command you nor subtract from it." 
Deuteronomy4:3 You have seen with your own eyes what the LORD did at Baal-peor: the LORD, your God, destroyed from your midst everyone that followed the Baal of Peor;" 
Deuteronomy4:4 but you, who clung to the LORD, your God, are all alive today." 
Deuteronomy4:5 Therefore, I teach you the statutes and decrees as the LORD, my God, has commanded me, that you may observe them in the land you are entering to occupy." 
Deuteronomy4:6 Observe them carefully, for thus will you give evidence of your wisdom and intelligence to the nations, who will hear of all these statutes and say, 'This great nation is truly a wise and intelligent people.'" 
Deuteronomy4:7 For what great nation is there that has gods so close to it as the LORD, our God, is to us whenever we call upon him?" 
Deuteronomy4:8 Or what great nation has statutes and decrees that are as just as this whole law which I am setting before you today? 
Deuteronomy4:9 However, take care and be earnestly on your guard not to forget the things which your own eyes have seen, nor let them slip from your memory as long as you live, but teach them to your children and to your children's children." 
Deuteronomy4:10 There was the day on which you stood before the LORD, your God, at Horeb, and he said to me, 'Assemble the people for me; I will have them hear my words, that they may learn to fear me as long as they live in the land and may so teach their children.'" 
Deuteronomy4:11 You came near and stood at the foot of the mountain, which blazed to the very sky with fire and was enveloped in a dense black cloud." 
Deuteronomy4:12 Then the LORD spoke to you from the midst of the fire. You heard the sound of the words, but saw no form; there was only a voice." 
Deuteronomy4:13 He proclaimed to you his covenant, which he commanded you to keep: the ten commandments, which he wrote on two tablets of stone." 
Deuteronomy4:14 The LORD charged me at that time to teach you the statutes and decrees which you are to observe over in the land you will occupy. 
Deuteronomy4:15 You saw no form at all on the day the LORD spoke to you at Horeb from the midst of the fire. Be strictly on your guard, therefore," 
Deuteronomy4:16 not to degrade yourselves by fashioning an idol to represent any figure, whether it be the form of a man or a woman," 
Deuteronomy4:17 of any animal on the earth or of any bird that flies in the sky," 
Deuteronomy4:18 of anything that crawls on the ground or of any fish in the waters under the earth. 
Deuteronomy4:19 And when you look up to the heavens and behold the sun or the moon or any star among the heavenly hosts, do not be led astray into adoring them and serving them. These the LORD, your God, has let fall to the lot of all other nations under the heavens;" 
Deuteronomy4:20 but you he has taken and led out of that iron foundry, Egypt, that you might be his very own people, as you are today." 
Deuteronomy4:21 Since the LORD was angered against me on your account and swore that I should not cross the Jordan nor enter the good land which he is giving you as a heritage," 
Deuteronomy4:22 I myself shall die in this country without crossing the Jordan; but you will cross over and take possession of that good land. 
Deuteronomy4:23 Take heed, therefore, lest, forgetting the covenant which the LORD, your God, has made with you, you fashion for yourselves against his command an idol in any form whatsoever." 
Deuteronomy4:24 For the LORD, your God, is a consuming fire, a jealous God." 
Deuteronomy4:25 When you have children and grandchildren, and have grown old in the land, should you then degrade yourselves by fashioning an idol in any form and by this evil done in his sight provoke the LORD, your God," 
Deuteronomy4:26 I call heaven and earth this day to witness against you, that you shall all quickly perish from the land which you will occupy when you cross the Jordan. You shall not live in it for any length of time but shall be promptly wiped out." 
Deuteronomy4:27 The LORD will scatter you among the nations, and there shall remain but a handful of you among the nations to which the LORD will lead you." 
Deuteronomy4:28 There you shall serve gods fashioned by the hands of man out of wood and stone, gods which can neither see nor hear, neither eat nor smell." 
Deuteronomy4:29 Yet there too you shall seek the LORD, your God; and you shall indeed find him when you search after him with your whole heart and your whole soul." 
Deuteronomy4:30 In your distress, when all these things shall have come upon you, you shall finally return to the LORD, your God, and heed his voice." 
Deuteronomy4:31 Since the LORD, your God, is a merciful God, he will not abandon and destroy you, nor forget the covenant which under oath he made with your fathers." 
Deuteronomy4:32 Ask now of the days of old, before your time, ever since God created man upon the earth; ask from one end of the sky to the other: Did anything so great ever happen before? Was it ever heard of?" 
Deuteronomy4:33 Did a people ever hear the voice of God speaking from the midst of fire, as you did, and live?" 
Deuteronomy4:34 Or did any god venture to go and take a nation for himself from the midst of another nation, by testings, by signs and wonders, by war, with his strong hand and outstretched arm, and by great terrors, all of which the LORD, your God, did for you in Egypt before your very eyes?" 
Deuteronomy4:35 All this you were allowed to see that you might know the LORD is God and there is no other. 
Deuteronomy4:36 Out of the heavens he let you hear his voice to discipline you; on earth he let you see his great fire, and you heard him speaking out of the fire." 
Deuteronomy4:37 For love of your fathers he chose their descendants and personally led you out of Egypt by his great power," 
Deuteronomy4:38 driving out of your way nations greater and mightier than you, so as to bring you in and to make their land your heritage, as it is today." 
Deuteronomy4:39 This is why you must now know, and fix in your heart, that the LORD is God in the heavens above and on earth below, and that there is no other." 
Deuteronomy4:40 You must keep his statutes and commandments which I enjoin on you today, that you and your children after you may prosper, and that you may have long life on the land which the LORD, your God, is giving you forever.""" 
Deuteronomy4:41 Then Moses set apart three cities in the region east of the Jordan," 
Deuteronomy4:42 that a homicide might take refuge there if he unwittingly killed his neighbor to whom he had previously borne no malice, and that he might save his life by fleeing to one of these cities:" 
Deuteronomy4:43 Bezer in the desert, in the region of the plateau, for the Reubenites; Ramoth in Gilead for the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan for the Manassehites." 
Deuteronomy4:44 This is the law which Moses set before the Israelites. 
Deuteronomy4:45 These are the ordinances, statutes and decrees which he proclaimed to them when they had come out of Egypt" 
Deuteronomy4:46 and were beyond the Jordan in the ravine opposite Beth-peor, in the land of Sihon, king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon and whom Moses and the Israelites defeated after coming out of Egypt." 
Deuteronomy4:47 They occupied his land and the land of Og, king of Bashan, as well, the land of these two kings of the Amorites in the region east of the Jordan:" 
Deuteronomy4:48 from Aroer on the edge of the Wadi Arnon to Mount Sion (that is Hermon) 
Deuteronomy4:49 and all the Arabah east of the Jordan, as far as the Arabah Sea under the slopes of Pisgah." 
Deuteronomy5:1 Moses summoned all Israel and said to them, ""Hear, O Israel, the statutes and decrees which I proclaim in your hearing this day, that you may learn them and take care to observe them." 
Deuteronomy5:2 The LORD, our God, made a covenant with us at Horeb;" 
Deuteronomy5:3 not with our fathers did he make this covenant, but with us, all of us who are alive here this day." 
Deuteronomy5:4 The LORD spoke with you face to face on the mountain from the midst of the fire. 
Deuteronomy5:5 Since you were afraid of the fire and would not go up the mountain, I stood between the LORD and you at that time, to announce to you these words of the LORD:" 
Deuteronomy5:6 'I, the LORD, am your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that place of slavery." 
Deuteronomy5:7 You shall not have other gods besides me. 
Deuteronomy5:8 You shall not carve idols for yourselves in the shape of anything in the sky above or on the earth below or in the waters beneath the earth;
