Job11:3 Shall your babblings keep men silent, and shall you deride and no one give rebuke?" 
Job11:4 Shall you say: My teaching is pure, and I am clean in your sight""?" 
Job11:5 But oh, that God would speak, and open his lips against you," 
Job11:6 And tell you that the secrets of wisdom are twice as effective: So you might learn that God will make you answer for your guilt. 
Job11:7 Can you penetrate the designs of God? Dare you vie with the perfection of the Almighty? 
Job11:8 It is higher than the heavens; what can you do? It is deeper than the nether world; what can you know? 
Job11:9 It is longer than the earth in measure, and broader than the sea." 
Job11:10 If he seize and imprison or call to judgment, who then can say him nay?" 
Job11:11 For he knows the worthlessness of men and sees iniquity; will he then ignore it? 
Job11:12 Will empty man then gain understanding, and the wild jackass be made docile?" 
Job11:13 If you set your heart aright and stretch out your hands toward him," 
Job11:14 If you remove all iniquity from your conduct, and let not injustice dwell in your tent," 
Job11:15 Surely then you may lift up your face in innocence; you may stand firm and unafraid. 
Job11:16 For then you shall forget your misery, or recall it like waters that have ebbed away." 
Job11:17 Then your life shall be brighter than the noonday; its gloom shall become as the morning," 
Job11:18 And you shall be secure, because there is hope; you shall look round you and lie down in safety," 
Job11:19 and you shall take your rest with none to disturb. Many shall entreat your favor," 
Job11:20 but the wicked, looking on, shall be consumed with envy. Escape shall be cut off from them, they shall wait to expire." 
Job12:1 Then Job replied and said: 
Job12:2 No doubt you are the intelligent folk, and with you wisdom shall die!" 
Job12:3 But I have intelligence as well as you; for who does not know such things as these? 
Job12:4 I have become the sport of my neighbors: The one whom God answers when he calls upon him, The just, the perfect man,"" is a laughing-stock;" 
Job12:5 The undisturbed esteem my downfall a disgrace such as awaits unsteady feet; 
Job12:6 Yet the tents of robbers are prosperous, and those who provoke God are secure." 
Job12:7 But now ask the beasts to teach you, and the birds of the air to tell you;" 
Job12:8 Or the reptiles on earth to instruct you, and the fish of the sea to inform you." 
Job12:9 Which of all these does not know that the hand of God has done this? 
Job12:10 In his hand is the soul of every living thing, and the life breath of all mankind." 
Job12:11 Does not the ear judge words as the mouth tastes food? 
Job12:12 So with old age is wisdom, and with length of days understanding." 
Job12:13 With him are wisdom and might; his are counsel and understanding. 
Job12:14 If he breaks a thing down, there is no rebuilding; if he imprisons a man, there is no release." 
Job12:15 He holds back the waters and there is drought; he sends them forth and they overwhelm the land. 
Job12:16 With him are strength and prudence; the misled and the misleaders are his. 
Job12:17 He sends counselors away barefoot, and of judges he makes fools." 
Job12:18 He loosens the bonds imposed by kings and leaves but a waistcloth to bind the king's own loins. 
Job12:19 and lets their never-failing waters flow away. 
Job12:20 He silences the trusted adviser, and takes discretion from the aged." 
Job12:21 He breaks down the barriers of the streams 
Job12:22 The recesses of the darkness he discloses, and brings the gloom forth to the light." 
Job12:23 He makes nations great and he destroys them; he spreads peoples abroad and he abandons them. 
Job12:24 He takes understanding from the leaders of the land," 
Job12:25 till they grope in the darkness without light; he makes them stagger like drunken men. 
Job13:1 Lo, all this my eye has seen; my ear has heard and perceived it." 
Job13:2 What you know, I also know; I fall not short of you." 
Job13:3 But I would speak with the Almighty; I wish to reason with God. 
Job13:4 You are glossing over falsehoods and offering vain remedies, every one of you!" 
Job13:5 Oh, that you would be altogether silent! This for you would be wisdom." 
Job13:6 Hear now the rebuke I shall utter and listen to the reproof from my lips. 
Job13:7 Is it for God that you speak falsehood? Is it for him that you utter deceit? 
Job13:8 Is it for him that you show partiality? Do you play advocate on behalf of God? 
Job13:9 Will it be well when he shall search you out? Would you impose on him as one does on men? 
Job13:10 He will openly rebuke you if even in secret you show partiality. 
Job13:11 Surely will his majesty affright you and the dread of him fall upon you. 
Job13:12 Your reminders are ashy maxims, your fabrications are mounds of clay." 
Job13:13 Be silent, let me alone! that I may speak and give vent to my feelings." 
Job13:14 I will carry my flesh between my teeth, and take my life in my hand." 
Job13:15 Slay me though he might, I will wait for him; I will defend my conduct before him." 
Job13:16 And this shall be my salvation, that no impious man can come into his presence." 
Job13:17 Pay careful heed to my speech, and give my statement a hearing." 
Job13:18 Behold, I have prepared my case, I know that I am in the right." 
Job13:19 If anyone can make a case against me, then I shall be silent and die." 
Job13:20 These things only do not use against me, then from your presence I need not hide:" 
Job13:21 Withdraw your hand far from me, and let not the terror of you frighten me." 
Job13:22 Then call me, and I will respond; or let me speak first, and answer me." 
Job13:23 What are my faults and my sins? My misdeeds and my sins make known to me! 
Job13:24 Why do you hide your face and consider me your enemy? 
Job13:25 Will you harass a wind-driven leaf, or pursue a withered straw?" 
Job13:26 For you draw up bitter indictments against me, and punish in me the faults of my youth." 
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks; you watch all my paths and trace out all my footsteps. 
Job13:28 Though he wears out like a leather bottle, like a garment that the moth has consumed?" 
Job14:1 Man born of woman is short-lived and full of trouble," 
Job14:2 Like a flower that springs up and fades, swift as a shadow that does not abide." 
Job14:3 Upon such a one will you cast your eyes so as to bring him into judgment before you," 
Job14:4 Can a man be found who is clean of defilement? There is none," 
Job14:5 however short his days. You know the number of his months; you have fixed the limit which he cannot pass. 
Job14:6 Look away from him and let him be, while, like a hireling, he completes his day." 
Job14:7 For a tree there is hope, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again and that its tender shoots will not cease." 
Job14:8 Even though its root grow old in the earth, and its stump die in the dust," 
Job14:9 Yet at the first whiff of water it may flourish again and put forth branches like a young plant. 
Job14:10 But when a man dies, all vigor leaves him; when man expires, where then is he?" 
Job14:11 As when the waters of a lake fail, or a stream grows dry and parches," 
Job14:12 So men lie down and rise not again. Till the heavens are no more, they shall not awake, nor be roused out of their sleep." 
Job14:13 Oh, that you would hide me in the nether world and keep me sheltered till your wrath is past; would fix a time for me, and then remember me!" 
Job14:14 When a man has died, were he to live again, all the days of my drudgery I would wait, until my relief should come." 
Job14:15 You would call, and I would answer you; you would esteem the work of your hands." 
Job14:16 Surely then you would count my steps, and not keep watch for sin in me." 
Job14:17 My misdeeds would be sealed up in a pouch, and you would cover over my guilt." 
Job14:18 But as a mountain falls at last and its rock is moved from its place," 
Job14:19 As waters wear away the stones and floods wash away the soil of the land, so you destroy the hope of man." 
Job14:20 You prevail once for all against him and he passes on; with changed appearance you send him away. 
Job14:21 If his sons are honored, he is not aware of it; if they are in disgrace, he does not know about them." 
Job14:22 Only his own flesh pains him, and his soul grieves for him." 
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite spoke and said: 
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer with airy opinions, or puff himself up with wind?" 
Job15:3 Should he argue in speech which does not avail, and in words which are to no profit?" 
Job15:4 You in fact do away with piety, and you lessen devotion toward God," 
Job15:5 Because your wickedness instructs your mouth, and you choose to speak like the crafty." 
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you, not I; your own lips refute you." 
Job15:7 Are you indeed the first-born of mankind, or were you brought forth before the hills?" 
Job15:8 Are you privy to the counsels of God, and do you restrict wisdom to yourself?" 
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What intelligence have you which we have not? 
Job15:10 There are gray-haired old men among us more advanced in years than your father. 
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God not enough for you, and speech that deals gently with you?" 
Job15:12 Why do your notions carry you away, and why do your eyes blink," 
Job15:13 So that you turn your anger against God and let such words escape your mouth! 
Job15:14 What is a man that he should be blameless, one born of woman that he should be righteous?" 
Job15:15 If in his holy ones God places no confidence, and if the heavens are not clean in his sight," 
Job15:16 How much less so is the abominable, the corrupt: man, who drinks in iniquity like water!" 
Job15:17 I will show you, if you listen to me; what I have seen I will tell," 
Job15:18 What wise men relate and have not contradicted since the days of their fathers," 
Job15:19 To whom alone the land was given, when no foreigner moved among them." 
Job15:20 The wicked man is in torment all his days, and limited years are in store for the tyrant;" 
Job15:21 The sound of terrors is in his ears; when all is prosperous, the spoiler comes upon him." 
Job15:22 He despairs of escaping the darkness, and looks ever for the sword;" 
Job15:23 A wanderer, food for the vultures, he knows that his destruction is imminent." 
Job15:24 By day the darkness fills him with dread; distress and anguish overpower him. 
Job15:25 Because he has stretched out his hand against God and bade defiance to the Almighty," 
Job15:26 One shall rush sternly upon him with the stout bosses of his shield, like a king prepared for the charge." 
Job15:27 Because he has blinded himself with his crassness, padding his loins with fat," 
Job15:28 He shall dwell in ruinous cities, in houses that are deserted, That are crumbling into clay" 
Job15:29 with no shadow to lengthen over the ground. He shall not be rich, and his possessions shall not endure;" 
Job15:30 A flame shall wither him up in his early growth, and with the wind his blossoms shall disappear." 
Job15:31 for vain shall be his bartering. 
Job15:32 His stalk shall wither before its time, and his branches shall be green no more." 
Job15:33 He shall be like a vine that sheds its grapes unripened, and like an olive tree casting off its bloom." 
Job15:34 For the breed of the impious shall be sterile, and fire shall consume the tents of extortioners." 
Job15:35 They conceive malice and bring forth emptiness; they give birth to failure. 
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said: 
Job16:2 I have heard this sort of thing many times. Wearisome comforters are you all! 
Job16:3 Is there no end to windy words? Or what sickness have you that you speak on? 
Job16:4 I also could talk as you do, were you in my place. I could declaim over you, or wag my head at you;" 
Job16:5 I could strengthen you with talk, or shake my head with silent lips." 
Job16:6 If I speak, this pain I have will not be checked; if I leave off, it will not depart from me." 
Job16:7 But now that I am exhausted and stunned, all my company has closed in on me." 
Job16:8 As a witness there rises up my traducer, speaking openly against me;" 
Job16:9 I am the prey his wrath assails, he gnashes his teeth against me. My enemies lord it over me;" 
Job16:10 their mouths are agape to bite me. They smite me on the cheek insultingly; they are all enlisted against me. 
Job16:11 God has given me over to the impious; into the clutches of the wicked he has cast me. 
Job16:12 I was in peace, but he dislodged me; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces. He has set me up for a target;" 
Job16:13 his arrows strike me from all directions, He pierces my sides without mercy, he pours out my gall upon the ground." 
Job16:14 He pierces me with thrust upon thrust; he attacks me like a warrior. 
Job16:15 I have fastened sackcloth over my skin, and have laid my brow in the dust." 
Job16:16 My face is inflamed with weeping and there is darkness over my eyes," 
Job16:17 Although my hands are free from violence, and my prayer is sincere." 
Job16:18 O earth, cover not my blood, nor let my outcry come to rest!" 
Job16:19 Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my spokesman is on high." 
Job16:20 My friends it is who wrong me; before God my eyes drop tears," 
Job16:21 That he may do justice for a mortal in his presence and decide between a man and his neighbor. 
Job16:22 For my years are numbered now, and I am on a journey from which I shall not return." 
Job17:1 My spirit is broken, my lamp of life extinguished; my burial is at hand." 
Job17:2 I am indeed mocked, and, as their provocation mounts, my eyes grow dim." 
Job17:3 Grant me one to offer you a pledge on my behalf: who is there that will give surety for me? 
Job17:4 You darken their minds to knowledge; therefore they do not understand. 
Job17:5 My lot is described as evil," 
Job17:6 and I am made a byword of the people; their object lesson I have become. 
Job17:7 My eye has grown blind with anguish, and all my frame is shrunken to a shadow." 
Job17:8 Upright men are astonished at this, and the innocent aroused against the wicked." 
Job17:9 Yet the righteous shall hold to his way, and he who has clean hands increase in strength." 
Job17:10 But turn now, and come on again; for I shall not find a wise man among you!" 
Job17:11 My days are passed away, my plans are at an end, the cherished purposes of my heart." 
Job17:12 Such men change the night into day; where there is darkness they talk of approaching light. 
Job17:13 If I look for the nether world as my dwelling, if I spread my couch in the darkness," 
Job17:14 If I must call corruption my father,"" and the maggot ""my mother"" and ""my sister,""" 
Job17:15 Where then is my hope, and my prosperity, who shall see?" 
Job17:16 Will they descend with me into the nether world? Shall we go down together into the dust? 
Job18:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite replied and said: 
Job18:2 When will you put an end to words? Reflect, and then we can have discussion." 
Job18:3 Why are we accounted like the beasts, their equals in your sight?" 
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in your anger, shall the earth be neglected on your account (or the rock be moved out of its place)?" 
Job18:5 Truly, the light of the wicked is extinguished; no flame brightens his hearth." 
Job18:6 The light is darkened in his tent; in spite of him, his lamp goes out." 
Job18:7 His vigorous steps are hemmed in, and his own counsel casts him down." 
Job18:8 For he rushes headlong into a net, and he wanders into a pitfall." 
Job18:9 A trap seizes him by the heel, and a snare lays hold of him." 
Job18:10 A noose for him is hid on the ground, and the toils for him on the way." 
Job18:11 On every side terrors affright him; they harry him at each step. 
Job18:12 Disaster is ready at his side," 
Job18:13 the first-born of death consumes his limbs. 
Job18:14 Fiery destruction lodges in his tent, and marches him off to the king of terrors. He is plucked from the security of his tent;" 
Job18:15 over his abode brimstone is scattered. 
Job18:16 Below, his roots dry up, and above, his branches wither." 
Job18:17 His memory perishes from the land, and he has no name on the earth." 
Job18:18 He is driven from light into darkness, and banished out of the world." 
Job18:19 He has neither son nor grandson among his people, nor any survior where once he dwelt." 
Job18:20 They who come after shall be appalled at his fate; they who went before are struck with horror. 
Job18:21 So is it then with the dwelling of the impious man, and such is the place of him who knows not God!" 
Job19:1 Then Job answered and said: 
Job19:2 How long will you vex my soul, grind me down with words?" 
Job19:3 These ten times you have reviled me, have assailed me without shame!" 
Job19:4 Be it indeed that I am at fault and that my fault remains with me," 
Job19:5 Even so, if you would vaunt yourselves against me and cast up to me any reproach," 
Job19:6 Know then that God has dealt unfairly with me, and compassed me round with his net." 
Job19:7 If I cry out Injustice! I am not heard. I cry for help, but there is no redress." 
Job19:8 He has barred my way and I cannot pass; he has veiled my path in darkness; 
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my glory, and taken the diadem from my brow." 
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side, and I am gone; my hope he has uprooted like a tree." 
Job19:11 His wrath he has kindled against me; he counts me among his enemies. 
Job19:12 His troops advance as one man; they build up their road to attack me, and they encamp around my tent." 
Job19:13 My brethren have withdrawn from me, and my friends are wholly estranged." 
Job19:14 My kinsfolk and companions neglect me, and my guests have forgotten me." 
Job19:15 Even my handmaids treat me as a stranger; I am an alien in their sight. 
Job19:16 I call my servant, but he gives no answer, though in my speech I plead with him." 
Job19:17 My breath is abhorred by my wife; I am loathsome to the men of my family. 
Job19:18 The young children, too, despise me; when I appear, they speak against me." 
Job19:19 All my intimate friends hold me in horror; those whom I loved have turned against me! 
Job19:20 My bones cleave to my skin, and I have escaped with my flesh between my teeth." 
Job19:21 Pity me, pity me, O you my friends, for the hand of God has struck me!" 
Job19:22 Why do you hound me as though you were divine, and insatiably prey upon me?" 
Job19:23 Oh, would that my words were written down! Would that they were inscribed in a record:" 
Job19:24 That with an iron chisel and with lead they were cut in the rock forever! 
Job19:25 But as for me, I know that my Vindicator lives, and that he will at last stand forth upon the dust;" 
Job19:26 And from my flesh I shall see God; my inmost being is consumed with longing. 
Job19:27 Whom I myself shall see: my own eyes, not another's, shall behold him," 
Job19:28 But you who say, ""How shall we persecute him, seeing that the root of the matter is found in him?""" 
Job19:29 Be afraid of the sword for yourselves, for these crimes deserve the sword; that you may know that there is a judgment." 
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite spoke and said: 
Job20:2 and because of this I am disturbed. So now my thoughts provide me with an answer, and from my understanding a spirit gives me a reply." 
Job20:3 A rebuke which puts me to shame I hear," 
Job20:4 Do you not know this from olden time, since man was placed upon the earth," 
Job20:5 That the triumph of the wicked is short and the joy of the impious but for a moment? 
Job20:6 Though his pride mount up to the heavens and his head reach to the clouds," 
Job20:7 Yet he perishes forever like the fuel of his fire, and the onlookers say, ""Where is he?""" 
Job20:8 Like a dream he takes flight and is not found again; he fades away like a vision of the night. 
Job20:9 The eye which saw him does so no more; nor shall his dwelling again behold him. 
Job20:10 and his hands shall yield up his riches. 
Job20:11 Though his frame is full of youthful vigor, this shall lie with him in the dust." 
Job20:12 Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth, and he hides it under his tongue," 
Job20:13 Though he retains it and will not let it go but keeps it still within his mouth," 
Job20:14 Yet in his stomach the food shall turn; it shall be venom of asps inside him. 
Job20:15 The riches he swallowed he shall disgorge; God shall compel his belly to disown them. 
Job20:16 The poison of asps he shall drink in; the viper's fangs shall slay him. 
Job20:17 He shall see no streams of oil, no torrents of honey or milk." 
Job20:18 Restoring his gains, he shall not enjoy them; though his wealth increases, he shall not rejoice." 
Job20:19 Because he has oppressed the poor, and stolen a patrimony he had not built up," 
Job20:20 Though he has known no quiet in his greed, his treasures shall not save him." 
Job20:21 Therefore his prosperity shall not endure," 
Job20:22 When he abounds to overflowing, he shall be brought into straits, and nought shall be left of his goods." 
Job20:23 God shall send against him the fury of his wrath and rain down his missiles of war upon him. 
Job20:24 Should he escape the iron weapon, the bow of bronze shall pierce him through;" 
Job20:25 The dart shall come out of his back; terrors shall fall upon him. 
Job20:26 Complete darkness is in store for him; the fire which shall consume him needs not to be fanned. 
Job20:27 The heavens shall reveal his guilt, and the earth shall rise up against him." 
Job20:28 The flood shall sweep away his house with the waters that run off in the day of God's anger. 
Job20:29 This is the portion of a wicked man, and the heritage appointed him by God." 
Job21:1 Then Job said in reply: 
Job21:2 At least listen to my words, and let that be the consolation you offer." 
Job21:3 Bear with me while I speak; and after I have spoken, you can mock!" 
Job21:4 Is my complaint toward man? And why should I not be impatient? 
Job21:5 Look at me and be astonished, put your hands over your mouths." 
Job21:6 When I think of it, I am dismayed, and horror takes hold on my flesh." 
Job21:7 Why do the wicked survive, grow old, become mighty in power?" 
Job21:8 Their progeny is secure in their sight; they see before them their kinsfolk and their offspring. 
Job21:9 Their homes are safe and without fear, nor is the scourge of God upon them." 
Job21:10 Their bulls gender without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry. 
Job21:11 These folk have infants numerous as lambs, and their children dance." 
Job21:12 They sing to the timbrel and harp, and make merry to the sound of the flute." 
Job21:13 They live out their days in prosperity, and tranquilly go down to the nether world." 
Job21:14 Yet they say to God, ""Depart from us, for we have no wish to learn your ways!" 
Job21:15 What is the Almighty that we should serve him? And what gain shall we have if we pray to him? 
Job21:16 If their happiness is not in their own hands and if the counsel of the wicked is repulsive to God," 
Job21:17 How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does destruction come upon them, the portion he allots in his anger?" 
Job21:18 Let them be like straw before the wind, and like chaff which the storm snatches away!" 
Job21:19 May God not store up the man's misery for his children; let him requite the man himself so that he feels it," 
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see the calamity, and the wrath of the Almighty let him drink!" 
Job21:21 For what interest has he in his family after him, when the number of his months is finished?" 
Job21:22 Can anyone teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those on high?" 
Job21:23 One dies in his full vigor, wholly at ease and content;" 
Job21:24 His figure is full and nourished, and his bones are rich in marrow." 
Job21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul, having never tasted happiness." 
Job21:26 Alike they lie down in the dust, and worms cover them both." 
Job21:27 Behold, I know your thoughts, and the arguments you rehearse against me." 
Job21:28 For you say, ""Where is the house of the magnate, and where the dwelling place of the wicked?""" 
Job21:29 Have you not asked the wayfarers and do you not recognize their monuments? 
Job21:30 Nay, the evil man is spared calamity when it comes;" 
Job21:31 Who will charge him with his conduct to his face, and for what he has done who will repay him?" 
Job21:32 and on the day he is carried to the grave 
Job21:33 Sweet to him are the clods of the valley, and over him the funeral mound keeps watch, While all the line of mankind follows him, and the countless others who have gone before." 
Job21:34 How then can you offer me vain comfort, while in your answers perfidy remains?" 
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said: 
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable to God? Though to himself a wise man be profitable! 
Job22:3 Is it of advantage to the Almighty if you are just? Or is it a gain to him if you make your ways perfect? 
Job22:4 Is it because of your piety that he reproves you, that he enters with you into judgment? 
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness manifold? Are not your iniquities endless? 
Job22:6 You have unjustly kept your kinsmen's goods in pawn, left them stripped naked of their clothing." 
Job22:7 To the thirsty you have given no water to drink, and from the hungry you have withheld bread;" 
Job22:8 As if the land belonged to the man of might, and only the privileged were to dwell in it." 
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty-handed, and the resources of orphans you have destroyed." 
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about you, and a sudden terror causes you dismay," 
Job22:11 Or darkness, in which you cannot see; a deluge of waters covers you." 
Job22:12 Does not God, in the heights of the heavens, behold the stars, high though they are?" 
Job22:13 Yet you say, ""What does God know? Can he judge through the thick darkness?" 
Job22:14 Clouds hide him so that he cannot see; he walks upon the vault of the heavens! 
Job22:15 Do you indeed keep to the ancient way trodden by worthless men," 
Job22:16 Who were snatched away before their time; whose foundations a flood swept away? 
Job22:17 These men said to God, ""Depart from us!"" and, ""What can the Almighty do to us?""" 
Job22:18 (Yet he had filled their houses with good things! But far be from me the mind of the impious!) 
Job22:19 The just look on and are gladdened, and the innocent deride them:" 
Job22:20 Truly these have been destroyed where they stood, and such as were left, fire has consumed!""" 
Job22:21 Come to terms with him to be at peace. In this shall good come to you: 
Job22:22 Receive instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in your heart." 
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored; if you put iniquity far from your tent," 
Job22:24 And treat raw gold like dust, and the fine gold of Ophir as pebbles from the brook," 
Job22:25 Then the Almighty himself shall be your gold and your sparkling silver. 
Job22:26 For then you shall delight in the Almighty and you shall lift up your face toward God. 
Job22:27 You shall entreat him and he will hear you, and your vows you shall fulfill." 
Job22:28 When you make a decision, it shall succeed for you, and upon your ways the light shall shine." 
Job22:29 For he brings down the pride of the haughty, but the man of humble mien he saves." 
Job22:30 God delivers him who is innocent; you shall be delivered through cleanness of hands. 
Job23:1 Again Job answered and said: 
Job23:2 Though I know my complaint is bitter, his hand is heavy upon me in my groanings." 
Job23:3 Oh, that today I might find him, that I might come to his judgment seat!" 
Job23:4 I would set out my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments;" 
Job23:5 I would learn the words with which he would answer, and understand what he would reply to me." 
Job23:6 Even should he contend against me with his great power, yet, would that he himself might heed me!" 
Job23:7 There the upright man might reason with him, and I should once and for all preserve my rights." 
Job23:8 But if I go to the east, he is not there; or to the west, I cannot perceive him;" 
Job23:9 Where the north enfolds him, I behold him not; by the south he is veiled, and I see him not." 
Job23:10 Yet he knows my way; if he proved me, I should come forth as gold." 
Job23:11 My foot has always walked in his steps; his way I have kept and have not turned aside. 
Job23:12 From the commands of his lips I have not departed; the words of his mouth I have treasured in my heart. 
Job23:13 But he had decided, and who can say him nay? What he desires, that he does." 
Job23:14 For he will carry out what is appointed for me; and many such things may yet be in his mind. 
Job23:15 Therefore am I dismayed before him; when I take thought, I fear him." 
Job23:16 Indeed God has made my courage fail; the Almighty has put me in dismay. 
Job23:17 Yes, would that I had vanished in darkness, and that thick gloom were before me to conceal me." 
Job24:1 Why are not times set by the Almighty, and why do his friends not see his days?" 
Job24:2 The wicked remove landmarks; they steal away herds and pasture them. 
Job24:3 The asses of orphans they drive away; they take the widow's ox for a pledge. 
Job24:4 They force the needy off the road; all the poor of the land are driven into hiding. 
Job24:5 Like wild asses in the desert, these go forth to their task of seeking food; The steppe provides food for the young among them;" 
Job24:6 they harvest at night in the untilled land. 
Job24:7 They pass the night naked, without clothing, for they have no covering against the cold;" 
Job24:8 They are drenched with the rain of the mountains, and for want of shelter they cling to the rock." 
Job24:9 *** 
Job24:10 and famished are those who carry the sheaves. 
Job24:11 Between the rows they press out the oil; they glean in the the vineyard of the wicked. They tread the wine presses, yet suffer thirst," 
Job24:12 From the dust the dying groan, and the souls of the wounded cry out (yet God does not treat it as unseemly)." 
Job24:13 There are those who are rebels against the light; they know not its ways; they abide not in its paths. 
Job24:14 When there is no light the murderer rises, to kill the poor and needy." 
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer watches for the twilight; he says, ""No eye will see me."" In the night the thief roams about, and he puts a mask over his face;" 
Job24:16 in the dark he breaks into houses. By day they shut themselves in; none of them know the light," 
Job24:17 for daylight they regard as darkness. 
Job24:18 Their portion in the land is accursed," 
Job24:19 *** 
Job24:20 and wickedness is splintered like wood. 
Job24:21 *** 
Job24:22 To him who rises without assurance of his life 
Job24:23 he gives safety and support. He sustains the mighty by his strength, and his eyes are on their ways." 
Job24:24 They are exalted for a while, and then they are gone; they are laid low and, like all others, are gathered up; like ears of grain they shrivel." 
Job24:25 If this be not so, who will confute me, and reduce my argument to nought?" 
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said: 
Job25:2 Dominion and awesomeness are his who brings about harmony in his heavens. 
Job25:3 Is there any numbering of his troops? Yet to which of them does not his light extend? 
Job25:4 How can a man be just in God's sight, or how can any woman's child be innocent?" 
Job25:5 Behold, even the moon is not bright and the stars are not clear in his sight." 
Job25:6 How much less man, who is but a maggot, the son of man, who is only a worm?" 
Job26:1 Then Job spoke again and said: 
Job26:2 What help you give to the powerless, what strength to the feeble arm!" 
Job26:3 How you counsel, as though he had no wisdom; how profuse is the advice you offer!" 
Job26:4 With whose help have you uttered those words, and whose is the breath that comes forth from you?" 
Job26:5 The shades beneath writhe in terror, the waters, and their inhabitants." 
Job26:6 Naked before him is the nether world, and Abaddon has no covering." 
Job26:7 He stretches out the North over empty space, and suspends the earth over nothing at all;" 
Job26:8 He binds up the waters in his clouds, yet the cloud is not rent by their weight;" 
Job26:9 He holds back the appearance of the full moon by spreading his clouds before it. 
Job26:10 He has marked out a circle on the surface of the deep as the boundary of light and darkness. 
Job26:11 The pillars of the heavens tremble and are stunned at his thunderous rebuke; 
Job26:12 By his power he stirs up the sea, and by his might he crushes Rahab;" 
Job26:13 With his angry breath he scatters the waters, and he hurls the lightning against them relentlessly; His hand pierces the fugitive dragon as from his hand it strives to flee." 
Job26:14 Lo, these are but the outlines of his ways, and how faint is the word we hear!" 
Job27:1 *** 
Job27:2 As God lives, who withholds my deserts, the Almighty, who has made bitter my soul," 
Job27:3 So long as I still have life in me and the breath of God is in my nostrils," 
Job27:4 My lips shall not speak falsehood, nor my tongue utter deceit!" 
Job27:5 Far be it from me to account you right; till I die I will not renounce my innocence. 
Job27:6 My justice I maintain and I will not relinquish it; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days. 
Job27:7 Let my enemy be as the wicked and my adversary as the unjust! 
Job27:8 For what can the impious man expect when he is cut off, when God requires his life?" 
Job27:9 Will God then attend to his cry when calamity comes upon him? 
Job27:10 Will he then delight in the Almighty and call upon him constantly? 
Job27:11 I will teach you the manner of God's dealings, and the way of the Almighty I will not conceal." 
Job27:12 Behold, you yourselves have all seen it; why then do you spend yourselves in idle words!" 
Job27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man from God, the inheritance an oppressor receives from the Almighty:" 
Job27:14 Though his children be many, the sword is their destiny. His offspring shall not be filled with bread." 
Job27:15 His survivors, when they die, shall have no burial, and their widows shall not be mourned." 
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver like dust and store away mounds of clothing," 
Job27:17 What he has stored the just man shall wear, and the innocent shall divide the silver." 
Job27:18 He builds his house as of cobwebs, or like a booth put up by the vine-keeper." 
Job27:19 He lies down a rich man, one last time; he opens his eyes and nothing remains to him." 
Job27:20 Terrors rush upon him by day; at night the tempest carries him off. 
Job27:21 The storm wind seizes him and he disappears; it sweeps him out of his place. 
Job28:1 There is indeed a mine for silver, and a place for gold which men refine." 
Job28:2 Iron is taken from the earth, and copper is melted out of stone." 
Job28:3 He has set a boundary for the darkness; to the farthest confines he penetrates. 
Job28:4 *** 
Job28:5 The earth, though out of it comes forth bread, is in fiery upheaval underneath." 
Job28:6 Its stones are the source of sapphires, and there is gold in its dust." 
Job28:7 The path to it no bird of prey knows, nor has the hawk's eye seen that path." 
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it, nor has the lion gone that way." 
Job28:9 He sets his hand to the flinty rock, and overturns the mountains at their foundations." 
Job28:10 He splits channels in the rocks; his eyes behold all that is precious. 
Job28:11 He probes the wellsprings of the streams, and brings hidden things to light." 
Job28:12 But whence can wisdom be obtained, and where is the place of understanding?" 
Job28:13 Man knows nothing to equal it, nor is it to be had in the land of the living." 
Job28:14 The abyss declares, ""It is not in me""; and the sea says, ""I have it not.""" 
Job28:15 Solid gold cannot purchase it, nor can its price be paid with silver." 
Job28:16 It cannot be bought with gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx or the sapphire." 
Job28:17 Gold or crystal cannot equal it, nor can golden vessels reach its worth." 
Job28:18 Neither coral nor jasper should be thought of; it surpasses pearls and 
Job28:19 Arabian topaz. 
Job28:20 Whence, then, comes wisdom, and where is the place of understanding?" 
Job28:21 It is hid from the eyes of any beast; from the birds of the air it is concealed. 
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say, ""Only by rumor have we heard of it.""" 
Job28:23 God knows the way to it; it is he who is familiar with its place. 
Job28:24 For he beholds the ends of the earth and sees all that is under the heavens. 
Job28:25 He has weighed out the wind, and fixed the scope of the waters;" 
Job28:26 When he made rules for the rain and a path for the thunderbolts," 
Job28:27 Then he saw wisdom and appraised it, gave it its setting, knew it through and through." 
Job28:28 And to man he said: Behold, the fear of the LORD is wisdom; and avoiding evil is understanding." 
Job29:1 Job took up his theme anew and said; 
Job29:2 Oh, that I were as in the months past! as in the days when God watched over me," 
Job29:3 While he kept his lamp shining above my head, and by his light I walked through darkness;" 
Job29:4 As I was in my flourishing days, when God sheltered my tent;" 
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me, and my children were round about me;" 
Job29:6 When my footsteps were bathed in milk, and the rock flowed with streams of oil;" 
Job29:7 When I went forth to the gate of the city and set up my seat in the square- 
Job29:8 Then the young men saw me and withdrew, while the elders rose up and stood;" 
Job29:9 The chief men refrained from speaking and covered their mouths with their hands; 
Job29:10 The voice of the princes was silenced, and their tongues stuck to the roofs of their mouths." 
Job29:11 Whoever heard of me blessed me; those who saw me commended me. 
Job29:12 For I rescued the poor who cried out for help, the orphans, and the unassisted;" 
Job29:13 The blessing of those in extremity came upon me, and the heart of the widow I made joyful." 
Job29:14 I wore my honesty like a garment; justice was my robe and my turban. 
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind, and feet to the lame was I;" 
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy; the rights of the stranger I studied," 
Job29:17 And I broke the jaws of the wicked man; from his teeth I forced the prey. 
Job29:18 Then I said: In my own nest I shall grow old; I shall multiply years like the phoenix. 
Job29:19 My root is spread out to the waters; the dew rests by night on my branches. 
Job29:20 My glory is fresh within me, and my bow is renewed in my hand!""" 
Job29:21 For me they listened and waited; they were silent for my counsel. 
Job29:22 Once I spoke, they said no more, but received my pronouncement drop by drop." 
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain; they drank in my words like the spring rains. 
Job29:24 When I smiled on them they were reassured; 
Job29:25 mourners took comfort from my cheerful glance. I chose out their way and presided; I took a king's place in the armed forces. 
Job30:1 But now they hold me in derision who are younger in years than I; Whose fathers I should have disdained to rank with the dogs of my flock. 
Job30:2 Such strength as they had, to me meant nought; they were utterly destitute." 
Job30:3 In want and hunger was their lot, they who fled to the parched wastelands:" 
Job30:4 They plucked saltwort and shrubs; the roots of the broom plant were their food. 
Job30:5 They were banished from among men, with an outcry like that against a thief," 
Job30:6 To dwell on the slopes of the wadies, in caves of sand and stone;" 
Job30:7 Among the bushes they raised their raucous cry; under the nettles they huddled together. 
Job30:8 Irresponsible, nameless men, they were driven out of the land." 
Job30:9 Yet now they sing of me in mockery; I am become a byword among them. 
Job30:10 They abhor me, they stand aloof from me, they do not hesitate to spit in my face!" 
Job30:11 Indeed, they have loosed their bonds; they lord it over me, and have thrown off restraint in my presence." 
Job30:12 To subvert my paths they rise up; they build their approaches for my ruin. 
Job30:13 To destroy me, they attack with none to stay them;" 
Job30:14 as through a wide breach they advance. Amid the uproar they come on in waves; 
Job30:15 over me rolls the terror. My dignity is borne off on the wind, and my welfare vanishes like a cloud." 
Job30:16 My soul ebbs away from me; 
Job30:17 My frame takes no rest by night; my inward parts seethe and will not be stilled. 
Job30:18 One with great power lays hold of my clothing; by the collar of my tunic he seizes me: 
Job30:19 He has cast me into the mire; I am leveled with the dust and ashes. 
Job30:20 I cry to you, but you do not answer me; you stand off and look at me," 
Job30:21 Then you turn upon me without mercy and with your strong hand you buffet me. 
Job30:22 You raise me up and drive me before the wind; I am tossed about by the tempest. 
Job30:23 Indeed I know you will turn me back in death to the destined place of everyone alive. 
Job30:24 Yet should not a hand be held out to help a wretched man in his calamity? 
Job30:25 Or have I not wept for the hardships of others; was not my soul grieved for the destitute? 
Job30:26 Yet when I looked for good, then evil came; when I expected light, then came darkness." 
Job30:27 days of affliction have overtaken me. 
Job30:28 I go about in gloom, without the sun; I rise up in public to voice my grief." 
Job30:29 I have become the brother of jackals, companion to the ostrich." 
Job30:30 My blackened skin falls away from me; the heat scorches my very frame. 
Job30:31 My harp is turned to mourning, and my reed pipe to sounds of weeping." 
Job31:1 If I have made an agreement with my eyes and entertained any thoughts against a maiden; 
Job31:2 But what is man's lot from God above, his inheritance from the Almighty on high?" 
Job31:3 Is it not calamity for the unrighteous, and woe for evildoers?" 
Job31:4 Does he not see my ways, and number all my steps?" 
Job31:5 If I have walked in falsehood and my foot has hastened to deceit; 
Job31:6 Let God weigh me in the scales of justice; thus will he know my innocence! 
Job31:7 If my steps have turned out of the way, and my heart has followed my eyes, or any stain clings to my hands," 
Job31:8 Then may I sow, but another eat of it, or may my planting be rooted up!" 
Job31:9 If my heart has been enticed toward a woman, and I have lain in wait at my neighbor's door;" 
Job31:10 Then may my wife grind for another, and may others cohabit with her!" 
Job31:11 For that would be heinous, a crime to be condemned;" 
Job31:12 A fire that should burn down to the abyss till it consumed all my possessions to the roots. 
Job31:13 Had I refused justice to my manservant or to my maid, when they had a claim against me," 
Job31:14 What then should I do when God rose up; what could I answer when he demanded an account? 
Job31:15 Did not he who made me in the womb make him? Did not the same One fashion us before our birth? 
Job31:16 If I have denied anything to the poor, or allowed the eyes of the widow to languish" 
Job31:17 While I ate my portion alone, with no share in it for the fatherless," 
Job31:18 Though like a father God has reared me from my youth, guiding me even from my mother's womb," 
Job31:19 If I have seen a wanderer without clothing, or a poor man without covering," 
Job31:20 Whose limbs have not blessed me when warmed with the fleece of my sheep; 
Job31:21 If I have raised my hand against the innocent because I saw that I had supporters at the gate, 
Job31:22 Then may my arm fall from the shoulder, my forearm be broken at the elbow!" 
Job31:23 For the dread of God will be upon me, and his majesty will overpower me." 
Job31:24 Had I put my trust in gold or called fine gold my security; 
Job31:25 Or had I rejoiced that my wealth was great, or that my hand had acquired abundance," 
Job31:26 Had I looked upon the sun as it shone, or the moon in the splendor of its progress," 
Job31:27 And had my heart been secretly enticed to waft them a kiss with my hand; 
Job31:28 This too would be a crime for condemnation, for I should have denied God above." 
Job31:29 Had I rejoiced at the destruction of my enemy or exulted when evil fell upon him," 
Job31:30 Even though I had not suffered my mouth to sin by uttering a curse against his life, 
Job31:31 Had not the men of my tent exclaimed, ""Who has not been fed with his meat!""" 
Job31:32 Because no stranger lodged in the street, but I opened my door to wayfarers," 
Job31:33 Had I, out of human weakness, hidden my sins and buried my guilt in my bosom" 
Job31:34 Because I feared the noisy multitude and the scorn of the tribes terrified me- then I should have remained silent, and not come out of doors!" 
Job31:35 Oh, that I had one to hear my case, and that my accuser would write out his indictment!" 
Job31:36 Surely, I should wear it on my shoulder or put it on me like a diadem;" 
Job31:37 Of all my steps I should give him an account; like a prince I should present myself before him. This is my final plea; let the Almighty answer me! The words of Job are ended. 
Job31:38 If my land has cried out against me till its very furrows complained; 
Job31:39 If I have eaten its produce without payment and grieved the hearts of its tenants; 
Job31:40 Then let the thistles grow instead of wheat and noxious weeds instead of barley! 
Job32:1 Then the three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes." 
Job32:2 But the anger of Elihu, son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, was kindled. He was angry with Job for considering himself rather than God to be in the right." 
Job32:3 He was angry also with the three friends because they had not found a good answer and had not condemned Job. 
Job32:4 But since these men were older than he, Elihu bided his time before addressing Job." 
Job32:5 When, however, Elihu saw that there was no reply in the mouths of the three men, his wrath was inflamed." 
Job32:6 So Elihu, son of Barachel the Buzite, spoke out and said: I am young and you are very old; therefore I held back and was afraid to declare to you my knowledge." 
Job32:7 Days should speak, I thought, and many years teach wisdom!" 
Job32:8 But it is a spirit in man, the breath of the Almighty, that gives him understanding." 
Job32:9 It is not those of many days who are wise, nor the aged who understand the right." 
Job32:10 Therefore I say, hearken to me; let me too set forth my knowledge!" 
Job32:11 Behold, I have waited for your discourses, and have given ear to your arguments." 
Job32:12 Yes, I followed you attentively as you searched out what to say; And behold, there is none who has convicted Job, not one of you who could refute his statements." 
Job32:13 Yet do not say, ""We have met wisdom. God may vanquish him but not man!""" 
Job32:14 For had he addressed his words to me, I should not then have answered him as you have done." 
Job32:15 They are dismayed, they make no more reply; words fail them." 
Job32:16 Must I wait? Now that they speak no more, and have ceased to make reply," 
Job32:17 I too will speak my part; I also will show my knowledge! 
Job32:18 For I am full of matters to utter; the spirit within me compels me. 
Job32:19 Like a new wineskin with wine under pressure, my bosom is ready to burst." 
Job32:20 Let me speak and obtain relief; let me open my lips, and make reply." 
Job32:21 I would not be partial to anyone, nor give flattering titles to any." 
Job32:22 For I know nought of flattery; if I did, my Maker would soon take me away." 
Job33:1 Therefore, O Job, hear my discourse, and hearken to all my words." 
Job33:2 Behold, now I open my mouth; my tongue and my voice form words." 
Job33:3 I will state directly what is in my mind, my lips shall utter knowledge sincerely;" 
Job33:4 For the spirit of God has made me, the breath of the Almighty keeps me alive." 
Job33:5 If you are able, refute me; draw up your arguments and stand forth." 
Job33:6 Behold I, like yourself, have been taken from the same clay by God." 
Job33:7 Therefore no fear of me should dismay you, nor should my presence weigh heavily upon you." 
Job33:8 But you have said in my hearing, as I listened to the sound of your words:" 
Job33:9 I am clean and without transgression; I am innocent; there is no guilt in me. 
Job33:10 Yet he invents pretexts against me and reckons me as his enemy. 
Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks; he watches all my ways! 
Job33:12 In this you are not just, let me tell you; for God is greater than man." 
Job33:13 Why, then, do you make complaint against him that he gives no account of his doings?" 
Job33:14 For God does speak, perhaps once, or even twice, though one perceive it not." 
Job33:15 In a dream, in a vision of the night, (when deep sleep falls upon men) as they slumber in their beds," 
Job33:16 It is then he opens the ears of men and as a warning to them, terrifies them;" 
Job33:17 By turning man from evil and keeping pride away from him," 
Job33:18 He withholds his soul from the pit and his life from passing to the grave. 
Job33:19 Or a man is chastened on his bed by pain and unceasing suffering within his frame," 
Job33:20 So that to his appetite food becomes repulsive, and his senses reject the choicest nourishment." 
Job33:21 His flesh is wasted so that it cannot be seen, and his bones, once invisible, appear;" 
Job33:22 His soul draws near to the pit, his life to the place of the dead." 
Job33:23 If then there be for him an angel, one out of a thousand, a mediator, To show him what is right for him and bring the man back to justice," 
Job33:24 He will take pity on him and say, ""Deliver him from going down to the pit; I have found him a ransom.""" 
Job33:25 Then his flesh shall become soft as a boy's; he shall be again as in the days of his youth. 
Job33:26 He shall pray and God will favor him; he shall see God's face with rejoicing. 
Job33:27 He shall sing before men and say, ""I sinned and did wrong, yet he has not punished me accordingly." 
Job33:28 He delivered my soul from passing to the pit, and I behold the light of life.""" 
Job33:29 Lo, all these things God does, twice, or thrice, for a man," 
Job33:30 Bringing back his soul from the pit to the light, in the land of the living." 
Job33:31 Be attentive, O Job; listen to me! Be silent and I will speak." 
Job33:32 If you have aught to say, then answer me. Speak out! I should like to see you justified." 
Job33:33 If not, then do you listen to me; be silent while I teach you wisdom." 
Job34:1 Then Elihu continued and said: 
Job34:2 Hear, O wise men, my discourse, and you that have knowledge, hear me!" 
Job34:3 For the ear tests words, as the taste does food." 
Job34:4 Let us discern for ourselves what is right; let us learn between us what is good. 
Job34:5 For Job has said, ""I am innocent, but God has taken what is my due." 
Job34:6 Notwithstanding my right I am set at nought; in my wound the arrow rankles, sinless though I am.""" 
Job34:7 What man is like Job? He drinks in blasphemies like water," 
Job34:8 Keeps company with evildoers and goes along with wicked men," 
Job34:9 When he says, ""It profits a man nought that he is pleasing to God.""" 
Job34:10 Therefore, men of understanding, hearken to me: far be it from God to do wickedness; far from the Almighty to do wrong!" 
Job34:11 Rather, he requites men for their conduct, and brings home to a man his way of life." 
Job34:12 Surely, God cannot act wickedly, the Almighty cannot violate justice." 
Job34:13 Who gave him government over the earth, or who else set all the land in its place?" 
Job34:14 If he were to take back his spirit to himself, withdraw to himself his breath," 
Job34:15 All flesh would perish together, and man would return to the dust." 
Job34:16 Now, do you, O Job, hear this! Hearken to the words I speak!" 
Job34:17 Can an enemy of justice indeed be in control, or will you condemn the supreme Just One," 
Job34:18 Who says to a king, ""You are worthless!"" and to nobles, ""You are wicked!""" 
Job34:19 Who neither favors the person of princes, nor respects the rich more than the poor? For they are all the work of his hands;" 
Job34:20 in a moment they die, even at midnight. He brings on nobles, and takes them away, removing the powerful without lifting a hand;" 
Job34:21 For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he beholds all his steps." 
Job34:22 There is no darkness so dense that evildoers can hide in it. 
Job34:23 For he forewarns no man of his time to come before God in judgment. 
Job34:24 Without a trial he breaks the mighty, and sets others in their stead," 
Job34:25 Therefore he discerns their works; he turns at night and crushes them. 
Job34:26 *** 
Job34:27 Because they turned away from him and heeded none of his ways," 
Job34:28 But caused the cries of the poor to reach him, so that he heard the plea of the afflicted." 
Job34:29 If he remains tranquil, who then can condemn? If he hides his face, who then can behold him?" 
Job34:30 *** 
Job34:31 When anyone says to God, ""I was misguided; I will offend no more." 
Job34:32 Teach me wherein I have sinned; if I have done wrong, I will do so no more,""" 
Job34:33 Would you then say that God must punish, since you reject what he is doing? It is you who must choose, not I; speak, therefore, what you know." 
Job34:34 Men of understanding will say to me, every wise man who hears my views:" 
Job34:35 Job speaks without intelligence, and his words are without sense.""" 
Job34:36 Let Job be tried to the limit, since his answers are those of the impious;" 
Job34:37 For he is adding rebellion to his sin by brushing off our arguments and addressing many words to God. 
Job35:1 Then Elihu proceeded and said: 
Job35:2 Do you think it right to say, ""I am just rather than God?""" 
Job35:3 To say, ""What does it profit me; what advantage have I more than if I had sinned?""" 
Job35:4 I have words for a reply to you and your three companions as well. 
Job35:5 Look up to the skies and behold; regard the heavens high above you. 
Job35:6 If you sin, what injury do you do to God? Even if your offenses are many, how do you hurt him?" 
Job35:7 If you are righteous, what do you give him, or what does he receive from your hand?" 
Job35:8 Your wickedness can affect only a man like yourself; and your justice only a fellow human being. 
Job35:9 In great oppression men cry out; they call for help because of the power of the mighty," 
Job35:10 Saying, ""Where is God, my Maker, who has given visions in the night," 
Job35:11 Taught us rather than the beasts of the earth, and made us wise rather than the birds of the heavens?""" 
Job35:12 Though thus they cry out, he answers not against the pride of the wicked." 
Job35:13 But it is idle to say God does not hear or that the Almighty does not take notice. 
Job35:14 Even though you say that you see him not, the case is before him; with trembling should you wait upon him." 
Job35:15 But now that you have done otherwise, God's anger punishes, nor does he show concern that a man will die." 
Job35:16 Yet Job to no purpose opens his mouth, and without knowledge multiplies words." 
Job36:1 Elihu proceeded further and said: 
Job36:2 Wait yet a little and I will instruct you, for there are still words to be said on God's behalf." 
Job36:3 I will bring my knowledge from afar, and to my Maker I will accord the right." 
Job36:4 For indeed, my theme cannot fail me: the one perfect in knowledge I set before you." 
Job36:5 Behold, God rejects the obstinate in heart; he preserves not the life of the wicked." 
Job36:6 He withholds not the just man's rights, but grants vindication to the oppressed," 
Job36:7 And with kings upon thrones he sets them, exalted forever." 
Job36:8 Or if they are bound with fetters and held fast by bonds of affliction," 
Job36:9 Then he makes known to them what they have done and their sins of boastful pride. 
Job36:10 He opens their ears to correction and exhorts them to turn back from evil. 
Job36:11 If they obey and serve him, they spend their days in prosperity, their years in happiness." 
Job36:12 But if they obey not, they perish; they die for lack of knowledge." 
Job36:13 The impious in heart lay up anger for themselves; they cry not for help when he enchains them; 
Job36:14 Therefore they expire in youth, and perish among the reprobate." 
Job36:15 But he saves the unfortunate through their affliction, and instructs them through distress." 
Job36:16 *** 
Job36:17 *** 
Job36:18 *** 
Job36:19 *** 
Job36:20 *** 
Job36:21 Take heed, turn not to evil; for you have preferred carousal to affliction." 
Job36:22 Behold, God is sublime in his power. What teacher is there like him?" 
Job36:23 Who prescribes for him his conduct, or who can say, ""You have done wrong""?" 
Job36:24 Remember, you should extol his work, which men have praised in song." 
Job36:25 All men contemplate it; man beholds it from afar. 
Job36:26 Lo, God is great beyond our knowledge; the number of his years is past searching out." 
Job36:27 He holds in check the waterdrops that filter in rain through his mists," 
Job36:28 Till the skies run with them and the showers rain down on mankind. 
Job36:29 *** 
Job36:30 Lo! he spreads the clouds in layers as the carpeting of his tent. 
Job36:31 For by these he nourishes the nations, and gives them food in abundance." 
Job36:32 In his hands he holds the lightning, and he commands it to strike the mark." 
Job36:33 His thunder speaks for him and incites the fury of the storm. 
Job37:1 At this my heart trembles and leaps out of its place," 
Job37:2 To hear his angry voice as it rumbles forth from his mouth! 
Job37:3 Everywhere under the heavens he sends it, with his lightning, to the ends of the earth." 
Job37:4 Again his voice roars, the majestic sound of his thunder. 
Job37:5 He does great things beyond our knowing; wonders past our searching out. 
Job37:6 For he says to the snow, ""Fall to the earth""; likewise to his heavy, drenching rain." 
Job37:7 He shuts up all mankind indoors; 
Job37:8 the wild beasts take to cover and remain quietly in their dens. 
Job37:9 Out of its chamber comes forth the tempest; from the north winds, the cold." 
Job37:10 With his breath God brings the frost, and the broad waters become congealed." 
Job37:11 With hail, also, the clouds are laden, as they scatter their flashes of light." 
Job37:12 He it is who changes their rounds, according to his plans, in their task upon the surface of the earth," 
Job37:13 whether for punishment or mercy, as he commands." 
Job37:14 Hearken to this, O Job! Stand and consider the wondrous works of God!" 
Job37:15 Do you know how God lays his commands upon them, and makes the light shine forth from his clouds?" 
Job37:16 Do you know how the clouds are banked, the wondrous work of him who is perfect in knowledge?" 
Job37:17 You, whom the streams of water fail when a calm from the south comes over the land," 
Job37:18 Do you spread out with him the firmament of the skies, hard as a brazen mirror?" 
Job37:19 Teach us then what we shall say to him; we cannot, for the darkness, make our plea." 
Job37:20 Will he be told about it when I speak, or when a man says he is being destroyed?" 
Job37:21 Nay, rather, it is as the light which men see not while it is obscured among the clouds, till the wind comes by and sweeps the clouds away." 
Job37:22 From the North the splendor comes, surrounding God's awesome majesty!" 
Job37:23 The Almighty! we cannot discover him, pre-eminent in power and judgment; his great justice owes no one an accounting." 
Job37:24 Therefore men revere him, though none can see him, however wise their hearts." 
Job38:1 Then the LORD addressed Job out of the storm and said: 
Job38:2 Who is this that obscures divine plans with words of ignorance? 
Job38:3 Gird up your loins now, like a man; I will question you, and you tell me the answers!" 
Job38:4 Where were you when I founded the earth? Tell me, if you have understanding." 
Job38:5 Who determined its size; do you know? Who stretched out the measuring line for it? 
Job38:6 Into what were its pedestals sunk, and who laid the cornerstone," 
Job38:7 While the morning stars sang in chorus and all the sons of God shouted for joy? 
Job38:8 And who shut within doors the sea, when it burst forth from the womb;" 
Job38:9 When I made the clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling bands? 
Job38:10 When I set limits for it and fastened the bar of its door," 
Job38:11 And said: Thus far shall you come but no farther, and here shall your proud waves be stilled!" 
Job38:12 Have you ever in your lifetime commanded the morning and shown the dawn its place 
Job38:13 For taking hold of the ends of the earth, till the wicked are shaken from its surface?" 
Job38:14 The earth is changed as is clay by the seal, and dyed as though it were a garment;" 
Job38:15 But from the wicked the light is withheld, and the arm of pride is shattered." 
Job38:16 Have you entered into the sources of the sea, or walked about in the depths of the abyss?" 
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been shown to you, or have you seen the gates of darkness?" 
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the breadth of the earth? Tell me, if you know all:" 
Job38:19 Which is the way to the dwelling place of light, and where is the abode of darkness," 
Job38:20 That you may take them to their boundaries and set them on their homeward paths? 
Job38:21 You know, because you were born before them, and the number of your years is great!" 
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouse of the snow, and seen the treasury of the hail" 
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for times of stress, for the days of war and of battle?" 
Job38:24 Which way to the parting of the winds, whence the east wind spreads over the earth?" 
Job38:25 Who has laid out a channel for the downpour and for the thunderstorm a path 
Job38:26 To bring rain to no man's land, the unpeopled wilderness;" 
Job38:27 To enrich the waste and desolate ground till the desert blooms with verdure? 
Job38:28 Has the rain a father; or who has begotten the drops of dew? 
Job38:29 Out of whose womb comes the ice, and who gives the hoarfrost its birth in the skies," 
Job38:30 When the waters lie covered as though with stone that holds captive the surface of the deep? 
Job38:31 Have you fitted a curb to the Pleiades, or loosened the bonds of Orion?" 
Job38:32 Can you bring forth the Mazzaroth in their season, or guide the Bear with its train?" 
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens; can you put into effect their plan on the earth? 
Job38:34 Can you raise your voice among the clouds, or veil yourself in the waters of the storm?" 
Job38:35 Can you send forth the lightnings on their way, or will they say to you, ""Here we are""?" 
Job38:36 Who puts wisdom in the heart, and gives the cock its understanding?" 
Job38:37 Who counts the clouds in his wisdom? Or who tilts the water jars of heaven 
Job38:38 So that the dust of earth is fused into a mass and its clods made solid? 
Job38:39 Do you hunt the prey for the lioness or appease the hunger of her cubs," 
Job38:40 While they crouch in their dens, or lie in wait in the thicket?" 
Job38:41 Who provides nourishment for the ravens when their young ones cry out to God, and they rove abroad without food?" 
Job39:1 Do you know about the birth of the mountain goats, watch for the birth pangs of the hinds," 
Job39:2 Number the months that they must fulfill, and fix the time of their bringing forth?" 
Job39:3 They crouch down and bear their young; they deliver their progeny in the desert. 
Job39:4 When their offspring thrive and grow, they leave and do not return." 
Job39:5 Who has given the wild ass his freedom, and who has loosed him from bonds?" 
Job39:6 I have made the wilderness his home and the salt flats his dwelling. 
Job39:7 He scoffs at the uproar of the city, and hears no shouts of a driver." 
Job39:8 He ranges the mountains for pasture, and seeks out every patch of green." 
Job39:9 Will the wild ox consent to serve you, and to pass the nights by your manger?" 
Job39:10 Will a rope bind him in the furrow, and will he harrow the valleys after you?" 
Job39:11 Will you trust him for his great strength and leave to him the fruits of your toil? 
Job39:12 Can you rely on him to thresh out your grain and gather in the yield of your threshing floor? 
Job39:13 The wings of the ostrich beat idly; her plumage is lacking in pinions. 
Job39:14 When she leaves her eggs on the ground and deposits them in the sand," 
Job39:15 Unmindful that a foot may crush them, that the wild beasts may trample them," 
Job39:16 She cruelly disowns her young and ruthlessly makes nought of her brood; 
Job39:17 For God has withheld wisdom from her and has given her no share in understanding. 
Job39:18 Yet in her swiftness of foot she makes sport of the horse and his rider. 
Job39:19 Do you give the horse his strength, and endow his neck with splendor?" 
Job39:20 Do you make the steed to quiver while his thunderous snorting spreads terror? 
Job39:21 He jubilantly paws the plain and rushes in his might against the weapons. 
Job39:22 He laughs at fear and cannot be deterred; he turns not back from the sword. 
Job39:23 Around him rattles the quiver, flashes the spear and the javelin." 
Job39:24 Frenzied and trembling he devours the ground; he holds not back at the sound of the trumpet," 
Job39:25 but at each blast he cries, ""Aha!"" Even from afar he scents the battle, the roar of the chiefs and the shouting." 
Job39:26 Is it by your discernment that the hawk soars, that he spreads his wings toward the south?" 
Job39:27 Does the eagle fly up at your command to build his nest aloft? 
Job39:28 On the cliff he dwells and spends the night, on the spur of the cliff or the fortress." 
Job39:29 From thence he watches for his prey; his eyes behold it afar off. 
Job39:30 His young ones greedily drink blood; where the slain are, there is he." 
Job40:1 The LORD then said to Job: 
Job40:2 Will we have arguing with the Almighty by the critic? Let him who would correct God give answer! 
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD and said: 
Job40:4 Behold, I am of little account; what can I answer you? I put my hand over my mouth." 
Job40:5 Though I have spoken once, I will not do so again; though twice, I will do so no more." 
Job40:6 Then the LORD addressed Job out of the storm and said: 
Job40:7 Gird up your loins now, like a man. I will question you, and you tell me the answers!" 
Job40:8 Would you refuse to acknowledge my right? Would you condemn me that you may be justified? 
Job40:9 Have you an arm like that of God, or can you thunder with a voice like his?" 
Job40:10 Adorn yourself with grandeur and majesty, and array yourself with glory and splendor." 
Job40:11 Let loose the fury of your wrath; 
Job40:12 tear down the wicked and shatter them. Bring down the haughty with a glance; 
Job40:13 bury them in the dust together; in the hidden world imprison them. 
Job40:14 Then will I too acknowledge that your own right hand can save you. 
Job40:15 See, besides you I made Behemoth, that feeds on grass like an ox." 
Job40:16 Behold the strength in his loins, and his vigor in the sinews of his belly." 
Job40:17 He carries his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are like cables. 
Job40:18 His bones are like tubes of bronze; his frame is like iron rods. 
Job40:19 He came at the beginning of God's ways, and was made the taskmaster of his fellows;" 
Job40:20 For the produce of the mountains is brought to him, and of all wild animals he makes sport." 
Job40:21 Under the lotus trees he lies, in coverts of the reedy swamp." 
Job40:22 The lotus trees cover him with their shade; all about him are the poplars on the bank. 
Job40:23 If the river grows violent, he is not disturbed; he is tranquil though the torrent surges about his mouth." 
Job40:24 Who can capture him by his eyes, or pierce his nose with a trap?" 
Job40:25 Can you lead about Leviathan with a hook, or curb his tongue with a bit?" 
Job40:26 Can you put a rope into his nose, or pierce through his cheek with a gaff?" 
Job40:27 Will he then plead with you, time after time, or address you with tender words?" 
Job40:28 Will he make an agreement with you that you may have him as a slave forever? 
Job40:29 Can you play with him, as with a bird? Can you put him in leash for your maidens?" 
Job40:30 Will the traders bargain for him? Will the merchants divide him up? 
Job40:31 Can you fill his hide with barbs, or his head with fish spears?" 
Job40:32 Once you but lay a hand upon him, no need to recall any other conflict!" 
Job41:1 Whoever might vainly hope to do so need only see him to be overthrown. 
Job41:2 Is he not relentless when aroused; who then dares stand before him? 
Job41:3 Who has assailed him and come off safe, Who under all the heavens? 
Job41:4 I need hardly mention his limbs, his strength, and the fitness of his armor." 
Job41:5 Who can strip off his outer garment, or penetrate his double corselet?" 
Job41:6 Who can force open the doors of his mouth, close to his terrible teeth?" 
Job41:7 Rows of scales are on his back, tightly sealed together;" 
Job41:8 They are fitted each so close to the next that no space intervenes; 
Job41:9 So joined one to another that they hold fast and cannot be parted. 
Job41:10 When he sneezes, light flashes forth; his eyes are like those of the dawn." 
Job41:11 Out of his mouth go forth firebrands; sparks of fire leap forth. 
Job41:12 From his nostrils issues steam, as from a seething pot or bowl." 
Job41:13 His breath sets coals afire; a flame pours from his mouth. 
Job41:14 Strength abides in his neck, and terror leaps before him." 
Job41:15 *** 
Job41:16 His heart is hard as stone; his flesh, as the lower millstone." 
Job41:17 When he rises up, the mighty are afraid; the waves of the sea fall back." 
Job41:18 Should the sword reach him, it will not avail; nor will the spear, nor the dart, nor the javelin." 
Job41:19 He regards iron as straw, and bronze as rotten wood." 
Job41:20 The arrow will not put him to flight; slingstones used against him are but straws. 
Job41:21 Clubs he esteems as splinters; he laughs at the crash of the spear. 
Job41:22 His belly is sharp as pottery fragments; he spreads like a threshing sledge upon the mire. 
Job41:23 He makes the depths boil like a pot; the sea he churns like perfume in a kettle. 
Job41:24 Behind him he leaves a shining path; you would think the deep had the hoary head of age. 
Job41:25 Upon the earth there is not his like, intrepid he was made." 
Job41:26 All, however lofty, fear him; he is king over all proud beasts." 
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD and said: 
Job42:2 I know that you can do all things, and that no purpose of yours can be hindered." 
Job42:3 I have dealt with great things that I do not understand; things too wonderful for me, which I cannot know." 
Job42:4 *** 
Job42:5 I had heard of you by word of mouth, but now my eye has seen you." 
Job42:6 Therefore I disown what I have said, and repent in dust and ashes." 
Job42:7 And it came to pass after the LORD had spoken these words to Job, that the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, ""I am angry with you and with your two friends; for you have not spoken rightly concerning me, as has my servant Job." 
Job42:8 Now, therefore, take seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up a holocaust for yourselves; and let my servant Job pray for you; for his prayer I will accept, not to punish you severely. For you have not spoken rightly concerning me, as has my servant Job.""" 
Job42:9 Then Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite, went and did as the LORD had commanded them. And the LORD accepted the intercession of Job." 
Job42:10 Also, the LORD restored the prosperity of Job, after he had prayed for his friends; the LORD even gave to Job twice as much as he had before." 
Job42:11 Then all his brethren and his sisters came to him, and all his former acquaintances, and they dined with him in his house. They condoled with him and comforted him for all the evil which the LORD had brought upon him; and each one gave him a piece of money and a gold ring." 
Job42:12 Thus the LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his earlier ones. For he had fourteen thousand sheep, six thousand camels, a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she-asses." 
Job42:13 And he had seven sons and three daughters," 
Job42:14 of whom he called the first Jemimah, the second Keziah, and the third Keren-happuch." 
Job42:15 In all the land no other women were as beautiful as the daughters of Job; and their father gave them an inheritance among their brethren. 
Job42:16 After this, Job lived a hundred and forty years; and he saw his children, his grandchildren, and even his great-grandchildren." 
Job42:17 Then Job died, old and full of years." 
Psalms1:1 Happy those who do not follow the counsel of the wicked, Nor go the way of sinners, nor sit in company with scoffers." 
Psalms1:2 Rather, the law of the LORD is their joy; God's law they study day and night." 
Psalms1:3 They are like a tree planted near streams of water, that yields its fruit in season; Its leaves never wither; whatever they do prospers." 
Psalms1:4 But not the wicked! They are like chaff driven by the wind. 
Psalms1:5 Therefore the wicked will not survive judgment, nor will sinners in the assembly of the just." 
Psalms1:6 The LORD watches over the way of the just, but the way of the wicked leads to ruin." 
Psalms2:1 Why do the nations protest and the peoples grumble in vain? 
Psalms2:2 Kings on earth rise up and princes plot together against the LORD and his anointed: 
Psalms2:3 Let us break their shackles and cast off their chains! 
Psalms2:4 The one enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord derides them," 
Psalms2:5 Then speaks to them in anger, terrifies them in wrath:" 
Psalms2:6 I myself have installed my king on Zion, my holy mountain.""" 
Psalms2:7 I will proclaim the decree of the LORD, who said to me, ""You are my son; today I am your father." 
Psalms2:8 Only ask it of me, and I will make your inheritance the nations, your possession the ends of the earth." 
Psalms2:9 With an iron rod you shall shepherd them, like a clay pot you will shatter them.""" 
Psalms2:10 And now, kings, give heed; take warning, rulers on earth." 
Psalms2:11 Serve the LORD with fear; with trembling bow down in homage, Lest God be angry and you perish from the way in a sudden blaze of anger. Happy are all who take refuge in God!" 
Psalms3:1 A psalm of David, when he fled from his son Absalom." 
Psalms3:2 How many are my foes, LORD! How many rise against me!" 
Psalms3:3 How many say of me, ""God will not save that one."" Selah" 
Psalms3:4 But you, LORD, are a shield around me; my glory, you keep my head high." 
Psalms3:5 Whenever I cried out to the LORD, I was answered from the holy mountain. Selah" 
Psalms3:6 Whenever I lay down and slept, the LORD preserved me to rise again." 
Psalms3:7 I do not fear, then, thousands of people arrayed against me on every side." 
Psalms3:8 Arise, LORD! Save me, my God! You will shatter the jaws of all my foes; you will break the teeth of the wicked." 
Psalms3:9 Safety comes from the LORD! Your blessing for your people! Selah 
Psalms4:1 For the leader; with stringed instruments. A psalm of David. 
Psalms4:2 Answer when I call, my saving God. In my troubles, you cleared a way; show me favor; hear my prayer." 
Psalms4:3 How long will you people mock my honor, love what is worthless, chase after lies? Selah" 
Psalms4:4 Know that the LORD works wonders for the faithful; the LORD hears when I call out. 
Psalms4:5 Tremble and do not sin; upon your beds ponder in silence. 
Psalms4:6 Offer fitting sacrifice and trust in the LORD. 
Psalms4:7 Many say, ""May we see better times! LORD, show us the light of your face!"" Selah" 
Psalms4:8 But you have given my heart more joy than they have when grain and wine abound. 
Psalms4:9 In peace I shall both lie down and sleep, for you alone, LORD, make me secure." 
Psalms5:1 For the leader; with wind instruments. A psalm of David. 
Psalms5:2 Hear my words, O LORD; listen to my sighing." 
Psalms5:3 Hear my cry for help, my king, my God! To you I pray, O LORD;" 
Psalms5:4 at dawn you will hear my cry; at dawn I will plead before you and wait. 
Psalms5:5 You are not a god who delights in evil; no wicked person finds refuge with you; 
Psalms5:6 the arrogant cannot stand before you. You hate all who do evil; 
Psalms5:7 you destroy all who speak falsely. Murderers and deceivers the LORD abhors. 
Psalms5:8 But I can enter your house because of your great love. I can worship in your holy temple because of my reverence for you, LORD." 
Psalms5:9 Guide me in your justice because of my foes; make straight your way before me. 
Psalms5:10 For there is no sincerity in their mouths; their hearts are corrupt. Their throats are open graves; on their tongues are subtle lies. 
Psalms5:11 Declare them guilty, God; make them fall by their own devices. Drive them out for their many sins; they have rebelled against you." 
Psalms5:12 Then all who take refuge in you will be glad and forever shout for joy. Protect them that you may be the joy of those who love your name. 
Psalms5:13 For you, LORD, bless the just; you surround them with favor like a shield." 
Psalms6:1 For the leader; with stringed instruments, ""upon the eighth."" A psalm of David." 
Psalms6:2 Do not reprove me in your anger, LORD, nor punish me in your wrath." 
Psalms6:3 Have pity on me, LORD, for I am weak; heal me, LORD, for my bones are trembling." 
Psalms6:4 In utter terror is my soul, and you, LORD, how long...?" 
Psalms6:5 Turn, LORD, save my life; in your mercy rescue me." 
Psalms6:6 For who among the dead remembers you? Who praises you in Sheol? 
Psalms6:7 I am wearied with sighing; all night long tears drench my bed; my couch is soaked with weeping. 
Psalms6:8 My eyes are dimmed with sorrow, worn out because of all my foes." 
Psalms6:9 Away from me, all who do evil! The LORD has heard my weeping." 
Psalms6:10 The LORD has heard my prayer; the LORD takes up my plea. 
Psalms6:11 My foes will be terrified and disgraced; all will fall back in sudden shame. 
Psalms7:1 A plaintive song of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning Cush, the Benjaminite." 
Psalms7:2 LORD my God, in you I take refuge; rescue me; save me from all who pursue me," 
Psalms7:3 Lest they maul me like lions, tear me to pieces with none to save." 
Psalms7:4 LORD my God, if I am at fault in this, if there is guilt on my hands," 
Psalms7:5 If I have repaid my friend with evil, I spared even those who hated me without cause, 
Psalms7:6 Then let my enemy pursue and overtake me, trample my life to the ground, and leave me dishonored in the dust.Selah" 
Psalms7:7 Rise up, LORD, in your anger; rise against the fury of my foes. Wake to judge as you have decreed." 
Psalms7:8 Have the assembly of the peoples gather about you; sit on your throne high above them," 
Psalms7:9 O LORD, judge of the nations. Grant me justice, LORD, for I am blameless, free of any guilt." 
Psalms7:10 Bring the malice of the wicked to an end; uphold the innocent, O God of justice, who tries hearts and minds." 
Psalms7:11 A shield before me is God who saves the honest heart. 
Psalms7:12 God is a just judge, who rebukes in anger every day." 
Psalms7:13 If sinners do not repent, God sharpens his sword, strings and readies the bow," 
Psalms7:14 Prepares his deadly shafts, makes arrows blazing thunderbolts." 
Psalms7:15 Sinners conceive iniquity; pregnant with mischief, they give birth to failure."
