Psalms7:16 They open a hole and dig it deep, but fall into the pit they have dug." 
Psalms7:17 Their mischief comes back upon themselves; their violence falls on their own heads. VI 
Psalms7:18 I praise the justice of the LORD; I celebrate the name of the LORD Most High. 
Psalms8:1 For the leader; upon the gittith. A psalm of David. 
Psalms8:2 O LORD, our Lord, how awesome is your name through all the earth! You have set your majesty above the heavens!" 
Psalms8:3 Out of the mouths of babes and infants you have drawn a defense against your foes, to silence enemy and avenger." 
Psalms8:4 When I see your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and stars that you set in place," 
Psalms8:5 What are humans that you are mindful of them, mere mortals that you care for them?" 
Psalms8:6 Yet you have made them little less than a god, crowned them with glory and honor." 
Psalms8:7 You have given them rule over the works of your hands, put all things at their feet:" 
Psalms8:8 All sheep and oxen, even the beasts of the field," 
Psalms8:9 The birds of the air, the fish of the sea, and whatever swims the paths of the seas." 
Psalms8:10 O LORD, our Lord, how awesome is your name through all the earth!" 
Psalms9:1 For the leader; according to Muth Labben. A psalm of David. 
Psalms9:2 I will praise you, LORD, with all my heart; I will declare all your wondrous deeds." 
Psalms9:3 I will delight and rejoice in you; I will sing hymns to your name, Most High." 
Psalms9:4 For my enemies turn back; they stumble and perish before you. 
Psalms9:5 You upheld my right and my cause, seated on your throne, judging justly." 
Psalms9:6 You rebuked the nations, you destroyed the wicked; their name you blotted out for all time" 
Psalms9:7 The enemies have been ruined forever; you destroyed their cities; their memory has perished. 
Psalms9:8 The LORD rules forever, has set up a throne for judgment." 
Psalms9:9 It is God who governs the world with justice, who judges the peoples with fairness." 
Psalms9:10 The LORD is a stronghold for the oppressed, stronghold in times of trouble." 
Psalms9:11 Those who honor your name trust in you; you never forsake those who seek you, LORD." 
Psalms9:12 Sing hymns to the LORD enthroned on Zion; proclaim God's deeds among the nations! 
Psalms9:13 For the avenger of bloodshed remembers, does not forget the cry of the afflicted." 
Psalms9:14 Have mercy on me, LORD; see how my foes afflict me! You alone can raise me from the gates of death." 
Psalms9:15 Then I will declare all your praises, sing joyously of your salvation in the gates of daughter Zion." 
Psalms9:16 The nations fall into the pit they dig; in the snare they hide, their own foot is caught." 
Psalms9:17 The LORD is revealed in this divine rule: by the deeds they do the wicked are trapped. Higgaion. Selah 
Psalms9:18 To Sheol the wicked will depart, all the nations that forget God." 
Psalms9:19 The needy will never be forgotten, nor will the hope of the afflicted ever fade." 
Psalms9:20 Arise, LORD, let no mortal prevail; let the nations be judged in your presence." 
Psalms9:21 Strike them with terror, LORD; show the nations they are mere mortals. Selah" 
Psalms10:1 Why, LORD, do you stand at a distance and pay no heed to these troubled times?" 
Psalms10:2 Arrogant scoundrels pursue the poor; they trap them by their cunning schemes. 
Psalms10:3 The wicked even boast of their greed; these robbers curse and scorn the LORD. 
Psalms10:4 In their insolence the wicked boast: God doesn't care, doesn't even exist.""" 
Psalms10:5 Yet their affairs always succeed; they ignore your judgment on high; they sneer at all who oppose them. 
Psalms10:6 They say in their hearts, ""We will never fall; never will we see misfortune.""" 
Psalms10:7 Their mouths are full of oaths, violence, and lies; discord and evil are under their tongues." 
Psalms10:8 They wait in ambush near towns; their eyes watch for the helpless. to murder the innocent in secret. 
Psalms10:9 They lurk in ambush like lions in a thicket, hide there to trap the poor, snare them and close the net." 
Psalms10:10 The helpless are crushed, laid low; they fall into the power of the wicked," 
Psalms10:11 Who say in their hearts, ""God pays no attention, shows no concern, never bothers to look.""" 
Psalms10:12 Rise up, LORD God! Raise your arm! Do not forget the poor!" 
Psalms10:13 Why should the wicked scorn God, say in their hearts, ""God doesn't care""?" 
Psalms10:14 But you do see; you do observe this misery and sorrow; you take the matter in hand. To you the helpless can entrust their cause; you are the defender of orphans. 
Psalms10:15 Break the arms of the wicked and depraved; make them account for their crimes; let none of them survive. 
Psalms10:16 The LORD is king forever; the nations have vanished from God's land. 
Psalms10:17 You listen, LORD, to the needs of the poor; you encourage them and hear their prayers." 
Psalms10:18 You win justice for the orphaned and oppressed; no one on earth will cause terror again. 
Psalms11:1 For the leader. Of David. I In the LORD I take refuge; how can you say to me, ""Flee like a bird to the mountains!" 
Psalms11:2 See how the wicked string their bows, fit their arrows to the string to shoot from the shadows at the upright." 
Psalms11:3 When foundations are being destroyed, what can the upright do?""" 
Psalms11:4 The LORD is in his holy temple; the LORD'S throne is in heaven. God's eyes keep careful watch; they test all peoples. 
Psalms11:5 The LORD tests the good and the bad, hates those who love violence," 
Psalms11:6 And rains upon the wicked fiery coals and brimstone, a scorching wind their allotted cup." 
Psalms11:7 The LORD is just and loves just deeds; the upright shall see his face. 
Psalms12:1 For the leader; upon the eighth. A psalm of David. 
Psalms12:2 Help, LORD, for no one loyal remains; the faithful have vanished from the human race" 
Psalms12:3 Those who tell lies to one another speak with deceiving lips and a double heart. 
Psalms12:4 May the LORD cut off all deceiving lips, and every boastful tongue," 
Psalms12:5 Those who say, ""By our tongues we prevail; when our lips speak, who can lord it over us?""" 
Psalms12:6 Because they rob the weak, and the needy groan, I will now arise,"" says the LORD; ""I will grant safety to whoever longs for it.""" 
Psalms12:7 The promises of the LORD are sure, silver refined in a crucible, silver purified seven times." 
Psalms12:8 LORD, protect us always; preserve us from this generation." 
Psalms12:9 On every side the wicked strut; the shameless are extolled by all. 
Psalms13:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms13:2 How long, LORD? Will you utterly forget me? How long will you hide your face from me?" 
Psalms13:3 How long must I carry sorrow in my soul, grief in my heart day after day? How long will my enemy triumph over me?" 
Psalms13:4 Look upon me, answer me, LORD, my God! Give light to my eyes lest I sleep in death," 
Psalms13:5 Lest my enemy say, ""I have prevailed,"" lest my foes rejoice at my downfall." 
Psalms13:6 I trust in your faithfulness. Grant my heart joy in your help, That I may sing of the LORD, ""How good our God has been to me!""" 
Psalms14:1 For the leader. Of David. I Fools say in their hearts, ""There is no God."" Their deeds are loathsome and corrupt; not one does what is right." 
Psalms14:2 The LORD looks down from heaven upon the human race, To see if even one is wise, if even one seeks God." 
Psalms14:3 All have gone astray; all alike are perverse. Not one does what is right, not even one." 
Psalms14:4 Will these evildoers never learn? They devour my people as they devour bread; they do not call upon the LORD. 
Psalms14:5 They have good reason, then, to fear; God is with the company of the just." 
Psalms14:6 They would crush the hopes of the poor, but the poor have the LORD as their refuge." 
Psalms14:7 Oh, that from Zion might come the deliverance of Israel, That Jacob may rejoice, and Israel be glad when the LORD restores his people!" 
Psalms15:1 A psalm of David. I LORD, who may abide in your tent? Who may dwell on your holy mountain?" 
Psalms15:2 Whoever walks without blame, doing what is right, speaking truth from the heart;" 
Psalms15:3 Who does not slander a neighbor, does no harm to another, never defames a friend;" 
Psalms15:4 Who disdains the wicked, but honors those who fear the LORD; Who keeps an oath despite the cost," 
Psalms15:5 lends no money at interest, accepts no bribe against the innocent. III Whoever acts like this shall never be shaken." 
Psalms16:1 A miktam of David. I Keep me safe, O God; in you I take refuge" 
Psalms16:2 I say to the Lord, you are my Lord, you are my only good." 
Psalms16:3 Worthless are all the false gods of the land. Accursed are all who delight in them. 
Psalms16:4 They multiply their sorrows who court other gods. Blood libations to them I will not pour out, nor will I take their names upon my lips." 
Psalms16:5 LORD, my allotted portion and my cup, you have made my destiny secure." 
Psalms16:6 Pleasant places were measured out for me; fair to me indeed is my inheritance. 
Psalms16:7 I bless the LORD who counsels me; even at night my heart exhorts me. 
Psalms16:8 I keep the LORD always before me; with the Lord at my right, I shall never be shaken." 
Psalms16:9 Therefore my heart is glad, my soul rejoices; my body also dwells secure," 
Psalms16:10 For you will not abandon me to Sheol, nor let your faithful servant see the pit." 
Psalms16:11 You will show me the path to life, abounding joy in your presence, the delights at your right hand forever." 
Psalms17:1 A prayer of David. I Hear, LORD, my plea for justice; pay heed to my cry; Listen to my prayer spoken without guile." 
Psalms17:2 From you let my vindication come; your eyes see what is right. 
Psalms17:3 You have tested my heart, searched it in the night. You have tried me by fire, but find no malice in me. My mouth has not transgressed" 
Psalms17:4 as humans often do. As your lips have instructed me, I have kept the way of the law." 
Psalms17:5 My steps have kept to your paths; my feet have not faltered. 
Psalms17:6 I call upon you; answer me, O God. Turn your ear to me; hear my prayer." 
Psalms17:7 Show your wonderful love, you who deliver with your right arm those who seek refuge from their foes." 
Psalms17:8 Keep me as the apple of your eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings 
Psalms17:9 from the violence of the wicked. 
Psalms17:10 My ravenous enemies press upon me; they close their hearts, they fill their mouths with proud roaring." 
Psalms17:11 Their steps even now encircle me; they watch closely, keeping low to the ground," 
Psalms17:12 Like lions eager for prey, like young lions lurking in ambush." 
Psalms17:13 Rise, O LORD, confront and cast them down; rescue me so from the wicked." 
Psalms17:14 Slay them with your sword; with your hand, LORD, slay them; snatch them from the world in their prime. Their bellies are being filled with your friends; their children are satisfied too, for they share what is left with their young." 
Psalms17:15 I am just, let me see your face; when I awake, let me be filled with your presence." 
Psalms18:1 For the leader. Of David, the servant of the LORD, who sang to the LORD the words of this song after the LORD had rescued him from the clutches of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul." 
Psalms18:2 He said: I love you, LORD, my strength," 
Psalms18:3 LORD, my rock, my fortress, my deliverer, My God, my rock of refuge, my shield, my saving horn, my stronghold!" 
Psalms18:4 Praised be the LORD, I exclaim! I have been delivered from my enemies." 
Psalms18:5 The breakers of death surged round about me; the menacing floods terrified me. 
Psalms18:6 The cords of Sheol tightened; the snares of death lay in wait for me. 
Psalms18:7 In my distress I called out: LORD! I cried out to my God. From his temple he heard my voice; my cry to him reached his ears. 
Psalms18:8 The earth rocked and shook; the foundations of the mountains trembled; they shook as his wrath flared up. 
Psalms18:9 Smoke rose in his nostrils, a devouring fire poured from his mouth; it kindled coals into flame." 
Psalms18:10 He parted the heavens and came down, a dark cloud under his feet." 
Psalms18:11 Mounted on a cherub he flew, borne along on the wings of the wind." 
Psalms18:12 He made darkness the cover about him; his canopy, heavy thunderheads." 
Psalms18:13 Before him scudded his clouds, hail and lightning too." 
Psalms18:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High made his voice resound. 
Psalms18:15 He let fly his arrows and scattered them; shot his lightning bolts and dispersed them. 
Psalms18:16 Then the bed of the sea appeared; the world's foundations lay bare, At the roar of the LORD, at the storming breath of his nostrils." 
Psalms18:17 He reached down from on high and seized me; drew me out of the deep waters. 
Psalms18:18 He rescued me from my mighty enemy, from foes too powerful for me." 
Psalms18:19 They attacked me on a day of distress, but the LORD came to my support." 
Psalms18:20 He set me free in the open; he rescued me because he loves me. 
Psalms18:21 The LORD acknowledged my righteousness, rewarded my clean hands." 
Psalms18:22 For I kept the ways of the LORD; I was not disloyal to my God. 
Psalms18:23 His laws were all before me, his decrees I did not cast aside." 
Psalms18:24 I was honest toward him; I was on guard against sin. 
Psalms18:25 So the LORD rewarded my righteousness, the cleanness of my hands in his sight." 
Psalms18:26 Toward the faithful you are faithful; to the honest you are honest; 
Psalms18:27 Toward the sincere, sincere; but to the perverse you are devious." 
Psalms18:28 Humble people you save; haughty eyes you bring low. 
Psalms18:29 You, LORD, give light to my lamp; my God brightens the darkness about me." 
Psalms18:30 With you I can rush an armed band, with my God to help I can leap a wall." 
Psalms18:31 God's way is unerring; the LORD'S promise is tried and true; he is a shield for all who trust in him. 
Psalms18:32 Truly, who is God except the LORD? Who but our God is the rock?" 
Psalms18:33 This God who girded me with might, kept my way unerring," 
Psalms18:34 Who made my feet swift as a deer's, set me safe on the heights," 
Psalms18:35 Who trained my hands for war, my arms to bend even a bow of bronze." 
Psalms18:36 You have given me your protecting shield; your right hand has upheld me; you stooped to make me great. 
Psalms18:37 You gave me room to stride; my feet never stumbled. 
Psalms18:38 I pursued my enemies and overtook them; I did not turn back till I destroyed them. 
Psalms18:39 I struck them down; they could not rise; they fell dead at my feet. 
Psalms18:40 You girded me with strength for war, subdued adversaries at my feet." 
Psalms18:41 My foes you put to flight before me; those who hated me I destroyed. 
Psalms18:42 They cried for help, but no one saved them; cried to the LORD but got no answer." 
Psalms18:43 I ground them fine as dust in the wind; like mud in the streets I trampled them down. 
Psalms18:44 You rescued me from the strife of peoples; you made me head over nations. A people I had not known became my slaves; 
Psalms18:45 as soon as they heard of me they obeyed. Foreigners cringed before me; 
Psalms18:46 their courage failed; they came trembling from their fortresses. 
Psalms18:47 The LORD lives! Blessed be my rock! Exalted be God, my savior!" 
Psalms18:48 O God who granted me vindication, made peoples subject to me," 
Psalms18:49 and preserved me from my enemies, Truly you have exalted me above my adversaries, from the violent you have rescued me." 
Psalms18:50 Thus I will proclaim you, LORD, among the nations; I will sing the praises of your name." 
Psalms18:51 You have given great victories to your king, and shown kindness to your anointed, to David and his posterity forever." 
Psalms19:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms19:2 The heavens declare the glory of God; the sky proclaims its builder's craft. 
Psalms19:3 One day to the next conveys that message; one night to the next imparts that knowledge. 
Psalms19:4 There is no word or sound; no voice is heard; 
Psalms19:5 Yet their report goes forth through all the earth, their message, to the ends of the world. God has pitched there a tent for the sun;" 
Psalms19:6 it comes forth like a bridegroom from his chamber, and like an athlete joyfully runs its course." 
Psalms19:7 From one end of the heavens it comes forth; its course runs through to the other; nothing escapes its heat. 
Psalms19:8 The law of the LORD is perfect, refreshing the soul. The decree of the LORD is trustworthy, giving wisdom to the simple." 
Psalms19:9 The precepts of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart. The command of the LORD is clear, enlightening the eye." 
Psalms19:10 The fear of the LORD is pure, enduring forever. The statutes of the LORD are true, all of them just;" 
Psalms19:11 More desirable than gold, than a hoard of purest gold, Sweeter also than honey or drippings from the comb." 
Psalms19:12 By them your servant is instructed; obeying them brings much reward. 
Psalms19:13 Who can detect heedless failings? Cleanse me from my unknown faults. 
Psalms19:14 But from willful sins keep your servant; let them never control me. Then shall I be blameless, innocent of grave sin." 
Psalms19:15 Let the words of my mouth meet with your favor, keep the thoughts of my heart before you, LORD, my rock and my redeemer."

Psalms20:2 The LORD answer you in time of distress; the name of the God of Jacob defend you! 
Psalms20:3 May God send you help from the temple, from Zion be your support." 
Psalms20:4 May God remember your every offering, graciously accept your holocaust, Selah" 
Psalms20:5 Grant what is in your heart, fulfill your every plan." 
Psalms20:6 May we shout for joy at your victory, raise the banners in the name of our God. The LORD grant your every prayer!" 
Psalms20:7 Now I know victory is given to the anointed of the LORD. God will answer him from the holy heavens with a strong arm that brings victory. 
Psalms20:8 Some rely on chariots, others on horses, but we on the name of the LORD our God." 
Psalms20:9 They collapse and fall, but we stand strong and firm." 
Psalms20:10 LORD, grant victory to the king; answer when we call upon you." 
Psalms21:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms21:2 LORD, the king finds joy in your power; in your victory how greatly he rejoices!" 
Psalms21:3 You have granted him his heart's desire; you did not refuse the prayer of his lips. Selah 
Psalms21:4 For you welcomed him with goodly blessings; you placed on his head a crown of pure gold. 
Psalms21:5 He asked life of you; you gave it to him, length of days forever." 
Psalms21:6 Great is his glory in your victory; majesty and splendor you confer upon him. 
Psalms21:7 You make him the pattern of blessings forever, you gladden him with the joy of your presence." 
Psalms21:8 For the king trusts in the LORD, stands firm through the love of the Most High." 
Psalms21:9 Your hand will reach all your enemies; your right hand will reach your foes! 
Psalms21:10 At the time of your coming you will drive them into a furnace. Then the LORD'S anger will consume them, devour them with fire." 
Psalms21:11 Even their descendants you will wipe out from the earth, their offspring from the human race." 
Psalms21:12 Though they intend evil against you, devising plots, they will not succeed," 
Psalms21:13 For you will put them to flight; you will aim at them with your bow. III 
Psalms21:14 Arise, LORD, in your power! We will sing and chant the praise of your might." 
Psalms22:1 For the leader; according to The deer of the dawn. A psalm of David. 
Psalms22:2 My God, my God, why have you abandoned me? Why so far from my call for help, from my cries of anguish?" 
Psalms22:3 My God, I call by day, but you do not answer; by night, but I have no relief." 
Psalms22:4 Yet you are enthroned as the Holy One; you are the glory of Israel. 
Psalms22:5 In you our ancestors trusted; they trusted and you rescued them. 
Psalms22:6 To you they cried out and they escaped; in you they trusted and were not disappointed. 
Psalms22:7 But I am a worm, hardly human, scorned by everyone, despised by the people." 
Psalms22:8 All who see me mock me; they curl their lips and jeer; they shake their heads at me: 
Psalms22:9 You relied on the LORD, let him deliver you; if he loves you, let him rescue you.""" 
Psalms22:10 Yet you drew me forth from the womb, made me safe at my mother's breast." 
Psalms22:11 Upon you I was thrust from the womb; since birth you are my God. 
Psalms22:12 Do not stay far from me, for trouble is near, and there is no one to help." 
Psalms22:13 Many bulls surround me; fierce bulls of Bashan encircle me. 
Psalms22:14 They open their mouths against me, lions that rend and roar." 
Psalms22:15 Like water my life drains away; all my bones grow soft. My heart has become like wax, it melts away within me." 
Psalms22:16 As dry as a potsherd is my throat; my tongue sticks to my palate; you lay me in the dust of death. 
Psalms22:17 Many dogs surround me; a pack of evildoers closes in on me. So wasted are my hands and feet 
Psalms22:18 that I can count all my bones. They stare at me and gloat; 
Psalms22:19 they divide my garments among them; for my clothing they cast lots. 
Psalms22:20 But you, LORD, do not stay far off; my strength, come quickly to help me." 
Psalms22:21 Deliver me from the sword, my forlorn life from the teeth of the dog." 
Psalms22:22 Save me from the lion's mouth, my poor life from the horns of wild bulls." 
Psalms22:23 Then I will proclaim your name to the assembly; in the community I will praise you: 
Psalms22:24 You who fear the LORD, give praise! All descendants of Jacob, give honor; show reverence, all descendants of Israel!" 
Psalms22:25 For God has not spurned or disdained the misery of this poor wretch, Did not turn away from me, but heard me when I cried out." 
Psalms22:26 I will offer praise in the great assembly; my vows I will fulfill before those who fear him. 
Psalms22:27 The poor will eat their fill; those who seek the LORD will offer praise. May your hearts enjoy life forever! 
Psalms22:28 All the ends of the earth will worship and turn to the LORD; All the families of nations will bow low before you. 
Psalms22:29 For kingship belongs to the LORD, the ruler over the nations." 
Psalms22:30 All who sleep in the earth will bow low before God; All who have gone down into the dust will kneel in homage. 
Psalms22:31 And I will live for the LORD; my descendants will serve you. 
Psalms22:32 The generation to come will be told of the Lord, that they may proclaim to a people yet unborn the deliverance you have brought." 
Psalms23:1 A psalm of David. I The LORD is my shepherd; there is nothing I lack. 
Psalms23:2 In green pastures you let me graze; to safe waters you lead me; 
Psalms23:3 you restore my strength. You guide me along the right path for the sake of your name. 
Psalms23:4 Even when I walk through a dark valley, I fear no harm for you are at my side; your rod and staff give me courage." 
Psalms23:5 You set a table before me as my enemies watch; You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. 
Psalms23:6 Only goodness and love will pursue me all the days of my life; I will dwell in the house of the LORD for years to come. 
Psalms24:1 A psalm of David. I The earth is the LORD'S and all it holds, the world and those who live there." 
Psalms24:2 For God founded it on the seas, established it over the rivers." 
Psalms24:3 Who may go up the mountain of the LORD? Who can stand in his holy place? 
Psalms24:4 The clean of hand and pure of heart, who are not devoted to idols, who have not sworn falsely." 
Psalms24:5 They will receive blessings from the LORD, and justice from their saving God." 
Psalms24:6 Such are the people that love the LORD, that seek the face of the God of Jacob."" Selah" 
Psalms24:7 Lift up your heads, O gates; rise up, you ancient portals, that the king of glory may enter." 
Psalms24:8 Who is this king of glory? The LORD, a mighty warrior, the LORD, mighty in battle." 
Psalms24:9 Lift up your heads, O gates; rise up, you ancient portals, that the king of glory may enter." 
Psalms24:10 Who is this king of glory? The LORD of hosts is the king of glory. Selah 
Psalms25:1 Of David. I I wait for you, O LORD; I lift up my soul" 
Psalms25:2 to my God. In you I trust; do not let me be disgraced; do not let my enemies gloat over me. 
Psalms25:3 No one is disgraced who waits for you, but only those who lightly break faith." 
Psalms25:4 Make known to me your ways, LORD; teach me your paths." 
Psalms25:5 Guide me in your truth and teach me, for you are God my savior. For you I wait all the long day, because of your goodness, LORD." 
Psalms25:6 Remember your compassion and love, O LORD; for they are ages old." 
Psalms25:7 Remember no more the sins of my youth; remember me only in light of your love. 
Psalms25:8 Good and upright is the LORD, who shows sinners the way," 
Psalms25:9 Guides the humble rightly, and teaches the humble the way." 
Psalms25:10 All the paths of the LORD are faithful love toward those who honor the covenant demands. 
Psalms25:11 For the sake of your name, LORD, pardon my guilt, though it is great." 
Psalms25:12 Who are those who fear the LORD? God shows them the way to choose. 
Psalms25:13 They live well and prosper, and their descendants inherit the land." 
Psalms25:14 The counsel of the LORD belongs to the faithful; the covenant instructs them. 
Psalms25:15 My eyes are ever upon the LORD, who frees my feet from the snare." 
Psalms25:16 Look upon me, have pity on me, for I am alone and afflicted." 
Psalms25:17 Relieve the troubles of my heart; bring me out of my distress. 
Psalms25:18 Put an end to my affliction and suffering; take away all my sins. 
Psalms25:19 See how many are my enemies, see how fiercely they hate me." 
Psalms25:20 Preserve my life and rescue me; do not let me be disgraced, for I trust in you." 
Psalms25:21 Let honesty and virtue preserve me; I wait for you, O LORD." 
Psalms25:22 Redeem Israel, God, from all its distress!" 
Psalms26:1 Of David. I Grant me justice, LORD! I have walked without blame. In the LORD I have trusted; I have not faltered." 
Psalms26:2 Test me, LORD, and try me; search my heart and mind." 
Psalms26:3 Your love is before my eyes; I walk guided by your faithfulness. 
Psalms26:4 I do not sit with deceivers, nor with hypocrites do I mingle." 
Psalms26:5 I hate the company of evildoers; with the wicked I do not sit. 
Psalms26:6 I will wash my hands in innocence and walk round your altar, LORD," 
Psalms26:7 Lifting my voice in thanks, recounting all your wondrous deeds." 
Psalms26:8 LORD, I love the house where you dwell, the tenting-place of your glory." 
Psalms26:9 Do not take me away with sinners, nor my life with the violent." 
Psalms26:10 Their hands carry out their schemes; their right hands are full of bribes. 
Psalms26:11 But I walk without blame; redeem me, be gracious to me!" 
Psalms27:1 Of David I The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom do I fear? The LORD is my life's refuge; of whom am I afraid? 
Psalms27:2 When evildoers come at me to devour my flesh, These my enemies and foes themselves stumble and fall." 
Psalms27:3 Though an army encamp against me, my heart does not fear; Though war be waged against me, even then do I trust." 
Psalms27:4 One thing I ask of the LORD; this I seek: To dwell in the LORD'S house all the days of my life, To gaze on the LORD'S beauty, to visit his temple." 
Psalms27:5 For God will hide me in his shelter in time of trouble, Will conceal me in the cover of his tent; and set me high upon a rock." 
Psalms27:6 Even now my head is held high above my enemies on every side! I will offer in his tent sacrifices with shouts of joy; I will sing and chant praise to the LORD. 
Psalms27:7 Hear my voice, LORD, when I call; have mercy on me and answer me." 
Psalms27:8 Come,"" says my heart, ""seek God's face""; your face, LORD, do I seek!" 
Psalms27:9 Do not hide your face from me; do not repel your servant in anger. You are my help; do not cast me off; do not forsake me, God my savior!" 
Psalms27:10 Even if my father and mother forsake me, the LORD will take me in." 
Psalms27:11 LORD, show me your way; lead me on a level path because of my enemies." 
Psalms27:12 Do not abandon me to the will of my foes; malicious and lying witnesses have risen against me. 
Psalms27:13 But I believe I shall enjoy the LORD'S goodness in the land of the living. 
Psalms27:14 Wait for the LORD, take courage; be stouthearted, wait for the LORD!" 
Psalms28:1 Of David. I To you, LORD, I call; my Rock, do not be deaf to me. If you fail to answer me, I will join those who go down to the pit." 
Psalms28:2 Hear the sound of my pleading when I cry to you, lifting my hands toward your holy place." 
Psalms28:3 Do not drag me off with the wicked, with those who do wrong, Who speak peace to their neighbors though evil is in their hearts." 
Psalms28:4 Repay them for their deeds, for the evil that they do. For the work of their hands repay them; give them what they deserve." 
Psalms28:5 They pay no heed to the LORD'S works, to the deeds of God's hands. God will tear them down, never to be rebuilt." 
Psalms28:6 Blessed be the LORD, who has heard the sound of my pleading." 
Psalms28:7 The LORD is my strength and my shield, in whom my heart trusted and found help. So my heart rejoices; with my song I praise my God." 
Psalms28:8 LORD, you are the strength of your people, the saving refuge of your anointed king." 
Psalms28:9 Save your people, bless your inheritance; feed and sustain them forever!" 
Psalms29:1 A psalm of David. I Give to the LORD, you heavenly beings, give to the LORD glory and might;" 
Psalms29:2 Give to the LORD the glory due God's name. Bow down before the LORD'S holy splendor! 
Psalms29:3 The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the LORD, over the mighty waters." 
Psalms29:4 The voice of the LORD is power; the voice of the LORD is splendor. 
Psalms29:5 The voice of the LORD cracks the cedars; the LORD splinters the cedars of Lebanon," 
Psalms29:6 Makes Lebanon leap like a calf, and Sirion like a young bull." 
Psalms29:7 The voice of the LORD strikes with fiery flame; 
Psalms29:8 the voice of the LORD rocks the desert; the LORD rocks the desert of Kadesh. 
Psalms29:9 The voice of the LORD twists the oaks and strips the forests bare. All in his palace say, ""Glory!""" 
Psalms29:10 The LORD sits enthroned above the flood! The LORD reigns as king forever! 
Psalms29:11 May the LORD give might to his people; may the LORD bless his people with peace! 
Psalms30:1 A psalm. A song for the dedication of the temple. Of David. 
Psalms30:2 I praise you, LORD, for you raised me up and did not let my enemies rejoice over me." 
Psalms30:3 O LORD, my God, I cried out to you and you healed me." 
Psalms30:4 LORD, you brought me up from Sheol; you kept me from going down to the pit." 
Psalms30:5 Sing praise to the LORD, you faithful; give thanks to God's holy name." 
Psalms30:6 For divine anger lasts but a moment; divine favor lasts a lifetime. At dusk weeping comes for the night; but at dawn there is rejoicing. 
Psalms30:7 Complacent, I once said, ""I shall never be shaken.""" 
Psalms30:8 LORD, when you showed me favor I stood like the mighty mountains. But when you hid your face I was struck with terror." 
Psalms30:9 To you, LORD, I cried out; with the Lord I pleaded for mercy:" 
Psalms30:10 What gain is there from my lifeblood, from my going down to the grave? Does dust give you thanks or declare your faithfulness?" 
Psalms30:11 Hear, O LORD, have mercy on me; LORD, be my helper.""" 
Psalms30:12 You changed my mourning into dancing; you took off my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness. 
Psalms30:13 With my whole being I sing endless praise to you. O LORD, my God, forever will I give you thanks." 
Psalms31:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms31:2 In you, LORD, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame. In your justice deliver me;" 
Psalms31:3 incline your ear to me; make haste to rescue me! Be my rock of refuge, a stronghold to save me." 
Psalms31:4 You are my rock and my fortress; for your name's sake lead and guide me. 
Psalms31:5 Free me from the net they have set for me, for you are my refuge." 
Psalms31:6 Into your hands I commend my spirit; you will redeem me, LORD, faithful God." 
Psalms31:7 You hate those who serve worthless idols, but I trust in the LORD." 
Psalms31:8 I will rejoice and be glad in your love, once you have seen my misery, observed my distress." 
Psalms31:9 You will not abandon me into enemy hands, but will set my feet in a free and open space." 
Psalms31:10 Be gracious to me, LORD, for I am in distress; with grief my eyes are wasted, my soul and body spent." 
Psalms31:11 My life is worn out by sorrow, my years by sighing. My strength fails in affliction; my bones are consumed." 
Psalms31:12 To all my foes I am a thing of scorn, to my neighbors, a dreaded sight, a horror to my friends. When they see me in the street, they quickly shy away." 
Psalms31:13 I am forgotten, out of mind like the dead; I am like a shattered dish." 
Psalms31:14 I hear the whispers of the crowd; terrors are all around me. They conspire against me; they plot to take my life. 
Psalms31:15 But I trust in you, LORD; I say, ""You are my God.""" 
Psalms31:16 My times are in your hands; rescue me from my enemies, from the hands of my pursuers." 
Psalms31:17 Let your face shine on your servant; save me in your kindness. 
Psalms31:18 Do not let me be put to shame, for I have called to you, LORD. Put the wicked to shame; reduce them to silence in Sheol." 
Psalms31:19 Strike dumb their lying lips, proud lips that attack the just in contempt and scorn." 
Psalms31:20 How great is your goodness, Lord, stored up for those who fear you. You display it for those who trust you, in the sight of all the people." 
Psalms31:21 You hide them in the shelter of your presence, safe from scheming enemies. You keep them in your abode, safe from plotting tongues." 
Psalms31:22 Blessed be the LORD, who has shown me wondrous love, and been for me a city most secure." 
Psalms31:23 Once I said in my anguish, ""I am shut out from your sight."" Yet you heard my plea, when I cried out to you." 
Psalms31:24 Love the LORD, all you faithful. The LORD protects the loyal, but repays the arrogant in full." 
Psalms31:25 Be strong and take heart, all you who hope in the LORD." 
Psalms32:1 Of David. A maskil. I Happy the sinner whose fault is removed, whose sin is forgiven." 
Psalms32:2 Happy those to whom the LORD imputes no guilt, in whose spirit is no deceit." 
Psalms32:3 As long as I kept silent, my bones wasted away; I groaned all the day." 
Psalms32:4 For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength withered as in dry summer heat. Selah 
Psalms32:5 Then I declared my sin to you; my guilt I did not hide. I said, ""I confess my faults to the LORD,"" and you took away the guilt of my sin. Selah" 
Psalms32:6 Thus should all your faithful pray in time of distress. Though flood waters threaten, they will never reach them." 
Psalms32:7 You are my shelter; from distress you keep me; with safety you ring me round. Selah 
Psalms32:8 I will instruct you and show you the way you should walk, give you counsel and watch over you." 
Psalms32:9 Do not be senseless like horses or mules; with bit and bridle their temper is curbed, else they will not come to you." 
Psalms32:10 Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but love surrounds those who trust in the LORD." 
Psalms32:11 Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you just; exult, all you upright of heart." 
Psalms33:1 Rejoice, you just, in the LORD; praise from the upright is fitting." 
Psalms33:2 Give thanks to the LORD on the harp; on the ten-stringed lyre offer praise. 
Psalms33:3 Sing to God a new song; skillfully play with joyful chant. 
Psalms33:4 For the LORD'S word is true; all his works are trustworthy. 
Psalms33:5 The LORD loves justice and right and fills the earth with goodness. 
Psalms33:6 By the LORD'S word the heavens were made; by the breath of his mouth all their host. 
Psalms33:7 The waters of the sea were gathered as in a bowl; in cellars the deep was confined. 
Psalms33:8 Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all who dwell in the world show reverence. 
Psalms33:9 For he spoke, and it came to be, commanded, and it stood in place." 
Psalms33:10 The LORD foils the plan of nations, frustrates the designs of peoples." 
Psalms33:11 But the plan of the LORD stands forever, wise designs through all generations." 
Psalms33:12 Happy the nation whose God is the LORD, the people chosen as his very own." 
Psalms33:13 From heaven the LORD looks down and observes the whole human race," 
Psalms33:14 Surveying from the royal throne all who dwell on earth. 
Psalms33:15 The one who fashioned the hearts of them all knows all their works. 
Psalms33:16 A king is not saved by a mighty army, nor a warrior delivered by great strength." 
Psalms33:17 Useless is the horse for safety; its great strength, no sure escape." 
Psalms33:18 But the LORD'S eyes are upon the reverent, upon those who hope for his gracious help," 
Psalms33:19 Delivering them from death, keeping them alive in times of famine." 
Psalms33:20 Our soul waits for the LORD, who is our help and shield." 
Psalms33:21 For in God our hearts rejoice; in your holy name we trust. 
Psalms33:22 May your kindness, LORD, be upon us; we have put our hope in you." 
Psalms34:1 Of David, when he feigned madness before Abimelech, who forced him to depart." 
Psalms34:2 I will bless the LORD at all times; praise shall be always in my mouth. 
Psalms34:3 My soul will glory in the LORD that the poor may hear and be glad. 
Psalms34:4 Magnify the LORD with me; let us exalt his name together. 
Psalms34:5 I sought the LORD, who answered me, delivered me from all my fears." 
Psalms34:6 Look to God that you may be radiant with joy and your faces may not blush for shame. 
Psalms34:7 In my misfortune I called, the LORD heard and saved me from all distress." 
Psalms34:8 The angel of the LORD, who encamps with them, delivers all who fear God." 
Psalms34:9 Learn to savor how good the LORD is; happy are those who take refuge in him. 
Psalms34:10 Fear the LORD, you holy ones; nothing is lacking to those who fear him." 
Psalms34:11 The powerful grow poor and hungry, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing." 
Psalms34:12 Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD." 
Psalms34:13 Who among you loves life, takes delight in prosperous days?" 
Psalms34:14 Keep your tongue from evil, your lips from speaking lies." 
Psalms34:15 Turn from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it. 
Psalms34:16 The LORD has eyes for the just and ears for their cry. 
Psalms34:17 The LORD'S face is against evildoers to wipe out their memory from the earth. 
Psalms34:18 When the just cry out, the LORD hears and rescues them from all distress." 
Psalms34:19 The LORD is close to the brokenhearted, saves those whose spirit is crushed." 
Psalms34:20 Many are the troubles of the just, but the LORD delivers from them all." 
Psalms34:21 God watches over all their bones; not a one shall be broken. 
Psalms34:22 Evil will slay the wicked; those who hate the just are condemned. 
Psalms34:23 The LORD redeems loyal servants; no one is condemned whose refuge is God. 
Psalms35:1 Of David. I Oppose, LORD, those who oppose me; war upon those who make war upon me." 
Psalms35:2 Take up the shield and buckler; rise up in my defense. 
Psalms35:3 Brandish lance and battle-ax against my pursuers. Say to my heart, ""I am your salvation.""" 
Psalms35:4 Let those who seek my life be put to shame and disgrace. Let those who plot evil against me be turned back and confounded. 
Psalms35:5 Make them like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them on." 
Psalms35:6 Make their way slippery and dark, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them." 
Psalms35:7 Without cause they set their snare for me; without cause they dug a pit for me. 
Psalms35:8 Let ruin overtake them unawares; let the snare they have set catch them; let them fall into the pit they have dug. 
Psalms35:9 Then I will rejoice in the LORD, exult in God's salvation." 
Psalms35:10 My very bones shall say, ""O LORD, who is like you, Who rescue the afflicted from the powerful, the afflicted and needy from the despoiler?""" 
Psalms35:11 Malicious witnesses come forward, accuse me of things I do not know." 
Psalms35:12 They repay me evil for good and I am all alone. 
Psalms35:13 Yet I, when they were ill, put on sackcloth, afflicted myself with fasting, sobbed my prayers upon my bosom." 
Psalms35:14 I went about in grief as for my brother, bent in mourning as for my mother." 
Psalms35:15 Yet when I stumbled they gathered with glee, gathered against me like strangers. They slandered me without ceasing;" 
Psalms35:16 without respect they mocked me, gnashed their teeth against me." 
Psalms35:17 Lord, how long will you look on? Save me from roaring beasts, my precious life from lions!" 
Psalms35:18 Then I will thank you in the great assembly; I will praise you before the mighty throng. 
Psalms35:19 Do not let lying foes smirk at me, my undeserved enemies wink knowingly." 
Psalms35:20 They speak no words of peace, but against the quiet in the land they fashion deceitful speech." 
Psalms35:21 They open wide their mouths against me. They say, ""Aha! Good! Our eyes relish the sight!""" 
Psalms35:22 You see this, LORD; do not be silent; Lord, do not withdraw from me." 
Psalms35:23 Awake, be vigilant in my defense, in my cause, my God and my Lord." 
Psalms35:24 Defend me because you are just, LORD; my God, do not let them gloat over me." 
Psalms35:25 Do not let them say in their hearts, ""Aha! Just what we wanted!"" Do not let them say, ""We have devoured that one!""" 
Psalms35:26 Put to shame and confound all who relish my misfortune. Clothe with shame and disgrace those who lord it over me. 
Psalms35:27 But let those who favor my just cause shout for joy and be glad. May they ever say, ""Exalted be the LORD who delights in the peace of his loyal servant.""" 
Psalms35:28 Then my tongue shall recount your justice, declare your praise, all the day long." 
Psalms36:1 For the leader. Of David, the servant of the LORD." 
Psalms36:2 Sin directs the heart of the wicked; their eyes are closed to the fear of God. 
Psalms36:3 For they live with the delusion: their guilt will not be known and hated. 
Psalms36:4 Empty and false are the words of their mouth; they have ceased to be wise and do good. 
Psalms36:5 In their beds they hatch plots; they set out on a wicked way; they do not reject evil. 
Psalms36:6 LORD, your love reaches to heaven; your fidelity, to the clouds." 
Psalms36:7 Your justice is like the highest mountains; your judgments, like the mighty deep; all living creatures you sustain, LORD." 
Psalms36:8 How precious is your love, O God! We take refuge in the shadow of your wings." 
Psalms36:9 We feast on the rich food of your house; from your delightful stream you give us drink. 
Psalms36:10 For with you is the fountain of life, and in your light we see light." 
Psalms36:11 Continue your kindness toward your friends, your just defense of the honest heart." 
Psalms36:12 Do not let the foot of the proud overtake me, nor the hand of the wicked disturb me." 
Psalms36:13 There make the evildoers fall; thrust them down, never to rise." 
Psalms37:1 Of David. Do not be provoked by evildoers; do not envy those who do wrong. 
Psalms37:2 Like grass they wither quickly; like green plants they wilt away. 
Psalms37:3 Trust in the LORD and do good that you may dwell in the land and live secure. 
Psalms37:4 Find your delight in the LORD who will give you your heart's desire. 
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the LORD; trust that God will act 
Psalms37:6 And make your integrity shine like the dawn, your vindication like noonday." 
Psalms37:7 Be still before the LORD; wait for God. Do not be provoked by the prosperous, nor by malicious schemers." 
Psalms37:8 Give up your anger, abandon your wrath; do not be provoked; it brings only harm." 
Psalms37:9 Those who do evil will be cut off, but those who wait for the LORD will possess the land." 
Psalms37:10 Wait a little, and the wicked will be no more; look for them and they will not be there." 
Psalms37:11 But the poor will possess the land, will delight in great prosperity." 
Psalms37:12 The wicked plot against the just and grind their teeth at them; 
Psalms37:13 But the LORD laughs at them, knowing their day is coming." 
Psalms37:14 The wicked draw their swords; they string their bows To fell the poor and oppressed, to slaughter those whose way is honest." 
Psalms37:15 Their swords will pierce their own hearts; their bows will be broken. 
Psalms37:16 Better the poverty of the just than the great wealth of the wicked. 
Psalms37:17 The arms of the wicked will be broken; the LORD will sustain the just. 
Psalms37:18 The LORD watches over the days of the blameless; their heritage lasts forever. 
Psalms37:19 They will not be disgraced when times are hard; in days of famine they will have plenty. 
Psalms37:20 The wicked perish, the enemies of the LORD; Like the beauty of meadows they vanish; like smoke they disappear." 
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrow but do not repay; the just are generous in giving. 
Psalms37:22 For those blessed by the Lord will possess the land, but those accursed will be cut off." 
Psalms37:23 Those whose steps are guided by the LORD; whose way God approves," 
Psalms37:24 May stumble, but they will never fall, for the LORD holds their hand." 
Psalms37:25 Neither in my youth, nor now in old age have I ever seen the just abandoned or their children begging bread." 
Psalms37:26 The just always lend generously, and their children become a blessing." 
Psalms37:27 Turn from evil and do good, that you may inhabit the land forever." 
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loves justice and does not abandon the faithful. When the unjust are destroyed, and the children of the wicked cut off," 
Psalms37:29 The just will possess the land and live in it forever. 
Psalms37:30 The mouths of the just utter wisdom; their tongues speak what is right. 
Psalms37:31 God's teaching is in their hearts; their steps do not falter. 
Psalms37:32 The wicked spy on the just and seek to kill them. 
Psalms37:33 But the LORD does not leave the just in their power, nor let them be condemned when tried." 
Psalms37:34 Wait eagerly for the LORD, and keep to the way; God will raise you to possess the land; you will gloat when the wicked are cut off." 
Psalms37:35 I have seen ruthless scoundrels, strong as flourishing cedars." 
Psalms37:36 When I passed by again, they were gone; though I searched, they could not be found." 
Psalms37:37 Observe the honest, mark the upright; those at peace with God have a future." 
Psalms37:38 But all sinners will be destroyed; the future of the wicked will be cut off. 
Psalms37:39 The salvation of the just is from the LORD, their refuge in time of distress." 
Psalms37:40 The LORD helps and rescues them, rescues and saves them from the wicked, because in God they take refuge." 
Psalms38:1 A psalm of David. For remembrance. 
Psalms38:2 LORD, punish me no more in your anger; in your wrath do not chastise me!" 
Psalms38:3 Your arrows have sunk deep in me; your hand has come down upon me. 
Psalms38:4 My flesh is afflicted because of your anger; my frame aches because of my sin. 
Psalms38:5 My iniquities overwhelm me, a burden beyond my strength." 
Psalms38:6 Foul and festering are my sores because of my folly. 
Psalms38:7 I am stooped and deeply bowed; all day I go about mourning. 
Psalms38:8 My loins burn with fever; my flesh is afflicted. 
Psalms38:9 I am numb and utterly crushed; I wail with anguish of heart. 
Psalms38:10 My Lord, my deepest yearning is before you; my groaning is not hidden from you." 
Psalms38:11 My heart shudders, my strength forsakes me; the very light of my eyes has failed." 
Psalms38:12 Friends and companions shun my pain; my neighbors stand far off. 
Psalms38:13 Those who seek my life lay snares for me; they seek my misfortune, they speak of ruin; they plot treachery all the day." 
Psalms38:14 But I am like the deaf, hearing nothing, like the dumb, saying nothing," 
Psalms38:15 Like someone who does not hear, who has no answer ready." 
Psalms38:16 LORD, I wait for you; O Lord, my God, answer me." 
Psalms38:17 For I fear they will gloat, exult over me if I stumble." 
Psalms38:18 I am very near to falling; my pain is with me always. 
Psalms38:19 I acknowledge my guilt and grieve over my sin. 
Psalms38:20 But many are my foes without cause, a multitude of enemies without reason," 
Psalms38:21 Repaying me evil for good, harassing me for pursuing good." 
Psalms38:22 Forsake me not, O LORD; my God, be not far from me!" 
Psalms38:23 Come quickly to help me, my Lord and my salvation!" 
Psalms39:1 For the leader, for Jeduthun. A psalm of David." 
Psalms39:2 I said, ""I will watch my ways, lest I sin with my tongue; I will set a curb on my mouth.""" 
Psalms39:3 Dumb and silent before the wicked, I refrained from any speech. But my sorrow increased;" 
Psalms39:4 my heart smoldered within me. In my thoughts a fire blazed up, and I broke into speech:" 
Psalms39:5 LORD, let me know my end, the number of my days, that I may learn how frail I am." 
Psalms39:6 You have given my days a very short span; my life is as nothing before you. All mortals are but a breath. Selah 
Psalms39:7 Mere phantoms, we go our way; mere vapor, our restless pursuits; we heap up stores without knowing for whom." 
Psalms39:8 And now, Lord, what future do I have? You are my only hope." 
Psalms39:9 From all my sins deliver me; let me not be the taunt of fools. 
Psalms39:10 I was silent and did not open my mouth because you were the one who did this. 
Psalms39:11 Take your plague away from me; I am ravaged by the touch of your hand. 
Psalms39:12 You rebuke our guilt and chasten us; you dissolve all we prize like a cobweb. All mortals are but a breath. Selah 
Psalms39:13 Listen to my prayer, LORD, hear my cry; do not be deaf to my weeping! I sojourn with you like a passing stranger, a guest, like all my ancestors." 
Psalms39:14 Turn your gaze from me, that I may find peace before I depart to be no more." 
Psalms40:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms40:2 I waited, waited for the LORD; who bent down and heard my cry," 
Psalms40:3 Drew me out of the pit of destruction, out of the mud of the swamp, Set my feet upon rock, steadied my steps," 
Psalms40:4 And put a new song in my mouth, a hymn to our God. Many shall look on in awe and they shall trust in the LORD." 
Psalms40:5 Happy those whose trust is the LORD, who turn not to idolatry or to those who stray after falsehood." 
Psalms40:6 How numerous, O LORD, my God, you have made your wondrous deeds! And in your plans for us there is none to equal you. Should I wish to declare or tell them, too many are they to recount." 
Psalms40:7 sacrifice and offering you do not want; but ears open to obedience you gave me. Holocausts and sin-offerings you do not require; 
Psalms40:8 so I said, ""Here I am; your commands for me are written in the scroll." 
Psalms40:9 To do your will is my delight; my God, your law is in my heart!""" 
Psalms40:10 I announced your deed to a great assembly; I did not restrain my lips; you, LORD, are my witness." 
Psalms40:11 Your deed I did not hide within my heart; your loyal deliverance I have proclaimed. I made no secret of your enduring kindness to a great assembly. 
Psalms40:12 LORD, do not withhold your compassion from me; may your enduring kindness ever preserve me." 
Psalms40:13 For all about me are evils beyond count; my sins so overcome me I cannot see. They are more than the hairs of my head; my courage fails me. 
Psalms40:14 LORD, graciously rescue me! Come quickly to help me, LORD!" 
Psalms40:15 Put to shame and confound all who seek to take my life. Turn back in disgrace those who desire my ruin. 
Psalms40:16 Let those who say Aha! know dismay and shame. 
Psalms40:17 But may all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. May those who long for your help always say, ""The LORD be glorified.""" 
Psalms40:18 Though I am afflicted and poor, the Lord keeps me in mind. You are my help and deliverer; my God, do not delay!" 
Psalms41:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms41:2 Happy those concerned for the lowly and poor; when misfortune strikes, the LORD delivers them." 
Psalms41:3 The LORD keeps and preserves them, makes them happy in the land, and does not betray them to their enemies." 
Psalms41:4 The LORD sustains them on their sickbed, allays the malady when they are ill." 
Psalms41:5 Once I prayed, ""LORD, have mercy on me; heal me, I have sinned against you." 
Psalms41:6 My enemies say the worst of me: 'When will that one die and be forgotten?' 
Psalms41:7 When people come to visit me, they speak without sincerity. Their hearts store up malice; they leave and spread their vicious lies." 
Psalms41:8 My foes all whisper against me; they imagine the worst about me: 
Psalms41:9 I have a deadly disease, they say; I will never rise from my sickbed." 
Psalms41:10 Even the friend who had my trust, who shared my table, has scorned me." 
Psalms41:11 But you, LORD, have mercy and raise me up that I may repay them as they deserve.""" 
Psalms41:12 By this I know you are pleased with me, that my enemy no longer jeers at me." 
Psalms41:13 For my integrity you have supported me and let me stand in your presence forever. 
Psalms41:14 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from all eternity and forever. Amen. Amen." 
Psalms42:1 For the leader. A maskil of the Korahites. 
Psalms42:2 As the deer longs for streams of water, so my soul longs for you, O God." 
Psalms42:3 My being thirsts for God, the living God. When can I go and see the face of God?" 
Psalms42:4 My tears have been my food day and night, as they ask daily,""Where is your God?""" 
Psalms42:5 Those times I recall as I pour out my soul, When I went in procession with the crowd, I went with them to the house of God, Amid loud cries of thanksgiving, with the multitude keeping festival." 
Psalms42:6 Why are you downcast, my soul; why do you groan within me? Wait for God, whom I shall praise again, my savior and my God." 
Psalms42:7 My soul is downcast within me; therefore I will remember you From the land of the Jordan and Hermon, from the land of Mount Mizar." 
Psalms42:8 Here deep calls to deep in the roar of your torrents. All your waves and breakers sweep over me. 
Psalms42:9 At dawn may the LORD bestow faithful love that I may sing praise through the night, praise to the God of my life." 
Psalms42:10 I say to God, ""My rock, why do you forget me? Why must I go about mourning with the enemy oppressing me?""" 
Psalms42:11 It shatters my bones, when my adversaries reproach me. They say to me daily: ""Where is your God?""" 
Psalms42:12 Why are you downcast, my soul, why do you groan within me? Wait for God, whom I shall praise again, my savior and my God." 
Psalms43:1 Grant me justice, God; defend me from a faithless people; from the deceitful and unjust rescue me." 
Psalms43:2 You, God, are my strength. Why then do you spurn me? Why must I go about mourning, with the enemy oppressing me?" 
Psalms43:3 Send your light and fidelity, that they may be my guide And bring me to your holy mountain, to the place of your dwelling," 
Psalms43:4 That I may come to the altar of God, to God, my joy, my delight. Then I will praise you with the harp, O God, my God." 
Psalms43:5 Why are you downcast, my soul? Why do you groan within me? Wait for God, whom I shall praise again, my savior and my God." 
Psalms44:1 For the leader. A maskil of the Korahites. 
Psalms44:2 O God, we have heard with our own ears; our ancestors have told us The deeds you did in their days, with your own hand in days of old:" 
Psalms44:3 You rooted out nations to plant them, crushed peoples to make room for them." 
Psalms44:4 Not with their own swords did they conquer the land, nor did their own arms bring victory; It was your right hand, your own arm, the light of your face for you favored them." 
Psalms44:5 You are my king and my God, who bestows victories on Jacob." 
Psalms44:6 Through you we batter our foes; through your name, trample our adversaries." 
Psalms44:7 Not in my bow do I trust, nor does my sword bring me victory." 
Psalms44:8 You have brought us victory over our enemies, shamed those who hate us." 
Psalms44:9 In God we have boasted all the day long; your name we will praise forever. Selah 
Psalms44:10 But now you have rejected and disgraced us; you do not march out with our armies. 
Psalms44:11 You make us retreat before the foe; those who hate us plunder us at will. 
Psalms44:12 You hand us over like sheep to be slaughtered, scatter us among the nations." 
Psalms44:13 You sell your people for nothing; you make no profit from their sale. 
Psalms44:14 You make us the reproach of our neighbors, the mockery and scorn of those around us." 
Psalms44:15 You make us a byword among the nations; the peoples shake their heads at us. 
Psalms44:16 All day long my disgrace is before me; shame has covered my face 
Psalms44:17 At the sound of those who taunt and revile, at the sight of the spiteful enemy." 
Psalms44:18 All this has come upon us, though we have not forgotten you, nor been disloyal to your covenant." 
Psalms44:19 Our hearts have not turned back, nor have our steps strayed from your path." 
Psalms44:20 Yet you have left us crushed, desolate in a place of jackals; you have covered us with darkness." 
Psalms44:21 If we had forgotten the name of our God, stretched out our hands to another god," 
Psalms44:22 Would not God have discovered this, God who knows the secrets of the heart?" 
Psalms44:23 For you we are slain all the day long, considered only as sheep to be slaughtered." 
Psalms44:24 Awake! Why do you sleep, O Lord? Rise up! Do not reject us forever!" 
Psalms44:25 Why do you hide your face; why forget our pain and misery? 
Psalms44:26 We are bowed down to the ground; our bodies are pressed to the earth. 
Psalms44:27 Rise up, help us! Redeem us as your love demands." 
Psalms45:1 For the leader; according to Lilies. A maskil of the Korahites. A love song. 
Psalms45:2 My heart is stirred by a noble theme, as I sing my ode to the king. My tongue is the pen of a nimble scribe." 
Psalms45:3 You are the most handsome of men; fair speech has graced your lips, for God has blessed you forever." 
Psalms45:4 Gird your sword upon your hip, mighty warrior! In splendor and majesty ride on triumphant!" 
Psalms45:5 In the cause of truth and justice may your right hand show you wondrous deeds. 
Psalms45:6 Your arrows are sharp; peoples will cower at your feet; the king's enemies will lose heart. 
Psalms45:7 Your throne, O god, stands forever; your royal scepter is a scepter for justice." 
Psalms45:8 You love justice and hate wrongdoing; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellow kings." 
Psalms45:9 With myrrh, aloes, and cassia your robes are fragrant. From ivory-paneled palaces stringed instruments bring you joy." 
Psalms45:10 Daughters of kings are your lovely wives; a princess arrayed in Ophir's gold comes to stand at your right hand. 
Psalms45:11 Listen, my daughter, and understand; pay me careful heed. Forget your people and your father's house," 
Psalms45:12 that the king might desire your beauty. He is your lord; 
Psalms45:13 honor him, daughter of Tyre. Then the richest of the people will seek your favor with gifts." 
Psalms45:14 All glorious is the king's daughter as she enters, her raiment threaded with gold;" 
Psalms45:15 In embroidered apparel she is led to the king. The maids of her train are presented to the king. 
Psalms45:16 They are led in with glad and joyous acclaim; they enter the palace of the king. 
Psalms45:17 The throne of your fathers your sons will have; you shall make them princes through all the land. 
Psalms45:18 I will make your name renowned through all generations; thus nations shall praise you forever. 
Psalms46:1 For the leader. A song of the Korahites. According to alamoth. 
Psalms46:2 God is our refuge and our strength, an ever-present help in distress." 
Psalms46:3 Thus we do not fear, though earth be shaken and mountains quake to the depths of the sea," 
Psalms46:4 Though its waters rage and foam and mountains totter at its surging. The LORD of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah 
Psalms46:5 Streams of the river gladden the city of God, the holy dwelling of the Most High." 
Psalms46:6 God is in its midst; it shall not be shaken; God will help it at break of day. 
Psalms46:7 Though nations rage and kingdoms totter, God's voice thunders and the earth trembles." 
Psalms46:8 The LORD of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah 
Psalms46:9 Come and see the works of the LORD, who has done fearsome deeds on earth;" 
Psalms46:10 Who stops wars to the ends of the earth, breaks the bow, splinters the spear, and burns the shields with fire;" 
Psalms46:11 Who says: Be still and confess that I am God! I am exalted among the nations, exalted on the earth.""" 
Psalms46:12 The LORD of hosts is with us; our stronghold is the God of Jacob. Selah 
Psalms47:1 For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites. 
Psalms47:2 All you peoples, clap your hands; shout to God with joyful cries." 
Psalms47:3 For the LORD, the Most High, inspires awe, the great king overall the earth," 
Psalms47:4 Who made people subject to us, brought nations under our feet," 
Psalms47:5 Who chose a land for our heritage, the glory of Jacob, the beloved. Selah" 
Psalms47:6 God mounts the throne amid shouts of joy; the LORD, amid trumpet blasts." 
Psalms47:7 Sing praise to God, sing praise; sing praise to our king, sing praise." 
Psalms47:8 God is king over all the earth; sing hymns of praise. 
Psalms47:9 God rules over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne. 
Psalms47:10 The princes of the peoples assemble with the people of the God of Abraham. For the rulers of the earth belong to God, who is enthroned on high." 
Psalms48:1 A psalm of the Korahites. A song. 
Psalms48:2 Great is the LORD and highly praised in the city of our God: The holy mountain," 
Psalms48:3 fairest of heights, the joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, the heights of Zaphon, the city of the great king." 
Psalms48:4 God is its citadel, renowned as a stronghold." 
Psalms48:5 See! The kings assembled, together they invaded." 
Psalms48:6 When they looked they were astounded; terrified, they were put to flight!" 
Psalms48:7 Trembling seized them there, anguish, like a woman's labor," 
Psalms48:8 As when the east wind wrecks the ships of Tarshish! 
Psalms48:9 What we had heard we now see in the city of the LORD of hosts, In the city of our God, founded to last forever. Selah" 
Psalms48:10 O God, within your temple we ponder your steadfast love." 
Psalms48:11 Like your name, O God, your praise reaches the ends of the earth. Your right hand is fully victorious." 
Psalms48:12 Mount Zion is glad! The cities of Judah rejoice because of your saving deeds! 
Psalms48:13 Go about Zion, walk all around it, note the number of its towers." 
Psalms48:14 Consider the ramparts, examine its citadels, that you may tell future generations:" 
Psalms48:15 Yes, so mighty is God, our God who leads us always!""" 
Psalms49:1 For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites. 
Psalms49:2 Hear this, all you peoples! Give ear, all who inhabit the world," 
Psalms49:3 You of lowly birth or high estate, rich and poor alike." 
Psalms49:4 My mouth shall speak wisdom, my heart shall offer insight." 
Psalms49:5 I will turn my attention to a problem, expound my question to the music of a lyre." 
Psalms49:6 Why should I fear in evil days, when my wicked pursuers ring me round," 
Psalms49:7 Those who trust in their wealth and boast of their abundant riches? 
Psalms49:8 One cannot redeem oneself, pay to God a ransom." 
Psalms49:9 Too high the price to redeem a life; one would never have enough 
Psalms49:10 To stay alive forever and never see the pit. 
Psalms49:11 Anyone can see that the wisest die, the fool and the senseless pass away too, and must leave their wealth to others." 
Psalms49:12 Tombs are their homes forever, their dwellings through all generations, though they gave their names to their lands." 
Psalms49:13 For all their riches mortals do not abide; they perish like the beasts. 
Psalms49:14 This is the destiny of those who trust in folly, the end of those so pleased with their wealth. Selah" 
Psalms49:15 Like sheep they are herded into Sheol, where death will be their shepherd. Straight to the grave they descend, where their form will waste away, Sheol will be their palace." 
Psalms49:16 But God will redeem my life, will take me from the power of Sheol. Selah" 
Psalms49:17 Do not fear when others become rich, when the wealth of their houses grows great." 
Psalms49:18 When they die they will take nothing with them, their wealth will not follow them down." 
Psalms49:19 When living, they congratulate themselves and say: ""All praise you, you do so well.""" 
Psalms49:20 But they will join the company of their forebears, never again to see the light." 
Psalms49:21 For all their riches, if mortals do not have wisdom, they perish like the beasts." 
Psalms50:1 A psalm of Asaph. I The LORD, the God of gods, has spoken and summoned the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting." 
Psalms50:2 From Zion God shines forth. perfect in beauty. 
Psalms50:3 Our God comes and will not be silent! Devouring fire precedes, storming fiercely round about." 
Psalms50:4 God summons the heavens above and the earth to the judgment of his people: 
Psalms50:5 Gather my faithful ones before me, those who made a covenant with me by sacrifice.""" 
Psalms50:6 The heavens proclaim divine justice, for God alone is the judge. Selah" 
Psalms50:7 Listen, my people, I will speak; Israel, I will testify against you; God, your God, am I." 
Psalms50:8 Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you, nor for your holocausts, set before me daily." 
Psalms50:9 I need no bullock from your house, no goats from your fold." 
Psalms50:10 For every animal of the forest is mine, beasts by the thousands on my mountains." 
Psalms50:11 I know every bird of the heavens; the creatures of the field belong to me. 
Psalms50:12 Were I hungry, I would not tell you, for mine is the world and all that fills it." 
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of goats? 
Psalms50:14 Offer praise as your sacrifice to God; fulfill your vows to the Most High. 
Psalms50:15 Then call on me in time of distress; I will rescue you, and you shall honor me.""" 
Psalms50:16 But to the wicked God says: Why do you recite my commandments and profess my covenant with your lips? 
Psalms50:17 You hate discipline; you cast my words behind you! 
Psalms50:18 When you see thieves, you befriend them; with adulterers you throw in your lot." 
Psalms50:19 You give your mouth free rein for evil; you harness your tongue to deceit. 
Psalms50:20 You sit maligning your own kin, slandering the child of your own mother." 
Psalms50:21 When you do these things should I be silent? Or do you think that I am like you? I accuse you, I lay the charge before you." 
Psalms50:22 Understand this, you who forget God, lest I attack you with no one to rescue." 
Psalms50:23 Those who offer praise as a sacrifice honor me; to the obedient I will show the salvation of God. 
Psalms51:1 For the leader. A psalm of David," 
Psalms51:2 when Nathan the prophet came to him after his affair with Bathsheba. 
Psalms51:3 Have mercy on me, God, in your goodness; in your abundant compassion blot out my offense." 
Psalms51:4 Wash away all my guilt; from my sin cleanse me. 
Psalms51:5 For I know my offense; my sin is always before me. 
Psalms51:6 Against you alone have I sinned; I have done such evil in your sight That you are just in your sentence, blameless when you condemn." 
Psalms51:7 True, I was born guilty, a sinner, even as my mother conceived me." 
Psalms51:8 Still, you insist on sincerity of heart; in my inmost being teach me wisdom." 
Psalms51:9 Cleanse me with hyssop, that I may be pure; wash me, make me whiter than snow." 
Psalms51:10 Let me hear sounds of joy and gladness; let the bones you have crushed rejoice. 
Psalms51:11 Turn away your face from my sins; blot out all my guilt. 
Psalms51:12 A clean heart create for me, God; renew in me a steadfast spirit." 
Psalms51:13 Do not drive me from your presence, nor take from me your holy spirit." 
Psalms51:14 Restore my joy in your salvation; sustain in me a willing spirit. 
Psalms51:15 I will teach the wicked your ways, that sinners may return to you." 
Psalms51:16 Rescue me from death, God, my saving God, that my tongue may praise your healing power." 
Psalms51:17 Lord, open my lips; my mouth will proclaim your praise." 
Psalms51:18 For you do not desire sacrifice; a burnt offering you would not accept. 
Psalms51:19 My sacrifice, God, is a broken spirit; God, do not spurn a broken, humbled heart. III" 
Psalms51:20 Make Zion prosper in your good pleasure; rebuild the walls of Jerusalem. 
Psalms51:21 Then you will be pleased with proper sacrifice, burnt offerings and holocausts; then bullocks will be offered on your altar." 
Psalms52:1 For the leader. A maskil of David," 
Psalms52:2 when Doeg the Edomite went and told Saul, ""David went to the house of Ahimelech.""" 
Psalms52:3 Why do you glory in evil, you scandalous liar? All day long" 
Psalms52:4 you plot destruction; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, you skillful deceiver." 
Psalms52:5 You love evil rather than good, lies rather than honest speech. Selah" 
Psalms52:6 You love any word that destroys, you deceitful tongue." 
Psalms52:7 Now God will strike you down, leave you crushed forever, Pluck you from your tent, uproot you from the land of the living. Selah" 
Psalms52:8 The righteous will look on with awe; they will jeer and say: 
Psalms52:9 That one did not take God as a refuge, but trusted in great wealth, relied on devious plots."" III" 
Psalms52:10 But I, like an olive tree in the house of God, trust in God's faithful love forever." 
Psalms52:11 I will praise you always for what you have done. I will proclaim before the faithful that your name is good. 
Psalms53:1 For the leader; according to Mahalath. A maskil of David. 
Psalms53:2 Fools say in their hearts, ""There is no God."" Their deeds are loathsome and corrupt; not one does what is right." 
Psalms53:3 God looks down from heaven upon the human race, To see if even one is wise, if even one seeks God." 
Psalms53:4 All have gone astray; all alike are perverse. Not one does what is right, not even one." 
Psalms53:5 Will these evildoers never learn? They devour my people as they devour bread; they do not call upon God. 
Psalms53:6 They have good reason to fear, though now they do not fear. For God will certainly scatter the bones of the godless. They will surely be put to shame, for God has rejected them. III" 
Psalms53:7 Oh, that from Zion might come the deliverance of Israel, That Jacob may rejoice and Israel be glad when God restores the people!" 
Psalms54:1 For the leader. On stringed instruments. A maskil of David," 
Psalms54:2 when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, ""David is hiding among us.""" 
Psalms54:3 O God, by your name save me. By your strength defend my cause." 
Psalms54:4 O God, hear my prayer. Listen to the words of my mouth." 
Psalms54:5 The arrogant have risen against me; the ruthless seek my life; they do not keep God before them. Selah 
Psalms54:6 God is present as my helper; the Lord sustains my life. 
Psalms54:7 Turn back the evil upon my foes; in your faithfulness, destroy them." 
Psalms54:8 Then I will offer you generous sacrifice and praise your gracious name, LORD," 
Psalms54:9 Because it has rescued me from every trouble, and my eyes look down on my foes." 
Psalms55:1 For the leader. On stringed instruments. A maskil of David. 
Psalms55:2 Listen, God, to my prayer; do not hide from my pleading;" 
Psalms55:3 hear me and give answer. I rock with grief; I groan 
Psalms55:4 at the uproar of the enemy, the clamor of the wicked. They heap trouble upon me, savagely accuse me." 
Psalms55:5 My heart pounds within me; death's terrors fall upon me. 
Psalms55:6 Fear and trembling overwhelm me; shuddering sweeps over me. 
Psalms55:7 I say, ""If only I had wings like a dove that I might fly away and find rest." 
Psalms55:8 Far away I would flee; I would stay in the desert. Selah 
Psalms55:9 I would soon find a shelter from the raging wind and storm. 
Psalms55:10 Lord, check and confuse their scheming. I see violence and strife in the city" 
Psalms55:11 making rounds on its walls day and night. Within are mischief and evil; 
Psalms55:12 treachery is there as well; oppression and fraud never leave its streets. 
Psalms55:13 If an enemy had reviled me, that I could bear; If my foe had viewed me with contempt, from that I could hide." 
Psalms55:14 But it was you, my other self, my comrade and friend," 
Psalms55:15 You, whose company I enjoyed, at whose side I walked in procession in the house of God." 
Psalms55:16 Let death take them by surprise; let them go down alive to Sheol, for evil is in their homes and hearts." 
Psalms55:17 But I will call upon God, and the LORD will save me." 
Psalms55:18 At dusk, dawn, and noon I will grieve and complain, and my prayer will be heard." 
Psalms55:19 God will give me freedom and peace from those who war against me, though there are many who oppose me." 
Psalms55:20 God, who sits enthroned forever, will hear me and humble them. For they will not mend their ways; they have no fear of God." 
Psalms55:21 They strike out at friends and go back on their promises. 
Psalms55:22 Softer than butter is their speech, but war is in their hearts. Smoother than oil are their words, but they are unsheathed swords." 
Psalms55:23 Cast your care upon the LORD, who will give you support. God will never allow the righteous to stumble." 
Psalms55:24 But you, God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction. These bloodthirsty liars will not live half their days, but I put my trust in you."
