Psalms56:2 Have mercy on me, God, for I am treated harshly; attackers press me all the day." 
Psalms56:3 My foes treat me harshly all the day; yes, many are my attackers. O Most High," 
Psalms56:4 when I am afraid, in you I place my trust." 
Psalms56:5 God, I praise your promise; in you I trust, I do not fear. What can mere flesh do to me?" 
Psalms56:6 All the day they foil my plans; their every thought is of evil against me. 
Psalms56:7 They hide together in ambush; they watch my every step; they lie in wait for my life. 
Psalms56:8 They are evil; watch them, God! Cast the nations down in your anger!" 
Psalms56:9 My wanderings you have noted; are my tears not stored in your vial, recorded in your book?" 
Psalms56:10 My foes turn back when I call on you. This I know: God is on my side. 
Psalms56:11 God, I praise your promise;" 
Psalms56:12 in you I trust, I do not fear. What can mere mortals do to me?" 
Psalms56:13 I have made vows to you, God; with offerings I will fulfill them," 
Psalms56:14 Once you have snatched me from death, kept my feet from stumbling, That I may walk before God in the light of the living." 
Psalms57:1 For the director. Do not destroy. A miktam of David, when he fled from Saul into a cave." 
Psalms57:2 Have mercy on me, God, have mercy on me. In you I seek shelter. In the shadow of your wings I seek shelter till harm pass by." 
Psalms57:3 I call to God Most High, to God who provides for me." 
Psalms57:4 May God send help from heaven to save me, shame those who trample upon me. May God send fidelity and love. Selah" 
Psalms57:5 I must lie down in the midst of lions hungry for human prey. Their teeth are spears and arrows; their tongue, a sharpened sword." 
Psalms57:6 Show yourself over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth." 
Psalms57:7 They have set a trap for my feet; my soul is bowed down; They have dug a pit before me. May they fall into it themselves! Selah 
Psalms57:8 My heart is steadfast, God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing and chant praise." 
Psalms57:9 Awake, my soul; awake, lyre and harp! I will wake the dawn." 
Psalms57:10 I will praise you among the peoples, Lord; I will chant your praise among the nations." 
Psalms57:11 For your love towers to the heavens; your faithfulness, to the skies." 
Psalms57:12 Show yourself over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth." 
Psalms58:1 For the leader. Do not destroy. A miktam of David. 
Psalms58:2 Do you indeed pronounce justice, O gods; do you judge mortals fairly?" 
Psalms58:3 No, you freely engage in crime; your hands dispense violence to the earth." 
Psalms58:4 The wicked have been corrupt since birth; liars from the womb, they have gone astray." 
Psalms58:5 Their poison is like the poison of a snake, like that of a serpent stopping its ears," 
Psalms58:6 So as not to hear the voice of the charmer who casts such cunning spells. 
Psalms58:7 O God, smash the teeth in their mouths; break the jaw-teeth of these lions, LORD!" 
Psalms58:8 Make them vanish like water flowing away; trodden down, let them wither like grass." 
Psalms58:9 Let them dissolve like a snail that oozes away, like an untimely birth that never sees the sun." 
Psalms58:10 Suddenly, like brambles or thistles, have the whirlwind snatch them away." 
Psalms58:11 Then the just shall rejoice to see the vengeance and bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked. 
Psalms58:12 Then it will be said: Truly there is a reward for the just; there is a God who is judge on earth! 
Psalms59:1 For the director. Do not destroy. A miktam of David, when Saul sent people to watch his house and kill him." 
Psalms59:2 Rescue me from my enemies, my God; lift me out of reach of my foes." 
Psalms59:3 Deliver me from evildoers; from the bloodthirsty save me. 
Psalms59:4 They have set an ambush for my life; the powerful conspire against me. For no offense or misdeed of mine, LORD," 
Psalms59:5 for no fault they hurry to take up arms. Come near and see my plight! 
Psalms59:6 You, LORD of hosts, are the God of Israel! Awake! Punish all the nations. Have no mercy on these worthless traitors. Selah" 
Psalms59:7 Each evening they return, growling like dogs, prowling the city." 
Psalms59:8 Their mouths pour out insult; sharp words are on their lips. They say: Who is there to hear? 
Psalms59:9 You, LORD, laugh at them; you deride all the nations." 
Psalms59:10 My strength, for you I watch; you, God, are my fortress," 
Psalms59:11 my loving God. 
Psalms59:12 May God go before me, and show me my fallen foes. Slay them, God, lest they deceive my people. Shake them by your power; Lord, our shield, bring them down." 
Psalms59:13 For the sinful words of their mouths and lips let them be caught in their pride. For the lies they have told under oath 
Psalms59:14 destroy them in anger, destroy till they are no more. Then people will know God rules over Jacob, yes, even to the ends of the earth. Selah" 
Psalms59:15 Each evening they return, growling like dogs, prowling the city." 
Psalms59:16 They roam about as scavengers; if they are not filled, they howl." 
Psalms59:17 But I shall sing of your strength, extol your love at dawn, For you are my fortress, my refuge in time of trouble." 
Psalms59:18 My strength, your praise I will sing; you, God, are my fortress, my loving God." 
Psalms60:1 For the leader; according to The Lily of.... A miktam of David (for teaching)," 
Psalms60:2 when he fought against Aram-Naharaim and Aram-Zobah; and Joab, coming back, killed twelve thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt." 
Psalms60:3 O God, you rejected us, broke our defenses; you were angry but now revive us." 
Psalms60:4 You rocked the earth, split it open; repair the cracks for it totters." 
Psalms60:5 You made your people go through hardship, made us stagger from the wine you gave us." 
Psalms60:6 Raise up a flag for those who revere you, a refuge for them out of bow shot. Selah" 
Psalms60:7 Help with your right hand and answer us that your loved ones may escape. 
Psalms60:8 In the sanctuary God promised: I will exult, will apportion Shechem; the valley of Succoth I will measure out." 
Psalms60:9 Gilead is mine, mine is Manasseh; Ephraim is the helmet for my head, Judah, my own scepter." 
Psalms60:10 Moab is my washbowl; upon Edom I cast my sandal. I will triumph over Philistia. 
Psalms60:11 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me into Edom? 
Psalms60:12 Was it not you who rejected us, God? Do you no longer march with our armies?" 
Psalms60:13 Give us aid against the foe; worthless is human help. 
Psalms60:14 We will triumph with the help of God, who will trample down our foes."

Psalms61:3 From the brink of Sheol I call; my heart grows faint. Raise me up, set me on a rock," 
Psalms61:4 for you are my refuge, a tower of strength against the foe." 
Psalms61:5 Then I will ever dwell in your tent, take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah" 
Psalms61:6 O God, when you accept my vows and hear the plea of those who revere your name in prayer:" 
Psalms61:7 Add to the days of the king's life; may his years be many generations; 
Psalms61:8 May he reign before God forever; may your love and fidelity preserve him, 
Psalms61:9 Then I will sing your name forever, fulfill my vows day after day."

Psalms62:2 My soul rests in God alone, from whom comes my salvation." 
Psalms62:3 God alone is my rock and salvation, my secure height; I shall never fall." 
Psalms62:4 How long will you set upon people, all of you beating them down, As though they were a sagging fence or a battered wall?" 
Psalms62:5 Even from my place on high they plot to dislodge me. They delight in lies; they bless with their mouths, but inwardly they curse. Selah" 
Psalms62:6 My soul, be at rest in God alone, from whom comes my hope." 
Psalms62:7 God alone is my rock and my salvation, my secure height; I shall not fall." 
Psalms62:8 My safety and glory are with God, my strong rock and refuge." 
Psalms62:9 Trust God at all times, my people! Pour out your hearts to God our refuge! Selah" 
Psalms62:10 Mortals are a mere breath, the powerful but an illusion; On a balance they rise; together they are lighter than air." 
Psalms62:11 Do not trust in extortion; in plunder put no empty hope. Though wealth increase, do not set your heart upon it." 
Psalms62:12 One thing God has said; two things I have heard: Power belongs to God; 
Psalms62:13 so too, Lord, does kindness, And you render to each of us according to our deeds." 
Psalms63:1 A psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah." 
Psalms63:2 O God, you are my God, for you I long! For you my body yearns; for you my soul thirsts, Like a land parched, lifeless, and without water." 
Psalms63:3 So I look to you in the sanctuary to see your power and glory. 
Psalms63:4 For your love is better than life; my lips offer you worship! 
Psalms63:5 I will bless you as long as I live; I will lift up my hands, calling on your name." 
Psalms63:6 My soul shall savor the rich banquet of praise, with joyous lips my mouth shall honor you!" 
Psalms63:7 When I think of you upon my bed, through the night watches I will recall" 
Psalms63:8 That you indeed are my help, and in the shadow of your wings I shout for joy." 
Psalms63:9 My soul clings fast to you; your right hand upholds me. 
Psalms63:10 But those who seek my life will come to ruin; they shall go down to the depths of the earth! 
Psalms63:11 They shall be handed over to the sword and become the prey of jackals! 
Psalms63:12 But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by the Lord shall exult, for the mouths of liars will be shut!"

Psalms64:2 O God, hear my anguished voice; from the foes I dread protect my life." 
Psalms64:3 Hide me from the malicious crowd, the mob of evildoers." 
Psalms64:4 They sharpen their tongues like swords, ready their bows for arrows of poison words." 
Psalms64:5 They shoot at the innocent from ambush, shoot without risk, catch them unawares." 
Psalms64:6 They resolve on their wicked plan; they conspire to set snares; they say: Who will see us? 
Psalms64:7 They devise wicked schemes, conceal the schemes they devise; the designs of their hearts are hidden." 
Psalms64:8 But God will shoot arrows at them and strike them unawares. 
Psalms64:9 They will be brought down by their own tongues; all who see them will shake their heads. 
Psalms64:10 Then all will fear and proclaim God's deed, pondering what has been done." 
Psalms64:11 The just will rejoice and take refuge in the LORD; all the upright will glory in their God. 
Psalms65:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. A song. 
Psalms65:2 To you we owe our hymn of praise, O God on Zion; To you our vows must be fulfilled," 
Psalms65:3 you who hear our prayers. To you all flesh must come 
Psalms65:4 with its burden of wicked deeds. We are overcome by our sins; only you can pardon them. 
Psalms65:5 Happy the chosen ones you bring to dwell in your courts. May we be filled with the good things of your house, the blessings of your holy temple!" 
Psalms65:6 You answer us with awesome deeds of justice, O God our savior, The hope of all the ends of the earth and of far distant islands." 
Psalms65:7 You are robed in power, you set up the mountains by your might." 
Psalms65:8 You still the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, the tumult of the peoples." 
Psalms65:9 Distant peoples stand in awe of your marvels; east and west you make resound with joy. 
Psalms65:10 You visit the earth and water it, make it abundantly fertile. God's stream is filled with water; with it you supply the world with grain. Thus do you prepare the earth:" 
Psalms65:11 you drench plowed furrows, and level their ridges. With showers you keep the ground soft, blessing its young sprouts." 
Psalms65:12 You adorn the year with your bounty; your paths drip with fruitful rain. 
Psalms65:13 The untilled meadows also drip; the hills are robed with joy. 
Psalms65:14 The pastures are clothed with flocks, the valleys blanketed with grain; they cheer and sing for joy."

Psalms66:2 Shout joyfully to God, all you on earth; sing of his glorious name; give him glorious praise." 
Psalms66:3 Say to God: How awesome your deeds! Before your great strength your enemies cringe. 
Psalms66:4 All on earth fall in worship before you; they sing of you, sing of your name!"" Selah" 
Psalms66:5 Come and see the works of God, awesome in the deeds done for us." 
Psalms66:6 He changed the sea to dry land; through the river they passed on foot. Therefore let us rejoice in him," 
Psalms66:7 who rules by might forever, Whose eyes are fixed upon the nations. Let no rebel rise to challenge! Selah" 
Psalms66:8 Bless our God, you peoples; loudly sound his praise," 
Psalms66:9 Who has kept us alive and not allowed our feet to slip. 
Psalms66:10 You tested us, O God, tried us as silver tried by fire." 
Psalms66:11 You led us into a snare; you bound us at the waist as captives. 
Psalms66:12 You let captors set foot on our neck; we went through fire and water; then you led us out to freedom. 
Psalms66:13 I will bring holocausts to your house; to you I will fulfill my vows," 
Psalms66:14 The vows my lips pronounced and my mouth spoke in distress. 
Psalms66:15 Holocausts of fatlings I will offer you and burnt offerings of rams; I will sacrifice oxen and goats. Selah 
Psalms66:16 Come and hear, all you who fear God, while I recount what has been done for me." 
Psalms66:17 I called to the Lord with my mouth; praise was upon my tongue. 
Psalms66:18 Had I cherished evil in my heart, the Lord would not have heard." 
Psalms66:19 But God did hear and listened to my voice in prayer. 
Psalms66:20 Blessed be God, who did not refuse me the kindness I sought in prayer."

Psalms68:1 For the leader. A psalm of David; a song. 
Psalms68:2 God will arise for battle; the enemy will be scattered; those who hate God will flee. 
Psalms68:3 The wind will disperse them like smoke; as wax is melted by fire, so the wicked will perish before God." 
Psalms68:4 Then the just will be glad; they will rejoice before God; they will celebrate with great joy. 
Psalms68:5 Sing to God, praise the divine name; exalt the rider of the clouds. Rejoice before this God whose name is the LORD." 
Psalms68:6 Father of the fatherless, defender of widows, this is the God whose abode is holy," 
Psalms68:7 Who gives a home to the forsaken, who leads prisoners out to prosperity, while rebels live in the desert." 
Psalms68:8 God, when you went forth before your people, when you marched through the desert, Selah" 
Psalms68:9 The earth quaked, the heavens shook, before God, the One of Sinai, before God, the God of Israel." 
Psalms68:10 You claimed a land as your own, O God;" 
Psalms68:11 your people settled there. There you poured abundant rains, God, graciously given to the poor in their need." 
Psalms68:12 The Lord announced the news of victory: All you people so numerous," 
Psalms68:13 The kings and their armies are in desperate flight. b) Every household will share the booty," 
Psalms68:14 will you stay by the sheepfolds? b) perhaps a dove sheathed with silver, c) its wings covered with yellow gold.""" 
Psalms68:15 When the Almighty routed the kings there, the spoils were scattered like snow on Zalmon." 
Psalms68:16 You high mountains of Bashan, you rugged mountains of Bashan," 
Psalms68:17 You rugged mountains, why look with envy at the mountain where God has chosen to dwell, where the LORD resides forever?" 
Psalms68:18 God's chariots were myriad, thousands upon thousands; from Sinai the Lord entered the holy place." 
Psalms68:19 You went up to its lofty height; you took captives, received slaves as tribute. No rebels can live in the presence of God." 
Psalms68:20 Blessed be the Lord day by day, God, our salvation, who carries us. Selah" 
Psalms68:21 Our God is a God who saves; escape from death is in the LORD God's hands. 
Psalms68:22 God will crush the skulls of the enemy, the hairy heads of those who walk in sin." 
Psalms68:23 The Lord has said: Even from Bashan I will fetch them, fetch them even from the depths of the sea." 
Psalms68:24 You will wash your feet in your enemy's blood; the tongues of your dogs will lap it up. 
Psalms68:25 Your procession comes into view, O God, your procession into the holy place, my God and king." 
Psalms68:26 The singers go first, the harpists follow; in their midst girls sound the timbrels." 
Psalms68:27 In your choirs, bless God; bless the LORD, you from Israel's assemblies." 
Psalms68:28 In the lead is Benjamin, few in number; there the princes of Judah, a large throng, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali, too." 
Psalms68:29 Summon again, O God, your power, the divine power you once showed for us." 
Psalms68:30 Show it from your temple on behalf of Jerusalem, that kings may bring you tribute." 
Psalms68:31 Roar at the wild beast of the reeds, the herd of mighty bulls, the lords of nations; scatter the nations that delight in war." 
Psalms68:32 Exact rich tribute from lower Egypt, from upper Egypt, gold and silver; make Ethiopia extend its hands to God." 
Psalms68:33 You kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; chant the praises of the Lord, Selah" 
Psalms68:34 Who rides the heights of the ancient heavens, whose voice is thunder, mighty thunder." 
Psalms68:35 Confess the power of God, whose majesty protects Israel, whose power is in the sky." 
Psalms68:36 Awesome is God in his holy place, the God of Israel, who gives power and strength to his people. Blessed be God!" 
Psalms69:1 For the leader; according to Lilies. Of David. 
Psalms69:2 Save me, God, for the waters have reached my neck." 
Psalms69:3 I have sunk into the mire of the deep, where there is no foothold. I have gone down to the watery depths; the flood overwhelms me." 
Psalms69:4 I am weary with crying out; my throat is parched. My eyes have failed, looking for my God." 
Psalms69:5 More numerous than the hairs of my head are those who hate me without cause. Too many for my strength are my treacherous enemies. Must I now restore what I did not steal? 
Psalms69:6 God, you know my folly; my faults are not hidden from you." 
Psalms69:7 Let those who wait for you, LORD of hosts, not be shamed through me. Let those who seek you, God of Israel, not be disgraced through me" 
Psalms69:8 For your sake I bear insult, shame covers my face." 
Psalms69:9 I have become an outcast to my kin, a stranger to my mother's children." 
Psalms69:10 Because zeal for your house consumes me, I am scorned by those who scorn you." 
Psalms69:11 I have wept and fasted, but this led only to scorn." 
Psalms69:12 I clothed myself in sackcloth; I became a byword for them. 
Psalms69:13 They who sit at the gate gossip about me; drunkards make me the butt of their songs. 
Psalms69:14 But I pray to you, LORD, for the time of your favor. God, in your great kindness answer me with your constant help." 
Psalms69:15 Rescue me from the mire; do not let me sink. Rescue me from my enemies and from the watery depths. 
Psalms69:16 Do not let the floodwaters overwhelm me, nor the deep swallow me, nor the mouth of the pit close over me." 
Psalms69:17 Answer me, LORD, in your generous love; in your great mercy turn to me." 
Psalms69:18 Do not hide your face from your servant; in my distress hasten to answer me. 
Psalms69:19 Come and ransom my life; because of my enemies redeem me. 
Psalms69:20 You know my reproach, my shame, my disgrace; before you stand all my foes." 
Psalms69:21 Insult has broken my heart, and I am weak; I looked for compassion, but there was none, for comforters, but found none." 
Psalms69:22 Instead they put gall in my food; for my thirst they gave me vinegar. 
Psalms69:23 Make their own table a snare for them, a trap for their friends." 
Psalms69:24 Make their eyes so dim they cannot see; keep their backs ever feeble. 
Psalms69:25 Pour out your wrath upon them; let the fury of your anger overtake them. 
Psalms69:26 Make their camp desolate, with none to dwell in their tents." 
Psalms69:27 For they pursued the one you struck, added to the pain of the one you wounded." 
Psalms69:28 Add that to their crimes; let them not attain to your reward. 
Psalms69:29 Strike them from the book of the living; do not count them among the just! 
Psalms69:30 But I am afflicted and in pain; let your saving help protect me, God," 
Psalms69:31 That I may praise God's name in song and glorify it with thanksgiving. 
Psalms69:32 My song will please the LORD more than oxen, more than bullocks with horns and hooves:" 
Psalms69:33 See, you lowly ones, and be glad; you who seek God, take heart!" 
Psalms69:34 For the LORD hears the poor, does not spurn those in bondage." 
Psalms69:35 Let the heavens and the earth sing praise, the seas and whatever moves in them!""" 
Psalms69:36 God will rescue Zion, rebuild the cities of Judah. God's servants shall dwell in the land and possess it;" 
Psalms69:37 it shall be the heritage of their descendants; those who love God's name shall dwell there. 
Psalms70:1 For the leader; of David. For remembrance. 
Psalms70:2 Graciously rescue me, God! Come quickly to help me, LORD!" 
Psalms70:3 Confound and put to shame those who seek my life. Turn back in disgrace those who desire my ruin. 
Psalms70:4 Let those who say Aha! turn back in their shame. 
Psalms70:5 But may all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. May those who long for your help always say, ""God be glorified!""" 
Psalms70:6 Here I am, afflicted and poor. God, come quickly! You are my help and deliverer. LORD, do not delay!" 
Psalms71:1 In you, LORD, I take refuge; let me never be put to shame." 
Psalms71:2 In your justice rescue and deliver me; listen to me and save me! 
Psalms71:3 Be my rock and refuge, my secure stronghold; for you are my rock and fortress." 
Psalms71:4 My God, rescue me from the power of the wicked, from the clutches of the violent." 
Psalms71:5 You are my hope, Lord; my trust, GOD, from my youth." 
Psalms71:6 On you I depend since birth; from my mother's womb you are my strength; my hope in you never wavers. 
Psalms71:7 I have become a portent to many, but you are my strong refuge!" 
Psalms71:8 My mouth shall be filled with your praise, shall sing your glory every day." 
Psalms71:9 Do not cast me aside in my old age; as my strength fails, do not forsake me." 
Psalms71:10 For my enemies speak against me; they watch and plot against me. 
Psalms71:11 They say, ""God has abandoned that one Pursue, seize the wretch! No one will come to the rescue!""" 
Psalms71:12 God, do not stand far from me; my God, hasten to help me." 
Psalms71:13 Bring to a shameful end those who attack me; Cover with contempt and scorn those who seek my ruin. 
Psalms71:14 I will always hope in you and add to all your praise. 
Psalms71:15 My mouth shall proclaim your just deeds, day after day your acts of deliverance, though I cannot number them all." 
Psalms71:16 I will speak of the mighty works of the Lord; O GOD, I will tell of your singular justice." 
Psalms71:17 God, you have taught me from my youth; to this day I proclaim your wondrous deeds." 
Psalms71:18 Now that I am old and gray, do not forsake me, God, That I may proclaim your might to all generations yet to come, Your power" 
Psalms71:19 and justice, God, to the highest heaven. You have done great things; O God, who is your equal?" 
Psalms71:20 You have sent me many bitter afflictions, but once more revive me. From the watery depths of the earth once more raise me up." 
Psalms71:21 Restore my honor; turn and comfort me," 
Psalms71:22 That I may praise you with the lyre for your faithfulness, my God, And sing to you with the harp, O Holy One of Israel!" 
Psalms71:23 My lips will shout for joy as I sing your praise; my soul, too, which you have redeemed." 
Psalms71:24 Yes, my tongue shall recount your justice day by day. For those who sought my ruin will have been shamed and disgraced." 
Psalms72:1 Of Solomon. 
Psalms72:2 I O God, give your judgment to the king; your justice to the son of kings; That he may govern your people with justice, your oppressed with right judgment," 
Psalms72:3 That the mountains may yield their bounty for the people, and the hills great abundance," 
Psalms72:4 That he may defend the oppressed among the people, save the poor and crush the oppressor." 
Psalms72:5 May he live as long as the sun endures, like the moon, through all generations." 
Psalms72:6 May he be like rain coming down upon the fields, like showers watering the earth," 
Psalms72:7 That abundance may flourish in his days, great bounty, till the moon be no more." 
Psalms72:8 May he rule from sea to sea, from the river to the ends of the earth." 
Psalms72:9 May his foes kneel before him, his enemies lick the dust." 
Psalms72:10 May the kings of Tarshish and the islands bring tribute, the kings of Arabia and Seba offer gifts." 
Psalms72:11 May all kings bow before him, all nations serve him." 
Psalms72:12 For he rescues the poor when they cry out, the oppressed who have no one to help." 
Psalms72:13 He shows pity to the needy and the poor and saves the lives of the poor. 
Psalms72:14 From extortion and violence he frees them, for precious is their blood in his sight." 
Psalms72:15 Long may he live, receiving gold from Arabia, prayed for without cease, blessed day by day." 
Psalms72:16 May wheat abound in the land, flourish even on the mountain heights. May his fruit increase like Lebanon's, his wheat like the grasses of the land." 
Psalms72:17 May his name be blessed forever; as long as the sun, may his name endure. May the tribes of the earth give blessings with his name; may all the nations regard him as favored." 
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who alone does wonderful deeds." 
Psalms72:19 Blessed be his glorious name forever; may all the earth be filled with the LORD'S glory. Amen and amen. 
Psalms72:20 The end of the psalms of David, son of Jesse." 
Psalms73:1 A psalm of Asaph. How good God is to the upright, the Lord, to those who are clean of heart!" 
Psalms73:2 But, as for me, I lost my balance; my feet all but slipped," 
Psalms73:3 Because I was envious of the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked. 
Psalms73:4 For they suffer no pain; their bodies are healthy and sleek. 
Psalms73:5 They are free of the burdens of life; they are not afflicted like others. 
Psalms73:6 Thus pride adorns them as a necklace; violence clothes them as a robe. 
Psalms73:7 Out of their stupidity comes sin; evil thoughts flood their hearts. 
Psalms73:8 They scoff and spout their malice; from on high they utter threats. 
Psalms73:9 They set their mouths against the heavens, their tongues roam the earth." 
Psalms73:10 So my people turn to them and drink deeply of their words. 
Psalms73:11 They say, ""Does God really know?"" ""Does the Most High have any knowledge?""" 
Psalms73:12 Such, then, are the wicked, always carefree, increasing their wealth." 
Psalms73:13 Is it in vain that I have kept my heart clean, washed my hands in innocence?" 
Psalms73:14 For I am afflicted day after day, chastised every morning." 
Psalms73:15 Had I thought, ""I will speak as they do,"" I would have betrayed your people." 
Psalms73:16 Though I tried to understand all this, it was too difficult for me," 
Psalms73:17 Till I entered the sanctuary of God and came to understand their end. 
Psalms73:18 You set them, indeed, on a slippery road; you hurl them down to ruin." 
Psalms73:19 How suddenly they are devastated; undone by disasters forever! 
Psalms73:20 They are like a dream after waking, Lord, dismissed like shadows when you arise." 
Psalms73:21 Since my heart was embittered and my soul deeply wounded," 
Psalms73:22 I was stupid and could not understand; I was like a brute beast in your presence. 
Psalms73:23 Yet I am always with you; you take hold of my right hand. 
Psalms73:24 With your counsel you guide me, and at the end receive me with honor." 
Psalms73:25 Whom else have I in the heavens? None beside you delights me on earth. 
Psalms73:26 Though my flesh and my heart fail, God is the rock of my heart, my portion forever." 
Psalms73:27 But those who are far from you perish; you destroy those unfaithful to you. 
Psalms73:28 As for me, to be near God is my good, to make the Lord GOD my refuge. I shall declare all your works in the gates of daughter Zion." 
Psalms74:1 A maskil of Asaph. I Why, God, have you cast us off forever? Why does your anger burn against the sheep of your pasture?" 
Psalms74:2 Remember your flock that you gathered of old, the tribe you redeemed as your very own. Remember Mount Zion where you dwell." 
Psalms74:3 Turn your steps toward the utter ruins, toward the sanctuary devastated by the enemy." 
Psalms74:4 Your foes roared triumphantly in your shrine; they set up their own tokens of victory. 
Psalms74:5 They hacked away like foresters gathering boughs, swinging their axes in a thicket of trees." 
Psalms74:6 They smashed all your engraved work, pounded it with hammer and pick." 
Psalms74:7 They set your sanctuary on fire; the abode of your name they razed and profaned. 
Psalms74:8 They said in their hearts, ""Destroy them all! Burn all the shrines of God in the land!""" 
Psalms74:9 Now we see no signs, we have no prophets, no one who knows how long." 
Psalms74:10 How long, O God, shall the enemy jeer? Shall the foe revile your name forever?" 
Psalms74:11 Why draw back your right hand, why keep it idle beneath your cloak?" 
Psalms74:12 Yet you, God, are my king from of old, winning victories throughout the earth." 
Psalms74:13 You stirred up the sea in your might; you smashed the heads of the dragons on the waters. 
Psalms74:14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan, tossed him for food to the sharks." 
Psalms74:15 You opened up springs and torrents, brought dry land out of the primeval waters." 
Psalms74:16 Yours the day and yours the night; you set the moon and sun in place. 
Psalms74:17 You fixed all the limits of the earth; summer and winter you made. 
Psalms74:18 Remember how the enemy has jeered, O LORD, how a foolish people has reviled your name." 
Psalms74:19 Do not surrender to beasts those who praise you; do not forget forever the life of your afflicted. 
Psalms74:20 Look to your covenant, for the land is filled with gloom; the pastures, with violence." 
Psalms74:21 Let not the oppressed turn back in shame; may the poor and needy praise your name. 
Psalms74:22 Arise, God, defend your cause; remember the constant jeers of the fools." 
Psalms74:23 Do not ignore the clamor of your foes, the unceasing uproar of your enemies." 
Psalms75:1 For the leader. Do not destroy! A psalm of Asaph; a song. 
Psalms75:2 We thank you, God, we give thanks; we call upon your name, declare your wonderful deeds. You said:" 
Psalms75:3 I will choose the time; I will judge fairly. 
Psalms75:4 The earth and all its inhabitants will quake, but I have firmly set its pillars."" Selah" 
Psalms75:5 So I say to the boastful: Do not boast! to the wicked: Do not raise your horns! 
Psalms75:6 Do not raise your horns against heaven! Do not speak arrogantly against the Rock! 
Psalms75:7 For judgment comes not from east or from west, not from the desert or from the mountains," 
Psalms75:8 But from God who decides, who brings some low and raises others high." 
Psalms75:9 Yes, a cup is in the LORD'S hand, foaming wine, fully spiced. When God pours it out, they will drain it even to the dregs; all the wicked of the earth must drink." 
Psalms75:10 But I will rejoice forever; I will sing praise to the God of Jacob," 
Psalms75:11 Who has said: I will break off all the horns of the wicked, but the horns of the just shall be lifted up.""" 
Psalms76:1 For the leader; a psalm with stringed instruments. A song of Asaph. 
Psalms76:2 I Renowned in Judah is God, whose name is great in Israel." 
Psalms76:3 On Salem is God's tent, a shelter on Zion." 
Psalms76:4 There the flashing arrows were shattered, shield, sword, and weapons of war. Selah" 
Psalms76:5 Terrible and awesome are you, stronger than the ancient mountains." 
Psalms76:6 Despoiled are the bold warriors; they sleep their final sleep; the hands of all the mighty have failed. 
Psalms76:7 At your roar, O God of Jacob, chariots and steeds lay still." 
Psalms76:8 So terrible and awesome are you; who can stand before you and your great anger? 
Psalms76:9 From the heavens you pronounced sentence; the earth was terrified and reduced to silence," 
Psalms76:10 When you arose, O God, for judgment to deliver the afflicted of the land. Selah" 
Psalms76:11 Even wrathful Edom praises you; the remnant of Hamath keeps your feast. 
Psalms76:12 Make and keep vows to the LORD your God. May all present bring gifts to this awesome God," 
Psalms76:13 Who checks the pride of princes, inspires awe among the kings of earth." 
Psalms77:1 For the leader; al Jeduthun. A psalm of Asaph. 
Psalms77:2 I cry aloud to God, cry to God to hear me." 
Psalms77:3 On the day of my distress I seek the Lord; by night my hands are raised unceasingly; I refuse to be consoled. 
Psalms77:4 When I think of God, I groan; as I ponder, my spirit grows faint. Selah" 
Psalms77:5 My eyes cannot close in sleep; I am troubled and cannot speak. 
Psalms77:6 I consider the days of old; the years long past 
Psalms77:7 I remember. In the night I meditate in my heart; I ponder and my spirit broods: 
Psalms77:8 Will the Lord reject us forever, never again show favor?" 
Psalms77:9 Has God's love ceased forever? Has the promise failed for all ages? 
Psalms77:10 Has God forgotten mercy, in anger withheld compassion?"" Selah" 
Psalms77:11 I conclude: My sorrow is this, the right hand of the Most High has left us.""" 
Psalms77:12 I will remember the deeds of the LORD; yes, your wonders of old I will remember." 
Psalms77:13 I will recite all your works; your exploits I will tell. 
Psalms77:14 Your way, O God, is holy; what god is as great as our God?" 
Psalms77:15 You alone are the God who did wonders; among the peoples you revealed your might. 
Psalms77:16 With your arm you redeemed your people, the descendants of Jacob and Joseph. Selah" 
Psalms77:17 The waters saw you, God; the waters saw you and lashed about, trembled even to their depths." 
Psalms77:18 The clouds poured down their rains; the thunderheads rumbled; your arrows flashed back and forth. 
Psalms77:19 The thunder of your chariot wheels resounded; your lightning lit up the world; the earth trembled and quaked. 
Psalms77:20 Through the sea was your path; your way, through the mighty waters, though your footsteps were unseen." 
Psalms77:21 You led your people like a flock under the care of Moses and Aaron. 
Psalms78:1 A maskil of Asaph. I Attend, my people, to my teaching; listen to the words of my mouth." 
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in story, drawing lessons from of old." 
Psalms78:3 We have heard them, we know them; our ancestors have recited them to us." 
Psalms78:4 We do not keep them from our children; we recite them to the next generation, The praiseworthy and mighty deeds of the LORD, the wonders that he performed." 
Psalms78:5 God set up a decree in Jacob, established a law in Israel: What he commanded our ancestors, they were to teach their children;" 
Psalms78:6 That the next generation might come to know, children yet to be born. In turn they were to recite them to their children," 
Psalms78:7 that they too might put their trust in God, And not forget the works of God, keeping his commandments." 
Psalms78:8 They were not to be like their ancestors, a rebellious and defiant generation, A generation whose heart was not constant, whose spirit was not faithful to God," 
Psalms78:9 Like the ranks of Ephraimite archers, who retreated on the day of battle." 
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God's covenant; they refused to walk by his law. 
Psalms78:11 They forgot his works, the wondrous deeds he had shown them." 
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their ancestors God did wonders, in the land of Egypt, the plain of Zoan." 
Psalms78:13 He split the sea and led them across, piling up the waters rigid as walls." 
Psalms78:14 God led them with a cloud by day, all night with the light of fire." 
Psalms78:15 He split rock in the desert, gave water to drink, abounding as the deep." 
Psalms78:16 He made streams flow from crags, drew out rivers of water." 
Psalms78:17 But they went on sinning against him, rebelling against the Most High in the desert." 
Psalms78:18 They tested God in their hearts, demanding the food they craved." 
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God, and said, ""Can God spread a table in the desert?" 
Psalms78:20 True, when he struck the rock, water gushed forth, the wadis flooded. But can he also provide bread, give meat to his people?""" 
Psalms78:21 The LORD heard and grew angry; fire blazed up against Jacob; anger flared up against Israel. 
Psalms78:22 For they did not believe in God, did not trust in his saving power." 
Psalms78:23 So he commanded the skies above; the doors of heaven he opened. 
Psalms78:24 God rained manna upon them for food; bread from heaven he gave them. 
Psalms78:25 All ate a meal fit for heroes; food he sent in abundance. 
Psalms78:26 He stirred up the east wind in the heavens; by his power God brought on the south wind. 
Psalms78:27 He rained meat upon them like dust, winged fowl like the sands of the sea," 
Psalms78:28 Brought them down in the midst of the camp, round about their tents." 
Psalms78:29 They ate and were well filled; he gave them what they had craved. 
Psalms78:30 But while they still wanted more, and the food was still in their mouths," 
Psalms78:31 God's anger attacked them, killed their best warriors, laid low the youth of Israel." 
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this they went on sinning, they did not believe in his wonders." 
Psalms78:33 God ended their days abruptly, their years in sudden death." 
Psalms78:34 When he slew them, they began to seek him; they again inquired of their God." 
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock, God Most High, their redeemer." 
Psalms78:36 But they deceived him with their mouths, lied to him with their tongues." 
Psalms78:37 Their hearts were not constant toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant. 
Psalms78:38 But God is merciful and forgave their sin; he did not utterly destroy them. Time and again he turned back his anger, unwilling to unleash all his rage." 
Psalms78:39 He was mindful that they were flesh, a breath that passes and does not return." 
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against God in the desert, grieved him in the wasteland." 
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God, provoked the Holy One of Israel." 
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power, the day he redeemed them from the foe," 
Psalms78:43 When he displayed his wonders in Egypt, his marvels in the plain of Zoan." 
Psalms78:44 God changed their rivers to blood; their streams they could not drink. 
Psalms78:45 He sent insects that devoured them, frogs that destroyed them." 
Psalms78:46 He gave their harvest to the caterpillar, the fruits of their labor to the locust." 
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hail, their sycamores with frost." 
Psalms78:48 He exposed their flocks to deadly hail, their cattle to lightning." 
Psalms78:49 He unleashed against them his fiery breath, roar, fury, and distress, storming messengers of death." 
Psalms78:50 He cleared a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death; he delivered their beasts to the plague. 
Psalms78:51 He struck all the firstborn of Egypt, love's first child in the tents of Ham." 
Psalms78:52 God led forth his people like sheep; he guided them through the desert like a flock. 
Psalms78:53 He led them on secure and unafraid, but the sea enveloped their enemies." 
Psalms78:54 He brought them to his holy land, the mountain his right hand had won." 
Psalms78:55 God drove out the nations before them, apportioned them a heritage by lot, settled the tribes of Israel in their tents." 
Psalms78:56 But they tested, rebelled against God Most High, his decrees they did not observe." 
Psalms78:57 They turned back, deceitful like their ancestors; they proved false like a bow with no tension." 
Psalms78:58 They enraged him with their high places; with their idols they goaded him. 
Psalms78:59 God heard and grew angry; he rejected Israel completely. 
Psalms78:60 He forsook the shrine at Shiloh, the tent where he dwelt with humans." 
Psalms78:61 He gave up his might into captivity, his glorious ark into the hands of the foe." 
Psalms78:62 God abandoned his people to the sword; he was enraged against his heritage. 
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed their young men; their young women heard no wedding songs. 
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword; their widows made no lamentation. 
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep, like a warrior from the effects of wine." 
Psalms78:66 He put his enemies to flight; everlasting shame he dealt them. 
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph, chose not the tribe of Ephraim." 
Psalms78:68 God chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he favored." 
Psalms78:69 He built his shrine like the heavens, like the earth which he founded forever." 
Psalms78:70 He chose David his servant, took him from the sheepfold." 
Psalms78:71 From tending sheep God brought him, to shepherd Jacob, his people, Israel, his heritage." 
Psalms78:72 He shepherded them with a pure heart; with skilled hands he guided them. 
Psalms79:1 A psalm of Asaph. I O God, the nations have invaded your heritage; they have defiled your holy temple, have laid Jerusalem in ruins." 
Psalms79:2 They have left the corpses of your servants as food for the birds of the heavens, the flesh of your faithful for the beasts of the earth." 
Psalms79:3 They have spilled their blood like water all around Jerusalem, and no one is left to bury them." 
Psalms79:4 We have become the reproach of our neighbors, the scorn and derision of those around us." 
Psalms79:5 How long, LORD? Will you be angry forever? Will your rage keep burning like fire?" 
Psalms79:6 Pour out your wrath on nations that reject you, on kingdoms that do not call on your name," 
Psalms79:7 For they have devoured Jacob, laid waste his home." 
Psalms79:8 Do not hold past iniquities against us; may your compassion come quickly, for we have been brought very low." 
Psalms79:9 Help us, God our savior, for the glory of your name. Deliver us, pardon our sins for your name's sake." 
Psalms79:10 Why should the nations say, ""Where is their God?"" Before our eyes make clear to the nations that you avenge the blood of your servants." 
Psalms79:11 Let the groans of prisoners come before you; by your great power free those doomed to death. 
Psalms79:12 Lord, inflict on our neighbors seven fold the disgrace they inflicted on you." 
Psalms79:13 Then we, your people, the sheep of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; through all ages we will declare your praise." 
Psalms80:1 For the leader; according to Lilies. Eduth. A psalm of Asaph. 
Psalms80:2 Shepherd of Israel, listen, guide of the flock of Joseph! From your throne upon the cherubim reveal yourself" 
Psalms80:3 to Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh. Stir up your power, come to save us." 
Psalms80:4 O LORD of hosts, restore us; Let your face shine upon us, that we may be saved." 
Psalms80:5 LORD of hosts, how long will you burn with anger while your people pray?" 
Psalms80:6 You have fed them the bread of tears, made them drink tears in abundance." 
Psalms80:7 You have left us to be fought over by our neighbors; our enemies deride us. 
Psalms80:8 O LORD of hosts, restore us; let your face shine upon us, that we may be saved." 
Psalms80:9 You brought a vine out of Egypt; you drove away the nations and planted it. 
Psalms80:10 You cleared the ground; it took root and filled the land. 
Psalms80:11 The mountains were covered by its shadow, the cedars of God by its branches." 
Psalms80:12 It sent out boughs as far as the sea, shoots as far as the river." 
Psalms80:13 Why have you broken down the walls, so that all who pass by pluck its fruit?" 
Psalms80:14 The boar from the forest strips the vine; the beast of the field feeds upon it. 
Psalms80:15 Turn again, LORD of hosts; look down from heaven and see; Attend to this vine," 
Psalms80:16 the shoot your right hand has planted. 
Psalms80:17 Those who would burn or cut it down, may they perish at your rebuke. 
Psalms80:18 May your help be with the man at your right hand, with the one whom you once made strong." 
Psalms80:19 Then we will not withdraw from you; revive us, and we will call on your name." 
Psalms80:20 LORD of hosts, restore us; let your face shine upon us, that we may be saved." 
Psalms81:1 For the leader; upon the gittith. Of Asaph. 
Psalms81:2 Sing joyfully to God our strength; shout in triumph to the God of Jacob! 
Psalms81:3 Take up a melody, sound the timbrel, the sweet-sounding harp and lyre." 
Psalms81:4 Blow the trumpet at the new moon, at the full moon, on our solemn feast." 
Psalms81:5 For this is a law in Israel, an edict of the God of Jacob," 
Psalms81:6 Who made it a decree for Joseph when he came out of the land of Egypt. II I hear a new oracle: 
Psalms81:7 I relieved their shoulders of the burden; their hands put down the basket. 
Psalms81:8 In distress you called and I rescued you; unseen, I spoke to you in thunder; At the waters of Meribah I tested you and said: Selah" 
Psalms81:9 'Listen, my people, I give you warning! If only you will obey me, Israel!" 
Psalms81:10 There must be no foreign god among you; you must not worship an alien god. 
Psalms81:11 I, the LORD, am your God, who brought you up from the land of Egypt. Open wide your mouth that I may fill it.'" 
Psalms81:12 But my people did not listen to my words; Israel did not obey me. 
Psalms81:13 So I gave them over to hardness of heart; they followed their own designs. 
Psalms81:14 But even now if my people would listen, if Israel would walk in my paths," 
Psalms81:15 In a moment I would subdue their foes, against their enemies unleash my hand." 
Psalms81:16 Those who hate the LORD would tremble, their doom sealed forever." 
Psalms81:17 But Israel I would feed with the finest wheat, satisfy them with honey from the rock.""" 
Psalms82:1 A psalm of Asaph. I God rises in the divine council, gives judgment in the midst of the gods." 
Psalms82:2 How long will you judge unjustly and favor the cause of the wicked? Selah 
Psalms82:3 Defend the lowly and fatherless; render justice to the afflicted and needy. 
Psalms82:4 Rescue the lowly and poor; deliver them from the hand of the wicked. 
Psalms82:5 The gods neither know nor understand, wandering about in darkness, and all the world's foundations shake." 
Psalms82:6 I declare: Gods though you be, offspring of the Most High all of you," 
Psalms82:7 Yet like any mortal you shall die; like any prince you shall fall. 
Psalms82:8 Arise, O God, judge the earth, for yours are all the nations." 
Psalms83:1 A song; a psalm of Asaph. 
Psalms83:2 God, do not be silent; God, be not still and unmoved!" 
Psalms83:3 See how your enemies rage; your foes proudly raise their heads. 
Psalms83:4 They conspire against your people, plot against those you protect." 
Psalms83:5 They say, ""Come, let us wipe out their nation; let Israel's name be mentioned no more!""" 
Psalms83:6 They scheme with one mind, in league against you:" 
Psalms83:7 The tents of Ishmael and Edom, the people of Moab and Hagar," 
Psalms83:8 Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia and the inhabitants of Tyre." 
Psalms83:9 Assyria, too, in league with them gives aid to the descendants of Lot. Selah" 
Psalms83:10 Deal with them as with Midian; as with Sisera and Jabin at the torrent Kishon," 
Psalms83:11 Those destroyed at Endor, who became dung for the ground." 
Psalms83:12 Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna," 
Psalms83:13 Who made a plan together, ""Let us seize the pastures of God.""" 
Psalms83:14 My God, turn them into withered grass, into chaff flying before the wind." 
Psalms83:15 As a fire raging through a forest, a flame setting mountains ablaze," 
Psalms83:16 Pursue them with your tempest; terrify them with your storm. 
Psalms83:17 Cover their faces with shame, till they pay you homage, LORD." 
Psalms83:18 Let them be dismayed and shamed forever; let them perish in disgrace. 
Psalms83:19 Show them you alone are the LORD, the Most High over all the earth." 
Psalms84:1 For the leader; upon the gittith. A psalm of the Korahites. 
Psalms84:2 How lovely your dwelling, O LORD of hosts!" 
Psalms84:3 My soul yearns and pines for the courts of the LORD. My heart and flesh cry out for the living God. 
Psalms84:4 As the sparrow finds a home and the swallow a nest to settle her young, My home is by your altars, LORD of hosts, my king and my God!" 
Psalms84:5 Happy are those who dwell in your house! They never cease to praise you. Selah 
Psalms84:6 Happy are those who find refuge in you, whose hearts are set on pilgrim roads." 
Psalms84:7 As they pass through the Baca valley, they find spring water to drink. Also from pools the Lord provides water for those who lose their way." 
Psalms84:8 They pass through outer and inner wall and see the God of gods on Zion. 
Psalms84:9 LORD of hosts, hear my prayer; listen, God of Jacob. Selah" 
Psalms84:10 O God, look kindly on our shield; look upon the face of your anointed." 
Psalms84:11 Better one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere. Better the threshold of the house of my God than a home in the tents of the wicked. 
Psalms84:12 For a sun and shield is the LORD God, bestowing all grace and glory. The LORD withholds no good thing from those who walk without reproach." 
Psalms84:13 O LORD of hosts, happy are those who trust in you!" 
Psalms85:1 For the leader. A psalm of the Korahites. 
Psalms85:2 You once favored, LORD, your land, restored the good fortune of Jacob." 
Psalms85:3 You forgave the guilt of your people, pardoned all their sins. Selah" 
Psalms85:4 You withdrew all your wrath, turned back your burning anger." 
Psalms85:5 Restore us once more, God our savior; abandon your wrath against us." 
Psalms85:6 Will you be angry with us forever, drag out your anger for all generations?" 
Psalms85:7 Please give us life again, that your people may rejoice in you." 
Psalms85:8 Show us, LORD, your love; grant us your salvation." 
Psalms85:9 I will listen for the word of God; surely the LORD will proclaim peace To his people, to the faithful, to those who trust in him." 
Psalms85:10 Near indeed is salvation for the loyal; prosperity will fill our land. 
Psalms85:11 Love and truth will meet; justice and peace will kiss. 
Psalms85:12 Truth will spring from the earth; justice will look down from heaven. 
Psalms85:13 The LORD will surely grant abundance; our land will yield its increase. 
Psalms85:14 Prosperity will march before the Lord, and good fortune will follow behind." 
Psalms86:1 A prayer of David. I Hear me, LORD, and answer me, for I am poor and oppressed." 
Psalms86:2 Preserve my life, for I am loyal; save your servant who trusts in you." 
Psalms86:3 You are my God; pity me, Lord; to you I call all the day." 
Psalms86:4 Gladden the soul of your servant; to you, Lord, I lift up my soul." 
Psalms86:5 Lord, you are kind and forgiving, most loving to all who call on you." 
Psalms86:6 LORD, hear my prayer; listen to my cry for help." 
Psalms86:7 In this time of trouble I call, for you will answer me." 
Psalms86:8 None among the gods can equal you, O Lord; nor can their deeds compare to yours." 
Psalms86:9 All the nations you have made shall come to bow before you, Lord, and give honor to your name." 
Psalms86:10 For you are great and do wondrous deeds; and you alone are God. 
Psalms86:11 Teach me, LORD, your way that I may walk in your truth, single-hearted and revering your name." 
Psalms86:12 I will praise you with all my heart, glorify your name forever, Lord my God." 
Psalms86:13 Your love for me is great; you have rescued me from the depths of Sheol. 
Psalms86:14 O God, the arrogant have risen against me; a ruthless band has sought my life; to you they pay no heed." 
Psalms86:15 But you, Lord, are a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger, most loving and true." 
Psalms86:16 Turn to me, have pity on me; give your strength to your servant; save this child of your handmaid." 
Psalms86:17 Give me a sign of your favor: make my enemies see, to their confusion, that you, LORD, help and comfort me." 
Psalms87:1 A psalm of the Korahites. A song. I The LORD loves the city founded on holy mountains," 
Psalms87:2 Loves the gates of Zion more than any dwelling in Jacob. 
Psalms87:3 Glorious things are said of you, O city of God! Selah" 
Psalms87:4 From Babylon and Egypt I count those who acknowledge the LORD. Philistia, Ethiopia, Tyre, of them it can be said: ""This one was born there.""" 
Psalms87:5 But of Zion it must be said: They all were born here. The Most High confirms this; 
Psalms87:6 the Lord notes in the register of the peoples: This one was born here. Selah 
Psalms87:7 So all sing in their festive dance: Within you is my true home. 
Psalms88:1 A song; a psalm of the Korahites. For the leader; according to Mahalath. For singing; a maskil of Heman the Ezrahite. 
Psalms88:1 A song; a psalm of the Korahites. For the leader; according to Mahalath. For singing; a maskil of Heman the Ezrahite. 
Psalms88:2 LORD, my God, I call out by day; at night I cry aloud in your presence." 
Psalms88:3 Let my prayer come before you; incline your ear to my cry. 
Psalms88:4 For my soul is filled with troubles; my life draws near to Sheol. 
Psalms88:5 I am reckoned with those who go down to the pit; I am weak, without strength." 
Psalms88:6 My couch is among the dead, with the slain who lie in the grave. You remember them no more; they are cut off from your care." 
Psalms88:7 You plunged me into the bottom of the pit, into the darkness of the abyss." 
Psalms88:8 Your wrath lies heavy upon me; all your waves crash over me. Selah 
Psalms88:9 Because of you my friends shun me; you make me loathsome to them; Caged in, I cannot escape;" 
Psalms88:10 my eyes grow dim from trouble. II All day I call on you, LORD; I stretch out my hands to you." 
Psalms88:11 Do you work wonders for the dead? Do the shades arise and praise you? Selah 
Psalms88:12 Is your love proclaimed in the grave, your fidelity in the tomb?" 
Psalms88:13 Are your marvels declared in the darkness, your righteous deeds in the land of oblivion?" 
Psalms88:14 But I cry out to you, LORD; in the morning my prayer comes before you." 
Psalms88:15 Why do you reject me, LORD? Why hide your face from me?" 
Psalms88:16 I am mortally afflicted since youth; lifeless, I suffer your terrible blows." 
Psalms88:17 Your wrath has swept over me; your terrors have reduced me to silence. 
Psalms88:18 All the day they surge round like a flood; from every side they close in on me. 
Psalms88:19 Because of you companions shun me; my only friend is darkness. 
Psalms89:1 A maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite. 
Psalms89:1 A maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite. 
Psalms89:2 The promises of the LORD I will sing forever, proclaim your loyalty through all ages." 
Psalms89:3 For you said, ""My love is established forever; my loyalty will stand as long as the heavens." 
Psalms89:4 I have made a covenant with my chosen one; I have sworn to David my servant: 
Psalms89:5 I will make your dynasty stand forever and establish your throne through all ages. Selah 
Psalms89:6 The heavens praise your marvels, LORD, your loyalty in the assembly of the holy ones." 
Psalms89:7 Who in the skies ranks with the LORD? Who is like the LORD among the gods? 
Psalms89:8 A God dreaded in the council of the holy ones, greater and more awesome than all who sit there!" 
Psalms89:9 LORD, God of hosts, who is like you? Mighty LORD, your loyalty is always present." 
Psalms89:10 You rule the raging sea; you still its swelling waves. 
Psalms89:11 You crushed Rahab with a mortal blow; your strong arm scattered your foes. 
Psalms89:12 Yours are the heavens, yours the earth; you founded the world and everything in it." 
Psalms89:13 Zaphon and Amanus you created; Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name. 
Psalms89:14 Mighty your arm, strong your hand, your right hand is ever exalted." 
Psalms89:15 Justice and judgment are the foundation of your throne; love and loyalty march before you. 
Psalms89:16 Happy the people who know you, LORD, who walk in the radiance of your face." 
Psalms89:17 In your name they sing joyfully all the day; at your victory they raise the festal shout. 
Psalms89:18 You are their majestic strength; by your favor our horn is exalted. 
Psalms89:19 Truly the LORD is our shield, the Holy One of Israel, our king!" 
Psalms89:20 Once you spoke in vision; to your faithful ones you said: I have set a leader over the warriors; I have raised up a hero from the army. 
Psalms89:21 I have chosen David, my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him." 
Psalms89:22 My hand will be with him; my arm will make him strong. 
Psalms89:23 No enemy shall outwit him, nor shall the wicked defeat him." 
Psalms89:24 I will crush his foes before him, strike down those who hate him." 
Psalms89:25 My loyalty and love will be with him; through my name his horn will be exalted. 
Psalms89:26 I will set his hand upon the sea, his right hand upon the rivers." 
Psalms89:27 He shall cry to me,'You are my father, my God, the Rock that brings me victory!'" 
Psalms89:28 I myself make him firstborn, Most High over the kings of the earth." 
Psalms89:29 Forever I will maintain my love for him; my covenant with him stands firm. 
Psalms89:30 I will establish his dynasty forever, his throne as the days of the heavens." 
Psalms89:31 If his descendants forsake my law, do not follow my decrees," 
Psalms89:32 If they fail to observe my statutes, do not keep my commandments," 
Psalms89:33 I will punish their crime with a rod and their guilt with lashes. 
Psalms89:34 But I will not take my love from him, nor will I betray my bond of loyalty." 
Psalms89:35 I will not violate my covenant; the promise of my lips I will not alter. 
Psalms89:36 By my holiness I swore once for all: I will never be false to David. 
Psalms89:37 His dynasty will continue forever, his throne, like the sun before me." 
Psalms89:38 Like the moon it will stand eternal, forever firm like the sky!"" Selah" 
Psalms89:39 But now you have rejected and spurned, been enraged at your anointed." 
Psalms89:40 You renounced the covenant with your servant, defiled his crown in the dust." 
Psalms89:41 You broke down all his defenses, left his strongholds in ruins." 
Psalms89:42 All who pass through seize plunder; his neighbors deride him. 
Psalms89:43 You have exalted the right hand of his foes, have gladdened all his enemies." 
Psalms89:44 You turned back his sharp sword, did not support him in battle." 
Psalms89:45 You brought to an end his splendor, hurled his throne to the ground." 
Psalms89:46 You cut short the days of his youth, covered him with shame. Selah" 
Psalms89:47 How long, LORD? Will you stay hidden forever? Must your wrath smolder like fire?" 
Psalms89:48 Remember how brief is my life, how frail the race you created!" 
Psalms89:49 What mortal can live and not see death? Who can escape the power of Sheol? Selah 
Psalms89:50 Where are your promises of old, Lord, the loyalty sworn to David?" 
Psalms89:51 Remember, Lord, the insults to your servants, how I bear all the slanders of the nations." 
Psalms89:52 Your enemies, LORD, insult your anointed; they insult my every endeavor." 
Psalms89:53 Blessed be the LORD forever! Amen and amen! 
Psalms90:1 A prayer of Moses, the man of God. I Lord, you have been our refuge through all generations." 
Psalms90:2 Before the mountains were born, the earth and the world brought forth, from eternity to eternity you are God." 
Psalms90:3 But humans you return to dust, saying, ""Return, you mortals!""" 
Psalms90:4 A thousand years in your eyes are merely a yesterday, Before a watch passes in the night," 
Psalms90:5 you have brought them to their end; They disappear like sleep at dawn; they are like grass that dies. 
Psalms90:6 It sprouts green in the morning; by evening it is dry and withered. 
Psalms90:7 Truly we are consumed by your anger, filled with terror by your wrath." 
Psalms90:8 You have kept our faults before you, our hidden sins exposed to your sight." 
Psalms90:9 Our life ebbs away under your wrath; our years end like a sigh. 
Psalms90:10 Seventy is the sum of our years, or eighty, if we are strong; Most of them are sorrow and toil; they pass quickly, we are all but gone." 
Psalms90:11 Who comprehends your terrible anger? Your wrath matches the fear it inspires. 
Psalms90:12 Teach us to count our days aright, that we may gain wisdom of heart." 
Psalms90:13 Relent, O LORD! How long? Have pity on your servants!" 
Psalms90:14 Fill us at daybreak with your love, that all our days we may sing for joy." 
Psalms90:15 Make us glad as many days as you humbled us, for as many years as we have seen trouble." 
Psalms90:16 Show your deeds to your servants, your glory to their children." 
Psalms90:17 May the favor of the Lord our God be ours. Prosper the work of our hands! Prosper the work of our hands! 
Psalms91:1 I You who dwell in the shelter of the Most High, who abide in the shadow of the Almighty," 
Psalms91:2 Say to the LORD, ""My refuge and fortress, my God in whom I trust.""" 
Psalms91:3 God will rescue you from the fowler's snare, from the destroying plague," 
Psalms91:4 Will shelter you with pinions, spread wings that you may take refuge; God's faithfulness is a protecting shield." 
Psalms91:5 You shall not fear the terror of the night nor the arrow that flies by day," 
Psalms91:6 Nor the pestilence that roams in darkness, nor the plague that ravages at noon." 
Psalms91:7 Though a thousand fall at your side, ten thousand at your right hand, near you it shall not come." 
Psalms91:8 You need simply watch; the punishment of the wicked you will see. 
Psalms91:9 You have the LORD for your refuge; you have made the Most High your stronghold. 
Psalms91:10 No evil shall befall you, no affliction come near your tent." 
Psalms91:11 For God commands the angels to guard you in all your ways. 
Psalms91:12 With their hands they shall support you, lest you strike your foot against a stone." 
Psalms91:13 You shall tread upon the asp and the viper, trample the lion and the dragon." 
Psalms91:14 Whoever clings to me I will deliver; whoever knows my name I will set on high. 
Psalms91:15 All who call upon me I will answer; I will be with them in distress; I will deliver them and give them honor. 
Psalms91:16 With length of days I will satisfy them and show them my saving power. 
Psalms92:1 A psalm. A sabbath song. 
Psalms92:2 It is good to give thanks to the LORD, to sing praise to your name, Most High," 
Psalms92:3 To proclaim your love in the morning, your faithfulness in the night," 
Psalms92:4 With the ten-stringed harp, with melody upon the lyre." 
Psalms92:5 For you make me jubilant, LORD, by your deeds; at the works of your hands I shout for joy." 
Psalms92:6 How great are your works, LORD! How profound your purpose!" 
Psalms92:7 A senseless person cannot know this; a fool cannot comprehend. 
Psalms92:8 Though the wicked flourish like grass and all sinners thrive, They are destined for eternal destruction;" 
Psalms92:9 for you, LORD, are forever on high." 
Psalms92:10 Indeed your enemies, LORD, indeed your enemies shall perish; all sinners shall be scattered." 
Psalms92:11 You have given me the strength of a wild bull; you have poured rich oil upon me. 
Psalms92:12 My eyes look with glee on my wicked enemies; my ears delight in the fall of my foes. 
Psalms92:13 The just shall flourish like the palm tree, shall grow like a cedar of Lebanon." 
Psalms92:14 Planted in the house of the LORD, they shall flourish in the courts of our God." 
Psalms92:15 They shall bear fruit even in old age, always vigorous and sturdy," 
Psalms92:16 As they proclaim: The LORD is just; our rock, in whom there is no wrong.""" 
Psalms93:1 The LORD is king, robed with majesty; the LORD is robed, girded with might. The world will surely stand in place, never to be moved." 
Psalms93:2 Your throne stands firm from of old; you are from everlasting, LORD." 
Psalms93:3 The flood has raised up, LORD; the flood has raised up its roar; the flood has raised its pounding waves." 
Psalms93:4 More powerful than the roar of many waters, more powerful than the breakers of the sea, powerful in the heavens is the LORD." 
Psalms93:5 Your decrees are firmly established; holiness belongs to your house, LORD, for all the length of days." 
Psalms94:1 LORD, avenging God, avenging God, shine forth!" 
Psalms94:2 Rise up, judge of the earth; give the proud what they deserve." 
Psalms94:3 How long, LORD, shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked glory?" 
Psalms94:4 How long will they mouth haughty speeches, go on boasting, all these evildoers?" 
Psalms94:5 They crush your people, LORD, torment your very own." 
Psalms94:6 They kill the widow and alien; the fatherless they murder. 
Psalms94:7 They say, ""The LORD does not see; the God of Jacob takes no notice.""" 
Psalms94:8 Understand, you stupid people! You fools, when will you be wise?" 
Psalms94:9 Does the one who shaped the ear not hear? The one who formed the eye not see? 
Psalms94:10 Does the one who guides nations not rebuke? The one who teaches humans not have knowledge? 
Psalms94:11 The LORD does know human plans; they are only puffs of air. 
Psalms94:12 Happy those whom you guide, LORD, whom you teach by your instruction." 
Psalms94:13 You give them rest from evil days, while a pit is being dug for the wicked." 
Psalms94:14 You, LORD, will not forsake your people, nor abandon your very own." 
Psalms94:15 Judgment shall again be just, and all the upright of heart will follow it." 
Psalms94:16 Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against evildoers? 
Psalms94:17 If the LORD were not my help, I would long have been silent in the grave." 
Psalms94:18 When I say, ""My foot is slipping,"" your love, LORD, holds me up." 
Psalms94:19 When cares increase within me, your comfort gives me joy." 
Psalms94:20 Can unjust judges be your allies, those who create burdens in the name of law," 
Psalms94:21 Those who conspire against the just and condemn the innocent to death? 
Psalms94:22 No, the LORD is my secure height, my God, the rock where I find refuge," 
Psalms94:23 Who will turn back their evil upon them and destroy them for their wickedness. Surely the LORD our God will destroy them! 
Psalms95:1 Come, let us sing joyfully to the LORD; cry out to the rock of our salvation." 
Psalms95:2 Let us greet him with a song of praise, joyfully sing out our psalms." 
Psalms95:3 For the LORD is the great God, the great king over all gods," 
Psalms95:4 Whose hand holds the depths of the earth; who owns the tops of the mountains. 
Psalms95:5 The sea and dry land belong to God, who made them, formed them by hand." 
Psalms95:6 Enter, let us bow down in worship; let us kneel before the LORD who made us." 
Psalms95:7 For this is our God, whose people we are, God's well-tended flock. III Oh, that today you would hear his voice:" 
Psalms95:8 Do not harden your hearts as at Meribah, as on the day of Massah in the desert." 
Psalms95:9 There your ancestors tested me; they tried me though they had seen my works. 
Psalms95:10 Forty years I loathed that generation; I said: This people's heart goes astray; they do not know my ways. 
Psalms95:11 Therefore I swore in my anger: They shall never enter my rest.

Psalms96:4 For great is the LORD and highly to be praised, to be feared above all gods." 
Psalms96:5 For the gods of the nations all do nothing, but the LORD made the heavens." 
Psalms96:6 Splendor and power go before him; power and grandeur are in his holy place.

Psalms96:10 say among the nations: The LORD is king. The world will surely stand fast, never to be moved. God rules the peoples with fairness."

Psalms97:3 Fire goes before him; everywhere it consumes the foes. 
Psalms97:4 Lightning illumines the world; the earth sees and trembles. 
Psalms97:5 The mountains melt like wax before the LORD, before the Lord of all the earth." 
Psalms97:6 The heavens proclaim God's justice; all peoples see his glory.

Psalms97:8 Zion hears and is glad, and the cities of Judah rejoice because of your judgments, O LORD."
