Psalms101:1 A psalm of David. I I sing of love and justice; to you, LORD, I sing praise." 
Psalms101:2 I follow the way of integrity; when will you come to me? I act with integrity of heart within my royal court. 
Psalms101:3 I do not allow into my presence anyone who speaks perversely. Whoever acts shamefully I hate; no such person can be my friend. 
Psalms101:4 I shun the devious of heart; the wicked I do not tolerate. 
Psalms101:5 Whoever slanders another in secret I reduce to silence. Haughty eyes and arrogant hearts I cannot endure. 
Psalms101:6 I look to the faithful of the land; they alone can be my companions. Those who follow the way of integrity, they alone can enter my service." 
Psalms101:7 No one who practices deceit can hold a post in my court. No one who speaks falsely can be among my advisors. 
Psalms101:8 Each morning I clear the wicked from the land, and rid the LORD'S city of all evildoers." 
Psalms102:1 The prayer of one afflicted and wasting away whose anguish is poured out before the LORD. 
Psalms102:2 LORD, hear my prayer; let my cry come to you." 
Psalms102:3 Do not hide your face from me now that I am in distress. Turn your ear to me; when I call, answer me quickly." 
Psalms102:4 For my days vanish like smoke; my bones burn away as in a furnace. 
Psalms102:5 I am withered, dried up like grass, too wasted to eat my food." 
Psalms102:6 From my loud groaning I become just skin and bones. 
Psalms102:7 I am like a desert owl, like an owl among the ruins." 
Psalms102:8 I lie awake and moan, like a lone sparrow on the roof." 
Psalms102:9 All day long my enemies taunt me; in their rage, they make my name a curse." 
Psalms102:10 I eat ashes like bread, mingle my drink with tears." 
Psalms102:11 Because of your furious wrath, you lifted me up just to cast me down." 
Psalms102:12 My days are like a lengthening shadow; I wither like the grass. 
Psalms102:13 But you, LORD, are enthroned forever; your renown is for all generations." 
Psalms102:14 You will again show mercy to Zion; now is the time for pity; the appointed time has come. 
Psalms102:15 Its stones are dear to your servants; its dust moves them to pity. 
Psalms102:16 The nations shall revere your name, LORD, all the kings of the earth, your glory," 
Psalms102:17 Once the LORD has rebuilt Zion and appeared in glory," 
Psalms102:18 Heeding the plea of the lowly, not scorning their prayer." 
Psalms102:19 Let this be written for the next generation, for a people not yet born, that they may praise the LORD:" 
Psalms102:20 The LORD looked down from the holy heights, viewed the earth from heaven," 
Psalms102:21 To attend to the groaning of the prisoners, to release those doomed to die.""" 
Psalms102:22 Then the LORD'S name will be declared on Zion, the praise of God in Jerusalem," 
Psalms102:23 When all peoples and kingdoms gather to worship the LORD. 
Psalms102:24 God has shattered my strength in mid-course, has cut short my days." 
Psalms102:25 I plead, O my God, do not take me in the midst of my days. Your years last through all generations." 
Psalms102:26 Of old you laid the earth's foundations; the heavens are the work of your hands. 
Psalms102:27 They perish, but you remain; they all wear out like a garment; Like clothing you change them and they are changed," 
Psalms102:28 but you are the same, your years have no end." 
Psalms102:29 May the children of your servants live on; may their descendants live in your presence. 
Psalms103:1 Of David. I Bless the LORD, my soul; all my being, bless his holy name!" 
Psalms103:2 Bless the LORD, my soul; do not forget all the gifts of God," 
Psalms103:3 Who pardons all your sins, heals all your ills," 
Psalms103:4 Delivers your life from the pit, surrounds you with love and compassion," 
Psalms103:5 Fills your days with good things; your youth is renewed like the eagle's. 
Psalms103:6 The LORD does righteous deeds, brings justice to all the oppressed." 
Psalms103:7 His ways were revealed to Moses, mighty deeds to the people of Israel." 
Psalms103:8 Merciful and gracious is the LORD, slow to anger, abounding in kindness." 
Psalms103:9 God does not always rebuke, nurses no lasting anger," 
Psalms103:10 Has not dealt with us as our sins merit, nor requited us as our deeds deserve." 
Psalms103:11 As the heavens tower over the earth, so God's love towers over the faithful." 
Psalms103:12 As far as the east is from the west, so far have our sins been removed from us." 
Psalms103:13 As a father has compassion on his children, so the LORD has compassion on the faithful." 
Psalms103:14 For he knows how we are formed, remembers that we are dust." 
Psalms103:15 Our days are like the grass; like flowers of the field we blossom. 
Psalms103:16 The wind sweeps over us and we are gone; our place knows us no more. 
Psalms103:17 But the LORD'S kindness is forever, toward the faithful from age to age. He favors the children's children" 
Psalms103:18 of those who keep his covenant, who take care to fulfill its precepts." 
Psalms103:19 The LORD'S throne is established in heaven; God's royal power rules over all. 
Psalms103:20 Bless the LORD, all you angels, mighty in strength and attentive, obedient to every command." 
Psalms103:21 Bless the LORD, all you hosts, ministers who do God's will." 
Psalms103:22 Bless the LORD, all creatures, everywhere in God's domain. Bless the LORD, my soul!" 
Psalms104:1 Bless the LORD, my soul! LORD, my God, you are great indeed! You are clothed with majesty and glory," 
Psalms104:2 robed in light as with a cloak. You spread out the heavens like a tent; 
Psalms104:3 you raised your palace upon the waters. You make the clouds your chariot; you travel on the wings of the wind. 
Psalms104:4 You make the winds your messengers; flaming fire, your ministers." 
Psalms104:5 You fixed the earth on its foundation, never to be moved." 
Psalms104:6 The ocean covered it like a garment; above the mountains stood the waters. 
Psalms104:7 At your roar they took flight; at the sound of your thunder they fled. 
Psalms104:8 They rushed up the mountains, down the valleys to the place you had fixed for them." 
Psalms104:9 You set a limit they cannot pass; never again will they cover the earth. 
Psalms104:10 You made springs flow into channels that wind among the mountains. 
Psalms104:11 They give drink to every beast of the field; here wild asses quench their thirst. 
Psalms104:12 Beside them the birds of heaven nest; among the branches they sing. 
Psalms104:13 You water the mountains from your palace; by your labor the earth abounds. 
Psalms104:14 You raise grass for the cattle and plants for our beasts of burden. You bring bread from the earth," 
Psalms104:15 and wine to gladden our hearts, Oil to make our faces gleam, food to build our strength." 
Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD drink their fill, the cedars of Lebanon, which you planted." 
Psalms104:17 There the birds build their nests; junipers are the home of the stork. 
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for wild goats; the rocky cliffs, a refuge for badgers." 
Psalms104:19 You made the moon to mark the seasons, the sun that knows the hour of its setting." 
Psalms104:20 You bring darkness and night falls, then all the beasts of the forest roam abroad." 
Psalms104:21 Young lions roar for prey; they seek their food from God. 
Psalms104:22 When the sun rises, they steal away and rest in their dens." 
Psalms104:23 People go forth to their work, to their labor till evening falls." 
Psalms104:24 How varied are your works, LORD! In wisdom you have wrought them all; the earth is full of your creatures." 
Psalms104:25 Look at the sea, great and wide! It teems with countless beings, living things both large and small." 
Psalms104:26 Here ships ply their course; here Leviathan, your creature, plays." 
Psalms104:27 All of these look to you to give them food in due time. 
Psalms104:28 When you give to them, they gather; when you open your hand, they are well filled." 
Psalms104:29 When you hide your face, they are lost. When you take away their breath, they perish and return to the dust from which they came." 
Psalms104:30 When you send forth your breath, they are created, and you renew the face of the earth." 
Psalms104:31 May the glory of the LORD endure forever; may the LORD be glad in these works! 
Psalms104:32 If God glares at the earth, it trembles; if God touches the mountains, they smoke!" 
Psalms104:33 I will sing to the LORD all my life; I will sing praise to my God while I live. 
Psalms104:34 May my theme be pleasing to God; I will rejoice in the LORD. 
Psalms104:35 May sinners vanish from the earth, and the wicked be no more. Bless the LORD, my soul! Hallelujah!" 
Psalms105:1 Give thanks to the LORD, invoke his name; make known among the peoples his deeds!" 
Psalms105:2 Sing praise, play music; proclaim all his wondrous deeds!" 
Psalms105:3 Glory in his holy name; rejoice, O hearts that seek the LORD!" 
Psalms105:4 Rely on the mighty LORD; constantly seek his face. 
Psalms105:5 Recall the wondrous deeds he has done, his signs and his words of judgment," 
Psalms105:6 You descendants of Abraham his servant, offspring of Jacob the chosen one!" 
Psalms105:7 The LORD is our God who rules the whole earth. 
Psalms105:8 He remembers forever his covenant, the pact imposed for a thousand generations," 
Psalms105:9 Which was made with Abraham, confirmed by oath to Isaac," 
Psalms105:10 And ratified as binding for Jacob, an everlasting covenant for Israel:" 
Psalms105:11 To you I give the land of Canaan, your own allotted heritage.""" 
Psalms105:12 When they were few in number, a handful, and strangers there," 
Psalms105:13 Wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another," 
Psalms105:14 He let no one oppress them; for their sake he rebuked kings: 
Psalms105:15  Do not touch my anointed, to my prophets do no harm.""" 
Psalms105:16 Then he called down a famine on the land, destroyed the grain that sustained them." 
Psalms105:17 He had sent a man ahead of them, Joseph, sold as a slave." 
Psalms105:18 They shackled his feet with chains; collared his neck in iron," 
Psalms105:19 Till his prediction came to pass, and the word of the LORD proved him true." 
Psalms105:20 The king sent and released him; the ruler of peoples set him free. 
Psalms105:21 He made him lord over his palace, ruler over all his possessions," 
Psalms105:22 To instruct his princes by his word, to teach his elders wisdom." 
Psalms105:23 Then Israel entered Egypt; Jacob lived in the land of Ham. 
Psalms105:24 God greatly increased his people, made them too many for their foes." 
Psalms105:25 He turned their hearts to hate his people, to treat his servants unfairly." 
Psalms105:26 He sent his servant Moses, Aaron whom he had chosen." 
Psalms105:27 They worked his signs in Egypt and wonders in the land of Ham. 
Psalms105:28 He sent darkness and it grew dark, but they rebelled against his word." 
Psalms105:29 He turned their waters into blood and killed all their fish. 
Psalms105:30 Their land swarmed with frogs, even the chambers of their kings." 
Psalms105:31 He spoke and there came swarms of flies, gnats through all their country." 
Psalms105:32 For rain he gave them hail, flashes of lightning throughout their land." 
Psalms105:33 He struck down their vines and fig trees, shattered the trees of their country." 
Psalms105:34 He spoke and the locusts came, grass hoppers without number." 
Psalms105:35 They devoured every plant in the land; they ravaged the crops of their fields. 
Psalms105:36 He struck down every firstborn in the land, the first fruits of all their vigor." 
Psalms105:37 He brought his people out, laden with silver and gold; no stragglers among the tribes." 
Psalms105:38 Egypt rejoiced when they left, for panic had seized them." 
Psalms105:39 He spread a cloud as a cover, and made a fire to light up the night." 
Psalms105:40 They asked and he brought them quail; with bread from heaven he filled them. 
Psalms105:41 He split the rock and water gushed forth; it flowed through the desert like a river. 
Psalms105:42 For he remembered his sacred word to Abraham his servant. 
Psalms105:43 He brought his people out with joy, his chosen ones with shouts of triumph." 
Psalms105:44 He gave them the lands of the nations, the wealth of the peoples to own," 
Psalms105:45 That they might keep his laws and observe his teachings. Hallelujah! 
Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Give thanks to the LORD, who is good, whose love endures forever." 
Psalms106:2 Who can tell the mighty deeds of the LORD, proclaim in full God's praise?" 
Psalms106:3 Happy those who do what is right, whose deeds are always just." 
Psalms106:4 Remember me, LORD, as you favor your people; come to me with your saving help," 
Psalms106:5 That I may see the prosperity of your chosen, rejoice in the joy of your people, and glory with your heritage." 
Psalms106:6 We have sinned like our ancestors; we have done wrong and are guilty. 
Psalms106:7 Our ancestors in Egypt did not attend to your wonders. They did not remember your great love; they defied the Most High at the Red Sea. 
Psalms106:8 Yet he saved them for his name's sake to make his power known. 
Psalms106:9 He roared at the Red Sea and it dried up. He led them through the deep as through a desert. 
Psalms106:10 He rescued them from hostile hands, freed them from the power of the enemy." 
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their oppressors; not one of them survived. 
Psalms106:12 Then they believed his words and sang songs of praise. 
Psalms106:13 But they soon forgot all he had done; they had no patience for his plan. 
Psalms106:14 In the desert they gave way to their cravings, tempted God in the wasteland." 
Psalms106:15 So he gave them what they asked and sent among them a wasting disease. 
Psalms106:16 In the camp they challenged Moses and Aaron, the holy one of the LORD." 
Psalms106:17 The earth opened and swallowed Dathan, it closed on the followers of Abiram." 
Psalms106:18 Against that company the fire blazed; flames consumed the wicked. 
Psalms106:19 At Horeb they fashioned a calf, worshiped a metal statue." 
Psalms106:20 They exchanged their glorious God for the image of a grass-eating bull. 
Psalms106:21 They forgot the God who saved them, who did great deeds in Egypt," 
Psalms106:22 Amazing deeds in the land of Ham, fearsome deeds at the Red Sea." 
Psalms106:23 He would have decreed their destruction, had not Moses, the chosen leader, Withstood him in the breach to turn back his destroying anger." 
Psalms106:24 Next they despised the beautiful land; they did not believe the promise. 
Psalms106:25 In their tents they complained; they did not obey the LORD. 
Psalms106:26 So with raised hand he swore to destroy them in the desert," 
Psalms106:27 To scatter their descendants among the nations, disperse them in foreign lands." 
Psalms106:28 They joined in the rites of Baal of Peor, ate food sacrificed to dead gods." 
Psalms106:29 They provoked him by their actions, and a plague broke out among them." 
Psalms106:30 Then Phinehas rose to intervene, and the plague was brought to a halt." 
Psalms106:31 This was counted for him as a righteous deed for all generations to come. 
Psalms106:32 At the waters of Meribah they angered God, and Moses suffered because of them." 
Psalms106:33 They so embittered his spirit that rash words crossed his lips. 
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples as the LORD had commanded them," 
Psalms106:35 But mingled with the nations and imitated their ways. 
Psalms106:36 They worshiped their idols and were ensnared by them. 
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed to the gods their own sons and daughters," 
Psalms106:38 Shedding innocent blood, the blood of their own sons and daughters, Whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan, desecrating the land with bloodshed." 
Psalms106:39 They defiled themselves by their actions, became adulterers by their conduct." 
Psalms106:40 So the LORD grew angry with his people, abhorred his own heritage." 
Psalms106:41 He handed them over to the nations, and their adversaries ruled them." 
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them, kept them under subjection." 
Psalms106:43 Many times did he rescue them, but they kept rebelling and scheming and were brought low by their own guilt." 
Psalms106:44 Still God had regard for their affliction when he heard their wailing. 
Psalms106:45 For their sake he remembered his covenant and relented in his abundant love," 
Psalms106:46 Winning for them compassion from all who held them captive. 
Psalms106:47 Save us, LORD, our God; gather us from among the nations That we may give thanks to your holy name and glory in praising you." 
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! Let all the people say, Amen! Hallelujah!" 
Psalms107:1 Give thanks to the LORD who is good, whose love endures forever!""" 
Psalms107:2 Let that be the prayer of the LORD'S redeemed, those redeemed from the hand of the foe," 
Psalms107:3 Those gathered from foreign lands, from east and west, from north and south." 
Psalms107:4 Some had lost their way in a barren desert; found no path toward a city to live in. 
Psalms107:5 They were hungry and thirsty; their life was ebbing away. 
Psalms107:6 In their distress they cried to the LORD, who rescued them in their peril," 
Psalms107:7 Guided them by a direct path so they reached a city to live in. 
Psalms107:8 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness, such wondrous deeds for mere mortals." 
Psalms107:9 For he satisfied the thirsty, filled the hungry with good things." 
Psalms107:10 Some lived in darkness and gloom, in prison, bound with chains," 
Psalms107:11 Because they rebelled against God's word, scorned the counsel of the Most High," 
Psalms107:12 Who humbled their hearts through hardship; they stumbled with no one to help. 
Psalms107:13 In their distress they cried to the LORD, who saved them in their peril," 
Psalms107:14 Led them forth from darkness and gloom and broke their chains asunder. 
Psalms107:15 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness, such wondrous deeds for mere mortals." 
Psalms107:16 For he broke down the gates of bronze and snapped the bars of iron. 
Psalms107:17 Some fell sick from their wicked ways, afflicted because of their sins." 
Psalms107:18 They loathed all manner of food; they were at the gates of death. 
Psalms107:19 In their distress they cried to the LORD, who saved them in their peril," 
Psalms107:20 Sent forth the word to heal them, snatched them from the grave." 
Psalms107:21 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness, such wondrous deeds for mere mortals." 
Psalms107:22 Let them offer a sacrifice in thanks, declare his works with shouts of joy." 
Psalms107:23 Some went off to sea in ships, plied their trade on the deep waters." 
Psalms107:24 They saw the works of the LORD, the wonders of God in the deep." 
Psalms107:25 He spoke and roused a storm wind; it tossed the waves on high. 
Psalms107:26 They rose up to the heavens, sank to the depths; their hearts trembled at the danger." 
Psalms107:27 They reeled, staggered like drunkards; their skill was of no avail." 
Psalms107:28 In their distress they cried to the LORD, who brought them out of their peril," 
Psalms107:29 Hushed the storm to a murmur; the waves of the sea were stilled. 
Psalms107:30 They rejoiced that the sea grew calm, that God brought them to the harbor they longed for." 
Psalms107:31 Let them thank the LORD for such kindness, such wondrous deeds for mere mortals." 
Psalms107:32 Let them praise him in the assembly of the people, give thanks in the council of the elders." 
Psalms107:33 God changed rivers into desert, springs of water into thirsty ground," 
Psalms107:34 Fruitful land into a salty waste, because of the wickedness of its people." 
Psalms107:35 He changed the desert into pools of water, arid land into springs of water," 
Psalms107:36 And settled the hungry there; they built a city to live in. 
Psalms107:37 They sowed fields and planted vineyards, brought in an abundant harvest." 
Psalms107:38 God blessed them, they became very many, and their livestock did not decrease." 
Psalms107:39 Where they were diminished and brought low through misery and cruel oppression," 
Psalms107:40 But he poured out contempt on princes, made them wander the trackless wastes," 
Psalms107:41 While the poor were released from their affliction; their families increased like their flocks. 
Psalms107:42 The upright saw this and rejoiced; all wickedness shut its mouth. 
Psalms107:43 Whoever is wise will take note of these things, will ponder the merciful deeds of the LORD." 
Psalms108:1 A song; a psalm of David 
Psalms108:2 My heart is steadfast, God; my heart is steadfast. I will sing and chant praise." 
Psalms108:3 Awake, my soul; awake, lyre and harp! I will wake the dawn." 
Psalms108:4 I will praise you among the peoples, LORD; I will chant your praise among the nations." 
Psalms108:5 For your love towers to the heavens; your faithfulness, to the skies." 
Psalms108:6 Appear on high over the heavens, God; may your glory appear above all the earth." 
Psalms108:7 Help with your right hand and answer us that your loved ones may escape. 
Psalms108:8 God promised in the sanctuary: I will exult, I will apportion Shechem; the valley of Succoth I will measure out." 
Psalms108:9 Gilead is mine, mine is Manasseh; Ephraim is the helmet for my head, Judah, my own scepter." 
Psalms108:10 Moab is my washbowl; upon Edom I cast my sandal; I will triumph over Philistia. 
Psalms108:11 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me into Edom? 
Psalms108:12 Was it not you who rejected us, God? Do you no longer march with our armies?" 
Psalms108:13 Give us aid against the foe; worthless is human help. 
Psalms108:14 We will triumph with the help of God, who will trample down our foes." 
Psalms109:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms109:2 O God, whom I praise, do not be silent, for wicked and treacherous mouths attack me. They speak against me with lying tongues;" 
Psalms109:3 with hateful words they surround me, attacking me without cause." 
Psalms109:4 In return for my love they slander me, even though I prayed for them." 
Psalms109:5 They repay me evil for good, hatred for my love. II My enemies say of me:" 
Psalms109:6 Find a lying witness, an accuser to stand by his right hand," 
Psalms109:7 That he may be judged and found guilty, that his plea may be in vain." 
Psalms109:8 May his days be few; may another take his office. 
Psalms109:9 May his children be fatherless, his wife, a widow." 
Psalms109:10 May his children be vagrant beggars, driven from their hovels." 
Psalms109:11 May the usurer snare all he owns, strangers plunder all he earns." 
Psalms109:12 May no one treat him kindly or pity his fatherless children. 
Psalms109:13 May his posterity be destroyed, his name cease in the next generation." 
Psalms109:14 May the LORD remember his fathers' guilt; his mother's sin not be canceled. 
Psalms109:15 May their guilt be always before the LORD, till their memory is banished from the earth," 
Psalms109:16 For he did not remember to show kindness, but hounded the wretched poor and brought death to the brokenhearted." 
Psalms109:17 He loved cursing; may it come upon him; he hated blessing; may none come to him. 
Psalms109:18 May cursing clothe him like a robe; may it enter his belly like water, seep into his bones like oil." 
Psalms109:19 May it be near as the clothes he wears, as the belt always around him.""" 
Psalms109:20 May the LORD bring all this upon my accusers, upon those who speak evil against me." 
Psalms109:21 But you, LORD, my God, deal kindly with me for your name's sake; in your great mercy rescue me." 
Psalms109:22 For I am sorely in need; my heart is pierced within me. 
Psalms109:23 Like a lengthening shadow I near my end, all but swept away like the locust." 
Psalms109:24 My knees totter from fasting; my flesh has wasted away. 
Psalms109:25 I have become a mockery to them; when they see me, they shake their heads." 
Psalms109:26 Help me, LORD, my God; save me in your kindness." 
Psalms109:27 Make them know this is your hand, that you, LORD, have acted." 
Psalms109:28 Though they curse, may you bless; shame my foes, that your servant may rejoice." 
Psalms109:29 Clothe my accusers with disgrace; make them wear shame like a mantle. 
Psalms109:30 I will give fervent thanks to the LORD; before all I will praise my God. 
Psalms109:31 For God stands at the right hand of the poor to defend them against unjust accusers. 
Psalms110:1 A psalm of David. The LORD says to you, my lord: ""Take your throne at my righthand, while I make your enemies your footstool.""" 
Psalms110:2 The scepter of your sovereign might the LORD will extend from Zion. The LORD says: Rule over your enemies! 
Psalms110:3 Yours is princely power from the day of your birth. In holy splendor before the daystar, like the dew I begot you.""" 
Psalms110:4 The LORD has sworn and will not waver: Like Melchizedek you are a priest forever. 
Psalms110:5 At your right hand is the Lord, who crushes kings on the day of wrath," 
Psalms110:6 Who, robed in splendor, judges nations, crushes heads across the wide earth," 
Psalms110:7 Who drinks from the brook by the wayside and thus holds high the head. 
Psalms111:1 Hallelujah. I will praise the LORD with all my heart in the assembled congregation of the upright. 
Psalms111:2 Great are the works of the LORD, to be treasured for all their delights." 
Psalms111:3 Majestic and glorious is your work, your wise design endures forever." 
Psalms111:4 You won renown for your wondrous deeds; gracious and merciful is the LORD. 
Psalms111:5 You gave food to those who fear you, mindful of your covenant forever." 
Psalms111:6 You showed powerful deeds to your people, giving them the lands of the nations." 
Psalms111:7 The works of your hands are right and true, reliable all your decrees," 
Psalms111:8 Established forever and ever, to be observed with loyalty and care." 
Psalms111:9 You sent deliverance to your people, ratified your covenant forever; holy and awesome is your name." 
Psalms111:10 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom; prudent are all who live by it. Your praise endures forever. 
Psalms112:1 Hallelujah! Happy are those who fear the LORD, who greatly delight in God's commands." 
Psalms112:2 Their descendants shall be mighty in the land, generation upright and blessed." 
Psalms112:3 Wealth and riches shall be in their homes; their prosperity shall endure forever. 
Psalms112:4 They shine through the darkness, a light for the upright; they are gracious, merciful, and just." 
Psalms112:5 All goes well for those gracious in lending, who conduct their affairs with justice." 
Psalms112:6 They shall never be shaken; the just shall be remembered forever. 
Psalms112:7 They shall not fear an ill report; their hearts are steadfast, trusting the LORD." 
Psalms112:8 Their hearts are tranquil, without fear, till at last they look down on their foes." 
Psalms112:9 Lavishly they give to the poor; their prosperity shall endure forever; their horn shall be exalted in honor. 
Psalms112:10 The wicked shall be angry to see this; they will gnash their teeth and waste away; the desires of the wicked come to nothing. 
Psalms113:1 Hallelujah! I Praise, you servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD." 
Psalms113:2 Blessed be the name of the LORD both now and forever. 
Psalms113:3 From the rising of the sun to its setting let the name of the LORD be praised. 
Psalms113:4 High above all nations is the LORD; above the heavens God's glory. 
Psalms113:5 Who is like the LORD, our God enthroned on high," 
Psalms113:6 looking down on heaven and earth? 
Psalms113:7 The LORD raises the needy from the dust, lifts the poor from the ash heap," 
Psalms113:8 Seats them with princes, the princes of the people," 
Psalms113:9 Gives the childless wife a home, the joyful mother of children. Hallelujah!" 
Psalms114:1 When Israel came forth from Egypt, the house of Jacob from an alien people," 
Psalms114:2 Judah became God's holy place, Israel, God's domain." 
Psalms114:3 The sea beheld and fled; the Jordan turned back. 
Psalms114:4 The mountains skipped like rams; the hills, like lambs of the flock." 
Psalms114:5 Why was it, sea, that you fled? Jordan, that you turned back?" 
Psalms114:6 You mountains, that you skipped like rams? You hills, like lambs of the flock?" 
Psalms114:7 Tremble, earth, before the Lord, before the God of Jacob," 
Psalms114:8 Who turned rock into pools of water, stone into flowing springs." 
Psalms115:1 Not to us, LORD, not to us but to your name give glory because of your faithfulness and love." 
Psalms115:2 Why should the nations say, ""Where is their God?""" 
Psalms115:3 Our God is in heaven; whatever God wills is done. 
Psalms115:4 Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands." 
Psalms115:5 They have mouths but do not speak, eyes but do not see." 
Psalms115:6 They have ears but do not hear, noses but do not smell." 
Psalms115:7 They have hands but do not feel, feet but do not walk, and no sound rises from their throats." 
Psalms115:8 Their makers shall be like them, all who trust in them." 
Psalms115:9 The house of Israel trusts in the LORD, who is their help and shield." 
Psalms115:10 The house of Aaron trusts in the LORD, who is their help and shield." 
Psalms115:11 Those who fear the LORD trust in the LORD, who is their help and shield." 
Psalms115:12 The LORD remembers us and will bless us, will bless the house of Israel, will bless the house of Aaron," 
Psalms115:13 Will bless those who fear the LORD, small and great alike." 
Psalms115:14 May the LORD increase your number, you and your descendants." 
Psalms115:15 May you be blessed by the LORD, who made heaven and earth." 
Psalms115:16 The heavens belong to the LORD, but the earth is given to us." 
Psalms115:17 The dead do not praise the LORD, all those gone down into silence." 
Psalms115:18 It is we who bless the LORD, both now and forever. Hallelujah!" 
Psalms116:1 I love the LORD, who listened to my voice in supplication," 
Psalms116:2 Who turned an ear to me on the day I called. 
Psalms116:3 I was caught by the cords of death; the snares of Sheol had seized me; I felt agony and dread. 
Psalms116:4 Then I called on the name of the LORD, ""O LORD, save my life!""" 
Psalms116:5 Gracious is the LORD and just; yes, our God is merciful." 
Psalms116:6 The LORD protects the simple; I was helpless, but God saved me." 
Psalms116:7 Return, my soul, to your rest; the LORD has been good to you." 
Psalms116:8 For my soul has been freed from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling." 
Psalms116:9 I shall walk before the LORD in the land of the living. 
Psalms116:10 I kept faith, even when I said, ""I am greatly afflicted!""" 
Psalms116:11 I said in my alarm, ""No one can be trusted!""" 
Psalms116:12 How can I repay the LORD for all the good done for me? 
Psalms116:13 I will raise the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD. 
Psalms116:14 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people. 
Psalms116:15 Too costly in the eyes of the LORD is the death of his faithful. 
Psalms116:16 LORD, I am your servant, your servant, the child of your maidservant; you have loosed my bonds." 
Psalms116:17 I will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving and call on the name of the LORD. 
Psalms116:18 I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people," 
Psalms116:19 In the courts of the house of the LORD, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Hallelujah!" 
Psalms117:1 Praise the LORD, all you nations! Give glory, all you peoples!" 
Psalms117:2 The LORD'S love for us is strong; the LORD is faithful forever. Hallelujah! 
Psalms118:1 Give thanks to the LORD, who is good, whose love endures forever." 
Psalms118:2 Let the house of Israel say: God's love endures forever. 
Psalms118:3 Let the house of Aaron say, God's love endures forever." 
Psalms118:4 Let those who fear the LORD say, God's love endures forever." 
Psalms118:5 In danger I called on the LORD; the LORD answered me and set me free. 
Psalms118:6 The LORD is with me; I am not afraid; what can mortals do against me? 
Psalms118:7 The LORD is with me as my helper; I shall look in triumph on my foes. 
Psalms118:8 Better to take refuge in the LORD than to put one's trust in mortals. 
Psalms118:9 Better to take refuge in the LORD than to put one's trust in princes. 
Psalms118:10 All the nations surrounded me; in the LORD'S name I crushed them. 
Psalms118:11 They surrounded me on every side; in the LORD'S name I crushed them. 
Psalms118:12 They surrounded me like bees; they blazed like fire among thorns; in the LORD'S name I crushed them. 
Psalms118:13 I was hard pressed and falling, but the LORD came to my help." 
Psalms118:14 The LORD, my strength and might, came to me as savior." 
Psalms118:15 The joyful shout of deliverance is heard in the tents of the victors: The LORD'S right hand strikes with power; 
Psalms118:16 the LORD'S right hand is raised; the LORD'S right hand strikes with power. 
Psalms118:17 I shall not die but live and declare the deeds of the LORD. 
Psalms118:18 The LORD chastised me harshly, but did not hand me over to death." 
Psalms118:19 Open the gates of victory; I will enter and thank the LORD. 
Psalms118:20 This is the LORD'S own gate, where the victors enter." 
Psalms118:21 I thank you for you answered me; you have been my savior. 
Psalms118:22 The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. 
Psalms118:23 By the LORD has this been done; it is wonderful in our eyes. 
Psalms118:24 This is the day the LORD has made; let us rejoice in it and be glad. 
Psalms118:25 LORD, grant salvation! LORD, grant good fortune!" 
Psalms118:26 Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD. We bless you from the LORD'S house. 
Psalms118:27 The LORD is God and has given us light. Join in procession with leafy branches up to the horns of the altar. 
Psalms118:28 You are my God, I give you thanks; my God, I offer you praise." 
Psalms118:29 Give thanks to the LORD, who is good, whose love endures forever." 
Psalms119:1 Happy those whose way is blameless, who walk by the teaching of the LORD." 
Psalms119:2 Happy those who observe God's decrees, who seek the LORD with all their heart." 
Psalms119:3 They do no wrong; they walk in God's ways. 
Psalms119:4 You have given them the command to keep your precepts with care. 
Psalms119:5 May my ways be firm in the observance of your laws! 
Psalms119:6 Then I will not be ashamed to ponder all your commands. 
Psalms119:7 I will praise you with sincere heart as I study your just edicts. 
Psalms119:8 I will keep your laws; do not leave me all alone. 
Psalms119:9 How can the young walk without fault? Only by keeping your words. 
Psalms119:10 With all my heart I seek you; do not let me stray from your commands. 
Psalms119:11 In my heart I treasure your promise, that I may not sin against you." 
Psalms119:12 Blessed are you, O LORD; teach me your laws." 
Psalms119:13 With my lips I recite all the edicts you have spoken. 
Psalms119:14 I find joy in the way of your decrees more than in all riches. 
Psalms119:15 I will ponder your precepts and consider your paths. 
Psalms119:16 In your laws I take delight; I will never forget your word. 
Psalms119:17 Be kind to your servant that I may live, that I may keep your word." 
Psalms119:18 Open my eyes to see clearly the wonders of your teachings. 
Psalms119:19 I am a sojourner in the land; do not hide your commands from me. 
Psalms119:20 At all times my soul is stirred with longing for your edicts. 
Psalms119:21 With a curse you rebuke the proud who stray from your commands. 
Psalms119:22 Free me from disgrace and contempt, for I observe your decrees." 
Psalms119:23 Though princes meet and talk against me, your servant studies your laws." 
Psalms119:24 Your decrees are my delight; they are my counselors. 
Psalms119:25 I lie prostrate in the dust; give me life in accord with your word. 
Psalms119:26 I disclosed my ways and you answered me; teach me your laws. 
Psalms119:27 Make me understand the way of your precepts; I will ponder your wondrous deeds. 
Psalms119:28 I weep in bitter pain; in accord with your word to strengthen me. 
Psalms119:29 Lead me from the way of deceit; favor me with your teaching. 
Psalms119:30 The way of loyalty I have chosen; I have set your edicts before me. 
Psalms119:31 I cling to your decrees, LORD; do not let me come to shame." 
Psalms119:32 I will run the way of your commands, for you open my docile heart." 
Psalms119:33 LORD, teach me the way of your laws; I shall observe them with care." 
Psalms119:34 Give me insight to observe your teaching, to keep it with all my heart." 
Psalms119:35 Lead me in the path of your commands, for that is my delight." 
Psalms119:36 Direct my heart toward your decrees and away from unjust gain. 
Psalms119:37 Avert my eyes from what is worthless; by your way give me life. 
Psalms119:38 For your servant fulfill your promise made to those who fear you. 
Psalms119:39 Turn away from me the taunts I dread, for your edicts bring good." 
Psalms119:40 See how I long for your precepts; in your justice give me life. 
Psalms119:41 Let your love come to me, LORD, salvation in accord with your promise." 
Psalms119:42 Let me answer my taunters with a word, for I trust in your word." 
Psalms119:43 Do not take the word of truth from my mouth, for in your edicts is my hope." 
Psalms119:44 I will keep your teachings always, for all time and forever." 
Psalms119:45 I will walk freely in an open space because I cherish your precepts. 
Psalms119:46 I will speak openly of your decrees without fear even before kings. 
Psalms119:47 I delight in your commands, which I dearly love." 
Psalms119:48 I lift up my hands to your commands; I study your laws, which I love." 
Psalms119:49 Remember your word to your servant by which you give me hope. 
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in affliction, your promise that gives me life." 
Psalms119:51 Though the arrogant utterly scorn me, I do not turn from your teaching." 
Psalms119:52 When I recite your edicts of old I am comforted, LORD." 
Psalms119:53 Rage seizes me because of the wicked; they forsake your teaching. 
Psalms119:54 Your laws become my songs wherever I make my home. 
Psalms119:55 Even at night I remember your name in observance of your teaching, LORD." 
Psalms119:56 This is my good fortune, for I have observed your precepts." 
Psalms119:57 My portion is the LORD; I promise to keep your words. 
Psalms119:58 I entreat you with all my heart: have mercy on me in accord with your promise. 
Psalms119:59 I have examined my ways and turned my steps to your decrees. 
Psalms119:60 I am prompt, I do not hesitate in keeping your commands." 
Psalms119:61 Though the snares of the wicked surround me, your teaching I do not forget." 
Psalms119:62 At midnight I rise to praise you because your edicts are just. 
Psalms119:63 I am the friend of all who fear you, of all who keep your precepts." 
Psalms119:64 The earth, LORD, is filled with your love; teach me your laws." 
Psalms119:65 You have treated your servant well, according to your word, O LORD." 
Psalms119:66 Teach me wisdom and knowledge, for in your commands I trust." 
Psalms119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I hold to your promise." 
Psalms119:68 You are good and do what is good; teach me your laws. 
Psalms119:69 The arrogant smear me with lies, but I observe your precepts with all my heart." 
Psalms119:70 Their hearts are gross and fat; as for me, your teaching is my delight." 
Psalms119:71 It was good for me to be afflicted, in order to learn your laws." 
Psalms119:72 Teaching from your lips is more precious to me than heaps of silver and gold. 
Psalms119:73 Your hands made me and fashioned me; give me insight to learn your commands. 
Psalms119:74 Those who fear you rejoice to see me, because I hope in your word." 
Psalms119:75 I know, LORD, that your edicts are just; though you afflict me, you are faithful." 
Psalms119:76 May your love comfort me in accord with your promise to your servant. 
Psalms119:77 Show me compassion that I may live, for your teaching is my delight." 
Psalms119:78 Shame the proud for oppressing me unjustly, that I may study your precepts." 
Psalms119:79 Let those who fear you turn to me, those who acknowledge your decrees." 
Psalms119:80 May I be wholehearted toward your laws, that I may not be put to shame." 
Psalms119:81 My soul longs for your salvation; I put my hope in your word. 
Psalms119:82 My eyes long to see your promise. When will you comfort me? 
Psalms119:83 I am like a wineskin shriveled by smoke, but I have not forgotten your laws." 
Psalms119:84 How long can your servant survive? When will your edict doom my foes? 
Psalms119:85 The arrogant have dug pits for me; defying your teaching. 
Psalms119:86 All your commands are steadfast. Help me! I am pursued without cause. 
Psalms119:87 They have almost ended my life on earth, but I do not forsake your precepts." 
Psalms119:88 In your kindness give me life, to keep the decrees you have spoken." 
Psalms119:89 Your word, LORD, stands forever; it is firm as the heavens." 
Psalms119:90 Through all generations your truth endures; fixed to stand firm like the earth. 
Psalms119:91 By your edicts they stand firm to this day, for all things are your servants." 
Psalms119:92 Had your teaching not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction." 
Psalms119:93 I will never forget your precepts; through them you give me life. 
Psalms119:94 I am yours; save me, for I cherish your precepts." 
Psalms119:95 The wicked hope to destroy me, but I pay heed to your decrees." 
Psalms119:96 I have seen the limits of all perfection, but your command is without bounds." 
Psalms119:97 How I love your teaching, Lord! I study it all day long." 
Psalms119:98 Your command makes me wiser than my foes, for it is always with me." 
Psalms119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers, because I ponder your decrees." 
Psalms119:100 I have more insight than my elders, because I observe your precepts." 
Psalms119:101 I keep my steps from every evil path, that I may obey your word." 
Psalms119:102 From your edicts I do not turn, for you have taught them to me." 
Psalms119:103 How sweet to my tongue is your promise, sweeter than honey to my mouth!" 
Psalms119:104 Through your precepts I gain insight; therefore I hate all false ways. 
Psalms119:105 Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light for my path." 
Psalms119:106 I make a solemn vow to keep your just edicts. 
Psalms119:107 I am very much afflicted, LORD; give me life in accord with your word." 
Psalms119:108 Accept my freely offered praise; LORD, teach me your decrees." 
Psalms119:109 My life is always at risk, but I do not forget your teaching." 
Psalms119:110 The wicked have set snares for me, but from your precepts I do not stray." 
Psalms119:111 Your decrees are my heritage forever; they are the joy of my heart. 
Psalms119:112 My heart is set on fulfilling your laws; they are my reward forever. 
Psalms119:113 I hate every hypocrite; your teaching I love. 
Psalms119:114 You are my refuge and shield; in your word I hope. 
Psalms119:115 Depart from me, you wicked, that I may observe the commands of my God." 
Psalms119:116 Sustain me by your promise that I may live; do not disappoint me in my hope. 
Psalms119:117 Strengthen me that I may be safe, ever to contemplate your laws." 
Psalms119:118 You reject all who stray from your laws, for vain is their deceit." 
Psalms119:119 Like dross you regard all the wicked on earth; therefore I love your decrees. 
Psalms119:120 My flesh shudders with dread of you; I hold your edicts in awe. 
Psalms119:121 I have fulfilled your just edict; do not abandon me to my oppressors. 
Psalms119:122 Guarantee your servant's welfare; do not let the arrogant oppress me. 
Psalms119:123 My eyes long to see your salvation and the justice of your promise. 
Psalms119:124 Act with kindness toward your servant; teach me your laws. 
Psalms119:125 I am your servant; give me discernment that I may know your decrees. 
Psalms119:126 It is time for the LORD to act; they have disobeyed your teaching. 
Psalms119:127 Truly I love your commands more than the finest gold. 
Psalms119:128 Thus I follow all your precepts; every wrong way I hate. 
Psalms119:129 Wonderful are your decrees; therefore I observe them. 
Psalms119:130 The revelation of your words sheds light, gives understanding to the simple." 
Psalms119:131 I sigh with open mouth, yearning for your commands." 
Psalms119:132 Turn to me and be gracious, your edict for lovers of your name." 
Psalms119:133 Steady my feet in accord with your promise; do not let iniquity lead me. 
Psalms119:134 Free me from human oppression, that I may keep your precepts." 
Psalms119:135 Let your face shine upon your servant; teach me your laws. 
Psalms119:136 My eyes shed streams of tears because your teaching is not followed. 
Psalms119:137 You are righteous, LORD, and just are your edicts." 
Psalms119:138 You have issued your decrees in justice and in surpassing faithfulness. 
Psalms119:139 I am consumed with rage, because my foes forget your words." 
Psalms119:140 Your servant loves your promise; it has been proved by fire. 
Psalms119:141 Though belittled and despised, I do not forget your precepts." 
Psalms119:142 Your justice is forever right, your teaching forever true." 
Psalms119:143 Though distress and anguish come upon me, your commands are my delight." 
Psalms119:144 Your decrees are forever just; give me discernment that I may live. 
Psalms119:145 I call with all my heart, O LORD; answer me that I may observe your laws." 
Psalms119:146 I call to you to save me that I may keep your decrees. 
Psalms119:147 I rise before dawn and cry out; I put my hope in your words. 
Psalms119:148 My eyes greet the night watches as I meditate on your promise. 
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice in your love, O LORD; by your edict give me life." 
Psalms119:150 Malicious persecutors draw near me; they are far from your teaching. 
Psalms119:151 You are near, O LORD; reliable are all your commands." 
Psalms119:152 Long have I known from your decrees that you have established them forever. 
Psalms119:153 Look at my affliction and rescue me, for I have not forgotten your teaching." 
Psalms119:154 Take up my cause and redeem me; for the sake of your promise give me life. 
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from sinners because they do not cherish your laws. 
Psalms119:156 Your compassion is great, O LORD; in accord with your edicts give me life." 
Psalms119:157 Though my persecutors and foes are many I do not turn from your decrees. 
Psalms119:158 I view the faithless with loathing, because they do not heed your promise." 
Psalms119:159 See how I love your precepts, LORD; in your kindness give me life." 
Psalms119:160 Your every word is enduring; all your just edicts are forever. 
Psalms119:161 Princes persecute me without reason, but my heart reveres only your word." 
Psalms119:162 I rejoice at your promise, as one who has found rich spoil." 
Psalms119:163 Falsehood I hate and abhor; your teaching I love. 
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day I praise you because your edicts are just. 
Psalms119:165 Lovers of your teaching have much peace; for them there is no stumbling block. 
Psalms119:166 I look for your salvation, LORD, and I fulfill your commands." 
Psalms119:167 I observe your decrees; I love them very much. 
Psalms119:168 I observe your precepts and decrees; all my ways are before you. 
Psalms119:169 Let my cry come before you, LORD; in keeping with your word give me discernment." 
Psalms119:170 Let my prayer come before you; rescue me according to your promise. 
Psalms119:171 May my lips pour forth your praise, because you teach me your laws." 
Psalms119:172 May my tongue sing of your promise, for all your commands are just." 
Psalms119:173 Keep your hand ready to help me, for I have chosen your precepts." 
Psalms119:174 I long for your salvation, LORD; your teaching is my delight." 
Psalms119:175 Let me live to praise you; may your edicts give me help. 
Psalms119:176 I have wandered like a lost sheep; seek out your servant, for I do not forget your commands." 
Psalms120:1 A song of ascents. I The LORD answered me when I called in my distress: 
Psalms120:2 LORD, deliver me from lying lips, from treacherous tongues." 
Psalms120:3 What will the Lord inflict on you, O treacherous tongue, and what more besides?" 
Psalms120:4 A warrior's sharpened arrows and fiery coals of brush wood! 
Psalms120:5 Alas, I was an alien in Meshech, I lived near the tents of Kedar!" 
Psalms120:6 Too long did I live among those who hated peace. 
Psalms120:7 When I spoke of peace, they were for war." 
Psalms121:1 A song of ascents. I I raise my eyes toward the mountains. From where will my help come? 
Psalms121:2 My help comes from the LORD, the maker of heaven and earth." 
Psalms121:3 God will not allow your foot to slip; your guardian does not sleep. 
Psalms121:4 Truly, the guardian of Israel never slumbers nor sleeps." 
Psalms121:5 The LORD is your guardian; the LORD is your shade at your righthand. 
Psalms121:6 By day the sun cannot harm you, nor the moon by night." 
Psalms121:7 The LORD will guard you from all evil, will always guard your life." 
Psalms121:8 The LORD will guard your coming and going both now and forever. 
Psalms122:1 A song of ascents. Of David. I I rejoiced when they said to me, ""Let us go to the house of the LORD.""" 
Psalms122:2 And now our feet are standing within your gates, Jerusalem." 
Psalms122:3 Jerusalem, built as a city, walled round about." 
Psalms122:4 Here the tribes have come, the tribes of the LORD, As it was decreed for Israel, to give thanks to the name of the LORD." 
Psalms122:5 Here are the thrones of justice, the thrones of the house of David." 
Psalms122:6 For the peace of Jerusalem pray: May those who love you prosper! 
Psalms122:7 May peace be within your ramparts, prosperity within your towers.""" 
Psalms122:8 For family and friends I say, ""May peace be yours.""" 
Psalms122:9 For the house of the LORD, our God, I pray, ""May blessings be yours.""" 
Psalms123:1 A song of ascents. To you I raise my eyes, to you enthroned in heaven." 
Psalms123:2 Yes, like the eyes of a servant on the hand of his master, Like the eyes of a maid on the hand of her mistress, So our eyes are on the LORD our God, till we are shown favor." 
Psalms123:3 Show us favor, LORD, show us favor, for we have our fill of contempt." 
Psalms123:4 We have our fill of insult from the insolent, of disdain from the arrogant." 
Psalms124:1 A song of ascents. Of David. I Had not the LORD been with us, let Israel say," 
Psalms124:2 Had not the LORD been with us, when people rose against us," 
Psalms124:3 They would have swallowed us alive, for their fury blazed against us." 
Psalms124:4 The waters would have engulfed us, the torrent overwhelmed us;" 
Psalms124:5 seething waters would have drowned us. 
Psalms124:6 Blessed be the LORD, who did not leave us to be torn by their fangs." 
Psalms124:7 We escaped with our lives like a bird from the fowler's snare; the snare was broken and we escaped. 
Psalms124:8 Our help is the name of the LORD, the maker of heaven and earth."

Psalms125:4 Do good, LORD, to the good, to those who are upright of heart." 
Psalms125:5 But those who turn aside to crooked ways may the LORD send down with the wicked. Peace upon Israel!

Psalms127:4 Like arrows in the hand of a warrior are the children born in one's youth. 
Psalms127:5 Blessed are they whose quivers are full. They will never be shamed contending with foes at the gate.

Psalms128:6 and live to see your children's children. Peace upon Israel!

Psalms129:4 But the just LORD cut me free from the ropes of the yoke of the wicked.

Psalms132:1 A song of ascents. I LORD, remember David and all his anxious care;" 
Psalms132:2 How he swore an oath to the LORD, vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:" 
Psalms132:3 I will not enter the house where I live, nor lie on the couch where I sleep;" 
Psalms132:4 I will give my eyes no sleep, my eyelids no rest," 
Psalms132:5 Till I find a home for the LORD, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.""" 
Psalms132:6 We have heard of it in Ephrathah; we have found it in the fields of Jaar. 
Psalms132:7 Let us enter God's dwelling; let us worship at God's footstool. 
Psalms132:8 Arise, LORD, come to your resting place, you and your majestic ark." 
Psalms132:9 Your priests will be clothed with justice; your faithful will shout for joy. 
Psalms132:10 For the sake of David your servant, do not reject your anointed." 
Psalms132:11 The LORD swore an oath to David, a pledge never to be broken: ""Your own offspring I will set upon your throne." 
Psalms132:12 If your sons observe my covenant, the laws I shall teach them, Their sons, in turn, shall sit forever on your throne.""" 
Psalms132:13 Yes, the LORD has chosen Zion, desired it for a dwelling:" 
Psalms132:14 This is my resting place forever; here I will dwell, for I desire it." 
Psalms132:15 I will bless Zion with meat; its poor I will fill with bread. 
Psalms132:16 I will clothe its priests with blessing; its faithful shall shout for joy. 
Psalms132:17 There I will make a horn sprout for David's line; I will set a lamp for my anointed. 
Psalms132:18 His foes I will clothe with shame, but on him my crown shall gleam."""

Psalms135:1 Hallelujah! I Praise the name of the LORD! Praise, you servants of the LORD," 
Psalms135:2 Who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God!" 
Psalms135:3 Praise the LORD; the LORD is good! Sing to God's name; it is gracious! 
Psalms135:4 For the LORD has chosen Jacob, Israel as a treasured possession." 
Psalms135:5 I know that the LORD is great, our Lord is greater than all gods." 
Psalms135:6 Whatever the LORD wishes he does in heaven and on earth, in the seas and in all the deeps." 
Psalms135:7 He raises storm clouds from the end of the earth, makes lightning and rain, brings forth wind from the storehouse." 
Psalms135:8 He struck down Egypt's firstborn, human and beast alike," 
Psalms135:9 And sent signs and portents against you, Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants." 
Psalms135:10 The Lord struck down many nations, slew mighty kings," 
Psalms135:11 Sihon, king of the Amorites, Og, king of Bashan, all the kings of Canaan," 
Psalms135:12 And made their land a heritage, a heritage for Israel his people." 
Psalms135:13 O LORD, your name is forever, your renown, from age to age!" 
Psalms135:14 For the LORD defends his people, shows mercy to his servants." 
Psalms135:15 The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands." 
Psalms135:16 They have mouths but speak not; they have eyes but see not; 
Psalms135:17 They have ears but hear not; no breath is in their mouths. 
Psalms135:18 Their makers shall be like them, all who trust in them." 
Psalms135:19 House of Israel, bless the LORD! House of Aaron, bless the LORD!" 
Psalms135:20 House of Levi, bless the LORD! You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD!" 
Psalms135:21 Blessed from Zion be the LORD, who dwells in Jerusalem! Hallelujah!" 
Psalms136:1 Praise the LORD, who is so good; God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:2 Praise the God of gods; God's love endures forever; 
Psalms136:3 Praise the Lord of lords; God's love endures forever; 
Psalms136:4 Who alone has done great wonders, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:5 Who skillfully made the heavens, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:6 Who spread the earth upon the waters, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:7 Who made the great lights, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:8 The sun to rule the day, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:9 The moon and stars to rule the night, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:10 Who struck down the firstborn of Egypt, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:11 And led Israel from their midst, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:12 With mighty hand and outstretched arm, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:13 Who split in two the Red Sea, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:14 And led Israel through, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:15 But swept Pharaoh and his army into the Red Sea, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:16 Who led the people through the desert, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:17 Who struck down great kings, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:18 Slew powerful kings, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:19 Sihon, king of the Amorites, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:20 Og, king of Bashan, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:21 And made their lands a heritage, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:22 A heritage for Israel, God's servant, God's love endures forever." 
Psalms136:23 The LORD remembered us in our misery, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:24 Freed us from our foes, God's love endures forever;" 
Psalms136:25 And gives food to all flesh, God's love endures forever." 
Psalms136:26 Praise the God of heaven, God's love endures forever." 
Psalms137:1 By the rivers of Babylon we sat mourning and weeping when we remembered Zion. 
Psalms137:2 On the poplars of that land we hung up our harps. 
Psalms137:3 There our captors asked us for the words of a song; Our tormentors, for a joyful song: ""Sing for us a song of Zion!""" 
Psalms137:4 But how could we sing a song of the LORD in a foreign land? 
Psalms137:5 If I forget you, Jerusalem, may my right hand wither." 
Psalms137:6 May my tongue stick to my palate if I do not remember you, If I do not exalt Jerusalem beyond all my delights." 
Psalms137:7 Remember, LORD, against Edom that day at Jerusalem. They said: ""Level it, level it down to its foundations!""" 
Psalms137:8 Fair Babylon, you destroyer, happy those who pay you back the evil you have done us!" 
Psalms137:9 Happy those who seize your children and smash them against a rock. 
Psalms138:1 Of David. I I thank you, LORD, with all my heart; before the gods to you I sing." 
Psalms138:2 I bow low toward your holy temple; I praise your name for your fidelity and love. For you have exalted over all your name and your promise. 
Psalms138:3 When I cried out, you answered; you strengthened my spirit." 
Psalms138:4 All the kings of earth will praise you, LORD, when they hear the words of your mouth." 
Psalms138:5 They will sing of the ways of the LORD: How great is the glory of the LORD! 
Psalms138:6 The LORD is on high, but cares for the lowly and knows the proud from afar." 
Psalms138:7 Though I walk in the midst of dangers, you guard my life when my enemies rage. You stretch out your hand; your right hand saves me." 
Psalms138:8 The LORD is with me to the end. LORD, your love endures forever. Never forsake the work of your hands!" 
Psalms139:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. I LORD, you have probed me, you know me:" 
Psalms139:2 you know when I sit and stand; you understand my thoughts from afar. 
Psalms139:3 My travels and my rest you mark; with all my ways you are familiar. 
Psalms139:4 Even before a word is on my tongue, LORD, you know it all." 
Psalms139:5 Behind and before you encircle me and rest your hand upon me. 
Psalms139:6 Such knowledge is beyond me, far too lofty for me to reach." 
Psalms139:7 Where can I hide from your spirit? From your presence, where can I flee?" 
Psalms139:8 If I ascend to the heavens, you are there; if I lie down in Sheol, you are there too." 
Psalms139:9 If I fly with the wings of dawn and alight beyond the sea," 
Psalms139:10 Even there your hand will guide me, your right hand hold me fast." 
Psalms139:11 If I say, ""Surely darkness shall hide me, and night shall be my light""," 
Psalms139:12 Darkness is not dark for you, and night shines as the day. Darkness and light are but one." 
Psalms139:13 You formed my inmost being; you knit me in my mother's womb. 
Psalms139:14 I praise you, so wonderfully you made me; wonderful are your works! My very self you knew;" 
Psalms139:15 my bones were not hidden from you, When I was being made in secret, fashioned as in the depths of the earth." 
Psalms139:16 Your eyes foresaw my actions; in your book all are written down; my days were shaped, before one came to be." 
Psalms139:17 How precious to me are your designs, O God; how vast the sum of them!" 
Psalms139:18 Were I to count, they would outnumber the sands; to finish, I would need eternity." 
Psalms139:19 If only you would destroy the wicked, O God, and the bloodthirsty would depart from me!" 
Psalms139:20 Deceitfully they invoke your name; your foes swear faithless oaths. 
Psalms139:21 Do I not hate, LORD, those who hate you? Those who rise against you, do I not loathe?" 
Psalms139:22 With fierce hatred I hate them, enemies I count as my own." 
Psalms139:23 Probe me, God, know my heart; try me, know my concerns." 
Psalms139:24 See if my way is crooked, then lead me in the ancient paths." 
Psalms140:1 For the leader. A psalm of David. 
Psalms140:2 Deliver me, LORD, from the wicked; preserve me from the violent," 
Psalms140:3 From those who plan evil in their hearts, who stir up conflicts every day," 
Psalms140:4 Who sharpen their tongues like serpents, venom of asps upon their lips. Selah" 
Psalms140:5 Keep me, LORD, from the clutches of the wicked; preserve me from the violent, who plot to trip me up." 
Psalms140:6 The arrogant have set a trap for me; villains have spread a net, laid snares for me by the wayside. Selah" 
Psalms140:7 I say to the LORD: You are my God; listen, LORD, to the words of my prayer." 
Psalms140:8 My revered LORD, my strong helper, my helmet on the day of battle." 
Psalms140:9 LORD, do not grant the desires of the wicked; do not let their plots succeed. Selah" 
Psalms140:10 Around me they raise their proud heads; may the mischief they threaten overwhelm them. 
Psalms140:11 May God rain burning coals upon them, cast them into the grave never more to rise." 
Psalms140:12 Slanderers will not survive on earth; evil will quickly entrap the violent. 
Psalms140:13 For I know the LORD will secure justice for the needy, their rights for the poor." 
Psalms140:14 Then the just will give thanks to your name; the upright will dwell in your presence. 
Psalms141:1 A psalm of David. LORD, I call to you; come quickly to help me; listen to my plea when I call." 
Psalms141:2 Let my prayer be incense before you; my uplifted hands an evening sacrifice. 
Psalms141:3 Set a guard, LORD, before my mouth, a gatekeeper at my lips." 
Psalms141:4 Do not let my heart incline to evil, or yield to any sin. I will never feast upon the fine food of evildoers." 
Psalms141:5 Let the just strike me; that is kindness; let them rebuke me; that is oil for my head. All this I shall not refuse, but will pray despite these trials." 
Psalms141:6 When their leaders are cast over the cliff, all will learn that my prayers were heard." 
Psalms141:7 As when a farmer plows a field into broken clods, so their bones will be strewn at the mouth of Sheol." 
Psalms141:8 My eyes are upon you, O GOD, my Lord; in you I take refuge; do not strip me of life." 
Psalms141:9 Guard me from the trap they have set for me, from the snares of evildoers." 
Psalms141:10 Into their own nets let all the wicked fall, while I make good my own escape." 
Psalms142:1 A maskil of David, when he was in the cave. A prayer." 
Psalms142:2 With full voice I cry to the LORD; with full voice I beseech the LORD. 
Psalms142:3 Before God I pour out my complaint, lay bare my distress." 
Psalms142:4 My spirit is faint within me, but you know my path. Along the way I walk they have hidden a trap for me." 
Psalms142:5 I look to my right hand, but no friend is there. There is no escape for me; no one cares for me." 
Psalms142:6 I cry out to you, LORD, I say, You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living." 
Psalms142:7 Listen to my cry for help, for I am brought very low. Rescue me from my pursuers, for they are too strong for me." 
Psalms142:8 Lead me out of my prison, that I may give thanks to your name. Then the just shall gather around me because you have been good to me." 
Psalms143:1 A psalm of David. LORD, hear my prayer; in your faithfulness listen to my pleading; answer me in your justice." 
Psalms143:2 Do not enter into judgment with your servant; before you no living being can be just. 
Psalms143:3 The enemy has pursued me; they have crushed my life to the ground. They have left me in darkness like those long dead. 
Psalms143:4 My spirit is faint within me; my heart is dismayed. 
Psalms143:5 I remember the days of old; I ponder all your deeds; the works of your hands I recall. 
Psalms143:6 I stretch out my hands to you; I thirst for you like a parched land. Selah 
Psalms143:7 Hasten to answer me, LORD; for my spirit fails me. Do not hide your face from me, lest I become like those descending to the pit." 
Psalms143:8 At dawn let me hear of your kindness, for in you I trust. Show me the path I should walk, for to you I entrust my life." 
Psalms143:9 Rescue me, LORD, from my foes, for in you I hope." 
Psalms143:10 Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. May your kind spirit guide me on ground that is level." 
Psalms143:11 For your name's sake, LORD, give me life; in your justice lead me out of distress." 
Psalms143:12 In your kindness put an end to my foes; destroy all who attack me, for I am your servant. Psalm" 
Psalms144:1 Of David. I Blessed be the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for battle, my fingers for war;" 
Psalms144:2 My safe guard and my fortress, my stronghold, my deliverer, My shield, in whom I trust, who subdues peoples under me." 
Psalms144:3 LORD, what are mortals that you notice them; human beings, that you take thought of them?" 
Psalms144:4 They are but a breath; their days are like a passing shadow. 
Psalms144:5 LORD, incline your heavens and come; touch the mountains and make them smoke." 
Psalms144:6 Flash forth lightning and scatter my foes; shoot your arrows and rout them. 
Psalms144:7 Reach out your hand from on high; deliver me from the many waters; rescue me from the hands of foreign foes. 
Psalms144:8 Their mouths speak untruth; their right hands are raised in lying oaths. 
Psalms144:9 O God, a new song I will sing to you; on a ten-stringed lyre I will play for you." 
Psalms144:10 You give victory to kings; you delivered David your servant. From the menacing sword 
Psalms144:11 deliver me; rescue me from the hands of foreign foes. Their mouths speak untruth; their right hands are raised in lying oaths. 
Psalms144:12 May our sons be like plants well nurtured from their youth, Our daughters, like carved columns, shapely as those of the temple." 
Psalms144:13 May our barns be full with every kind of store. May our sheep increase by thousands, by tens of thousands in our fields; may our oxen be well fattened." 
Psalms144:14 May there be no breach in the walls, no exile, no outcry in our streets." 
Psalms144:15 Happy the people so blessed; happy the people whose God is the LORD. 
Psalms145:1 Praise. Of David. I will extol you, my God and king; I will bless your name forever." 
Psalms145:2 Every day I will bless you; I will praise your name forever. 
Psalms145:3 Great is the LORD and worthy of high praise; God's grandeur is beyond understanding. 
Psalms145:4 One generation praises your deeds to the next and proclaims your mighty works. 
Psalms145:5 They speak of the splendor of your majestic glory, tell of your wonderful deeds." 
Psalms145:6 They speak of your fearsome power and attest to your great deeds. 
Psalms145:7 They publish the renown of your abounding goodness and joyfully sing of your justice. 
Psalms145:8 The LORD is gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in love." 
Psalms145:9 The LORD is good to all, compassionate to every creature." 
Psalms145:10 All your works give you thanks, O LORD and your faithful bless you." 
Psalms145:11 They speak of the glory of your reign and tell of your great works," 
Psalms145:12 Making known to all your power, the glorious splendor of your rule." 
Psalms145:13 Your reign is a reign for all ages, your dominion for all generations. The LORD is trustworthy in every word, and faithful in every work." 
Psalms145:14 The LORD supports all who are falling and raises up all who are bowed down. 
Psalms145:15 The eyes of all look hopefully to you; you give them their food in due season. 
Psalms145:16 You open wide your hand and satisfy the desire of every living thing. 
Psalms145:17 You, LORD, are just in all your ways, faithful in all your works." 
Psalms145:18 You, LORD, are near to all who call upon you, to all who call upon you in truth." 
Psalms145:19 You satisfy the desire of those who fear you; you hear their cry and save them. 
Psalms145:20 You, LORD, watch over all who love you, but all the wicked you destroy." 
Psalms145:21 My mouth will speak your praises, LORD; all flesh will bless your holy name forever." 
Psalms146:1 Hallelujah! 
Psalms146:2 Praise the LORD, my soul; I shall praise the LORD all my life, sing praise to my God while I live." 
Psalms146:3 Put no trust in princes, in mere mortals powerless to save." 
Psalms146:4 When they breathe their last, they return to the earth; that day all their planning comes to nothing." 
Psalms146:5 Happy those whose help is Jacob's God, whose hope is in the LORD, their God," 
Psalms146:6 The maker of heaven and earth, the seas and all that is in them, Who keeps faith forever," 
Psalms146:7 secures justice for the oppressed, gives food to the hungry. The LORD sets prisoners free;" 
Psalms146:8 the LORD gives sight to the blind. The LORD raises up those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous. 
Psalms146:9 The LORD protects the stranger, sustains the orphan and the widow, but thwarts the way of the wicked." 
Psalms146:10 The LORD shall reign forever, your God, Zion, through all generations! Hallelujah!" 
Psalms147:1 Hallelujah! I How good to celebrate our God in song; how sweet to give fitting praise. 
Psalms147:2 The LORD rebuilds Jerusalem, gathers the dispersed of Israel," 
Psalms147:3 Heals the brokenhearted, binds up their wounds," 
Psalms147:4 Numbers all the stars, calls each of them by name." 
Psalms147:4 Numbers all the stars, calls each of them by name." 
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord, vast in power, with wisdom beyond measure." 
Psalms147:6 The LORD sustains the poor, but casts the wicked to the ground." 
Psalms147:7 Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; with the lyre celebrate our God," 
Psalms147:8 Who covers the heavens with clouds, provides rain for the earth, makes grass sprout on the mountains," 
Psalms147:9 Who gives animals their food and ravens what they cry for. 
Psalms147:10 God takes no delight in the strength of horses, no pleasure in the runner's stride." 
Psalms147:11 Rather the LORD takes pleasure in the devout, those who await his faithful care." 
Psalms147:12 Glorify the LORD, Jerusalem; Zion, offer praise to your God," 
Psalms147:13 Who has strengthened the bars of your gates, blessed your children within you," 
Psalms147:14 Brought peace to your borders, and filled you with finest wheat." 
Psalms147:15 The LORD sends a command to earth; his word runs swiftly! 
Psalms147:16 Thus snow is spread like wool, frost is scattered like ash," 
Psalms147:17 Hail is dispersed like crumbs; before such cold the waters freeze. 
Psalms147:18 Again he sends his word and they melt; the wind is unleashed and the waters flow. 
Psalms147:19 The LORD also proclaims his word to Jacob, decrees and laws to Israel." 
Psalms147:20 God has not done this for other nations; of such laws they know nothing. Hallelujah! 
Psalms148:1 Hallelujah! I Praise the LORD from the heavens; give praise in the heights. 
Psalms148:2 Praise him, all you angels; give praise, all you hosts." 
Psalms148:3 Praise him, sun and moon; give praise, all shining stars." 
Psalms148:4 Praise him, highest heavens, you waters above the heavens." 
Psalms148:5 Let them all praise the LORD'S name; for the LORD commanded and they were created," 
Psalms148:6 Assigned them duties forever, gave them tasks that will never change." 
Psalms148:7 Praise the LORD from the earth, you sea monsters and all deep waters;" 
Psalms148:8 You lightning and hail, snow and clouds, storm winds that fulfill his command;" 
Psalms148:9 You mountains and all hills, fruit trees and all cedars;" 
Psalms148:10 You animals wild and tame, you creatures that crawl and fly;" 
Psalms148:11 You kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all who govern on earth;" 
Psalms148:12 Young men and women too, old and young alike." 
Psalms148:13 Let them all praise the LORD'S name, for his name alone is exalted, majestic above earth and heaven." 
Psalms148:14 The LORD has lifted high the horn of his people; to the glory of all the faithful, of Israel, the people near to their God. Hallelujah!" 
Psalms149:1 Hallelujah! Sing to the LORD a new song, a hymn in the assembly of the faithful." 
Psalms149:2 Let Israel be glad in their maker, the people of Zion rejoice in their king." 
Psalms149:3 Let them praise his name in festive dance, make music with tambourine and lyre." 
Psalms149:4 For the LORD takes delight in his people, honors the poor with victory." 
Psalms149:5 Let the faithful rejoice in their glory, cry out for joy at their banquet," 
Psalms149:6 With the praise of God in their mouths, and a two-edged sword in their hands," 
Psalms149:7 To bring retribution on the nations, punishment on the peoples," 
Psalms149:8 To bind their kings with chains, shackle their nobles with irons," 
Psalms149:9 To execute the judgments decreed for them, such is the glory of all God's faithful. Hallelujah! 
Psalms150:1 Hallelujah! Praise God in his holy sanctuary; give praise in the mighty dome of heaven. 
Psalms150:2 Give praise for his mighty deeds, praise him for his great majesty." 
Psalms150:3 Give praise with blasts upon the horn, praise him with harp and lyre." 
Psalms150:4 Give praise with tambourines and dance, praise him with flutes and strings." 
Psalms150:5 Give praise with crashing cymbals, praise him with sounding cymbals." 
Psalms150:6 Let everything that has breath give praise to the LORD! Hallelujah! 
Proverbs1:1 The Proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:" 
Proverbs1:2 That men may appreciate wisdom and discipline, may understand words of intelligence;" 
Proverbs1:3 May receive training in wise conduct, in what is right, just and honest;" 
Proverbs1:4 That resourcefulness may be imparted to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion." 
Proverbs1:5 A wise man by hearing them will advance in learning, an intelligent man will gain sound guidance," 
Proverbs1:6 That he may comprehend proverb and parable, the words of the wise and their riddles." 
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; wisdom and instruction fools despise. 
Proverbs1:8 Hear, my son, your father's instruction, and reject not your mother's teaching;" 
Proverbs1:9 A graceful diadem will they be for your head; a torque for your neck. 
Proverbs1:10 My son, should sinners entice you," 
Proverbs1:11 and say, ""Come along with us! Let us lie in wait for the honest man, let us, unprovoked, set a trap for the innocent;" 
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them up, as the nether world does, alive, in the prime of life, like those who go down to the pit!" 
Proverbs1:13 All kinds of precious wealth shall we gain, we shall fill our houses with booty;" 
Proverbs1:14 Cast in your lot with us, we shall all have one purse!""," 
Proverbs1:15 My son, walk not in the way with them, hold back your foot from their path!" 
Proverbs1:16 (For their feet run to evil, they hasten to shed blood.)" 
Proverbs1:17 It is in vain that a net is spread before the eyes of any bird, 
Proverbs1:18 These men lie in wait for their own blood, they set a trap for their own lives." 
Proverbs1:19 This is the fate of everyone greedy of loot: unlawful gain takes away the life of him who acquires it. 
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom cries aloud in the street, in the open squares she raises her voice;" 
Proverbs1:21 Down the crowded ways she calls out, at the city gates she utters her words:" 
Proverbs1:22 How long, you simple ones, will you love inanity," 
Proverbs1:23 how long will you turn away at my reproof? Lo! I will pour out to you my spirit, I will acquaint you with my words." 
Proverbs1:24 Because I called and you refused, I extended my hand and no one took notice;" 
Proverbs1:25 Because you disdained all my counsel, and my reproof you ignored," 
Proverbs1:26 I, in my turn, will laugh at your doom; I will mock when terror overtakes you;" 
Proverbs1:27 When terror comes upon you like a storm, and your doom approaches like a whirlwind; when distress and anguish befall you." 
Proverbs1:28 Then they call me, but I answer not; they seek me, but find me not;" 
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge, and chose not the fear of the LORD;" 
Proverbs1:30 They ignored my counsel, they spurned all my reproof; And in their arrogance they preferred arrogance, and like fools they hated knowledge:" 
Proverbs1:31 Now they must eat the fruit of their own way, and with their own devices be glutted." 
Proverbs1:32 For the self-will of the simple kills them, the smugness of fools destroys them." 
Proverbs1:33 But he who obeys me dwells in security, in peace, without fear of harm.""" 
Proverbs2:1 My son, if you receive my words and treasure my commands," 
Proverbs2:2 Turning your ear to wisdom, inclining your heart to understanding;" 
Proverbs2:3 Yes, if you call to intelligence, and to understanding raise your voice;" 
Proverbs2:4 If you seek her like silver, and like hidden treasures search her out:" 
Proverbs2:5 Then will you understand the fear of the LORD; the knowledge of God you will find; 
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom, from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;" 
Proverbs2:7 He has counsel in store for the upright, he is the shield of those who walk honestly," 
Proverbs2:8 Guarding the paths of justice, protecting the way of his pious ones." 
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand rectitude and justice, honesty, every good path;" 
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart, knowledge will please your soul," 
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will watch over you, understanding will guard you;" 
Proverbs2:12 Saving you from the way of evil men, from men of perverse speech," 
Proverbs2:13 Who leave the straight paths to walk in the way of darkness," 
Proverbs2:14 Who delight in doing evil, rejoice in perversity;" 
Proverbs2:15 Whose ways are crooked, and devious their paths;" 
Proverbs2:16 Saving you from the wife of another, from the adulteress with her smooth words," 
Proverbs2:17 Who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the pact with her God; 
Proverbs2:18 For her path sinks down to death, and her footsteps lead to the shades;" 
Proverbs2:19 None who enter thereon come back again, or gain the paths of life." 
Proverbs2:20 Thus you may walk in the way of good men, and keep to the paths of the just." 
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will dwell in the land, the honest will remain in it;" 
Proverbs2:22 But the wicked will be cut off from the land, the faithless will be rooted out of it." 
Proverbs3:1 My son, forget not my teaching, keep in mind my commands;" 
Proverbs3:2 For many days, and years of life, and peace, will they bring you." 
Proverbs3:3 Let not kindness and fidelity leave you; bind them around your neck; 
Proverbs3:4 Then will you win favor and good esteem before God and man. 
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart, on your own intelligence rely not;" 
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways be mindful of him, and he will make straight your paths." 
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes, fear the LORD and turn away from evil;"
